Future enhancements include improved noise resistance, cross-platform support, and AI-based optimizations for greater efficiency and stealth.

title: Design and Development of an Automated Plant Sowing Machine for Agricultural Efficiency
authors: Lope U. Codilla, Alissa Mae R. Celeste, Jerry I. Teleron, Tracy P. Pantilo, Jovierick B. Adlao
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: This study focuses on the design and development of an automated plant-sowing machine to enhance production efficiency and minimize human effort. The machine operates through five key stages: detection of the seedling tray via IR sensors, soil dispensing, water sprinkling, seed dispersion, additional soil layering, and final water sprinkling. A manually fabricated PCB, powered by an Atmega328p microcontroller, controls the machine’s operations, while a custom-built power supply energizes the microcontroller and water pump. The system integrates a 220 V AC motor to drive the conveyor belt and soil roller, alongside an MG996R servo motor for seed dispensing. The study follows the Deming cycle methodology, focusing on continuous improvement through its iterative fourstep process. The machine was successfully installed and tested, achieving a production rate of 84.5 units per hour over a fourhour operational period. The production rate is influenced by the size of the seedling tray, which affects seed dispersion and soil distribution, demonstrating the machine’s high efficiency. This research aligns with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), particularly Goal 2 (Zero Hunger) and Goal 9 (Industry, Innovation, and Infrastructure). By increasing agricultural productivity and efficiency, the developed machine addresses global food security challenges while promoting sustainable and innovative practices in farming. The study contributes to government initiatives and global efforts aimed at supporting small-scale farmers, reducing manual labor, and advancing sustainable agricultural development.

title: Optimization Methodology for a GaN-based Single-Phase Online UPS Using Combined Variable On-Time and Fixed-Frequency Control
authors: Xuancen Wu, Syed Saeed Rashid, Abdullah Saboor, Khurram K. Afridi
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents an optimization methodology for a novel GaN-based online Uninterruptible Power Supply (UPS). This UPS features a new multi-mode transformerless topology with a single dc bus. Both the rectification and inversion stages of the multi-mode UPS operate in buck, boost and buck-boost modes depending on the line-voltage magnitudes and polarities, enabling universal input/output voltage capability while using 650-V GaN transistors. A control strategy that combines variable on-time and fixed-frequency control within a single line cycle is applied to improve efficiency. The proposed optimization methodology generates multi-mode UPS designs within an expanded design space that includes the transition angle in the combined control between variable on-time and fixed-frequency control within a line cycle, along with other design parameters. Pareto-optimal designs are identified in terms of loss, volume, and cost. The proposed approach yields more efficient and compact designs compared with approaches that assume only variable on- time control or only fixed-frequency control. A 1.2-kVA prototype of the UPS is designed, built and tested. The power density of the main power stage is designed to be 46.3W/in<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>. The combined control is experimentally validated up to 800 W, achieving a peak efficiency of 97.4%, outperforming fixed-frequency control (96.5% efficiency) with a 24.9% experimental loss reduction.

title: Comparative Evaluation of DenseNet, ResNet, and GoogLeNet Architectures
authors: Mosleh M. Abualhaj, Abdallah Hyassat, Ahmad Shalaldeh, Sultan Aldossary, Manjur Kolhar, Sumaya Al-Khatib, Iyas Qaddara, Motasem M. Elshourbagy
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: Image classification is an essential task in computer vision. Due to the increasing demand for precise and effective visual recognition systems. Convolutional neural networks have shown strong capability in learning expressive feature representations directly from data, eliminating the need for manual hand crafted feature design and features selection. This paper presents a comparative evaluation of three widely adopted deep learning models. The DenseNet, ResNet, and GoogLeNet are tested using the CIFAR-100 dataset. The evaluation is performed using four standard metrics, classification accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score to provide a complete assessment of the models performance. In addition, a grayscale-based data augmentation strategy is applied to improve robustness during training. Experimental results show that DenseNet consistently achieves higher performance across all evaluation metrics compared to the other architectures. The results suggest that dense feature reuse and effective information flow contribute significantly to improved classification performance. Overall, the study highlights the effectiveness of DenseNet as a reliable architecture for image classification tasks.

title: A Closed-Corpus Hybrid Retrieval-Augmented Legal Assistant and Large Language Models for Lex9165 System
authors: John Matthew T. Pagdanganan, Ricardo T. Gayagoy, Jamie Del Rosario, Lysa V. Comia
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: Legal question-answering systems require high factual accuracy, grounding, and source traceability due to the risks associated with misinformation. This paper presents Lex9165, a closed-corpus legal assistant developed for Republic Act No. 9165 (Comprehensive Dangerous Drugs Act of 2002) of the Philippines. The system adopts a hybrid RetrievalAugmented Generation (RAG) architecture that combines BM25 sparse retrieval, BGE-base-en-v1.5 dense embeddings, HNSW vector indexing, a cross-encoder reranker (bge-reranker-large), and LingMessCoref for coreference resolution. Response generation is performed using Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct under a strictly restricted corpus configuration to prevent external knowledge access during inference. The evaluation uses 100 curated legal queries, split into 50 for retrieval evaluation and 50 for response evaluation using the RAGAS framework. Retrieval results demonstrate strong performance, achieving Precision@20 of 5.5%, Recall@20 of 79.7%, Hit@20 of 94%, MAP@20 of 64.4%, MRR@20 of 77.4%, and nDCG@20 of 71.3%. Response-level evaluation shows high factual reliability with Faithfulness of 77.9%, Groundedness of 97.5%, Context Relevance of 93.0%, and Response Relevancy of 79.8%. A comparative analysis across selected Qwen2.5-Instruct model variants (3B-32B) indicates that Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct provides the best balance between response quality, factual grounding, and computational efficiency under the closed-corpus RAG pipeline. These results demonstrate that hybrid retrieval combined with strict corpus restriction significantly improves legal grounding and reduces hallucination risk, establishing a reliable approach for domain-specific statutory assistance systems.

title: Fault-tolerant Operation of 5L-ANPC Inverter with High Performance Modulation and Control
authors: Pengwei Li, Yi Luo, Ali Bazzi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Intelligent Design and Control of Automation and Drive Systems (IDCD)
abstract: Multilevel inverters have been adopted in medium power level industry applications due to their merits, including lower dv/dt, topology redundancy, and low total harmonics distortion. Among classical multilevel inverters, a hybrid 5L-ANPC topology embedding a flying capacitor has attracted attention recently. Motivated by applications in single-phase PV system inverters, this paper presents a robust modulation strategy that considers switch-fault conditions and harmonic compensation. The switching-state analysis and phase-shifted (PS) modulation are detailed. Considering open-circuit (OC) and short-circuit (SC) faults in semiconductor devices, a real-time fault-tolerant operation method is proposed and discussed. Its fault-tolerance capability is evaluated and compared with the other three counterparts’ topologies. In the meantime, to improve the quality of the output voltage and reduce AC THD, a neutral-point voltage (NPV) observer is proposed to compensate for fluctuations and reduce AC output distortion. The proposed high-performance modulation scheme is validated in the experimental setup. Comparison results verified the advanced fault-tolerance capability and improved output power quality.

title: An Empirical Evaluation of Open-Source LLMs for Philippine Labor Law Question Answering Using Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Cedric Isaiah D. Bautista, Alisandro N. Domingo, John Paul Benedict R. Monteon, Lysa V. Comia
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: This study evaluates the effectiveness of opensource large language models (LLMs) for answering questions about the Philippine Labor Code using a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework. Three instruction-tuned models-LLaMA 3.1 8B, Qwen2.5 7B, and Gemma 2 9B-were deployed within an identical hybrid retrieval pipeline combining BM25, dense embeddings, reciprocal rank fusion, and cross-encoder reranking. Evaluation was conducted on 30 benchmark queries (27 in-scope and 3 out-of-scope) using metrics including semantic similarity, faithfulness, answer relevancy, citation accuracy, LLM-as-a-Judge scoring, and response latency. Retrieval performance was identical across models (Recall $\approx 0.99$; Precision $\approx 0.31$), while differences emerged in generation quality. Gemma 29 B achieved the highest semantic similarity (0.859), citation accuracy (0.935), and judge score (0.733), indicating stronger legal grounding. Qwen2.5 7B showed the fastest responses (8.89 s) and highest faithfulness, while LLaMA 3.1 8B achieved slightly higher answer relevancy. All models correctly refused 100% of out-ofscope queries. The results demonstrate that RAG-enhanced open-source LLMs can provide reliable and accessible legal information systems for Philippine labor law assistance.

title: Artificial Neural Network Prediction of Young Modulus in PLA Bone Scaffold Fabricated Using Additive Manufacturing Process
authors: Ros Atikah Abdul Kadir, Ab Aziz Mohd Yusof, Muhammad Hussain Ismail, Mohd Shahrom Ismail, Jamaluddin Mahmud
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: The fabrication of bone scaffolds using Additive Manufacturing (AM) demands precise control of mechanical properties to ensure biomechanical compatibility with surrounding tissue and to mitigate stress shielding effects. Conventionally, the characterization of these properties relies on destructive testing method which are both time consuming and resource intensive. This study investigates the application of an Artificial Neural Network (ANN) for predicting the Young Modulus of Polylactic (PLA) bone scaffolds fabricated via Fused Deposition Modelling (FDM). Cylindrical Scaffolds with porosities range from $40 \%$ to $70 \%$ were manufactured and subjected to uniaxial compression testing to generate experimental data for model training. A three-layer feedforward neural network (FFNN) trained using the Levenberg Marquardt algorithm was developed to capture the non-linear relationship between the input parameters (porosity, stress, strain) and the output parameter (Young’s Modulus). The model showed an exceptional predictive performance with Regression Coefficient $(R)$ equal to 0.9974. The ANN predicted stiffness values with an absolute percentage error of less than 3% for majority samples with a maximum deviation of $\mathbf{1 0. 5 1 \%}$ only at high porosity levels. Furthermore, validation in Simulink proved that the model’s effectiveness as virtual testing platform. These findings suggest that ANN-based modelling offers a cost effective and precise method over traditional mechanical experimental works for optimizing bio-scaffolds structures.

title: Multilevel Step-Up DC–DC Converter for Direct Interfacing of Offshore Wind Turbine to HVDC Grid: Modal Analysis and Experimental Validation
authors: Shibaji Basu, Praveen Jain
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel multilevel step-up DC-DC converter architecture for directly connecting offshore wind turbines to HVDC grid. The converter features a multi-primary, single common secondary adder transformer configuration, where multiple primaries are powered by high-frequency resonant inverters, and the resulting secondary voltage is rectified by a voltage doubler rectifier. The voltage doubler is implemented using half-bridge modular multilevel converter (MMC) modules, making the converter ideal for HVDC applications. While initial design considerations were introduced in [1], this paper presents a more comprehensive modal analysis that defines MMC module filter design principles and informs operating specification adjustments to maintain HVDC bus voltage under varying input power. Furthermore, it provides a preliminary introduction to a more comprehensive adder transformer design methodology validated through ANSYS simulation. Designed to generate 160 kV at 1.5–10 MW from a rectified variable-speed wind turbine generator, the converter achieves high peak efficiency (98.2%), natural current balancing in the primary resonant inverters despite component tolerances, MMC module capacitor voltage balancing without current sensors, and soft-switching for all switches under all operating conditions. The concept is demonstrated by a scaled experimental prototype achieving peak efficiency of 97.48% with a power range of 0.15-0.5 kW and input voltage range of 34-90 V.

title: IPOS LLC Resonant Converter with Reconfigurable Output for 400V and 800V Architectures in EV Charging
authors: Yu Zuo, Yang Li, Qingcheng Sui, Diego Bernal Cobaleda, Bangli Du, Jiaze Kong, Xiaobing Shen, Wilmar Martinez
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Electric vehicle charging is transitioning from 400V to 800V architectures, enabling higher power and faster charging. Off-board DC-DC converters are required to accommodate ultra-wide voltage and power ranges to ensure compatibility with both architectures. Currently, wide output voltage ranges are typically achieved through topology modifications, two-stage conversions, or hybrid modulation, with series-parallel modular operation commonly used to increase output power. Different from existing approaches, this paper introduces an input-parallel output-serial converter with a simple on-off relay switch, enabling two distinct operating modes. This design not only extends the output voltage range without requiring auxiliary circuits or complex modulation but also preserves the advantages of modular operation. Additionally, the converter provides automatic voltage balancing for the series-connected output side, while coupled inductors facilitate current sharing, even with intentional inconsistencies in resonant parameter values on the parallel input side. Furthermore, the converter’s control is simplified through pulse frequency modulation within a narrow switching frequency range, leveraging the characteristics of LLC resonant converters to regulate the output voltage for both 400V and 800V charging systems. Moreover, soft-switching is maintained across the entire voltage range. Finally, experimental results from a 1kW prototype with a 400V input and a 200-800V output validate the proposed approach, achieving a peak efficiency of 93.7%, and above 92% overall voltage range.

title: A Novel Active Power Control for Wind Energy System for Improved Frequency Regulation
authors: Md Hasnain Arifin, Md Shamim Hasan, Sukumar Kamalasadan, Michael Smith, Andrew Crosby, Krishna Murari
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: The increasing integration of renewable energy resources (RES) introduces new challenges for power system stability, primarily due to their intermittent nature and converter-driven dynamics. Among RES, wind energy systems (WES) represent a major share, making their effective control critical for maintaining frequency stability in low-inertia grids. This paper proposes an improved active power control (APC) strategy for WES by incorporating a simplified pitch control together with modified machine-side and grid-side inverter control. The proposed controller is designed to enhance the frequency support capability of wind turbines and mitigate instability following disturbances. System performance is evaluated using key frequency stability metrics, including the rate of change of frequency (RoCoF) and frequency nadir. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed APC improves frequency regulation by approximately 19% in Frequency Nadir compared to conventional WES control approaches, thereby enhancing the overall stability of RES-dominated power systems.

title: A Bridge-Pair Magnetic Field Detection Architecture for Enhanced dv/dt Rejection in Switch Node Proximity Current Sensing
authors: Hossein Niakan, Ali Farahani, James Gafford, Babak Parkhideh
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Modern power conversion systems incorporate switch-mode power supplies to efficiently manage and control power throughout the network. The growing demand for higher efficiency and power density has enabled megahertz conversion using wide-bandgap devices, where instrumentation becomes prone to unwanted interference and noise. In this work, a novel approach provides current-sensing immunity against extreme $\frac{{dv}}{{dt}}$ incidents. This extends proximity magnetic-field bridge detectors to switch-node integration, a key enabler of compact power-management units. The experimental results demonstrate effective compensation for high $\frac{{dv}}{{dt}}$–coupled noise on the switching node of a synchronous GaN HEMT buck converter operating at 500 kHz and experiencing transients above 14 V/ns. The approach is implemented using two same-plane sensing dies, increasing sensitivity and maintaining a compact, surface-mount form factor.

title: AI-Powered Hypertension Prediction: Leveraging Machine Learning for Early Detection
authors: Nidal Turab, Shahad Abu Hedeel, Batool Al Zubaidi, Suhila Abuowaida, Ahmad Aburomman
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: Machine learning algorithms can enhance early diagnosis, treatment, and patient outcomes for hypertension, a significant worldwide health concern. This study classifies the presence of hypertension or not (yes, no) into classes using a Kaggle dataset that includes demographic and physical features. To assess their ability for prediction, the model integrates classifiers built using machine learning techniques such as Support Vector Machine (SVM), Naive Bayes (NB), and Random Forest (RF). The effectiveness of each classifier was evaluated using performance metrics such as area Under Curve (AUC), accuracy, F1-score, and precision. The maximum Area Under the Curve (AUC) was 98.50% for the RF method, 97.40% with NB, and 91.70% for SVM. These findings show that sophisticated machine learning algorithms may be used to accurately forecast hypertension, which will help medical professionals make well-informed decisions about patient care.

title: Deep Learning Framework for Multi-Class Noise Classification in Mid-Infrared Supercontinuum Generation
authors: Rakayet Rafi, Rifat Ibne Musa, M. R. Karim, M. Shah Alam
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: Supercontinuum generation (SCG) is a nonlinear phenomenon that is highly sensitive to noise. Noise can cause spectral fluctuations that degrade coherence and stability in practical applications. While recent machine learning approaches have mainly focused on regression-based spectral prediction, classifying noise-induced degradation has received comparatively little attention. In this work, we introduce a deep learning framework for multi-class classification of noise levels in supercontinuum spectra generated in a dispersion-engineered silicon nitride $\left(\operatorname{Si}_{3} \mathrm{~N}_{4}\right)$ waveguide. A dataset of 1920 spectra is created using generalized nonlinear Schrödinger equation (GNLSE) simulations under varying pump wavelengths, pulse widths, and peak powers with controlled phase noise of different levels. Noise strength is quantified through average spectral deviation about the -40 dB spectral bandwidth and divided into five discrete classes ranging from No Noise to High Noise as per the magnitudes. The proposed hybrid architecture combines a one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D-CNN) for spectral feature extraction with a multilayer perceptron (MLP) that ensembles the input laser parameters. The optimized model achieves an accuracy of $94.27 \%$ and a macro-averaged F 1 -score of 0.94, reliably classifying all noise levels. These results demonstrate that classification-based noise evaluation offers an interpretable and scalable approach for monitoring spectral quality in integrated broadband sources.

title: Unsupervised Video Anomaly Detection with Swin Transformer and Temporal-Context Modeling
authors: Yi Sun, Bryan W. Scotney, Xiushan Nie, Shuai Zhang, Xingbo Liu, Lanting Qiu
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: Video anomaly detection has broad applications in public safety, health monitoring, and emergency response. Existing transformer-based methods often struggle to capture multi-scale spatio-temporal patterns effectively. In this paper, we propose an unsupervised video anomaly detection framework that enhances feature representation by combining a 3D Swin Transformer with temporal shift modules and dynamic large kernel (DLK) convolutions. The 3D encoder-decoder structure models normal behavior from video sequences, and anomalies are identified through reconstruction errors. We validate our method on three public datasets: ShanghaiTech, Avenue, and Ped2. Our model achieves competitive accuracy, demonstrating improved capacity for capturing temporal dynamics and contextual details.

title: Integrated Planar Transformer with Embedded Zero-Sequence Inductance for Active Power Decoupling in Three-Phase Dual Active Bridge Converters
authors: Yi Han, Satyam Sa, Olivier Trescases
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The power density of battery chargers is constrained by the large dc-link capacitors required to buffer double-line-frequency (DLF) ripple power, which limit achievable volumetric power density and lifetime. Active power decoupling (APD) can reduce the capacitor volume requirement, but it typically relies on an additional inductor. The zero-sequence inductance (ZSI) of the three-phase (TP) transformer is shown to serve as the APD inductor in a TP dual-active-bridge (DAB) converter, enabling up to 40% reduction in capacitor volume when buffering half of the ripple power, thereby motivating a practical implementation. A planar TP transformer with integrated ZSI is presented for a 300-kHz GaN-based TP-DAB converter. A five-limb gapped core structure and a low-capacitance PCB winding layout were developed to achieve the required ZSI and support high-frequency switching. The flux distribution was analyzed via finite-element simulation, and the analytical ZSI model was validated using hardware measurements from 10-turn and 14-turn prototypes. Experimental results from a 2-kW dc-dc prototype demonstrate effective ripple-power buffering through the transformer neutral port, with the higher-ZSI 14-turn design halving the APD current ripple and incurring only a 3% efficiency penalty relative to the 96.4% baseline. These results verify that the transformer consolidates power-transfer and decoupling functions, enabling APD without a separate inductor.

title: RL-Driven Proactive Self-Healing in 5G Core Networks Using Semantic Log Anomaly Detection
authors: M. Ayyadurai, Nandha Kumar P, Varun Kumar V, Sujith S S
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: The level of complexity and volume of data of 5G networks make the conventional reactive fault management approach unsustainable. To solve this, we propose a proactive, closed-loop self-healing system which is operated by means of Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) and semantic readings of logs. Our system generates semantic embeddings out of unstructured logs so that we can perform unsupervised detection of anomalies of subtle abnormalities. When a DRL agent notices it, it automatically runs an optimal solution to the problem of remediation that could be component restarts or redirecting the traffic to ensure that service disruption is minimal. Moreover, an integrated Explainable AI(XAI) system offers clear, non-mathematic reasonings explaining to the operator the rationale behind these automated processes. The experimental results prove that the framework is superior and it has a high rate of remediation about 90 percent and a considerable low resolution time compared to the standard baselines.

title: Crestomics: Analyzing Carotid Plaques in the Crest-2 Trial with a New Additive Classification Model
authors: Pranav Kulkarni, Brajesh K. Lal, Georges Jreij, Sai Vallamchetla, Langford Green, Jenifer Voeks, John Huston, Lloyd Edwards, George Howard, Bradley A. Maron, Thomas G. Brott, James F. Meschia, Florence X. Doo, Heng Huang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Accurate characterization of carotid plaques is critical for stroke prevention in patients with carotid stenosis. We analyze 500 plaques from CREST-2, a multi-center clinical trial, to identify radiomics-based markers from B-mode ultrasound images linked with high-risk. We propose a new kernel-based additive model, combining coherence loss with group-sparse regularization for nonlinear classification. Group-wise additive effects of each feature group are visualized using partial dependence plots. Results indicate our method accurately and interpretably assesses plaques, revealing a strong association between plaque texture and clinical risk.

title: Real-Time Telemetry Modernization Using Distributed Stream Analytics for Global Product Monitoring Systems
authors: Manushi Manish Sheth
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: The accelerated development of Industry 4.0 and intelligent manufacturing has heightened the need to find effective real-time and predictive maintenance services in global production. This paper presents a real-time telemetry modernization system with distributed stream analytics and machine learning for the global product monitoring systems. The framework uses the Real-Time IoT-Driven Production System dataset that has <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2, 4 6 0}$</tex> records and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 4}$</tex> telemetry features to create predictive models to classify maintenance and identify anomalies. The extensive preprocessing of data, feature engineering, SMOTE-ENN balancing of the classes. Three XGBoost, Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) and Isolation Forest models were applied and tested on the basis of accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, MCC, and ROC-AUC. The experiments revealed that the XGBoost model recorded the highest accuracy of 0.9878, whereas MLP model recorded the highest ROC-AUC with an accuracy of 0.9858. The baseline of the Isolation Forest had a lower performance of 0.7967 accuracy and 0.4639 ROC-AUC. The explainable Artificial Intelligence models like SHAP and LIME have been used to understand the model predictions and determine the important features that affect the maintenance decisions. The suggested framework enhances tremendously predictive precision, transparency and real-time industrial telemetry monitoring with global product monitoring systems.

title: Fabrication Tolerant Topology Optimized Multi-Layer Foundry Grating Couplers
authors: Michael J. Probst, Jacob M. Hiesener, Arjun Khurana, Stephen E. Ralph
abstract: We experimentally demonstrate a layer restriction method for topology optimization to improve performance in multi-layer structures with unwanted conformal layering. We design grating couplers with and without the layer restriction and demonstrate 0.5 dB closer alignment to simulation in the variant with the restriction technique.

title: DM Conducted EMI Noise Prediction for Variable-Frequency, Variable-On-Time Interleaved Integrated TCM Totem-Pole PFC Converters
authors: Zhehui Guo, Yusheng Chen, Ripun Phukan, Misha Kumar, Peter Barbosa
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents a numerical modeling method based on the Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT) for predicting the differential-mode (DM) conducted electromagnetic interference (EMI) noise of two-phase interleaved integrated triangular current mode (iTCM) totem-pole PFC converters. The proposed method incorporates the time-varying switching-frequency and on-time characteristics of iTCM operation, models the drain-to-source voltages as noise sources, and uses STFT to extract their instantaneous harmonic spectra over the line cycle. The dominant harmonic contributions and frequency-domain peak envelopes are identified for both single-phase and interleaved operation, providing detailed insight into the noise-generation mechanism. DM noise transfer gains (DMTGs) are analytically derived for both single-phase and interleaved circuits. The predicted DM noise spectra are validated on a 4-kW iTCM totem-pole PFC prototype. Experimental results show close agreement with the predictions, with peak DM noise measured at 100.78 dBμV at 137 kHz for single-phase operation and reduced to 91.32 dBμV at 274.7 kHz under interleaving. The proposed STFT-based modeling framework offers an accurate and efficient tool for DM EMI noise prediction and DM filter pre-design over varying input-voltage and load operating conditions.

title: Federated Learning: A Novel Framework for Parkinson’s Disease Detection
authors: Kausar Ali, Maaz Shamim, Mohd Adnan, Aditya Narendra
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)
abstract: Inadequate dopamine production from brain dopamine-producing cells leads to Parkinson’s disease which causes dysfunction of body movements. The main disability factors of this disorder include impaired mobility together with impaired speech and vision difficulties while excretory function disruptions emerge as well. The detection algorithms for Parkinson’s disease based on machine learning and deep learning show noteworthy progress yet they depend mainly on concentrated data collection systems that present major difficulties. The combination of data security risks and medical information exposure to hackers together with excessive data transfer expenses towards centralized repositories results in major patient privacy and data protection implications. The purpose of this work involves implementing federated learning techniques for Parkinson’s disease diagnosis because traditional machine learning methods lack robust data security mechanisms and substantial privacy protections and centralized data computational limitations. The research uses different classification methods in a Federated Learning context to accurately identify Parkinson’s disease through proper resolution of detected limitations. Model implementation and result analysis constitute the final parts of the methodology alongside data collection, preprocessing and exploration stages. The research utilized and evaluated Artificial Neural Network (ANN) together with Ridge Classifier and Logistic Regression. The Ridge Classifier executed as the most effective model by reaching 87.17% overall classification accuracy. The assessment shows that federated learning delivers superior performance when compared to conventional machine learning with minor benefits while overcoming issues of classic machine learning approaches.

title: Adaptive Reinforcement Learning for Advanced Interference Mitigation in Future Wireless Networks
authors: Saif Mohamed Baraa Alsabti, Ghassan Hamed Farhan, Aymen Mohammed Al-Dulaimi, Ali Obied, Mustafa Abdulirees Jebur, Shashi Kant Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: The recent developments of 5G and beyond (B5G) wireless networks have placed the issue of interference management as a highly important problem, especially in dynamic channel conditions and radio-frequency (RF) hardware constraints. Nevertheless, several of the current methods of mitigating interference are either non-adaptive or not adequately so in response to real-time changes, which limits their applicability in practice. The given paper suggests a flexible hybrid system of interference mitigation, which combines traditional signal processing with deep learning (DL) and reinforcement learning (RL) to enhance interference mitigation in wireless communication networks. The implementation and test of the proposed method is performed in MATLAB/Simulink when realistic RF impairments, such as the I/Q imbalance, the phase noise, and the nonlinearity of the power-amplifier, are considered. Error Vector Magnitude (EVM), Adjacent Channel Rejection (ACR) and Bit Error Rate (BER) are used to measure system performance. The findings show that there is better interference mitigation performance and system robustness using the proposed adaptive DL-RL strategy. In the reported situations, the RL-enhanced technique minimizes BER of 0.7 at 0 dB (severe interference condition) to $1 \times 106$ at 15 dB SIR and ACR values of 0.48 at 0 dB SIR and 0.35 at 5 dB SIR are achieved under the chosen evaluation environment. Less variability in performance and greater predictability are also indicated by CDF-based analysis. These results indicate that the integration of DL and RL together with traditional methods can offer a viable and workable solution regarding the minimization of interference, throughput improvement, and dependable communication in 5G/B5G networks in the future.

title: Gain-scheduling Offline MPC for Air Path qLPV System Control in Spark Ignition Engine using Electric Supercharger
authors: Rosana C. B. Rego, Camila O. De Lima, Matheus H. R. Miranda, Jony J. Eckert, Andre V. Bueno, Grace Deaecto, Ludmila C. A. Silva
abstract: Electric superchargers eliminate turbo lag in spark ignition (SI) engines by providing instantaneous boost pressure response independent of exhaust gas energy. However, coordinating the throttle valve and eBoost motor to track driver torque demands poses significant control challenges due to highly nonlinear air path dynamics, strict actuator constraints, and real-time execution requirements on resource-constrained automotive Electronic Control Units (ECUs). This paper presents a quasi-Linear Parameter-Varying (qLPV) modeling and gain-scheduled offline Model Predictive Control (MPC) with integral action for air path regulation in SI engines equipped with electric superchargers. A qLPV model is derived through exact factorization of nonlinear throttle flow, volumetric efficiency, and compressor torque terms into three scheduling parameters. The controller gains are computed offline via Linear Matrix Inequality (LMI)-based optimization and scheduled online through trilinear interpolation, enabling real-time implementation. Simulation results on standardized (WLTC, US06) and real-world driving cycles demonstrate superior tracking performance, constraint satisfaction, and computational efficiency compared to PID, Fuzzy, and nonlinear MPC controllers. The proposed strategy achieves fuel savings exceeding 33% relative to baseline PID control while maintaining execution times compatible with automotive sampling rates, demonstrating its viability for real-time embedded implementation on production ECUs.

title: Hyperchaotic Privacy-Preserving Cooperative Adaptive Control With Application to Multimotor Systems
authors: Hui Ma, Qi Zhou, Hongru Ren, Hongjing Liang
abstract: This article investigates the privacy-preserving cooperative adaptive control problem for nonlinear multiagent systems with unknown disturbances. First, by leveraging the hyperchaotic phenomenon of the Lorenz–Stenflo system, a masking function is proposed, which endows it with uncertainty and unpredictability and thus greatly improves the encryption performance. Meanwhile, a time-assist function is designed to achieve privacy protection within a preassigned period, which enhances the flexibility of the mechanism. In addition, according to the gradient descent method, a dynamically self-adjustable gain parameter is designed in the disturbance observer. The utilization of this parameter enables the proposed observer not only to handle unknown disturbances, but also to better improve the performance of the system. Afterward, the Lyapunov stability theorem is utilized to prove that all signals of the closed-loop system are semiglobally uniformly ultimately bounded. Finally, experimental results validate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm, which successfully accomplishes speed synchronization across multiple permanent magnet synchronous motors, thereby ensuring secure data transmission.

title: A Method to Balance Thermal Distribution of Paralleled Press-Pack IGBTs Based on Gradient Copper-Molybdenum Alloy Ratio Layout
authors: Suhang Wei, Jiaxin Liu, Weishan Lv, Yuan Zhang, Siqi Dai, Xuchen Sun, Cai Chen, Yong Kang, Wei Jiang, Pengyang Wang, Bin Lai
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Press-pack IGBTs (PP-IGBTs) are widely used due to their high power, double-sided cooling, and easy series connection. However, the external pressure on the large-area boss is uneven due to the non-uniform strain caused by the mismatched thermal expansion coefficients (CTE) between different materials. This can lead to problems such as edge warping, temperature concentration, and chip cracking during operation. This paper proposes a method to replace the traditional pure molybdenum plates with a gradient copper-molybdenum alloy ratio layout. The results show that increasing the copper proportion in the central region appropriately can reduce the maximum junction temperature by 1 °C, improve the temperature and pressure uniformity of the module chip by up to 7.47% and 14.12% respectively, along a single path, and by 7% and 2% across the entire module. However, a large gradient can impair the stress transfer mechanism, leading to an excessive level of internal stress. This simple and effective method is applicable for high-power applications of PP IGBTs and scenarios with good heat dissipation conditions.

title: DBCoreset: A Density-Based Coreset Algorithm for Computationally Efficient Chest X-Ray Diagnosis
authors: Jayant Mahawar, Angshuman Paul
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Coreset generation is a data reduction method that facilitates efficient deep learning training. A good coreset can achieve comparable performance using a much smaller number of training samples. However, traditional coreset methods exhibit density bias. This bias leads to poor representation of subregions that are sparsely populated by data points within a class. To solve this, we propose a novel Density-based Coreset Generation algorithm to facilitate computationally efficient chest x-ray diagnosis. We first partition the feature space into fine-grained, clinically-relevant miniclusters utilizing density. We then introduce a scoring-based selection mechanism. It helps in finding out the representative datapoints to eventually train a classifier model. Furthermore, we enforce a minimum inclusion criterion for every minicluster. This step aims to mitigate density bias so that coverage of all pathological patterns may be possible. Training the downstream classifier on the curated coreset demonstrates improved performance and balance. On the publicly available dataset, we used only about one-third of the training data and achieved a 40% reduction in computational resources. Even with this reduction, we have achieved as close as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$83.01 \% \pm 0.36$</tex> with our method compared to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$83.37 \% \pm 0.78$</tex> on the full dataset. We also outperform other coreset-based methods. Our method also reduces skewness in the classwise data distribution. The code is available at https://github.com/mahawar2/DbCoreset.git.

title: The Impact of Switch Commutation on Voltage Balancing in Three-Level Flying Capacitor Multilevel Converters
authors: Noah J. B. Hosein, Peter W. Lehn
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Recent literature has showcased the flying capacitor multilevel converter as a solution for improving power density and efficiency in many power electronics applications. In practice, however, balancing the flying capacitors at their nominal voltages has proven to be a major challenge for these designs. Prior research has studied flying capacitor balancing through advanced modelling approaches, but such models typically represent the switches as ideal devices with instantaneous commutation at the switching transitions. In contrast, this work investigates how practical commutation events can affect the balancing dynamics. The analysis is performed by examining the commutation process in terms of charge transfers, which are defined by the semiconductor properties. Then, through quantitative analysis and qualitative reasoning, the impact of each charge transfer is assessed to determine if it promotes or degrades the steady-state balancing. Useful design insights are realized from the analysis, including an expression for the strength of the switch capacitance balancing effect, and an illustration of how body diode reverse recovery and asymmetric cell design can degrade balancing.

title: Direction-Guided Watershed for Adherent Cell Instance Segmentation
authors: Ruochen Liu, Yi Tian, Yalin Zheng, Yuxuan Zhao, Jingxin Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of adherent cells remains a critical challenge in computational pathology. While relation-based modeling excels at pixel-wise prediction, its reliance on watershed post-processing renders it susceptible to noise, potentially leading to over- or under-segmentation in complex adherent regions. To address this, we introduce directional guidance to augment the watershed algorithm via two complementary modules: a Directional Disparity Boundary Discernment (DDBD) module and an Oriented-Attentive Watershed Augmentation (OAWA) module. Together with high-gradient pixel screening, DDBD forms a dual-cue edge screening by assessing directional disparity, effectively discriminating true boundaries from noise. OAWA then accentuates these valid boundaries by weighting the neighborhoods along their dominant gradient directions, guiding the watershed to focus on salient ridges. Extensive experiments on the CytoDArk0 dataset demonstrate state-of-the-art performance across multiple magnifications, improving the accuracy and robustness of adherent cell instance segmentation.

title: Cross-Modal Graph Attention Network for Parkinson's Disease Diagnosis
authors: Haojie Song, Xiaojun Guan, Ao Zhang, Xiaojie Duanmu, Zihao Zhu, Jiaqiang Li, Ronglin Zhang, Yujiao Zhang, Peng Yang, Tianfu Wang, Xiaojun Xu, Baiying Lei
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Accurate diagnosis of Parkinson's disease (PD) remains challenging due to heterogeneous symptoms and subtle neuroanatomical changes. Although multimodal Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) provides complementary information, existing methods often fail to effectively model cross-modal interactions and brain anatomical connectivity. We propose a cross-modal graph attention network with self-supervised contrastive pretraining for PD diagnosis. Contrastive learning extracts robust volumetric representations from limited data, while a bidirectional cross-modal attention module enables deep interaction between T1-weighted MRI and quantitative susceptibility mapping features. An anatomically-informed graph attention network further models inter-regional dependencies to enhance diagnostic reliability and interpretability. Experiments on a hospital cohort demonstrate superior performance over representative methods.

title: Physically Constrained Generative Model for Visualizing Climate-Induced Wildfires Using Satillite Imagery
authors: Ahamed Ishaq, Jeya R
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Effective climate risk communication, environmental assessment, and disaster preparedness all depend on the visual representation of climate-induced wildfires. The need for reliable visualization techniques using satellite imagery is highlighted by the increasing frequency and intensity of wildfires. While recent advances in generative models enable the creation of realistic satellite imagery, unrestricted generation can produce physically unrealistic wildfire patterns, reducing their usefulness in risksensitive situations. In this work, a physically limited conditional image-to-image translation framework for satellite-based wildfire visualization is presented. To direct the image synthesis process, a high-resolution conditional generative adversarial network is integrated with wildfire-specific physical constraints, including vegetation-based indicators and burn severity. The model generates post-wildfire satellite imagery that accurately highlights affected regions while maintaining geographical consistency by conditioning generation on physically meaningful constraint maps. According to experimental results, the suggested framework reduces implausible artifacts frequently seen in unconstrained generative models while producing visually realistic and spatially coherent wildfire visualizations. These results demonstrate how well physically limited generative modeling works for reliable wildfire visualization and climate-related decision support.

title: Semi-Dense Semantic Covisibility Learning for Multi-Robot Embodied Visual Localization
authors: Wenshuai Wang, Shiyu Liu, Peifeng Jiang, Hong Liu
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: Accurate and robust visual localization is a formidable challenge for multi-robot systems due to viewpoint ambiguities, appearance changes, and dynamic occlusions. Conventional hand-crafted operators often lead to incorrect matches without domain adaptation, while existing deep learning models are characterized by suboptimal accuracy and limited generalizability. However, semantic information is considered the key to stable scene representation and remains to be fully exploited at multiple granularities. In this paper, we propose a semi-dense semantic covisibility learning framework for multi-robot embodied visual localization (SDS-VL) to enhance the efficacy of image retrieval and pose estimation by enforcing semantic consistency constraints. Specifically, the attention-driven semantic scene graph (AdSSG) is introduced for multi-view image retrieval among heterogeneous robots in a global environment, and a semantic-guided pose estimation strategy is designed to filter and prioritize matched keypoints through fine-grained scene understanding. Experimental results on 4 public datasets fully verify the remarkable effectiveness and robustness of SDS-VL in various complex scenes both indoors and outdoors, thereby providing a solid solution for embodied perception in practical applications.

title: A Geometry-Scalable DC I–V Calibration Methodology for a Commercial Bulk CMOS Process Design Kit for Cryogenic ICs Operating Near 1 K
authors: Hee-Cheol Joo, Seunghoon Yi, Yoochang Kim, Seung Chae Jung, Hyunwoo Kim, Ryungyu Lee, Seungyun Ham, Hojin Lee, Young-Ha Hwang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: This paper presents a systematic methodology for calibrating a commercial process design kit (PDK) for cryogenic CMOS (cryo-CMOS) integrated circuit (IC) design. Built on the PSP compact model adopted in the baseline PDK, the proposed approach calibrates cryogenic DC I–V characteristics across multiple device geometries through a structured geometry-aware parameter-tuning flow based on cryogenic DC measurements. By leveraging geometry-dependent parameterization supported by the PSP compact model, the methodology preserves compatibility with standard electronic design automation (EDA) toolchains. Using this methodology, a prototype cryo-CMOS PDK (cryo-PDK) is developed by calibrating a commercial 28-nm bulk CMOS PDK with a selected set of tuning-point (TP) devices. Its prediction accuracy at 1.58 K is then evaluated across both the TP devices and additional cross-point (CP) devices, which use an intermediate width and/or length within the TP boundary dimensions and are excluded from tuning. Compared with the baseline PDK predictions at 1.58 K, the proposed cryo-PDK reduces the mean drain-current error, normalized to the measured drain current, by up to 67.8 percentage points for NMOS TP devices, 66.3 percentage points for NMOS CP devices, 40.6 percentage points for PMOS TP devices, and 45.5 percentage points for PMOS CP devices. These results demonstrate substantial error reduction in cryogenic DC I–V prediction and the geometry scalability of the proposed calibration methodology.

title: IEEE Computer Graphics and Applications
authors: N/A

title: Robust Multi-Stream Massive MIMO Satellite Systems Based on Statistical CSI
authors: Hangsong Yan, Alexei Ashikhmin, Hong Yang, Bin Song, Shu Sun
abstract: This paper investigates multi-stream downlink precoding for massive multiple-input multiple-output low-Earth-orbit satellite (SAT) communication systems. We adopt a delay and Doppler precompensation approach to achieve coherent transmission. Under this setting, we formulate a signal transmission model that incorporates the near-independent properties of inter-SAT interference and compensation errors. We then demonstrate that moving beyond single-stream transmission requires both multi-SAT cooperation and multi-antenna user terminals. Based on this configuration and the established signal transmission model, we derive the first- and second-order statistical channel characteristics and utilize them to design locally optimal precoding algorithms for both total power constraint (TPC) and per-antenna power constraint (PAPC) conditions, which rely only on statistical channel state information (sCSI). In particular, the designed PAPC algorithm achieves linear complexity with respect to the number of antennas on the cooperative SATs. To reduce the computational complexity of the locally optimal precoder under TPC, we propose a low-complexity and robust precoding scheme targeting both the minimum mean squared error and sum-rate maximization objectives. Using majorization theory, we also provide a rigorous theoretical analysis of the optimal precoding structure under TPC. Moreover, the Lanczos algorithm is adopted to further reduce the complexity of the proposed robust designs. Simulation results show that when each SAT is equipped with a sufficiently large number of antennas, the proposed sCSI-based designs achieve performance comparable to that of instantaneous CSI-based designs.

title: Segmentation From Partial Views Via Patient-Specific Shape Priors
authors: Jared Vicory, Dženan Zukić, Balazs Vagvolgyi, Peter Kazanzides, Andinet Enquobahrie, Emad Boctor
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Precision surgical interventions rely on accurate integration of preoperative and intraoperative imaging to guide clinical decision-making and improve patient outcomes. Traditionally, most 3D imaging modalities capture the entirety of the target object, allowing for the segmentation of its entire volume. However, some medical imaging devices trade full field-of-view for other considerations, such as size and ability to access the target organ in unique ways. This is particularly true of ultrasound, where imaging deeper organs from outside the body is not viable due to attenuation, thus motivating the need for alternative imaging strategies. In scenarios such as image-guided intervention, it is often necessary to put this partial view of the object in its full anatomical context, such as alignment to a pre-operative MRI. We propose a method for jointly segmenting the portion of the object visible in the acquired image and estimating its full shape. We do this by combining a pre-trained shape prior with a patient-specific expectation of organ shape acquired via pre-operative imaging. In a simulated dataset for prostate MRI/US fusion, we show the ability to accurately estimate the prostate shape from ultrasound images capturing only a fraction of its total volume.

title: MFS-VQ-VAE: Synthesizing 7T-like MR images from 1.5T MR images with Prompt Attention in VQ-VAE
authors: XingHe Xie, LuYi Han, Yue Sun, MengZhu Liu, MingFeng Jiang, HingChiu Chang, Tao Tan
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) images exhibit significant quality differences at varying magnetic field strengths, such as 1.5T, 3T, and 7T. Higher magnetic field strengths generally provide better tissue contrast and richer structural details, thereby enhancing diagnostic accuracy. However, the high cost and limited accessibility of 7T MRI scanners restrict their clinical adoption, while 1.5T and 3T MRI systems are more commonly used in routine medical practice. To bridge the quality gap between low-field (1.5T/3T) and high-field (7T) MRI images, this study proposes a dynamic MRI synthesis framework that integrates a vector-quantized variational autoencoder (VQ-VAE) with a shared latent space to enable cross-field image synthesis. The proposed model extracts shared feature representations from MRI images acquired at different magnetic field strengths and, through a proposed prompt attention module combined with a field strength code, maps these features to the desired field strength, thereby achieving single-step synthesis across arbitrary field strengths. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms existing approaches across multiple key quantitative metrics, showing particularly strong performance in synthesis tasks between 1.5T and 3T, as well as between 3T and 7T. Furthermore, the framework enables high-quality one-step synthesis from 1.5T to 7T, effectively mitigating the cumulative errors commonly observed in multi-step synthesis methods.

title: Design of N-S-Iron Interleaved Asymmetric Pole Arc Coefficient 24-slot/20-pole CPPM Machine with Rotor Inner Slots
authors: Qiyun Zhang, Jiyao Wang, Youkang Hu, Wei Xu
abstract: This paper presents two novel 24-slot/20-pole consequent-pole permanent magnet (CPPM) machines incorporating an N-S-iron interleaved rotor topology with asymmetric pole arc coefficients and rotor inner slots. The proposed N-S-iron interleaved configuration effectively eliminates unipolar leakage flux observed in N(S)-iron CPPM machines, while simultaneously reducing the amount of permanent magnets (PMs) used. Furthermore, radial slots are strategically incorporated along the inner rotor surface corresponding to the N-S sequence, leading to enhanced average torque and suppressed torque ripple. The asymmetric pole arc coefficients of both machine designs are optimized to further elevate torque performance. Following elaboration of the topology and operational principles, the electromagnetic characteristics are evaluated using finite element analysis (FEA). Simulation results confirm that both proposed CPPM achieve superior torque along with significant torque ripple reduction.

title: Deep Acoustic Monitoring for Illegal Logging Detection Using Temporal-Context Aware Sound Event Classification
authors: Sallauddin Mohmmad, Suresh Kumar Sanampudi
abstract: Monitoring illegal logging in forest environments can be achieved through sound event detection. Acoustic detection of tree-cutting activity required to differentiate sound waves over time and detect temporally evolving acoustic patterns. This research proposed a hybrid deep learning framework that integrated the Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCN), Transformer encoders, multi-head attention mechanisms, and Gaussian noise to detect tree-cutting sounds in a forest environment. The TCNs capture local temporal values with dilated convolutions, and Transformer attentions used to find the global contextual relationships for longer time sequences. Gaussian noise introduced to improve robustness against environmental variability. The proposed approach included pre-processing, multi-feature extraction, hybrid temporal encoding, attention-based contextual analysis, and classification using an artificial neural network. The dataset used in this research consists of 10,501 audio samples across four classes: chainsaw, machine saw, axe-cutting (synthetically generated to increase variability), and negative environmental sounds. The results on feature groups show that the proposed TCN–Transformer–Attention architecture achieved 97.48% of accuracy with Group-1 features and 96.20% with Group-5 features. The embedding quality evaluated using temporal and contextual metrics such as Temporal Coherence Kernel (TCK), Contextual Continuity Index (CCI), and Temporal-Contextual Separation Index (TCSI). The TCK obtained value of 0.4136, the CCI value equals to 0.6892, and the TCSI obtained value of 1.9065. These results indicate that the model performed better at preserving temporal stability, contextual continuity, and discriminative separation within the learned embedding space.

title: EQUI-ViT: Rotational Equivariant Vision Transformer for Robust Histopathology Analysis
authors: Fuyao Chen, Yuexi Du, Eléonore V. Lieffrig, Nicha C. Dvorne, John A. Onofrey
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Vision Transformers (ViTs) have gained rapid adoption in computational pathology for their ability to model long-range dependencies through self-attention, addressing the limitations of convolutional neural networks that excel at local pattern capture but struggle with global contextual reasoning. Recent pathology-specific foundation models have further advanced performance by leveraging large-scale pretraining. However, standard ViTs remain inherently non-equivariant to transformations such as rotations and reflections, which are ubiquitous variations in histopathology imaging. To address this limitation, we propose Equi-ViT, which integrates an equivariant convolution kernel into the patch embedding stage of a ViT architecture, imparting built-in rotational equivariance to learned representations. Equi-ViT achieves superior rotation-consistent patch embeddings and stable classification performance across image orientations. Our results on a public colorectal cancer dataset demonstrate that incorporating equivariant patch embedding enhances data efficiency and robustness, achieving an accuracy (mean ± SD) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$86.8 \pm 0.59 \%$</tex> across rotations and outperforming standard ViT performance (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$83.1 \pm 6.93 \%$</tex>), suggesting that equivariant transformers could potentially serve as more generalizable backbones for the application of ViT in histopathology such as digital pathology foundation models. The code is available at https://github.com/fyc423/Equi-ViT.

title: Calibration-Aware Real-Time 6-DoF Pose Estimation Using ArUco Fiducials with Kalman Filtering Enhancement
authors: Abdullah R. Radam, Ahmed J. Abid, Adel A. Obed, Abbas A. Kareem
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: Accurate pose estimation in real time is of vital importance in the field of computer vision, robotics and automatic navigation. Vision based approaches are in widespread use because of their compact cost and flexibility. ArUco fiducial markers allow efficient six degrees freedom (6DoF) pose estimation on monocular cameras with PnP algorithms. However, practical implementations have a problem with frame-to-frame variations due to image noise, lighting variation and imperfect feature detections, within longer distances and non-zero viewing angles. Limited experimental studies have been made on how the orientation and distance errors depend on range and angular displacement. This work introduces a monocular real-time framework based on a combination of ArUco based pose estimation, Kalman filtering, calibration and correction of the camera and distortion effects. Experimental evaluations on different distances (56.5$150 \mathrm{~cm})$ and angles (0° and 30°) prove the stability improvement by filtering and errors increased with the distance and angular deviation which gives an idea to reliable low-cost vision systems. A demonstration video of the proposed system is available online [14].

title: Dual-Domain Mapping: Sensor Fusion of UAV-LiDAR and Ground Penetrating Radar for High-Fidelity 3D Utility Mapping
authors: Nurul Izzati Abd Karim, Amer Hamzah Taib, Wan Anom Wan Aris
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: Accurate mapping of underground utilities is essential for safe urban development but remains challenging due to the separation between surface observations and subsurface geophysical measurements. This study presents a sensor-fusion framework integrating Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) Light Detection and Ranging (LiDAR) with Ground Penetrating Radar (GPR) to generate a unified threedimensional geospatial representation of surface and subsurface infrastructure. The workflow establishes a semantic linkage between surface utility indicators such as manholes and valve covers extracted from LiDAR data and subsurface pipe or cable reflections detected from radargrams. Field data were collected at Lingkaran Ilmu, Universiti Teknologi Malaysia using a RIEGL VUX-1UAV LiDAR system and an IDS GeoRadar platform. Ground Control Points (GCPs) measured using GNSS were used to georeference both datasets and establish spatial correspondence between the aerial and ground surveys. The integrated processing workflow combines LiDAR-derived terrain modelling with GPR interpretation to enable accurate spatial registration of subsurface utilities. The resulting fused model achieved a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.08 m, demonstrating sub-decimetre positional accuracy suitable for high-quality utility mapping. The integration also reduced interpretation ambiguity by linking radar anomalies with contextual surface features. Although the test site provided adequate GNSS coverage, the framework can be adapted for GNSS-limited environments through the integration of inertial navigation and SLAM-based positioning techniques. The results demonstrate that combining UAV-LiDAR surface modelling with GPR subsurface detection provides a reliable approach for high-fidelity three-dimensional underground mapping.

title: Performance Analysis of Cloud-Based Disaster Recovery for Workday Applications
authors: Monu Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: The increasing reliance on cloud-native enterprise services such as Workday has intensified the need for robust disaster recovery mechanisms to ensure business continuity and service availability. As a leading provider of cloud-based Human Capital Management (HCM) and Financial Management solutions, Workday supports mission-critical business operations that require high availability, strong data integrity, and rapid recovery in the event of system failures or large-scale outages. Traditional off-site disaster recovery approaches are often constrained by high infrastructure costs, limited scalability, and prolonged recovery times, making them unsuitable for Workday’s dynamic, multi-tenant, and data-intensive cloud environment. Cloud-based disaster recovery, commonly referred to as Disaster Recovery as a Service (DRaaS), has emerged as an effective alternative by leveraging virtualization, automation, and geographically distributed cloud infrastructure. This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of cloud-based disaster recovery solutions in the context of Workday, focusing on system architecture, recovery mechanisms, and performance evaluation. A Workday-oriented disaster recovery framework is proposed that integrates automated data replication, orchestration, and continuous monitoring to achieve low Recovery Time Objective (RTO) and Recovery Point Objective (RPO) while adhering to enterprise security and availability requirements. Comparative analysis demonstrates that cloud-based disaster recovery outperforms traditional approaches in terms of cost efficiency, elastic scalability, high availability, and operational resilience. Key challenges, including data security, regulatory compliance, and performance optimization in multi-region cloud deployments, are also discussed, along with directions for future research aimed at enhancing disaster recovery capabilities in Workday’s evolving cloud ecosystem.

title: Intelligent Safety Monitoring for Shredding System Via Computer Vision
authors: P. Jacob Vijaya Kumar, G. Rajasekhar Reddy, N. Bhargavi, P. Sowmya, K. V. Surekha, D. Mokshita
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: Industrial shredding machines can be dangerous due to their fast-moving blades and close interaction with workers. Accidental hand contact with hazardous areas may lead to serious injuries. This paper presents a computer vision-based safety monitoring system that detects and tracks hand movements in real time using OpenCV and a pre-trained SSD MobileNet V1 model. Virtual safety zones are defined around critical parts of the machine, and when a hand enters a restricted area, the system issues a warning or activates an emergency stop. The proposed approach improves workplace safety by reducing the risk of accidents and offers a practical, low-cost solution for industrial environments.

title: FakeVision: A Vision Transformer Framework for Deepfake Detection
authors: Shriya Shukla, Ishan Bhalla, Pratistha Sharma, Abhishek Singhal
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: This paper proposes a family of frameworks for the detection of DeepFake and other techniques, which can generate synthetic media. These usually refer to techniques such as Face Swap, Face-to-Face, and Neural Textures. The basic idea of the work is to use a Vision Transformer, specifically Swin Transformer, along with Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN). The CNNs are good at capturing spatial and frequency artifacts that are present during manipulation. The Swin Transformer captures both local and global visual patterns efficiently using shifted attention windows. A bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) analyzes temporal information. This hybrid model helps in covering every inconsistency from spatial to frequency to temporal, resulting in overall generalization across all synthetic content generation techniques. The model is trained on FaceForensics++ dataset, and evaluated on that and CelebDFv2, which ensures that different types of manipulation techniques are covered. The evaluation of the model is done primarily based on Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve (AUC-ROC), which provides us with an assessment of detection capability and robustness. This work reveals that the proposed family of swin-based architecture provides decent intra-dataset and great cross-dataset performance (0.9822 and 0.8478 are the highest respective AUC values) when detecting deepfakes in contrast to existing models, that too with varying computational needs.

title: Speech Emotion Recognition Based on the Enhanced Initialization and Emotion-Sensitive Frequency Channel Modulation
authors: Jun Li, Chunyan Liang, Zhiguo Liu, Houbin Fan, Fengpei Ge
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: To improve the effectiveness of speech emotion recognition (SER), this study introduces a novel model termed SXAGCT-BiLSTM, which integrates a speech-adaptive Xavier initialization scheme and an emotion-sensitive frequency channel modulation mechanism. The proposed initialization method refines weight distribution by incorporating a correction factor to control variance, thereby alleviating signal amplification issues and mitigating gradient explosion, which contributes to more stable training. The emotion-sensitive modulation component, referred to as the amplified range and frequency gated channel transformation (ARFGCT), adopts a scaling strategy combined with frequency-band attention to highlight emotionally informative spectral cues, achieving fine-grained channel weighting and enhancing both classification precision and model robustness. During optimization, a joint loss function is employed for collaborative supervision, promoting intra-class cohesion while enlarging inter-class divergence, thus strengthening the discriminative representation and emotional inference capability. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that, compared with baseline approaches, the proposed SXAGCT-BiLSTM yields relative gains of 4.79% and 6.25% in weighted accuracy (WA), and 4.81% and 5.71% in unweighted accuracy (UA) on the RAVDESS and CASIA datasets, respectively.

title: Detecting AI-Generated Text Using Multilingual DistilBERT Fine-Tuning on the Kaggle AI vs Human Dataset
authors: Ahlam Migdady, Riyad AlShalabi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: With the recent developments in the field of Generative Artificial Intelligence, it has become possible for the system to produce text that is nearly indistinguishable from the text written by humans, and this has led to several serious concerns with regards to academic integrity and the authenticity of digital content. This has, therefore, led to the growing need for the development of systems that can detect the difference between text written by humans and text written by AI. This paper proposes a solution to this problem by using an intelligent model for classifying text using the Multilingual DistilBERT model, a lean transformer model that has been designed to retain high language understanding capabilities while still maintaining efficiency and fast inference times. The approach takes a balanced dataset of 20,000 pieces of text, half of which are human-generated while the other half are AI-generated. Experimental evaluation on an unseen set of 4,000 samples showed that the model performed outstandingly. The overall accuracy of the model is 98.22%, and the AUC value obtained from the Receiver Operating Characteristic curve is 0.9980, which is outstanding in distinguishing human-written texts from AI-generated texts.

title: Magnetic Properties Measurement and Analysis of Ultra-thin Grain-oriented Silicon Steel Considering Uniaxial and Biaxial Stress Loads
authors: Yu Dou, Xueying Zhu, Tao Chen, Jiapeng Zhou, Yongjian Li, Jianguo Zhu
abstract: Ultra-thin grain-oriented (GO) silicon steel is gradually being applied in high-power medium-frequency transformers and reactors due to its excellent magnetic properties. However, during assembly and operation, mechanical components such as clips and bolts impose complex stresses on the core material. Although these stresses significantly influence magnetic properties of the core materials, the stress behavior of magnetic materials is often overlooked during the electrical equipment design phase due to testing difficulties. To decrease the design errors and enhance equipment reliability, it is necessary to construct a magnetic properties tester considering stress conditions and conduct corresponding tests. In this paper, piezoelectric actuators are employed to apply uniaxial and biaxial stresses to the samples. The hysteresis loops and core loss of ultra-thin GO steel GT100 are measured under various stress conditions to investigate the magneto-mechanical mechanisms. In addition, the measured core losses are separated into hysteresis losses, classical eddy current losses, and excess losses based on the loss separation theory. This approach allows for quantifying the influence of stress on loss components of ultra-thin GO silicon steel.

title: TopoGate: Quality-Aware Topology-Stabilized Gated Fusion for Longitudinal Low-Dose CT New-Lesion Prediction
authors: Seungik Cho
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Longitudinal low-dose CT follow-ups vary in noise, reconstruction kernels, and registration quality. These differences destabilize subtraction images and can trigger false “newlesion” alarms. We present TopoGate, a lightweight model that combines the follow-up appearance view with the subtraction view and controls their influence through a learned, quality-aware gate. The gate is driven by three case-specific signals: CT appearance quality, registration consistency, and stability of anatomical topology measured with topological metrics. On the NLST-New-Lesion-LongCT cohort comprising 152 pairs from 122 patients, TopoGate improves discrimination and calibration over single-view baselines, achieving an area under the ROC curve of 0.65 with a standard deviation of 0.05 and a Brier score of 0.14. Removing corrupted or low-quality pairs, identified by the quality scores, further increases the area under the ROC curve from 0.62 to 0.68 and reduces the Brier score from 0.14 to 0.12. The gate responds predictably to degradation, placing more weight on appearance when noise grows, which mirrors radiologist practice. The approach is simple, interpretable, and practical for reliable longitudinal LDCT triage.

title: Program Committee
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)

title: Switching Loss Characterization with Uncertainty Quantification for 3.3 kV Monolithic Versus Discrete SiC Bidirectional FETs
authors: Pengyu Fu, Zhining Zhang, Shuwei He, Jin Wang, Anant Agarwal, Stephen A. Mancini, Woongje Sung, Adam J. Morgan
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This work presents a systematic investigation into the switching losses of 3.3 kV SiC bidirectional field-effect transistors (BiDFETs) in common-drain configuration. Hard-switching characterization is performed at 2 kV bus voltage, 5–20 A load currents, and 30–150°C case temperatures. The first quantitative comparison between monolithic and discrete (two-chip) architectures reveals that monolithic integration exhibits higher switching losses due to tighter capacitive coupling. Gate drive configuration impact is also quantified: turn-on energy exhibits negligible sensitivity to single- versus dual-gate driving, while turn-off energy shows measurable reduction under dual-gate operation due to reduced body diode reverse recovery. These findings are enabled by a power-based integration framework with uncertainty quantification, which maintains measurement confidence in the presence of post-switching oscillations extending beyond standard IEC 60747-8 windows. The results provide foundational switching characteristics and architecture selection guidelines for medium-voltage SiC BiDFET implementation in circuits.

title: A Proximal Approach for Stain Separation and Normalization of Whole-Slide Histopathological Images
authors: Aymen Sadraoui, Astrid Laurent-Bellue, Mounir Kaaniche, Amel Benazza-Benyahia, Catherine Guettier, Jean-Christophe Pesquet
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Stain variability in histopathological images presents a major obstacle to developing accurate and reliable computer-aided diagnosis systems. In this paper, we introduce a novel stain normalization framework based on a stain separation proximal algorithm. Specifically, we first propose a new stain separation method that extracts individual stain components from a given stained image. Subsequently, we perform stain normalization by aligning the separated source stains with those of a target reference image. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach effectively reduces inter-image color variations while preserving the essential structural details of the tissue.

title: Cross-Language Performance and Usability Comparison of JSON, XML and Protocol Buffers in Java, JavaScript and Python
authors: Khaoula Ait Bel Mehdi, Nisrine Lachguer, Mohamed Lachgar, Driss Essabbar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: Selecting an appropriate data serialization method is critical for managing latency, network bandwidth, and system maintainability in modern distributed architectures. In this study, a detailed, cross-platform performance and usability analysis comparing JSON, XML, and Protocol Buffers is provided across three prominent language ecosystems: Java (JVM), JavaScript (Node.js), and Python (CPython). The methodology relies on a consistent e-commerce data schema and standardized workloads with varying payload sizes to evaluate key metrics, including message footprint, (de)serialization latency, operational throughput, and peak memory usage.
