Beyond raw performance, qualitative usability dimensions such as schema management, code generation requirements, and debugging ease are assessed. It is indicated that Protocol Buffers excels in compactness and speed within Java and Python environments. Conversely, JSON offers an optimal compromise between interoperability and ease of use, showing exceptional performance in Node.js thanks to engine-specific optimizations. XML remains the most resource-intensive format, making it mostly relevant for legacy integrations. These results culminate in a set of practical recommendations designed to help engineers select the most suitable format based on specific performance goals and runtime constraints.

title: Real-Time Camera-Based Heart Rate Estimation System Using Deep Learning on Edge Device
authors: Chiquita Ahsanunnisa, Fariska Z. Ruskanda, Robithoh Annur, Rinaldi Munir, Nur Ahmadi
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Remote photoplethysmography (rPPG) provides a non-contact solution for heart rate (HR) monitoring, but deploying deep learning-based rPPG algorithms on resource-limited edge devices remains challenging. This work presents a real-time camera-based HR estimation system running on the Raspberry Pi AI Kit equipped with a Hailo-8 AI accelerator. The proposed pipeline integrates face detection, tracking, rPPG signal extraction, and HR estimation in a fully end-to-end framework. Through benchmarking on the WIDERFACE and UBFC-rPPG datasets, SCRFD-2.5G was selected for face detection and DeepPhys for rPPG extraction based on accu-racy-latency trade-offs. Quantization of DeepPhys, including a mixed-precision 20 % UINT4 and 80 % UINT8 scheme with equalization, reduced latency from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$163,493.80 ~\text{ms}$</tex> to 28.94 ms while preserving accuracy (MAE 3.01 bpm). Additional pipeline optimizations-window chunking, batch inference, and incremental statistics-further improved real-time performance. Results demonstrate that deep learning-based rPPG systems can be efficiently deployed on edge device while meeting both accuracy and real-time requirements for practical use.

title: dStation-chain: A Decentralized Solution for Ground Stations as a Service
authors: Edilson Filho, Coutinho Filho, César Marcon, Jarbas Silveira, Walter dos Santos, Laurent Vercouter, Alexandre Coelho
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: The increasing demand for Low Earth Orbit (LEO) and commercial satellite communications highlights the limitations of centralized ground station networks, which are often underutilized and inflexible. This paper introduces DStation-Chain, a decentralized and reward-incentivized solution for Ground Station as a Service (GSaaS) that enhances communication capacity between satellites and control centers. Leveraging smart contracts and stablecoin payments, the system enables transparent, real-time, and on-demand resource allocation. A dynamic pricing and allocation model optimizes ground station selection based on bandwidth, link priority, operational frequency, link type, and signal quality. DStation-Chain offers a scalable, secure, and efficient approach that maximizes ground station utilization while improving operational flexibility and reliability for satellite operators.

title: Sensing Performance Optimization for ISAC with ITS and IRS
authors: Wannian Du, Zheng Chu, Pei Xiao, Meng Hua, Zhengyu Zhu, Yi Wang, Qun Wang
abstract: In this paper, a novel integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) system architecture is introduced that incorpo rates an intelligent transmission surface (ITS) in the transmitter and an intelligent reflecting surface (IRS) in the transmission channel. The ITS introduces a novel signal modulation architecture for coordinated operation with the base station (BS), while the IRS facilitates efficient signal transmission for the communication users and the sensing target. The main goal is to maximize the beam response gain from the IRS to the sensing target while satisfying the demand of the communication performance for the users. This is realized by collaboratively designing hybrid beamforming strategies at the BS for the simultaneous transmission of communication and sensing signals, along with the ITS and IRS phase shifts. Then, an effective algorithm is proposed that utilizes penalty factors to simplify the optimization problem. Furthermore, an alternating optimization (AO) method is employed to iteratively optimize the relevant variables. Subproblems in this framework are further solved using techniques such as the Lagrange multiplier method, bi section search, the successive convex approximation (SCA), and the alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM). Finally, simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme yields a significant enhancement in beam response gain and energy efficiency compared to benchmark schemes.

title: DINO-MambaSeg: A Foundation-Guided Network for Patchy Atrophy Segmentation
authors: Siqi Wang, Juexue Wang, Zhuo Deng, Wenbin Wei, Lan Ma
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Pathological myopia (PM) is a leading cause of irreversible vision loss, where patchy chorioretinal atrophy (PA) serves as a crucial biomarker of disease progression. Accurate segmentation of PA lesions remains challenging due to low contrast, irregular morphology, and limited annotations. To address these issues, we propose DINO-MambaSeg, a novel segmentation framework driven by foundation models. Our method integrates a frozen DINOv3 encoder with a hybrid Mamba-ASPP module for joint global-local feature refinement. Furthermore, we incorporate an optic disc exclusion prior into the loss function to suppress false positives and enhance anatomical plausibility. We also construct PA-Fundus, a new pixel-level annotated dataset dedicated to PA lesions. Extensive experiments on PA-Fundus demonstrate that DINO-MambaSeg achieves state-of-the-art accuracy and robustness, validating the effectiveness of combining foundation priors with lightweight structural refinement.

title: SCORA: An Open-Source Coherent SFCW Radar Framework on Low-Cost SDRs for Subsurface Remote Sensing
authors: Hui Huang, Kiyoshy Nakamura, Yangjie Xu, Jin Zhang, Radu State
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: High-resolution Ground Penetrating Radar (GPR) is a critical tool for subsurface remote sensing, but the high cost and strict size, weight, and power constraints of traditional systems limit their widespread deployment, particularly on Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs). While Software-Defined Radios (SDRs) offer a flexible alternative, implementing high-resolution Stepped-Frequency Continuous-Wave (SFCW) radar on low-cost SDRs introduces fundamental challenges: synthesizer phase incoherence caused by independent Phase-Locked Loops and severe data transport bottlenecks. This paper introduces the Stepped-frequency Coherent Radar Architecture (SCORA), an open-source framework designed to transform a low-cost, commercial SDR into a fully coherent SFCW radar for near-surface geophysics. The framework features built-in support for both Continuous Wave (CW) and Linear Frequency Modulated (LFM) sub-pulses, utilizing an extensible architecture that allows users to easily integrate custom, advanced waveforms. SCORA utilizes reference-channel compensation and incorporates system latency characterizations to optimize PRF and prevent spatial aliasing. The framework is experimentally validated through freespace ranging, sandbox landmine detection, and an airborne UAV survey that successfully mapped the buried infrastructure. This open-source release significantly reduces the barrier to entry for agile GPR instrumentation.

title: Modeling and Analysis of Partial Discharge in High-Voltage Printed Circuit Boards
authors: Matthew J. Pompa, Andrew N. Lemmon
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Partial discharge (PD) is a critical reliability concern in high-voltage (HV) printed circuit boards (PCBs), where insulation degradation may initiate well below breakdown voltage. Existing PCB spacing standards such as IPC-2221 are primarily intended to prevent arcing and do not explicitly address PD inception mechanisms. This paper experimentally evaluates PD inception voltage (PDIV) in uncoated HV PCBs fabricated to IPC-2221 spacing guidelines and introduces stacked and offset conductor geometries to assess geometry-dependent PD behavior. Finite-element simulations are used to compare a traditional peak-field PD criterion with a critical-area-based metric that integrates electric field magnitude over air regions exceeding a breakdown threshold. Experimental results show that stacked conductor geometries significantly reduce PDIV due to concentrated fields, while offset geometries increase PDIV without increasing nominal spacing. The results demonstrate that IPC-2221 spacing rules are conservative for PD prevention and motivate geometry-aware PD design guidelines for HV PCBs.

title: Design and Implementation of an Active Continuous Conduction Mode Boost Converter for Power Factor
authors: Yogesh Sharma, Satyabrata Behera, Venkata Ramana Naik N
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: In modern industrial, commercial, and residential applications, most electronic devices require rectification of the input AC voltage for proper operation. However, the use of non-linear loads introduces significant non-linearity in input characteristics, leading to distortion in the input current waveform and deterioration of the power factor. This results in the flow of non-sinusoidal currents, which not only stresses the power grid but also adversely impacts other connected users. To address these challenges, input current harmonics must be minimized, and the current waveform must be shaped to follow the input voltage. Power Factor Correction (PFC) is a critical solution, typically implemented using either Passive or Active techniques. This paper presents the design and hardware implementation of an Active PFC system using a conventional Boost converter topology. The proposed Boost PFC approach offers continuous input current, thereby reducing Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) and significantly improving the power factor.

title: DrlGoFPGA 2.0: FPGA Global Placement Based on Multi-Agent Graph Transformers and Nonlinear Placement Co-Optimization
authors: Kang Yang, Jianwang Zhai, Liuyu Xiang, Kang Zhao, Wei Li, Ming Lei, Zhaofeng He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: The speed and quality of global placement (GP) are crucial for FPGA performance. The existing nonlinear placers provide higher computational efficiency than heuristic algorithms, but their insufficient attention to the initialization of GP distributions limits rapid convergence towards high-quality global optimal solutions. To resolve this, we propose a GP framework, DrlGoFPGA 2.0, combining LUT, FF, DSP, and RAM (LFDR) instance placement generation based on multi-agent graph transformer network (MAGTN) and LFDR initial placement fine-tuning based on nonlinear placement. A MAGTN model for LFDR initial placement is designed, which utilizes distributed training to circumvent the issues of sub-optimal placement models and prolonged training times due to the non-uniform distribution of large-scale LFDR sites. A lightweight LFDR line-network relationships (LNR) graph supporting MAGTN co-decision is created by separating, renumbering, and deduplicating the LNR graph containing all instances, and inputting the LFDR LNR graph into MAGTN to obtain the connections between LFDRs. The experimental results on ISPD’2016/2017 benchmarks show that compared with the state-of-the-art (SOTA) nonlinear placer, DrlGoFPGA 2.0 can improve GP speed by 27.3% ∼ 82.1%, reduce half-perimeter wirelength by 0.6% ∼ 4.6%, and routed wirelength by 0.4% ∼ 2.8%.

title: Retrieving the Design Parameters of Silicon Photonic Devices from Optical Responses Using an Inverse Neural Network Model
authors: Amir Masominia, Daniele Melati, Dan-Xia Xu, Yuri Grinberg, Muhammad Al-Digeil
abstract: We present a bidirectional framework for silicon photonic grating couplers, enabling both forward prediction of optical spectra from geometry and inverse retrieval of design parameters from target responses, achieving high accuracy and efficiency across extensive simulated datasets.

title: Controllable FrFT-Driven Y-Net for Cross-Modal Image Fusion Scenarios
authors: Yifan Jiang, Kemin Li, Wenyuan Li, Guoheng Huang, Jietao Yang, Xiaochen Yuan, Xuhang Chen, Bingo Wing-Kuen Ling, Chi-Man Pun, Shunlan Wang
abstract: Image fusion aims to synthesize high-quality representations by integrating complementary information from multimodal sources. While recent deep learning approaches have explored spectral domain learning to overcome the localized receptive fields of spatial convolutions, they predominantly rely on the standard Fourier Transform. However, two critical bottlenecks remain: standard Fourier bases assume signal stationarity (ill-suited for non-stationary natural images) and lack adaptive frequency-adaptive feature weighting, leading to spectral leakage, ineffective high-frequency-noise decoupling, and texture smoothing. To break these limitations, we propose the Fractional-Order Dynamic Perceptive Y-Net (FDP Y-Net), a unified framework driven by Controllable Fractional Fourier Transform (FrFT) Convolutions. By generalizing the spectral domain, our model adaptively rotates the time-frequency axis to optimally represent non-stationary features and dynamically weights frequency components. Specifically, a Dynamic Perceptive Module spatially localizes salient regions, the Controllable FrFT Block captures time-varying spectral characteristics, and an Information Enhancement Module explicitly reconstructs high-frequency residuals. Orchestrated within a Y-shaped architecture with specialized skip connections, these components ensure holistic feature fusion. Extensive experiments across Infrared-Visible, Medical, and Multifocus datasets demonstrate that FDP Y-Net effectively resolves spectral limitations and frequency weighting deficiencies, achieving state-of-the-art performance in visual fidelity and quantitative metrics. The code is available at: https://github.com/mousi30/FrFT-Driven-Y-Net.

title: Levenberg–Marquardt Trained Neural Network Based Fault Current Limitation in Synchronverter
authors: Lipun Kumar Naik, Animesh Kumar Sahoo, Mayukha Pal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: Synchronverters are grid-forming inverters that mimic synchronous generator behavior in modern power systems. A key challenge in their control is limiting fault current during grid disturbances, which can otherwise damage equipment or destabilize the grid. Conventional virtual impedance-based fault current limitation methods often lack adaptability and real-time responsiveness under fast-changing conditions. This paper proposes a neural network-based approach to regulate inverter voltage references for effective fault current suppression. A shallow feedforward neural network is trained using the Levenberg– Marquardt algorithm to map system states to control actions with high precision and robustness. The network dynamically adjusts dq-axis voltage references in response to overcurrent conditions, keeping current levels within safe operational limits without requiring fixed parameter tuning. Simulation results show high prediction accuracy, rapid response, and strong generalization across varied operating scenarios. Compared to traditional control strategies, the proposed method offers a flexible, data-driven alternative that significantly enhances protection, adaptability, and dynamic stability in synchronverter-based systems.

title: Silicon Microring Resonator Gated by High Mobility Hydrogen-Doped Indium Oxide
authors: Sudipta Biswas, Saika Muntaha Bari, Alan X. Wang
abstract: We present a silicon MOSCAP microring resonator with high-mobility hydrogen-doped indium oxide, achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$175 \text{pm} / \mathrm{V}$</tex> wavelength tuning efficiency, a Q-factor <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>10000$</tex>, and sub-volt phase control with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{V}_{\pi} \mathrm{L}$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.09 ~\mathrm{V} \cdot ~\text{cm}$</tex>. The device shows great potential for energy-efficient optical communication.

title: Transformer-Based Modeling of Global Microwave Land Surface Emissivity Using GPM
authors: Fraser King, Sarah Ringerud
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Global land surface microwave emissivity estimates are critical for data assimilation, numerical weather prediction, and quantitative precipitation estimation, yet explicit calculations at multiple frequencies remains problematic. We introduce a machine learning model that converts 10.65 GHz H-polarization emissivity retrieved under clear-sky conditions into emissivity for the 12 higher-frequency channels of the GPM Microwave Imager (GMI). The model is a feature-tokenizer transformer that ingests ancillary surface variables such as skin temperature, leaf area index, soil texture, and soil moisture. Treating each variable as an individual token lets the transformer direct attention between features and learn nonlinear, spatiotemporally consistent relationships across five years of data [2015-2019]. The network reproduces channel-specific emissivities with R2 > 0.80 and mean absolute percentage error < 1%, and it simulates top-of-atmosphere brightness temperatures with R2 = 0.77 − 0.95 and mean biases < 4 K for every channel up to 89 GHz when compared to collocated GMI observations. Using independent TELSEM2 10.65 GHz H-pol inputs produces similar skill, suggesting that the learned mapping is robust to the input source, and captures transferable physical structures. The model is lightweight and can be embedded in satellite retrieval systems, reanalysis pipelines, and Earth-system models to accelerate microwave forward modeling and improve global precipitation monitoring.

title: Low DC-link Voltage Operation of Three-Phase Bidirectional AC-DC Converter with Fixed Voltage Modulation Index
authors: Cheolmin Hwang, Seongwon Lee, Jung-Ik Ha
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The operation of conventional AC-DC converters is premised on a boosted DC-link voltage to ensure an adequate voltage margin. As a result, expanding the operating range toward a lower DC-link voltage has been overlooked. A lower DC-link voltage reduces switching losses in both the AC-DC converter and the subsequent load converter, while also enhancing low-speed efficiency in variable frequency drive applications. To facilitate low DC-link voltage control, the traditional decoupled d- and q-axis current controls operate in the overmodulation (OVM) region to maximize voltage utilization. However, operating in the OVM region causes an error between the voltage reference and the converter's output voltage, which then introduces a disturbance into the current control loop. This paper proposes the one-degree-of-freedom (1-DOF) control that utilizes the voltage angle as the sole DOF, thereby improving current control performance in low DC-link voltage region. Furthermore, the paper evaluates the voltage margin in rectification and regenerative modes and proposes an appropriate DC-link voltage reference modification strategy to maximize the voltage utilization.

title: Explainable AI in Rare Cancers: Lessons from Neuroendocrine Tumors for Disease Modeling
authors: Nowsheena, Shaima Qureshi
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)
abstract: Neuroendocrine tumors (NETs), classified as rare tumor variants, are constrained by limited data and complex clinical variables, although their incidence is increasing. The diagnosis and prognosis require interpretable models to support reliable clinical decision-making and explainability. Explainable AI (XAI) addressing the black-box nature of AI provides interpretable insights into model predictions for such rare diseases. The research investigates XAI techniques for identifying key biomarkers, implicated genes, and therapeutic strategies to improve the reliability and clinical applicability of AI-driven decision support. We propose an XAI-driven modelling framework and demonstrate it on the GEO GSE119063 gene-expression microarray dataset by training an SVM classifier with leave-one- out cross-validation and interpreting feature attributions using SHAP and LIME. The model achieves 0.8889 accuracy (recall 0.8; F1-score 0.8889) in tumor vs normal classification, and the explanations highlight the gene features that most strongly drive predictions for transparent decision support. Additionally, the research reveals linked patterns and their correlations with disease prognosis, thereby elucidating that interpretable models function as dependable and practical tools for improving precision medicine in oncology settings with limited data, promoting transparent and credible decision support.

title: Weakly Supervised Segmentation and Classification of Alpha-Synuclein Aggregates in Brightfield Midbrain Images
authors: Erwan Dereure, Robin Louiset, Laura Parkkinen, David A Menassa, David Holcman
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Parkinson's disease (PD) is a neurodegenerative disorder associated with the accumulation of misfolded alpha-synuclein aggregates, forming Lewy bodies and neuritic shape used for pathology diagnostics. Automatic analysis of immunohistochemistry histopathological images with Deep Learning provides a promising tool for better understanding the spatial organization of these aggregates. In this study, we develop an automated image processing pipeline to segment and classify these aggregates in whole-slide images (WSIs) of midbrain tissue from PD and incidental Lewy Body Disease (iLBD) cases based on weakly supervised segmentation, robust to immunohistochemical labelling variability, with a ResNet50 classifier. Our approach allows to differentiate between major aggregate morphologies, including Lewy bodies and neurites with a balanced accuracy of 80 %. This framework paves the way for large-scale characterization of the spatial distribution and heterogeneity of alpha-synuclein aggregates in brightfield immunohistochemical tissue, and for investigating their poorly understood relationships with surrounding cells such as microglia and astrocytes.

title: Evaluation of Neurocombat and Deep Learning Harmonization for Multi-Site Magnetic Resonance Neuroimaging in Youth with Prenatal Alcohol Exposure
authors: Chloe Scholten, Elyssa M. McMaster, Adam M. Saunders, Michael E. Kim, Gaurav Rudravaram, Elias Levy, Bryce Geeraert, Lianrui Zuo, Simon Vandekar, Catherine Lebel, Bennett A. Landman
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: In cases of prevalent diseases and disorders, such as Prenatal Alcohol Exposure (PAE), multi-site data collection allows for increased study samples. However, multi-site studies introduce additional variability through heterogeneous collection materials, such as scanner and acquisition protocols, which confound with biologically relevant signals. Neuroscientists often utilize statistical methods on imagederived metrics, such as volume of regions of interest, after all image processing to minimize site-related variance. HACA3, a deep learning harmonization method, offers an opportunity to harmonize image signals prior to metric quantification; however, HACA3 has not yet been validated in a pediatric cohort. In this work, we investigate HACA3's ability to remove site-related variance and preserve biologically relevant signal compared to a statistical method, neuroCombat, and pair HACA3 processing with neuroCombat to evaluate the efficacy of multiple harmonization methods in a pediatric (age 7 to 21) population across three unique scanners with controls and cases of PAE with downstream MaCRUISE volume metrics. We find that HACA3 qualitatively improves inter-site contrast variations, but statistical methods reduce greater site-related variance within the MaCRUISE volume metrics following an ANCOVA test, and HACA3 relies on follow-up statistical methods to approach maximal biological preservation in this context.

title: Comprehensive Design Strategy for High Performance DC/AC and AC/DC Flying Capacitor Multilevel Converters
authors: Logan Horowitz, Robert C. N. Pilawa-Podgurski
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The popularity of electric vehicles has soared in recent years, with projections forecasting continued growth. Similarly, the advent of artificial intelligence has led to an exponential rise in data center power requirements. Both of these sectors require next-generation, high-performance power conversion architectures to keep up with the rapid pace of development. The flying capacitor multilevel converter (FCML) has shown particular promise in DC/AC and AC/DC conversion applications. In this work, a comprehensive procedure is presented to optimize every aspect of FCML converter design. This approach is utilized to develop a 600 V, 6.6 kW hardware prototype which achieves 800 kW/L volumetric and 250 kW/kg gravimetric power density respectively, the highest values reported to date.

title: Structured Time-Frequency Feature Driven Meta Learning for Fall Detection with mmWave Radar
authors: Kaiyu Chen, Shaoxi Wang, Yongxin Guo, Hao Zhang
abstract: Millimeter-wave radar has emerged as a preferred modality for privacy-preserving human activity recognition. However, the effectiveness of current deep learning approaches is often severely compromised by data scarcity and incomplete feature representation in complex, real-world environments. To address these challenges, this paper presents a novel meta-learning framework tailored for few-shot radar recognition. Structured time-frequency feature strategy is proposed, representing the systematic extraction of multi-spectral joint temporal-spectral features from raw IQ data. Unlike traditional passive mapping, this strategy utilizes structured temporal alignment to construct distinctive two-dimensional maps that actively compensate for information loss. Synergizing with this, we propose the SAMSCNN architecture, which departs from conventional layer stacking to employ a customized hierarchical multi-scale topology. This architecture innovatively integrates spatially-aware self-attention with meta-learning optimization, enabling the network to rapidly adapt to new tasks while preserving fine-grained signal structures. Experimental results demonstrate state-of-the-art performance, achieving 99.21% accuracy specifically in fall detection. Notably, under severe few-shot conditions with only five training samples, the method sustains an average accuracy of 90.24% across six distinct human activities, significantly outperforming existing methods. Extensive comparative analyses further confirm the framework’s superior robustness across varying scene configurations and environmental interference.

title: Design and Performance Assessment of a Virtualized IED for Digital Substations
authors: Alailton J. Alves Junior, Denis V. Coury, Ricardo A. S. Fernandes
abstract: Digital substations have significantly enhanced power grid protection by replacing traditional copper wiring with fiber-optic communication and integrating IEC 61850-compliant Intelligent Electronic Devices (IEDs), resulting in greater efficiency, reliability, and interoperability. While these advancements provide improved interoperability, challenges such as high costs, complex networks, and limited upgrade-ability persist. To mitigate these issues, the virtualization of IEDs has emerged as a cost-effective solution, offering scalability, simplified maintenance, and reduced hardware costs by replacing traditional hardware-based IEDs with software-based counterparts. However, the performance and reliability of virtual IEDs (vIED) must be rigorously evaluated to ensure their robustness in real-time applications. This paper develops, implements, and evaluates a vIED designed to match the performance of its hardware-based counterparts. The vIED was deployed on a server using virtual machines, with its core logic implemented in low-level programming languages to ensure high-speed, deterministic behavior. The performance was evaluated using real-time simulations, focusing on the response times of the protection functions. The results demonstrated that the vIEDs achieved acceptable response times, validating their suitability for deployment in critical time-sensitive environments within digital substations.

title: Reduced graphene oxide-based electrochemical sensors to detect Rhodamine B molecules: A Review
authors: Xikun Lu, Wenqiang Guan, Monalisa Ghosh, Aniket Chakraborthy, Granthana Maulik, Md. Eshrat E Alahi, Anindya Nag
abstract: The paper presents a substantial review of the use of reduced graphene oxide (rGO)-based electrochemical sensors. rGO, a physicochemical derivative of graphene, is a pivotal carbon nanostructure for multimodal sensing applications. The high electrical conductivity and excellent charge-transfer properties enabled rGO to be functionalized and integrated to form efficient electrochemical sensors. It has been integrated with other nanomaterials to increase the overall selectivity and specificity of the resultant nanocomposites towards the target molecule. Rhodamine B is one of the toxic dyes used as a colouring agent in various food products. Detecting this chemical in food products is necessary because its accumulation has detrimental effects on human health. The paper highlights the capability of rGO-based composite sensors to detect Rhodamine B molecules. The research examples presented here have been divided into two groups based on the conjugation of rGO with metal oxides and non-metal oxides. It also shows the ability of the graphene-based sensors to remove Rhodamine B from contaminated real-time samples. Finally, the paper highlights the limitations of current sensors, potential remedies, and future trajectories for these sensors.

title: Trustworthy Longitudinal Brain MRI Completion: A Deformation-Based Approach with KAN-Enhanced Diffusion Model
authors: Tianli Tao, Ziyang Wang, Delong Yang, Han Zhang, Le Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Longitudinal brain MRI is essential for lifespan study, yet high attrition rates often lead to missing data, complicating analysis. Deep generative models have been explored, but most rely solely on image intensity, leading to two key limitations: 1) the fidelity or trustworthiness of the generated brain images are limited, making downstream studies questionable; 2) the usage flexibility is restricted due to fixed guidance rooted in the model structure, restricting full ability to versatile application scenarios. To address these challenges, we introduce DF-DiffCom, a Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KAN)-enhanced diffusion model that smartly leverages deformation fields for trustworthy longitudinal brain image completion. Trained on OASIS-3, DF-DiffCom outperforms state-of-the-art methods, improving PSNR by 5.6 % and SSIM by 0.12. More importantly, its modality-agnostic nature allows smooth extension to varied MRI modalities, even to attribute maps such as brain tissue segmentation results.

title: Study on current distribution characteristics of the Bi-2223 stacked-tape cable
authors: R. Shen, J. Jiang, X.S. Yang, X.B. Gao, Y.P. Zhu, J.W. Liu, X.F. Chen, H. Zhang, X.B. Hu
abstract: Bi-2223 high-temperature superconducting (HTS) tapes, with their relatively high upper critical field and current-carrying capability, the fine multi-filamentary microstructure, the excellent thermal stability under high magnetic fields and mature manufacturing processes, exhibit significant application potential in large-scale high-field magnets. The current distribution among Bi-2223 stacked tapes is a critical factor governing the stable operation of Bi-2223 stacked-tape cables. In this work, a five-layer Bi-2223 stacked-tape cable sample was investigated using an integrated measurement–inversion–simulation–validation approach, with a focus on the influence of joint resistance non-uniformity on the current distribution in multi-tape stacked cables. An annular Hall sensor array was employed to measure the circumferential magnetic field response of a short stacked-tape cable sample, and the current redistribution within the cable was identified and tracked through features of the derivatives of the magnetic field as a function of current. Taking account of the strongly nonlinear E–J characteristics and the anisotropy of the critical current density of the superconducting material, a two-dimensional finite element method (FEM) based on the H-formulation coupled with an equivalent circuit model (ECM) was used to investigate the effects of the non-uniformity of the joint resistance on the internal current distribution at different current ramping rates. A good agreement between experimental measurements and numerical simulations was obtained. The results provide experimental and theoretical support for joint resistance optimization, uniform current sharing, and current redistribution monitoring in HTS cables, and offer guidance for improving the current-carrying performance and operational stability of HTS stacked-tape cables for large-scale high-field magnets.

title: Distributed Communication Link Failures Compensation of Multiple Flexible Spacecrafts in ODE–PDE Form
authors: Dong Zhao, Mengfei Li, Yajie Ma, Wenjing Ren
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: This paper develops a distributed adaptive communication link failures compensation strategy for multiple flexible spacecrafts in ODE-PDE form. Obtaining and compensating the influence of vibration on attitude control enables the system to design control strategies separately for the ODE and PDE parts. To compensate the impact of communication link failures, an adaptive method is adopted. The asympotical stability of attitude control and the exponential stability of flexible vibration are achieved. The simulation also demonstrates that the distributed adaptive communication link failures compensation strategy is effective.

title: Combined Experimental-Numerical Spatio-Temporal Mapping of RF-EMF Exposure in City Environments
authors: Matthias Leeman, Kenneth Deprez, Jeroen Van Der Straeten, Mart Verlaek, Michel Goethals, Karen Van Campenhout, Günter Vermeeren, Wout Joseph
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: The rollout of 5G has increased spatial and temporal variability in RF-EMF exposure due to higher frequency bands, beamforming, and denser deployments, challenging conventional maps that rely only on simulations or sparse measurements. We present a hybrid framework that fuses deterministic ray-tracing with heterogeneous data (static sensor node data gathered over a two-year period, a mobile spectrum analyzer (SA) campaign, and personal exposimeter measurements) to generate high-resolution spatio-temporal exposure maps for a dense urban area of 2.5 km2. At its core is the Mean-Matched Gradient-Preserving Optimization (MMGPO), which estimates a smooth multiplicative correction field over the simulated grid to remove bias while preserving logarithmic gradients. When validated against 28 independent SA measurements, MMGPO achieves an RMSE = 5.47 dB and correlation r = 0.70 for the total electric field exposure over all frequency bands. By incorporating temporally varying node data, this approach provides policymakers, researchers, and the public with reliable, high-resolution exposure assessments across multiple time windows, capturing spatial detail while reflecting temporal variations in modern wireless networks.

title: Multi-Tier Hierarchical Intelligence for Joint RIS–NOMA Optimization in O-RAN-Enabled 6G Networks
authors: Ahmed Abdelreheem, Aya Allah A. Elserwe, Ahmed H. Zahran, Mohamed Abdel-Nasser
abstract: The evolution toward sixth-generation (6G) networks demands architectures that integrate intelligence, flexibility, and spectral efficiency beyond the limits of conventional fifth-generation (5G) systems. This paper presents a multi-tier hierarchical intelligence framework for joint optimization of Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) and Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access (NOMA) within an Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) architecture. The proposed framework takes advantage of the intrinsic control hierarchy—comprising of O-RAN: encompassing Service Management and Orchestration (SMO), RAN Intelligent Controller (RIC in Non-Real time (non-RT) and Near-Real-Time (near-RT) RIC, to decompose the joint optimization of RIS–NOMA into distributed subproblems on multiple timescales. This approach ensures scalable, interference-resilient and fairness-aware operation under realistic control, latency, and quantization constraints. The analytical results establish polynomial-time convergence under strong convexity assumptions, with approximation bounds expressed in terms of explicitly characterized error terms (decomposition, delay, quantization, and CSI errors). Extensive simulations confirm that the proposed framework significantly improves spectral efficiency, fairness, and outage probability compared to conventional centralized approaches. By unifying O-RAN’s architectural intelligence with RIS adaptability and NOMA’s spectral efficiency, this work provides a practical and theoretically grounded foundation for intelligent software-defined 6G heterogeneous networks (HetNets).

title: Approximate and Weighted Data Reconstruction Attack in Federated Learning
authors: Yongcun Song, Ziqi Wang, Enrique Zuazua
abstract: Federated learning (FL) is a distributed learning paradigm that enables multiple clients to collaboratively train a machine learning model without sharing private data. Although FL is normally regarded as privacy-preserving by design, recent data reconstruction attacks demonstrate that adversaries can recover clients' training data from the shared model updates. However, existing attack methods frequently fail in the widely used Federated Averaging (FedAvg) setting, where clients only transmit model parameters after executing multiple local training steps. To overcome this limitation, we propose an interpolation-based approximation method that renders attacks on FedAvg feasible by effectively estimating the intermediate model updates generated during local training. Furthermore, we design a layer-wise weighted loss function to enhance reconstruction quality. Specifically, weights are assigned to different layers based on the neural network architecture and are systematically tuned via Bayesian optimization. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approximate and weighted attack method outperforms existing state-of-the-art approaches, yielding substantial improvements across multiple image reconstruction metrics.

title: A Wideband Directional Coupler With Stable Coupling Factor Based on Spoof Surface Plasmon Polaritons
authors: Jiawei Zheng, Yuhui Ren, Cheng Xu, Yiqing Li, Yingxi Liu, Handong Wu, Ke Li, Fuwei Wang
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter presents a broadband miniaturized directional coupler based on two parallel spoof surface plasmon polariton (SSPP) transmission lines (TLs). The design leverages the intrinsic dispersion and slow wave characteristics of SSPPs to significantly compress the guided wavelength, thereby achieving substantial miniaturization of the device. To balance the phase velocities of the odd and even modes in the coupled microstrip lines, the capacitive parasitic patches are introduced into the coupling region, resulting in a flatter coupling response. Furthermore, the lumped resistors are incorporated between the parasitic patches and the TL to enable flexible control of the coupling factor while simultaneously enhancing the directivity across a wide operating bandwidth. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed coupler achieves a directivity of at least 14dB over a 64% fractional bandwidth (2.64–5.1 GHz), with a coupling coefficient maintained at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10~\pm ~0.9$</tex-math> </inline-formula> dB. Owing to its wide bandwidth, high directivity, compact size, and stable coupling performance, the coupler meets the stringent requirements of modern wireless systems for high-performance components.

title: CFBoost : Capacity-Boosting Feature Augmentation via Spatial-Geometrical Coordination
authors: Pin Liu, Yutao Li, Ruyue Xin, Yuzhu Wang, Bin Shi
abstract: Fine-grained image classification constitutes a long-standing challenge, with its performance bottlenecks stemming primarily from high inter-class similarity and insufficient discriminative feature. Feature augmentation alleviates this constraint through cross-image feature fusion, and as an online augmentation approach, integrates seamlessly into network architectures without requiring independent training phases. However, existing methods typically exhibit either random region selection mechanisms or constrained spatial awareness. Such inherent shortcomings critically constrain their ability to generate adequately capacity discriminative feature necessary for fine-grained classification. To this end, we propose CFBoost, a feature augmentation method that incorporates spatial-geometrical coordination to generate diverse features with sufficient capacity. It adaptively modulates spatial-geometric properties within fusion regions while simultaneously constructing feature-capacity-aware semantic labels. CFBoost integrates three core strategies: precisely locating fusion regions and positions to prevent occlusion of essential features, independently smoothing edges in all orientations of the fusion region to mitigate strong edge effects, and generating soft labels from a semantic weight matrix to improve the representational capacity of sample labels. Experiments across four benchmark datasets demonstrate CFBoost's superiority, achieving an average improvement of 2.48% relative to the optimal baseline, with particularly pronounced performance gains on images containing dispersed high-capacity regions.

title: Elevating GNSS-R Data Reliability: A Deep Learning Framework for Robust RFI Anomaly Detection
authors: Qianyang Shen, Yuhang Li, Pengbo Ju, Qiming Li, Deguo Zeng, Zhiyin Wang, Xuanpeng Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Global Navigation Satellite System Reflectometry (GNSS-R) data is highly vulnerable to radio frequency interference (RFI), which originates from the reflections of regional GNSS and Satellite-Based Augmentation Systems (SBAS), and external L-band interference signals from the ground. These influences show different textures in the delay-Doppler Map (DDM) of GNSS-R, but existing RFI anomaly detection methods cannot effectively distinguish their characteristics, resulting in high false positives and false negatives. To address these limitations, we propose a deep learning method that integrates contrastive self-supervised pretraining (SimCLR) on large-scale unlabeled DDMs using DDM-aware augmentation and a Swin backbone, followed by supervised fine-tuning of a Swin Transformer classifier under class-imbalance-aware optimization. The self-supervised pretraining stage extracts intrinsic DDM representations robust to scene and constellation variability, and the subsequent fine-tuning leverages limited labeled data to sharpen decision boundaries and curb both FPR and FNR. Experimental results show that the proposed method elevates RFI anomaly detection accuracy from approximately 25% to over 95%, with strong generalization across multiple GNSS constellations and operational scenarios. This approach substantially outperforms traditional mechanisms, reducing false alarms and missed detections, and thereby offering a reliable solution for real-world GNSS-R applications.

title: Phase Compensator Placement and Design for Subsynchronous Oscillation Damping of Multi-loop Grid-Forming Converters
authors: Chenguang Yan, Qinzhi Liu, Ying Liu, Jun Deng, Hongxi Yang, Yichun Wang, Baohui Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Under stiff grid conditions, grid-forming (GFM) converters are prone to subsynchronous oscillations (SSOs). Phase compensators are widely used as effective means to address SSO issues. However, owing to the lack of precise design regarding the location and configuration, the performance of phase compensators within multi-loop GFM control remains underutilized. To this end, the performance of the phase compensator is quantitatively evaluated by the negative damping area in the impedance model. With a parametric analysis of this area, the SSO-critical control loops and governing parameters are pinpointed, thereby identifying four optimal embedding locations for compensators. To effectively demonstrate the robustness of SSO damping under varying grid conditions, the required configuration properties of the compensator for each location are proposed, and a feasible and general compensator form is provided. Furthermore, the proposed design proves effective in a model incorporating frequency coupling. The hardware-in-loop (HIL) experimental results validate the theoretical insights as well as the effectiveness and robustness of the presented damping strategy. The systematic design framework can be adapted to guide the phase compensator in diverse multi-loop GFM controllers for effective SSO damping.

title: Bita: A Conversational Assistant for Fairness Testing
authors: Keeryn Johnson, Cleyton Magalhães, Ronnie de Souza Santos
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 7th International Workshop on Bots and Agents in Software Engineering
abstract: None

title: STATE: Safe and Threatening Adversarial Trajectory Engine_supp1-3694239.pdf
authors: Valerio La Gatta
abstract: As terrorists ramp up drone use, the need to protect cities, regions, and utilities from drone-based terror attacks has become an urgent priority. We present STATE, a novel GAN-based framework to automatically generate a set of safe and threatening drone trajectories over a region. Using STATE, security officials can generate synthetic trajectories for regions over which no such trajectories were previously recorded, enabling them to better test planned defenses. Jointly with security officials from The Hague, we show that STATE beats six recent baselines, achieving up to 75.8% improvement in trajectory plausibility and 35.8% in threat alignment, as evaluated by police experts.

title: ICEIT 2026 Content Announcement Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)

title: Multi-Point Simultaneous Tactile Force and Location Sensing through Soft Acoustic Waveguides
authors: Yufan Ye, Yuan Lin, Huiming Pan, Xinjun Sheng, Jianjun Meng, Shuo Jiang, Peter B. Shull
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Multi-point tactile sensing is fundamental for dexterous manipulation and wearable human-machine interactions. However, existing paradigms rely on complex and expensive array designs prone to significant crosstalk interference and typically fail to decouple force and location measurements across multiple contact points. Thus, we propose a soft acoustic waveguide with dual-end piezoelectric transducers for multi-point tactile sensing through Waveform-feature Integrated Tactile Sensing that extracts time-frequency features from echo signals and decouples multi-point tactile information. Multi-point contact experiment was performed by indenting at various locations with different depths, covering a working range of 60 mm and an applied force range of 0–1.6 N. The waveguide achieved simultaneous force and location measurements with mean absolute errors of 0.067 N and 1.35 mm, achieving 68% and 42% reductions in measurement errors compared to the latest acoustic waveguide based on time domain echo peak detection. The waveguide enables high-precision multi-point simultaneous tactile force and location sensing, providing fundamental capabilities for dexterous manipulation systems.

title: IEEE Women in Engineering
authors: N/A

title: An Air-Jet Continuum Robot for Industrial Integrated Fluidic Circuit Inspection_supp7-3682403.mp4
authors: Yongchang Zhang
abstract: Industrial fluidic circuits operating under long-term extreme conditions are prone to defects such as cavitation, corrosion, and cracks, raising a critical demand for in-situ robotic inspection. Traditional manual disassembly-based methods are inefficient, while existing robots lack the ability to simultaneously adapt to complex geometries and maintain stable adsorption on surfaces of varying curvature. This study proposes an air-jet continuum robot (AJCR) that integrates three core modules: air-jet modules, a unit flexible continuum skeleton (UFCS), and stiffness-adjustable flexible suction cups (FSCs), enabling effective crawling and inspection inside integrated industrial fluid circuits. The UFCS provides omnidirectional bending capability of up to 196° enabling navigation through complex channels including abrupt turns of 120° inside stop valves. Under an input pressure of 0.8 MPa, the AJCR generates a maximum air-jet counter-thrust of 4.72 N for adsorption and 4.16 N for driving, ensuring reliable locomotion on unstructured, slippery, and discontinuous surfaces. Based on the sliding suction strategy, the FSC offers a maximum static adsorption force of 35.1 N, reduces sliding friction to 2.25 N on stainless steel, and adapts to curved surfaces with radii as small as 125 mm, facilitating stable sliding adsorption even across welded seams. Experimental results demonstrate that the AJCR smoothly navigates pipeline systems, enters stop valves, mixed-flow pumps and pressure vessels, and operates in liquid-filled environments, significantly improving inspection coverage and operational safety in fluidic delivery systems.

Traditional manual disassembly-based methods are inefficient, while existing robots lack the ability to simultaneously adapt to complex geometries and maintain stable adsorption on surfaces of varying curvature. This study proposes an air-jet continuum robot (AJCR) that integrates three core modules: air-jet modules, a unit flexible continuum skeleton (UFCS), and stiffness-adjustable flexible suction cups (FSCs), enabling effective crawling and inspection inside integrated industrial fluid circuits. The UFCS provides omnidirectional bending capability of up to 196° enabling navigation through complex channels including abrupt turns of 120° inside stop valves. Under an input pressure of 0.8 MPa, the AJCR generates a maximum air-jet counter-thrust of 4.72 N for adsorption and 4.16 N for driving, ensuring reliable locomotion on unstructured, slippery, and discontinuous surfaces. title: Hybrid Modeling and Simulation for Urban Mobility: A Systematic Review and Future Research Agenda
authors: José Da Silva Ferreira Junior, Josiane Palma Lima, Alexandre Ferreira De Pinho
abstract: This study investigates the relationship between Hybrid Modeling and Simulation (HMS) and urban mobility research through a systematic literature review conducted in accordance with the PRISMA protocol. This review synthesizes applied studies from the last five years, indexed in the Scopus database, to to map the current state of research on HMS in urban mobility. Based on the identification, screening, and eligibility criteria, 55 studies were selected and analyzed using a combined bibliometric approach and in-depth qualitative analysis, considering six urban mobility domains and four central research questions. The findings indicate that while convergence between these fields remains nascent, signs of maturation are emerging, particularly in studies employing empirical data and practical model applications. Recurrent challenges were identified, including the lack of terminological standardization for HMS, the inherent complexity of hybrid models, and limitations related to computational validation, issues that are also highlighted in the existing literature. Conversely, the analysis revealed a consistent set of research opportunities grouped into ten thematic axes: data enhancement, methodological integration, paradigm expansion, and emerging applications in complex urban environments. This study contributes by providing a consolidated overview of the state-of-the-art and outlining promising directions for future research at the intersection of these fields.

title: Fine-Grained Fashion Feature Embedding for Global, Local, and Contextual Analysis in Fashion Social Media Popularity Prediction_supp1-3694039.pdf

title: Vector-Guided Embezzler: A Reconstruction Attack Against Retrieval-Augmented Generation in Agentic AI Communication
authors: Lishuang Hu, Hao Wang, Tao Xiang, Zhuo Chen, Jialing He, Shangwei Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Agentic AI systems increasingly rely on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to ground autonomous decision-making in external knowledge. They become critically dependent on distributed, often third-party, infrastructure — introducing new vulnerabilities at the intersection of embodied intelligence and networked communication. While prior work has examined privacy risks from end-users, we identify and formalize a more systemic threat: the LLM API provider itself acting as a malicious adversary within the agentic ecosystem. In this paper, we introduce a new threat model where the LLM API provider is the malicious adversary and demonstrate this vulnerability. Specifically, we propose a novel three-stage attack, Embezzler, to reconstruct the private knowledge base of a RAG system. The proposed Embezzler is to first infer the embedding model of the RAG system and use this surrogate model to prob new, non-redundant text chunks within the embedding space, rather than the semantic space. Then, we systematically explores and refines the embedding space to maximize the amount of unique chunks reconstructed with minimal adversarial queries. We theoretically analyze the effectiveness of our Embezzler and conduct extensive experiments under various system settings. Our results show that our Embezzler consistently and significantly outperforms four baseline methods and reconstructs nearly 90% of the RAG knowledge base. A component-wise ablation study further validates the critical role of each stage. Our findings highlight the severe privacy risks of deploying RAG systems with third-party LLM APIs in Agentic AI communication.

title: Heat Transfer Modeling of MHPA-Cooled Electrical Machines Using Modular 3D LPTN with Parameter Calibration
authors: Kun Wang, Bo Ma, Sheng Huang, Ji Zhang, Gang Lei, Shuo Wang
abstract: The application of advanced cooling technologies has promoted the development of high-performance motors, but also imposes challenges on thermal analysis. In response to this demand, this paper proposes a method aimed at improving the accuracy and efficiency of motor thermal analysis. The research focuses on a micro heat pipe array (MHPA)-integrated efficient cooling motor with an asymmetric structure. First, a three-dimensional lumped parameter thermal network (3D LPTN) is established based on the modular cell segmentation method, and the key thermal parameters in the model have been derived. After evaluating the correlation between different parameters and motor thermal performance, a calibration approach is employed to adjust the parameters’ initial values by using the optimization algorithm. Finally, the prototype is fabricated and tested, both numerical simulations and experiments validate the method’s accuracy for temperature prediction under different working conditions, which comprehensive confirms the effectiveness of this method.

title: Building an Inclusive Framework for Chinese Language Learning Supported by Generative AI: A Case-Based Analysis
authors: Jiexuan Zhu, Yu Zhao, Ana María Pinto-Llorente
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: In recent years, the global demand for Chinese as a Second Language (CSL) education has grown rapidly, driven by China’s increasing cultural and economic influence. At the same time, the advancement of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GAI), particularly large language model (LLM)-based tools, has introduced new opportunities for personalized, interactive, and inclusive language learning. Although GAI has the potential to enhance learner engagement and provide adaptive feedback, empirical research on its use in CSL classrooms is limited. Most studies focus on English or general higher education contexts. As a result, it remains unclear how GAI can address learner diversity, promote intercultural competence, and support equitable learning in CSL settings. This study aims to bridge this gap by analyzing five empirical cases of GAI-assisted CSL teaching and proposing a GAI-Supported Inclusive CSL Framework, which integrates learner characteristics, inclusive pedagogical practices, and AI-enabled technologies. The framework seeks to provide both theoretical guidance and practical reference for enhancing inclusivity, personalization, cultural responsiveness, and ethical implementation in CSL education

title: Agentic Artificial Intelligence Framework for Automated Adverse Drug Reaction Detection and Analysis
authors: Venkata Sivaranjani Vedanabhatla, N.Krishnaraj
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Adverse Drug Reactions (ADRs) remain one of the leading causes of patient morbidity, mortality, as well as growing healthcare cost across the world. The existing pharmacovigilance systems rely mostly on spontaneous reporting systems and manual and expert-based analytical processes, thus being limited in their scalability, timeliness, and breadth. The rapid growth of real-world data stores such as electronic health records, adverse event databases, and patient-generated drug reviews necessitates deployment of intelligent automation in order to develop an effective drug-safety monitoring. In this paper, we present a proposal of a framework by means of Agentic Artificial Intelligence (AI) that aims at supporting the automated identification, validation, and description of ADRs. The architecture is designed in terms of a modular, multi-agent system that includes Data Collection, ADR Extraction, Signal Detection, Validation, and Comparative Analysis agents. All the agents perform autonomous and specialized duties, but as a centralized body of ADR knowledge. The models of natural language processing are used to identify drug-ADR relationships in textual data that lacks structure, and statistical signal-detection methods are used to find nascent safety signals in structured databases, such as FAERS. The validation processes uses maintained repository of knowledge repositories, especially, SIDER, to increase reliability and reduce false-positive identifications. The framework allows the continuous monitoring, provides explainable outputs and it can be deployed at scale. According to the results of empirical studies, agentic AI is capable of significantly increasing the accuracy of ADR detection, minimizing the cost of operations, and enhancing patient safety. This study highlights the suitability of agentic AI systems as a new generation paradigm of intelligent pharmacovigilance.

title: Semantic Intention-Driven Service Composition in SOA: A Context-Aware Case Study in Smart Buildings
authors: Emna Fki, Ismael Bouassida Rodriguez
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Smart building environments require service-composition mechanisms that remain aligned with user intentions while adapting to rapidly changing contextual conditions. Classical SOA-based compositions, typically driven by static I/O matching or predefined schemas, are unable to guarantee stability, comfort, and responsiveness when daylight, occupancy, device availability, or sensor reliability fluctuate. This paper introduces a semantic, intention-driven, and context-aware dynamic composition approach tailored for smart buildings. The method combines (i) enriched intention graphs embedding comfort bands, equipment capabilities, and uncertainty profiles, (ii) weighted semantic matching between intentions and abstract services, (iii) multi-source context-aware semantic affinity for coherent chaining, and (iv) a hysteresis-enabled conditional refinement mechanism ensuring stable decisions under fluctuating environmental conditions. We define a comprehensive evaluation protocol based on realistic HVAC–lighting scenarios, including daylight transitions, glare episodes, thermal load variations, and sensor imperfections. Our results demonstrate that localized recomposition—guided by context validity predicates and fragment-level QoS propagation—significantly improves responsiveness, stability, and comfort compared to static or partially contextual baselines. The proposed approach provides a scalable and dependable foundation for adaptive cyber-physical orchestration in smart building settings.

title: Research on the Investigation and Improvement Path of College English Teachers' Digital Intelligence Literacy Based on the Use of Digital Intelligence Platform
authors: Weirong Li
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: In the context of the in-depth promotion of educational digital intelligence transformation, digital intelligence platform has become the core carrier driving the transformation of college English teaching paradigm, and the improvement of teachers' digital intelligence literacy is the key premise to release the teaching efficiency of the platform. Taking 32 college English teachers as the research object, this paper uses the questionnaire survey method to explore the use characteristics of teachers' digital intelligence platform, the status of digital intelligence literacy and the promotion dilemma, constructs a four-dimensional digital intelligence literacy evaluation model of "technology application, teaching integration, data governance, and safety ethics", and puts forward targeted improvement paths. The study finds that teachers' utilization rate of basic digital intelligence platform is high (such as Chaoxing Xuexitong 90.63%), but the application of high-order functions was insufficient; digital intelligence literacy is characterized by an imbalance of "up to standard basic ability and weak high-level ability", and data governance ability (3.59 points) is the core weakness; the main constraints are the imperfect training system, insufficient platform adaptability and imperfect guarantee mechanism. Through empirical data support and path optimization, this study provides theoretical reference and practical paradigm for promoting the transformation of digital intelligence in college English teaching and improving the quality of teachers' professional development, and enriches the empirical results of teachers' digital intelligence literacy research in the disciplinary context.

title: Digital Era Insights into Online Technical Readiness and Teaching Effectiveness: The Mediating Role of Teaching Presence
authors: Yuxin Jiang, Yuxia Du, Yuli Li, Xuemin Gao, Daoming Fu, Mei Zhang
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Under the dual imperatives of "digital transformation in education" and the "normalization of blended learning," online teaching has developed into an integral component of elementary education. However, effectively organizing online classrooms, sustaining student attention, and ensuring the quality of instructional interaction have emerged as pressing practical challenges for online teachers. To identify factors affecting the effectiveness of online teaching for primary school teachers and improve teaching quality, we developed a model that reveals the relationship between online teaching technical readiness, teaching presence, and teaching efficacy based on literature and survey research. We analyzed data collected from 1,647 primary school teachers in China using SPSS. The data showed that online teaching technical readiness positively predicts online instructional efficacy and presence, with the latter partially mediating the effect of the former on online teaching efficacy. These findings have practical implications for improving teacher professional development and enhancing the abilities necessary for successful online teaching among primary school teachers, trainers, principals, and researchers.

title: Modeling of Fundamental Manufacturing Process Based on Multi-Level Directed Acyclic Graph
authors: Jiawei Shen, Jiajia Zheng, Yi He, Zhenyu Gu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: In view of the lack of description of the process organization relationship in the existing process modeling research, which can not adapt to the flexible and dynamic changes of the fundamental manufacturing process structure, a dynamic modeling method of fundamental manufacturing process based on multi-level directed acyclic graph is proposed. Firstly, the concept of node, coordinate, pipeline, hyperlink and other model elements is put forward by analyzing the common characteristics of fundamental manufacturing process, and a multi-level directed acyclic graph is constructed. Secondly, dynamic modeling algorithm is designed to bind process data to multi-level directed acyclic graph, generate process data flow graph, and realize process digital modeling. Then, the fundamental manufacturing process dynamic modeling verification system is designed and developed. Finally, an example of machining process modeling is given to illustrate the application of the proposed method in process modeling.

title: Aero-Engine Remaining Useful Life Prediction Based on Temporal Attention-Augmented Dual-Stream Feature Fusion
authors: Siyu Jia, Qiang Yang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Predicting the remaining useful life (RUL) of aero-engines is very important for flight safety and maintenance decisions. Traditional LSTM networks have trouble capturing both local fault impacts and global degradation trends when dealing with long time-series sensor data. Their prediction accuracy drops in cases with complex faults or high-frequency noise. To solve this, this paper proposes a new RUL prediction framework based on temporal attention enhancement and dual-stream feature fusion. The model uses a parallel structure to process sensor data. One branch is an attention-enhanced LSTM that captures local time-series features and key degradation stages. The other branch uses global average pooling to reduce high- frequency noise and extract the overall degradation trend. Experiments on the NASA C-MAPSS dataset (subsets FD001 and FD003) show that this dual-stream design effectively reduces feature interference. In the more complex condition FD003, the RMSE reaches 12.07, which is about 9% lower than baseline models. The method has strong noise resistance and good potential for real engineering use.

title: Gen-PBL in Vocational Big Data Education: Exploring Cognitive Offloading Behaviors via Structured GenAI Scaffolding
authors: Shu Chen, Fenghua Liu
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The integration of Generative AI (GenAI) into programming education accelerates code production but introduces pedagogical challenges regarding novice schema construction. This exploratory study investigates the behavioral dynamics of human-GenAI collaboration within a Project-Based Learning (PBL) environment, specifically examining how variations in instructional constraints influence learners’ cognitive offloading behaviors. Through a 16-week quasi-experimental design involving 82 vocational college students, we longitudinally tracked two cohorts with divergent prior knowledge baselines as they interacted with GenAI under structured scaffolding (mandatory prompt templates) versus free exploration conditions. Our quantitative analysis suggests that the quality of cognitive engagement outweighs interaction volume. Hierarchical regression shows that prompt engineering quality is a significant predictor of project success (β = 0.42, p < 0.001), while raw GenAI querying frequency is not statistically significant. Furthermore, behavioral log data demonstrates that when structural scaffolds are abruptly removed, novice learners exhibit a distinct regression toward a "trial-and-error" regulation mode—marked by a high-frequency, low-quality querying loop and diminished project performance. These findings suggest that in GenAI-assisted environments, mandatory prompt structuring may serve as an external regulatory script. By intentionally introducing "cognitive friction," this approach compels learners to articulate problem decomposition before automated generation, thereby mitigating dysfunctional cognitive offloading. Based on these empirical observations, the study proposes design principles for preserving germane cognitive load through gradual scaffold fading and the integration of process-oriented assessments in future human-GenAI collaborative programming interfaces.

title: Contrastive Regularization for Spectral Graph Networks in Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Chaima Khemiri, Wissem Inoubli, Mohamed Farah
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Hyperspectral image classification (HSIC) remains challenging due to the intrinsic nature of hyperspectral imagery (HSI): high spectral dimensionality, strong within-class variation, and limited annotated pixels. Recent Graph Convolutional Network (GCN)-based studies show that making better use of spectral structure can significantly improve representation quality, and many existing models introduce contrastive objectives to strengthen this behavior.In this work, we integrate a lightweight Centroid Contrastive Learning Loss (CCL) module into a spectral-graph GCN framework providing a simpler and more stable alternative. The graph is built at the pixel level using a Gaussian spectral affinity, a common strategy for modeling relationships in hyperspectral data. The GCN then propagates spectral information across this graph, while the CCL term encourages embeddings to move closer to their class centroid and remain better separated from other classes.Experiments on the Indian Pines and Pavia University datasets show that this GCN+CCL formulation reduces overfitting and produces better-structured latent representations compared to a standard spectral GCN, confirming the benefit of a centroid-driven metric constraint in HSIC.

title: Learning Adaptive Rules and Confidence-Based Engine for Finding Security Incidents by Pattern Correlation
authors: Khushi Bhargava, Metilda Florence
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The Security Operation Centers (SOCs) handle a broad range of security logs, which can increase alert fatigue for the staff. Traditional Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) systems use static correlation rules, which tend to be outdated and often produce many false positives, while machine learning methods can act as black boxes and be computationally expensive.This paper presents an adaptive and transparent correlation system. We present a technique that learns probabilistic correlation rules from enterprise logs and assigns risk scores to individual sessions using a model that groups entities into confidence levels. The system designs a log format standardization process for various log sources, combines events into user-host sessions with a fixed time duration, and uses association rule mining with sliding time windows. The proposed scoring metric combines rule confidence, lift, severity, and temporal aging to measure correlation strength in a limited and transparent manner.Transparency is one of the key features of the proposed system. The technique can be implemented efficiently on a CPU-only system and does not depend on black-box predictive models. The system is tested on the CERT Insider Threat Dataset, Threat Dataset v6.2, and the results show a dramatic reduction in alerts while maintaining a conservative detection level in the scenarios used for validating proxies. The results obtained show that adaptive learning of rules with confidence correlation can be a viable alternative to traditional SIEM correlation engines and opaque systems for the detection of anomalous activity in an enterprise SOC.

title: Inverting the Cross-Modal Experience of a Soft Haptic Display for Virtual Touch Interactions at a Science Festival
authors: Femke E. van Beek, Irene A. Kuling
abstract: Visual displays present XR users with a virtual world, but haptic displays make this virtual world tangible and thus more interactable. The first generation of haptic displays were based on rigid feedback technology, but soft (pneumatic) haptic displays are now becoming more ubiquitous. We previously designed such a display and take it to science festivals to test the public’s opinion about haptics. In our first experiment at a science festival, participants showed quite consistent patterns when tuning the actuation parameters of our soft device to match visual images. In the current experiment, we tested the inverse paradigm at another science festival, by presenting 87 participants with the 13 preferred haptic stimuli from our previous experiment. For each haptic stimulus, we asked participants to choose the matching image from a set of three. Participants performed significantly above the chance level, with a score of 51±14%. Nonetheless, a direct comparison of our previous and current results shows no significant linear relationship between the two experiments. This indicates that the current experiment was inherently different from the previous one. Our results show that it is possible to find a matching image, when presented with a haptic stimulus on our device, but that the paradigm matters. Secondly, they indicate that large-scale experiments at science festivals can be used for basic perceptual research, such as comparing perceptual paradigms and testing cross-modal asymmetries. Finally, they underline that the multi-sensory experimental design is crucial for targeting the intended experience.

title: HERMES: An FPGA-Based Multi-Cipher HSM for Resource-Constrained IoT Systems
authors: Adithi Ambatipudi, Samarth Nadig, Sravanthi Nallajonna, Areeb Khan, Vadiraja Acharya
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: The rapid growth of Internet of Things (IoT) deployments has increased the demand for security mechanisms that provide low latency, scalability, and flexible deployment of cryptographic algorithms at the network edge. Software-only cryptographic solutions on devices with limited resources increase performance and energy costs. In contrast, fixed-function hardware options, such as ASIC-based hardware security modules (HSMs), lack the flexibility to adapt to diverse and evolving IoT environments. This paper introduces HERMES (Hardware-Enabled Reconfigurable Modular Edge Security), a centralized multi-cipher FPGA-based Hardware Security Module built on the PYNQ-Z2 platform for on-site cryptographic offloading in resource-constrained heterogeneous IoT networks. The system integrates AES-128, SIMON, and PRESENT as AXI4-compliant cryptographic IP cores that support parallel execution and batch processing. This design allows for efficient offloading of cryptographic tasks from low-power micro-controller-based IoT nodes while maintaining centralized key management. The experimental evaluation shows clear performance and resource trade-offs among the supported ciphers in a centralized HSM architecture. Lightweight ciphers reduce the complexity of the datapath, but their system-level overhead depends on the session management frequency and batching behavior. Under continuous batch-processing workloads, the proposed system runs steadily, with average CPU usage staying below 15% and memory usage below 50%. These results confirm that HERMES offers a scalable and flexible hardware-assisted security solution that can support diverse, resource-limited IoT edge deployments.

title: Integrate Augmented and Virtual Reality Settings for Digital Twins: Coordinated Representation and Management of Continuous Manufacturing Operations
authors: B. Uma Rani, Mohammad Kanan, P. Yamini Devi, Ramesh Banoth, Padmavathi N, Alok Jain
abstract: Industry 4.0 has accelerated the development of digital twins for monitoring, modeling, and optimizing industrial processes in real time. Conventional digital twin platforms suffer from two-dimensional dashboards and inadequate visualization interfaces, which hinder natural engagement and situational awareness. The goal of this project is to illustrate and manage continuous manufacturing processes by combining augmented reality, virtual reality, and digital twin technologies. Physical and virtual assets are brought into harmony in the proposed architecture using cloud-edge computing infrastructure, industrial IoT sensors, and real-time data streaming. Augmented reality interfaces provide on-site operators with operational analytics, predictive warnings, and maintenance insights, while virtual reality settings allow for immersive simulation, training, and distant collaboration. With the help of the bidirectional architecture, immersive digital models and equipment can receive input in real-time. Immersive environments are ideal for optimizing processes and doing predictive maintenance due to data analytics and machine learning. Evaluation of performance improves operational transparency, operator training, downtime, and decision-making compared to conventional monitoring systems.
