Group-level myelin estimates derived from synthetic <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$T_{2}$</tex> w MRIs correlate highly with those from genuine <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$T_{2}$</tex> w MRIs (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r=0.75$</tex>), while individual-level correlations confirm subjectwise fidelity (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r=0.53 \pm 0.094$</tex>). T2VNet lowers acquisition barriers to facilitate population studies of myelination, support research in under-resourced settings, and advance diagnosis for demyelinating conditions.

title: State Space Modeling of User Intent Drift for Confidence-Calibrated News Recommendation
authors: N M Vedhinee, Rajalakshmi D, Janisa Ria, Prakratee Singh
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: News Article suggestion models these days often rely on user click histories that are based on static preference estimates without any measure of the confidence level of their recommendations. Problems arise when user interests change dynamically, and their news article recommendations are not aligned with their preferences. We aim to tackle this situation with the help of the State Space Model (SSM) using Large Language Model (LLM) that keeps a hidden state, which tracks evolving user interest and preference changes over time. Using the MIND dataset, the LLM grasps the meaning of the new articles and stores them as dense vectors. Along with this, the user's progress as well as their changes in articles of interest are tracked using SSM's hidden state. While traditional recommendation engines show you articles ranked by relevance alone, our model additionally includes a second dimension: quantifying how confident it is about its recommendation. This shift reduces the over-confidence in the model's predictions, which proves valuable for readers with sporadic changes in topics of interest. By building uncertainty into the engine itself, our goal is to recommend news articles to users without hiding how the choices were made, even when new trends emerge rapidly.

title: Millimeter Wave Inverse Pinhole Imaging
authors: Akarsh Prabhakara, Yawen Liu, Aswin C. Sankaranarayanan, Anthony Rowe, Swarun Kumar
abstract: High-resolution radio frequency (RF) imaging relies on large antenna arrays or Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR). It is challenging to enhance aperture without significant hardware complexity and cost of arrays, or high resolution motor encoder needs of SAR. We introduce Umbra, a high-resolution imaging system built using just a single millimeter-wave (mmWave) and rotating “inverse pinholes” to enhance angular resolution. This paper presents the imaging system model, design, and evaluation of mmWave inverse pinholes. The inverse pinhole is attractive for its lightweight nature, which enables low-power rotation. As they rotate in the field of view, they implicitly leave signatures in RF measurements that can be used to track pinhole location to sub-wavelength precision even on cheap, low end DC motors without encoders. We finally also show how ordinary rotating objects, such as fans can act as natural mmWave inverse pinholes. One can opportunistically upgrade a single antenna system just by carefully positioning such objects in the antenna’s field of view. Our evaluation shows Umbra resolving up to 2.5° with just a single antenna, a 5× improvement compared to 14° from a multi-antenna baseline.

title: Opportunistic Transmission Empowered Rate-Splitting Multiple Access for Military Communications: Approaches and Challenges
authors: Xi-Ran Zhang, Wen-Bin Sun, Zhaolin Zhang, Haochen Liu, Ling Wang, Ang Gao, Wei-Xiao Meng
abstract: Rate splitting multiple access (RSMA) is a promising multiple access (MA) technology that can be used to enhance the performance of military multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems. By virtue of its rate-splitting method, this technology can flexibly regulate the interference management strategy, achieving superior spectrum efficiency (SE) compared to traditional MA schemes. It is well-suited to meet the requirements of the modern battlefield, such as secure tactical data transmission, enhanced energy efficiency (EE)-enabled edge computing offloading, and satellite-terrestrial tactical communication. However, due to the complexity of the actual combat electromagnetic environment, it is extremely difficult to obtain perfect channel state information (CSI), which results in performance limitations. The opportunistic beamforming (OBF) technique can only feed back signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) values and utilize a multi-tactical-node selection mechanism to free the system from the constraints of perfect CSI, thereby improving the robustness of the military networks. This article analyzes the advantages brought by the combination of RSMA and OBF technologies, and proposes an OBF-aided RSMA (ORSMA) scheme. The article describes three types of performance improvements that ORSMA can achieve and analyzes current applicable tactical scenarios. Subsequently, we analyze the challenges faced by ORSMA in future combat scenarios.

title: COSMO: O-RAN-Based Service Management and Orchestration for Cross-Technology Multi-Tenant Radio Access Networks
authors: Miguel Catalan-Cid, Joan Josep Aleixendri, Jorge Pueyo, Pau Tomas, Daniel Camps-Mur
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: The evolution toward 6G networks envisions a heterogeneous Radio Access Network (RAN) comprising diverse access technologies, such as private 5G, public 4G/5G, and Wi-Fi, managed by multiple stakeholders. While considerable research effort has been devoted to O-RAN-based frameworks enabling rApp and xApp implementation and validation, few works provide integrated support for cross-technology RAN orchestration, end-to-end multi-tenancy, and a unified subset of SMO functionalities, including Non-RT RIC components. This paper introduces COSMO, a novel RAN Service Management and Orchestration platform designed to support heterogeneous 3GPP (5G NR, LTE) and non-3GPP (Wi-Fi) access networks. COSMO enables cross-technology multi-tenancy, defined as the capability to allow multiple tenants to dynamically share heterogeneous RAN resources with explicit resource allocation guarantees based on Service Level Agreements (SLAs). This is achieved through management primitives that support flexible and on-demand resource allocation. Additionally, the platform includes a cross-technology Non-Real-Time RAN Intelligent Controller (Non-RT RIC) that enables the development of intelligent rApps for closed-loop control and network orchestration. Beyond its architectural design, COSMO improves resource utilization and operational flexibility through unified orchestration of heterogeneous multi-tenant RAN resources. Through prototyping and benchmarking, we demonstrate the effectiveness of COSMO in resource allocation, SLA enforcement, and scalability. In our prototype, the SLA-based rApp reduces SLA violation from approximately 21% to below 10% under dynamic traffic conditions in a heterogeneous RAN deployment including 5G, 4G, and Wi-Fi access networks. Our results confirm that COSMO offers an efficient solution for managing and orchestrating future multi-tenant cross-technology RAN environments.

title: PhysHVS-LDCT: Dose-Controllable Physics-Based Low-Dose CT Synthesis for Benchmarking and Data Augmentation
authors: Yousra Taifour, Marouane Tliba, Nour Aburaed, Azeddine Beghdadi, Faouzi Alaya Cheikh, Aladine Chetouani, Zuheng Ming, Rachid Jennane
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Reducing radiation exposure in Computed Tomography (CT) remains a clinical priority, particularly for radiosensitive groups such as pediatric and pregnant patients. Although Low-Dose CT (LDCT) reduces radiation-related risk, lowering the tube current increases noise and artifacts that can compromise diagnostic quality. Progress in LDCT reconstruction is further limited by the scarcity of paired full-dose and low-dose data, as ethical constraints prohibit multi-dose acquisitions. Moreover, most public CT datasets contain only clinical-dose scans, restricting their use for low-dose imaging research. To address this limitation, we introduce a benchmarking-oriented generative framework for synthesizing realistic LDCT images from existing clinical-dose data. We further present PhysHVS-LDCT, a dose-controllable synthesis pipeline designed as an effective dataset augmentation tool. By utilizing both physics-based and perceptual priors informed by human contrast sensitivity, PhysHVS-LDCT produces LDCT images that closely match real low-dose scans. Our results highlight that PhysHVS-LDCT achieves superior performance across all dose levels when compared with SOTA GAN- and VAE-based baselines, producing more accurate and realistic LDCT simulations.

title: GarmentRec: towards Individual Garment Reconstruction from a Monocular Human Image_supp1-3691682.pdf
authors: XB Gao
abstract: Reconstructing high-quality garment models from monocular images is important as it provides a practical and effective solution for human digitization and virtual try-on etc. Recent implicit function-based garment reconstruction methods recover free-form geometry but struggle to reconstruct individual garment meshes from human images and tend to produce disembodied limbs or degenerate shapes for novel views. In contrast, explicit parametric garment template models can be utilised to construct separate meshes and constrain the shape reconstruction robustly. However, this limits the reconstruction of garment details and shape variations, such as the wrinkles and pockets etc. To address this problem, in this paper, we introduce a novel explicit garment template that is designed for both closed and open garment topology. Powered by our new garment template, we further propose a detailed garment reconstruction method based on a monocular view that can process both the closed and open types for shape recovery. To capture those challenging parts with unknown geometry and topology, we predict displacement maps on the parameterization domain for the target garment from the monocular image and elaborate it to the 3D garment surface via the UV coordinates, achieving realistic details on the 3D garment shape. Extensive experiments demonstrate the accuracy and robustness of our method and show that realistic details like garment wrinkles and pockets can be faithfully recovered in an explicit way. The code and dataset are available at https://github.com/worryDes/GarmentRec.

title: ISAC Beamforming for Underwater Acoustic Networks Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Hengyi Lin, Tianle Zhang, Xiaowen Ye, Xianxin Song, Yi Wu
abstract: This paper proposes DeepUIB, a deep reinforcement learning-based beamforming scheme for integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) in underwater acoustic (UWA) networks. The scheme aims to maximize the communication sum-rate under sensing constraints by addressing a key UWA challenge: long propagation delays that cause echoes from past transmissions to interfere with subsequent ISAC signals. Besides, a symmetric experience augmentation mechanism is developed for DeepUIB to accelerate convergence. Simulation results show that DeepUIB attains a higher sum-rate, converges faster, and exhibits stronger robustness than other schemes under various simulation setups.

title: Multi-Port DCX Converter for EV On-Board Charging and Low-Voltage Power Distribution
authors: Héctor Sarnago, Óscar Lucía
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Modern battery electric vehicles (EVs) include a 400/800 V high-voltage network, mainly used for charging and motor drive purposes, and a 12/48 V low-voltage network, used to power all the low voltage loads in the vehicle. Current power conversion schemes use different power converters and architectures, leading to complex and costly implementations. On the one hand, on-board chargers (OBC) are used to provide ac-charging in an efficient and convenient way. On the other hand, the low-voltage battery and network are managed by dc-dc converters, playing a key role in providing a reliable low-voltage dc-network for ancillary loads. However, new trends on bidirectional power conversion and low-voltage battery elimination, as well as the need for cost-effective and high-power-density power conversion, require redesigned power conversion architectures. The main aim of this paper is to provide an integrated power converter consisting of a quadruple-port implementation featuring a dc-transformer power conversion scheme (QDCX). It enables improved implementation with high power-density that simultaneously provides on-board charging capabilities and low-voltage power distribution. The topology is experimentally validated on an 11 kW, 2×400V OBC prototype supplying simultaneously a 12-V low-voltage generation.

title: Radiomics LUS Transformer for Liver Tumour Segmentation
authors: Ali Khalife, Mohammad Alkhatib, Erol Ozgur, Emmanuel Buc, Bertrand Le Roy, Youcef Mezouar, Adrien Bartoli
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Accurately segmenting liver tumours in laparoscopic ultrasonography (LUS) is critical for computer-assisted intervention guidance with augmented reality (AR). However, LUS images are often degraded by speckle noise, low contrast, and acoustic artifacts, while the liver's complex anatomy and tumour heterogeneity further complicate segmentation. These challenges are exacerbated by the probe's limited field of view and restricted manoeuvrability, which frequently produce partial or inconsistent tumour appearances across frames. Moreover, isoechoic tumours exhibit echogenicity similar to the surrounding liver tissue, rendering them nearly invisible in individual frames. To address these limitations, we propose RadioLUS, a hybrid deep-learning framework that operates on sequences of LUS frames. It integrates hierarchical visual features from a Swin-UNet backbone with handcrafted radiomics descriptors that capture speckle, texture, and shape characteristics. By aggregating temporal information across frames, the framework enhances weak tumour boundaries and improves the detection of subtle tissue variations. Experimental results demonstrate that RadioLUS outperforms the state-of-the-art under challenging LUS imaging conditions.

title: Beyond Dichotomization: Ordinal Prediction of Functional Stroke Outcomes from 4D CTP and Clinical Metadata Using Deep Learning
authors: Kimberly Amador, Anthony J. Winder, Jens Fiehler, Philip A. Barber, Matthias Wilms, Nils D. Forkert
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Predicting functional outcomes after acute ischemic stroke is critical for prognosis and treatment planning, as it could help identify patients most likely to benefit from interventions. These outcomes are typically measured by the 90-day modified Rankin Scale (mRS90), a seven-level disability score. However, most deep learning models for functional outcome prediction reduce this to a binary variable, losing information about intermediate recovery levels and limiting clinical interpretability. So far, only a few studies have attempted to model the full mRS90 range using multiclass classification, ignoring the ordered nature of the scale. To address this, we propose a multimodal deep learning model that predicts mRS90 from pre-treatment 4D computed tomography perfusion (CTP) imaging and clinical metadata using CORAL-based ordinal regression. Using data from 111 thrombectomy-treated patients, our results suggest that the proposed ordinal approach outperforms standard multiclass and linear regression models by better leveraging the ordered nature of stroke outcomes.

title: Synergizing Deep Learning and Biological Heuristics for Extreme Long-Tail White Blood Cell Classification
authors: Duc T. Nguyen, Hoang-Long Nguyen, Huy-Hieu Pham
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Automated white blood cell (WBC) classification is essential for leukemia screening yet remains challenging under extreme class imbalance and domain shift. These limitations often cause deep models to overfit dominant classes while failing to generalize to rare pathological subtypes. To address this issue, we propose a three-stage hybrid framework. First, a self-supervised Pix2Pix restoration module mitigates synthetic noise and restores high frequency cytoplasmic details. Second, we integrate a Swin Transformer ensemble with MedSigLIP contrastive embeddings to enhance rare-class semantic representation. Finally, we introduce a biologically inspired refinement strategy combining geometric spikiness analysis and Mahalanobis-based morphological constraints to explicitly rescue suppressed minority predictions. Our hybrid framework achieves a Macro-F1 score of 0.77139 on the private leaderboard, demonstrating strong robustness under extreme long-tail distributions.

title: Federated Learning with Layer-Wise Contribution Estimation and Consensus-Based Parameter Personalization for Medical Image Classification
authors: Ming Cai, Tianpeng Deng, Jing Li, Zaiyi Liu, Ying Wang, Guoqiang Han, Chu Han
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Personalized Federated Learning (PFL) presents a promising paradigm for mitigating privacy concerns and addressing data heterogeneity in medical images. However, the presence of data heterogeneity can introduce bias into the global model. As a result, the biased global model may degrade local performance in the personalization phase. To tackle this challenge, we propose FedLP, a novel framework that enhances global aggregation fairness and improves personalized performance. First, we propose a layer-wise aggregation strategy on the server, where it dynamically estimates each client's contribution based on the gradient discrepancies. Consider the aggregated global model may not consistently align with local distributions, we then introduce an adaptive parameter personalization process on the client side. By selectively instituting local parameters with the global model parameter with maximizing consensus optimization, clients can better retain global knowledge they need and maintain important model parameters to local data distributions. Experimental results demonstrate that FedLP outperforms state-of-the-art methods and achieves fairer performance for several medical image classification tasks.

title: Data-Efficient Motor Condition Monitoring with Time Series Foundation Models
authors: Deyu Li, Xinyuan Liao, Shaowei Chen, Shuai Zhao
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Motor condition monitoring is essential for ensuring system reliability and preventing catastrophic failures. However, data-driven diagnostic methods often suffer from sparse fault labels and severe class imbalance, which limit their effectiveness in real-world applications. This paper proposes a motor condition monitoring framework that leverages the general features learned during pre-training of two time series foundation models, MOMENT and Mantis, to address these challenges. By transferring broad temporal representations from large-scale pre-training, the proposed approach significantly reduces dependence on labeled data while maintaining high diagnostic accuracy. Experimental results show that MOMENT achieves nearly twice the performance of conventional deep learning models using only 1% of the training data, whereas Mantis surpasses state-of-the-art baselines by 22%, reaching 90% accuracy with the same data ratio. These results demonstrate the strong generalization and data efficiency of time series foundation models in fault diagnosis, providing new insights into scalable and adaptive frameworks for intelligent motor condition monitoring.

title: Low-Loss Pavement-Embedded Multi-MHz Capacitive Wireless Power Transfer System using Optimized Curved Couplers
authors: Syed Saeed Rashid, Raquel Sarabia-Soto, Dheeraj Etta, Khurram K. Afridi
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) systems for electric vehicles (EVs) enable dynamic charging while in motion and offer a promising solution to concerns of limited driving range and long charging times. Although inductive WPT systems have been widely studied, they are bulky, costly, and constrained by ferrite losses. Capacitive WPT systems avoid ferrites and are lighter and less expensive, but when embedded in pavements they suffer high dielectric losses due to fringing electric fields at the coupler edges. This work proposes novel curved couplers that suppresses fringing electric fields, thereby reducing losses in pavement materials. A 6.78-MHz, 1-kW capacitive WPT system using the proposed curved couplers is designed and analyzed. In simulations, the optimized curved couplers achieve a 70% reduction in pavement losses and a 40% reduction in surrounding fringing fields compared to conventional flat couplers. Two 6.78-MHz 1-kW experimental prototypes, one using flat couplers and the other using the proposed curved couplers, are constructed and tested within asphalt. The experimental prototype using curved couplers embedded in pavement achieves 45% loss reduction in asphalt slab compared to the conventional flat couplers system.

title: Enhanced Flying-Capacitor Boost Multilevel Inverter (EFC-MLI) with Optimized Voltage Balancing and Current Spike Elimination
authors: Ahmed Awadelseed, Arkadiusz Lewicki, Charles Odeh
abstract: This paper proposes an Enhanced Flying-Capacitor Boost Multilevel Inverter (EFC-MLI) with optimized capacitor voltage balance technique. The proposed inverter configuration is derived from integration of H-Bridge (main cell) with new flying capacitor multilevel inverter (auxiliary cell). Moreover, the auxiliary cell generates the required voltage levels, while the main cell generates the base of the output voltage waveform. The proposed EFC-MLI utilized single dc source to achieve 9-level with dual voltage gain. The lower component count, reduced capacitor voltage rating, lowering cost, eliminates capacitor charging current spikes, high voltage gain, and capacitor voltage balance makes the proposed inverter attract+ive for renewable energy applications such as, Photovoltaic (PV) system, Fuel Cell system, and hybrid PV-FC system. Furthermore, to enhance the efficiency of the proposed EFC-MLI, a Hybrid PWM strategy with FC voltage balancing algorithm was implemented to ensure precise voltage regulation across capacitor while optimizing inverter performance. It minimizes voltage ripple, reduces switching losses, eliminates FC current spikes, and improves THD. Additionally, the proposed inverter was compared with recently proposed SC-MLIs. To support the proposed work, experimental results are provided which shows a good agreement with the analytical approach.

title: Semi-Supervised Carotid Plaque Segmentation with Multi-Scale Deep Supervision and Consistency Learning for Multi-View Vulnerable Plaque Classification
authors: Long Yang, Xiaoyin Chen, Bing Wang, Pengpeng Ou, Shaomin Niu, Peipei Zhang, Zeping Gao, Teng Ma
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Carotid plaque vulnerability is closely associated with cerebrovascular events, making accurate plaque analysis important for clinical risk assessment. In this study, we propose a framework for automated carotid ultrasound plaque analysis integrating semi-supervised segmentation and classification. A multi-scale deeply supervised dual-view DenseNet model with a UniMatch-based semi-supervised strategy was used for plaque segmentation, enabling effective utilization of labeled and unlabeled data. Radiomic features extracted from plaque, lumen, and union regions were used for vulnerable plaque classification with TabPFN and semi-supervised training. Evaluation on ultrasound examinations yielded a segmentation score of 61.72 and an F1-score of 63.83, with an efficient processing time of 163.54 ms per case (time score: 87.61), securing 7th place overall and demonstrating the potential of our framework for automated plaque analysis.

title: A Single-Phase Non-Isolated Six-Switch Buck-Boost Inverter using GaN Power Devices
authors: Halladi Shashwatha Kumara Kedlaya, Saleh Farzamkia, Vishal Kumar Meena, Alex Q. Huang
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel single-stage non-isolated single-phase inverter based on a six-switch buck-boost (SSBB) converter followed by an unfolding bridge. Due to the flying capacitor in the boost-stage of the SSBB, the converter can achieve high gain with small passive components, leading to higher power density. Moreover, single-stage topology reduces switching losses and enables high power efficiency. In this paper, the operating modes and modulation techniques of the SSBB for generating a half-sine voltage are explained. The performance of the proposed inverter and its modulation technique is validated through simulation and experiments.

title: Cyber Modelling of Advanced Metering Infrastructure using ns-3
authors: Issa Mlimbila, Amit Kumar, Himanshu Jain
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: Digital communication is an integral component of smart grids. However, while physical flow of electricity has been modelled for decades, it is difficult to find instances of simulating the communications network for smart grid planning and operations. This paper addresses this gap by presenting an approach to simulate advanced metering infrastructure (AMI) communication model, firewalls and cyberattacks in ns-3. The performance of the developed model is evaluated on several IEEE-13, 34, 37, and 123 node feeder models. Based on the simulations performed using these models it was observed that propagation delay and system loss are key parameters that influence the performance of the AMI system. Results for the impact of cyberattacks with and without the consideration of firewalls show significant differences in the time taken for data to be received at the desired locations. Further, the wall-clock time required to simulate the IEEE 123-node feeder is around 19 seconds, compared to 3 seconds it takes to simulate the IEEE 13-node feeder. Although the amount of data exchanged increased 11 folds, the time taken increased only 6 times, demonstrating the scalability potential of ns-3 for modelling AMI systems.

title: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
authors: N/A

title: IEEE Foundation
authors: N/A

title: Human-Machine Collaboration in Cybersecurity Operations for Faster Threat Response
authors: Rajesh Tiwari, Ramgopal Kashyap, Gopi Mannava, Pawan Kumar, Balpreet Singh, Sruthi P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: The paper provides a human-machine working model that would enhance speed of threat investigation during internet security missions. The rising sophistication and pace of cyberattacks require solutions that would integrate the analytical pace of automated solutions, to the contextual reasoning of human specialists. To help solve the problem, the researcher suggests the Human-Machine Collaborative Threat Response (HMCTR) approach, which incorporates the machine-based threat response (detection, prioritization, and alert correlation), and the validation of the human-in-the-loop (decision) alongside the refinement of the response. The suggested approach allows the large-scale security data to be processed by automated tools in real time, and a human analyst to give a level of strategic judgment to decrease the false positive and increase the accuracy of response. The researcher undertakes the evaluation of HMCTR based on operational analysis improvement of the response time, situational awareness and the mitigation effect have been improved against fully automated response or human-only. The findings suggest that the collaborative model would greatly decrease the delay in threat response and contribute to how the adaptive defense mechanisms are applied in the dynamic cyberspace. The paper concludes that HMCTR provides a flexible and effective model of the current cybersecurity operations through synergistic human strengths and machine strengths. This framework will be very appropriate in the security operation centers that require faster and more dependable means of handling a threat in high volumes of alerts. The suggested approach attains a comprehensive detection accuracy of 97.4%.

title: Modulation Method for Reducing DC-Link Current Ripple in Open-End Winding Motor Drive Systems
authors: Kota Sato, Ryusei Yamada, Kenta Emori
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper proposes a new modulation method that achieves a substantial reduction in DC-link current ripple for three-phase full-bridge inverters driving open-end winding motors. Although various ripple-suppression techniques have been proposed, most are designed for three-phase half-bridge inverters driving star-connected winding motors and cannot be applied to three-phase full-bridge inverters driving open-end winding motors due to fundamental differences in DC-link current behavior and the strict requirement for suppressing zero-sequence current. To overcome these limitations, this study analyzes the distinctive characteristics of DC-link current ripple in full-bridge inverters and the constraints imposed by zero-sequence current control and then proposes a modulation method that resolves both challenges simultaneously. The proposed method achieves a remarkable ripple reduction exceeding 70% across the practical operating range of traction motors, enabling a significant reduction—approximately 40%—in DC-link capacitor volume based on analytical evaluation. Importantly, the method can be fully compatible with zero-sequence current suppression and employs a carrier-based formulation, ensuring low computational burden suitable for real-time implementation without requiring any hardware modifications. The validity of the proposed approach is further confirmed through experiments on a full-scale prototype, demonstrating strong agreement with analytical predictions and verifying its effectiveness in practical applications.

title: SiC-MOSFET/Si-IGBT Hybrid Dual-Side Auxiliary Edge Resonant Cells Soft-Switching PWM Inverter
authors: Tomokazu Mishima, Kazuki Sato, Shigehiro Yamamoto, Ching-Ming Lai
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: A soft switched PWM inverter topology with SiC-MOSFET/Si-IGBT hybridized resonant cells and its pulse patterns are explored in this paper. The dual-side active edge-resonant cells(DARC)-assisted auxiliary resonant commutation pole (ARCP) is the topological feature where no large chemical capacitors are demanded, which contributes for a high power density and long life cycle dc-ac inverter applications. The basic and extended pulse patterns are comparatively studied in terms of reactive power and recycling processes in the ARCP, whereby power conversion efficiency and commutation are theoretically revealed. Performances and practical effectiveness of the newly-developed soft switching half-bridge PWM inverter with 40 kHz SiC/Si power transistors hybrid implementation are demonstrated by experiments with a mini-scale prototype, and compared with the hard switching and RCD snubber PWM inverters.

title: A Master-Slave Gate Driver Architecture for Paralleling of Medium-Voltage SiC MOSFET Power Module With Oscillation Feedback Path Blocking
authors: Zhixing Yan, Gao Liu, Hongbo Zhao, Stig Munk-Nielsen
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Gate oscillations in paralleled SiC MOSFET power modules occur during switching transients, potentially causing false switching behavior and device damage. In our previous work [1], mitigation strategies by adjusting parasitic inductances were proposed. However, as the number of paralleled modules increases, mechanical design constraints limit the effectiveness of optimizing parasitic distribution. To overcome this a master-slave gate driver architecture that simplifies the gate-driver circuit for multiple paralleled modules is introduced in this paper. Blocking inductors and damping resistors are incorporated into the slave driver board to suppress oscillation feedback paths. Experiments on a double pulse test platform with three paralleled 10 kV modules confirm the architecture’s effectiveness.

title: Weakly Supervised Counterfactual Spatio-Temporal Multiple Instance Learning for Inflammation Detection in Endoscopic Inflammatory Bowel Disease Assessment
authors: Bisi Bode Kolawole, Ujwala Chaudhari, Irene Zammarchi, Giovanni Santacroce, Rocio Del Amor, Pablo Meseguer, Cecilia Pugliano, Valery Naranjo, Subrata Ghosh, Marietta Iacucci, Enrico Grisan
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) significantly impacts patients' quality of life, and accurate endoscopic assessment is critical for effective management. Conventional deep learning methods require frame-level annotations, which are labor-intensive and impractical for large video datasets. We propose a weakly supervised counterfactual spatio-temporal attention multiple instance learning (CFSpatioMIL) framework that captures spatial and temporal dependencies using only video-level labels. To enhance robustness and interpretability, we introduce a top-<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex> instance loss to encourage attention on multiple discriminative frames, and a counterfactual consistency mechanism that enforces stable predictions when highly attended frames are removed or replaced. We evaluate our approach on two multi-center IBD video datasets, training on one and externally validating on the other. CFSpatioMIL consistently outperforms state-of-the-art MIL baselines in F1 score and AUC, demonstrating its potential as a scalable approach for standardized inflammation assessment.

title: A Continuous and Interpretable Morphometric for Robust Quantification of Dynamic Biological Shapes
authors: Roua Rouatbi, Juan-Esteban Suarez Cardona, Alba Villaronga-Luque, Jesse V. Veenvliet, Ivo F. Sbalzarini
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: We introduce the Push-Forward Signed Distance Morphometric (PF-SDM) for shape quantification in biomedical imaging. The PF-SDM compactly encodes geometric and topological properties of closed shapes, including their skeleton and symmetries. This provides robust and interpretable features for shape comparison and machine learning. The PF-SDM is mathematically smooth, providing access to gradients and differential-geometric quantities. It also extends to temporal dynamics and allows fusing spatial intensity distributions, such as genetic markers, with shape dynamics. We present the PF-SDM theory, benchmark it on synthetic data, and apply it to predicting body-axis formation in mouse gastruloids, outperforming a CNN baseline in both accuracy and speed.

title: Unlinkable and Decentralized Credential Management for Agentic AI
authors: Qi Liu, Peng Jiang, Jun Zhu, Shaolong Tang, Wei Wang, Liehuang Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: The evolution toward agentic AI is transforming edge devices from passive sensors into autonomous agents capable of collaborative decision-making. However, the open interaction among these agents exposes the system to malicious intrusions, making robust authentication essential to validate legitimate agents. Traditional authentication mechanisms still suffer from sensitive information leakage due to the potential exposure of agents' real-time locations and session linkability. Furthermore, previous privacy-preserving techniques often employ a centralized manager to prevent information leakage, whose compromise breaks the security of the whole system. In this paper, we propose B-CM, a blockchain-based credential management mechanism for agentic AI. B-CM augments traditional authentication by integrating anonymous credentials with blockchain technology to enable unlinkable and auditable agent authentication in a decentralized manner. To optimize efficiency, we leverage off-chain computation performed by edge servers and fast on-chain verification, accompanied by credential aggregation. We implement B-CM over Hyperledger Fabric to evaluate its performance. Experimental results show that the off-chain computation remains highly efficient, requiring only 8.35 s for distributed key generation and 9.7 ms for credential issuance, while the on-chain computational overhead is nearly negligible.

title: 3D DoseGLA: Gated Linear Attention with Wavelet-Domain Constraint for Efficient 3D Radiotherapy Dose Prediction
authors: Ku Zhao, Xianhao Zhou, Wenjun Liao, Shichuan Zhang, Shaoting Zhang, Guotai Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Dose calculation, as the core of radiotherapy, remains a timeconsuming and experience-dependent process. While deep learning methods have been proposed to improve efficiency, they often face challenges in global modeling and computational complexity, especially with 3D data, limiting their clinical applicability. Moreover, their predicted dose maps are often over-smoothed due to pixel-wise loss functions such as L1 loss that ignore inter-pixel relationship. To address these issues, we propose an efficient framework DoseGLA for 3D dose prediction. First, we propose a Gated Linear Attention-based UNet (GLAUNet) that incorporates an improved gating mechanism inspired by Mamba into linear attention to enhance global modeling capacity while retaining linear computational complexity. Second, a Wavelet-domain Constraint Loss (WDCL) is proposed to guide the model in learning inter-pixel relationship with better prediction contrasts in frequency domain. Experiments on the OpenKBP dataset show that our framework significantly outperforms the state-of-theart model with around 80% fewer parameters, achieving a Dose Score of 2.33 and a DVH Score of 1.46.

title: UAV-Based Thermal Sensing: Community-Based Learning and Research
authors: Kelvin Kang Wee Tang, Alvin Lau Meng Shin, Tan Liat Choon, Muhammad Hafiz Mohd Yatim, Norhadija Darwin, Nor Alafiza Yunus
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: Today rapid urbanization has intensified urban heat islands (UHI) effects, particularly in the tropical regions where high solar irradiance and limited nocturnal cooling amplify surface heat retention. Consequently, these overwhelming temperatures bring negative consequences to the environmental ecosystem and human health. Surface material properties, specifically those constructed urban pavements and building facades, significantly contribute towards UHI effect due to their thermal properties, including albedo and thermal inertial absorption or solar radiation. Over the years, satellitederived land surface temperature (LST) has been extensively used to characterize broad and long-term thermal variations; nevertheless, micro-scale thermal contributions in tropical urban environments remain poorly quantified. To capture the localized dynamics of heat distribution, unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) or drone equipped with thermal sensors were deployed to generate high-resolution thermal maps with less than $3 \times 3$ centimeters per pixel of a public streetscape in Taman Teratai, Johor Bahru. Surface materials including asphalt, grass, concrete, ceramic roofs, car roofs, water and metal bodies were classified and analyzed. UAV-derived LST was validated against in-situ infrared thermometer measurements using RMSE and correlation analysis. Results demonstrate that industrial zones exhibit significantly higher ambient air temperatures (up to $\mathbf{1 - 5 ^ { \circ }} \mathbf{C}$) compared to residential areas, with metal, ceramic roofs and asphalt surfaces contributing disproportionately to daytime heat accumulation. Strong agreement between UAV-based LST and in-situ temperature data ($\mathrm{R}^{2}=0.91$, $\mathrm{RMSE}=1.87^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$) confirms the reliability of UAV thermal sensing for micro-scale UHI assessment. The findings provide quantitative evidence to support material-based heat mitigation strategies and demonstrate the value of UAV thermal imaging for urban climate studies in tropical environments and also highlights the potential of UAV thermal sensing as a valuable tool for urban microclimate monitoring, sustainable design and climate-resilient city planning.

title: Need-Oriented VR Interaction Design for Adults with Intellectual Disabilities
authors: Lihui Liu, Yiran Peng, Xin Lyu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Vision, Advanced Imaging and Computer Technology (VAICT)
abstract: Virtual reality (VR) is increasingly used in rehabilitation and assistive technologies, yet many deployments remain technology-driven, emphasizing immersion and novelty over the everyday needs of people with disabilities. This paper asks how VR systems for rehabilitation and vocational training can be designed around practical user needs rather than technical capabilities alone. We propose a need-oriented design framework with three layers that link practical needs, usage context and interaction design, and demonstrate it through a case study of a VR vocational training system for adults with intellectual disabilities. The results show that integrating VR into bakery training roughly doubled productivity compared with conventional training. Task-structured, multimodal and error-tolerant interactions helped trainees rehearse complex workflows safely, reduced cognitive load and supported their self-confidence. Compared with general accessibility and universal design frameworks, the proposed three-layer framework focuses on step-by-step reasoning from everyday disability-related needs to concrete interaction choices in a specific training setting. This work offers HCI and VR researchers a practical way to translate disability-related needs and real-world constraints into concrete, accessible interaction designs.

title: APEC 2026 Contributor Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)

title: Comprehensive Control of Bipolar DC Solid-State Transformers Based on ISOP-QAB Converters
authors: Jonghun Yun, Seongmin Moon, Jaesang Yoo, Giovanni De Carne, Shenghui Cui
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper proposes a comprehensive control strategy for bipolar dc solid-state transformers (SSTs) based on input-series output-parallel (ISOP) quad active-bridge (QAB) converters. While existing works have addressed controls for SST in ISOP structure, or for QAB converters, individually, an integrated control scheme that harmonizes the entire system has remained unaddressed. The proposed strategy ensures both voltage and power balancing of the bipolar dc SST by leveraging a straightforward conversion of the power flows into phase-shift angles within each QAB module. The effectiveness of the proposed strategy is validated through experimental tests on an SST prototype with ±750 V / 36 kW ratings, composed of six QAB modules.

title: Towards Robust Urban Obstacle Perception for Visually Impaired Navigation: A Dataset and Baseline Study
authors: Maryam Koraichi, Samira Chabaa, El Mehdi Cherrat, Abdelouhab Zeroual, Mohamed Zarboubi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: Urban environments pose significant mobility challenges for visually impaired individuals due to the presence of diverse obstacles and complex scenes, while many existing assistive navigation approaches rely on generic datasets or simplifying assumptions that limit their applicability to real-world urban use. This paper presents a foundational study toward urban obstacle perception for visually impaired navigation by introducing a custom vision-based dataset comprising eight urban obstacle classes relevant to assistive mobility, manually annotated in YOLO format and reflecting realistic multi-object scenes. A lightweight YOLO-based model is trained as a baseline to assess detection performance on the proposed dataset. Experimental results demonstrate the feasibility of multi-class urban obstacle detection while highlighting challenges related to scale variability and scene complexity. This work establishes an initial benchmark and dataset to support future research on robust and deployable assistive navigation systems.

title: SA-UNetv2: Rethinking Spatial Attention U-Net for Retinal Vessel Segmentation
authors: Changlu Guo, Anders Nymark Christensen, Anders Bjorholm Dahl, Yugen Yi, Morten Rieger Hannemose
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Retinal vessel segmentation is a crucial yet challenging task for early diagnosis of diseases such as diabetes and hypertension. SA-UNet achieves promising performance by introducing spatial attention in the bottleneck, but it underuses attention in skip connections and fails to address the severe foreground-background imbalance. We propose SA-UNetv2, a lightweight model that injects cross-scale spatial attention into all skip connections to strengthen multi-scale feature fusion and adopts a weighted Binary Cross-Entropy (BCE) + Matthews Correlation Coefficient (MCC) loss to improve robustness to class imbalance. On the public DRIVE and STARE datasets, SA-UNetv2 achieves state-of-the-art performance with only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 2 M B}$</tex> memory and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 2 6 M}$</tex> parameters (less than 50% of SA-UNet), and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1}$</tex> second CPU inference on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$592 \times 592 \times 3$</tex> images, demonstrating strong efficiency and deployability in resource-constrained, CPU-only settings. The code is available at github.com/clguo/SA-UNetv2.

title: Direct Power Control for the Battery Port of a Multisource Inverter
authors: Chang Liu, Bing Li, Guozhu Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The multisource inverter is promising in integrating batteries with renewable energy sources, as the DC-DC converter normally required for battery power control can be eliminated. This single-stage conversion structure can reduce system cost and improve overall efficiency. This paper proposes a direct power control strategy for the battery connected to a multisource inverter. The proposed control strategy is directly implemented within the modulation process. A zero-loop-width hysteresis controller is employed to regulate the battery current by injecting an appropriate positive or negative zero-sequence signal. The method eliminates the need for control parameter design and features simple implementation and low computational burden, making it promising for practical applications. Steady-state and dynamic experimental results under different degrees of capacitor voltage imbalance demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Bipolar Boost Converter for Transformerless Battery Energy Storage Integration in the Medium Voltage Distribution Grid
authors: Sabrina R. Helbig, Jacob A. Mueller, Jack Flicker, Brandon M. Grainger
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: A bidirectional bipolar boost converter was studied for integration of utility-scale battery energy storage systems (BESS) in medium voltage (MV) distribution grids. The converter accommodates the lower voltages produced by BESS and transforms them to a DC voltage level suitable for direct (transformerless) interconnection to an MV point of connection. For the application of BESS integration, this work analyzes the bipolar boost converter topology; develops a PI-based, dual-loop control method to track a voltage reference and to balance DC link capacitor voltages; and derives a representative dynamic model of the system. The dynamic model and control approach are validated in a scaled-down 80 V : 400 V, 1 kW prototype of the MV storage interface converter.

title: Adaptive Reinforcement Learning (ARL) Control of a Multi-port Resonant Converter in UAV Systems
authors: Erdem Asa, Kerim Colak
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This study presents an adaptive reinforcement learning (ARL) control framework for a multi-port resonant converter used in hybrid unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) power systems. The converter integrates high-frequency half-bridge input ports connected to a rectified engine–generator set and a battery energy storage system, along with a semi-bridgeless active rectifier supplying the propulsion load. A deep RL agent is trained to dynamically regulate inter-port phase-shift commands in real time based on flight conditions and load power demand. The ARL controller autonomously identifies phase-shift combinations that maximize conversion efficiency while maintaining stable and coordinated power flow, even under rapidly varying operating scenarios. This data-driven approach eliminates the need for explicit system modeling or extensive manual tuning and enables coordinated control among multiple power ports without inter-port communication. Experimental results validate that the ARL based strategy achieves reliable power sharing and consistently high-efficiency operation across diverse UAV operating conditions.

title: Text-Guided Approach for Accurate Lesion Detection Using Vision-Language
authors: J. Sofia Priya Dharshini, Pogabuddi Abdul Thouhid, Gorige Indhu, Akula Vamsi
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: The role of accurate lesion segmentation is important in medical image assessment and diagnosis. Segmentation of key regions in images can be challenging, as these areas may vary in size and position. Common methods that rely solely on visual information often miss small or randomly located details, leading to reduced accuracy. This work aims to introduce a method for detecting lesions more accurately through text-guided training using a vision-language learning approach. During the training phase, this strategy enables the vision-based system to learn both image features and structured text descriptions. During inference, users do not need to provide structured text descriptions to identify lesion patterns. The model employs a cross-modality learning method to connect textual cues with visual patterns, which helps improve the detection of both large and small lesions. Experiments on the COVID-CT dataset show enhanced segmentation performance, achieving a Dice coefficient of 77.08 %, an Intersection over Union (IoU) of 66.06 %, precision of 83.54 %, and recall of 75.31 %, with an average processing time of 0.0189 seconds per image.

title: From Volumes to Slices: Computationally Efficient Contrastive Learning for Sequential Abdominal CT Analysis
authors: Po-Kai Chiu, Hung-Hsuan Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: The requirement for expert annotations limits the effectiveness of deep learning for medical image analysis. Although 3D self-supervised methods like volume contrast learning (VoCo) are powerful and partially address the labeling scarcity issue, their high computational cost and memory consumption are barriers. We propose 2D-VoCo, an efficient adaptation of the VoCo framework for slice-level self-supervised pre-training that learns spatial-semantic features from unlabeled 2D CT slices via contrastive learning. The pre-trained CNN backbone is then integrated into a CNN-LSTM architecture to classify multi-organ injuries. In the RSNA 2023 Abdominal Trauma dataset, 2D-VoCo pretraining significantly improves mAP, precision, recall, and RSNA score over training from scratch. Our framework provides a practical method to reduce the dependency on labeled data and enhance model performance in clinical CT analysis. We release the code for reproducibility. <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>1https://github.com/tkz05/2D-VoCo-CT-Classifier

title: End-to-End Optimization of Sparse Ultrasound Linear Probes
authors: Sergio Urrea, Adrian Basarab, Hervé Liebgott, Henry Arguello
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Ultrasound imaging faces a trade-off between image quality and hardware complexity caused by dense transducers. Sparse arrays are one popular solution to mitigate this challenge. This work proposes an end-to-end optimization framework that jointly learns sparse array configuration and image reconstruction. The framework integrates a differentiable Image Formation Model with a HARD Straight Thought Estimator (STE) selection mask, unrolled Iterative SoftThresholding Algorithm (ISTA) deconvolution, and a residual Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). The objective combines physical consistency (Point Spread Function (PSF) and convolutional formation model) with structural fidelity (contrast, Side-Lobe-Ratio (SLR), entropy, and row diversity). Simulations using a 3.5 MHz probe show that the learned configuration preserves axial and lateral resolution with half of the active elements. This physics-guided, data-driven approach enables compact, cost-efficient ultrasound probe design without sacrificing image quality, and it is expandable to 3-D volumetric imaging.

title: A Unified Off-Board Charging System for Integrated Wired and Wireless EV Charging
authors: Cheol-Hee Jo, Subhajyoti Mukherjee, Mostak Mohammad, Omer Onar
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This work explores a unified off-board charger approach that enables both wired and wireless charging within a single system architecture. Instead of deploying two independent converters, the proposed configuration switches between an LLC-based operation for wired charging and an LCC-S–type operation for wireless charging by rearranging the excitation of commonly used components. With this reconfiguration, the same switch bridge, resonant elements, and magnetic coupling coils can be utilized in both modes without relying on relays or additional hardware paths. To assess the basic operation of the proposed system, PLECS circuit simulations were performed for both wired and wireless charging modes. A 3.3-kW prototype was also built, and representative experimental results for each mode confirm that the unified converter can operate properly while reducing hardware duplication.

title: Distributed and Crowdsourced Artificial Intelligence: Enabling a Robust and Scalable 6G Network Architecture
authors: John S. Vardakas, Golshan Famitafreshi, Christoforos Christoforou, Iacovos Ioannou, Antonella Molinaro, Chaouki Ben Issaid, Juan Jose Vegas Olmos, Thanasis Charemis, Marius Iordache, Marios Sophocleous, Giacomo Bernini, Christos Verikoukis
abstract: The sixth generation of mobile networks is expected to revolutionize the way people, devices, and services connect by combining ultralow latency, massive scalability, and pervasive intelligence. To achieve these ambitious goals, a complete redefinition of the network architecture and the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) as a core enabler are equally important. A flexible and adaptive architecture is essential for supporting diverse requirements, ranging from immersive applications and autonomous systems to real-time digital twins and mission-critical communications. This article explores the pivotal role of AI in shaping 6G, with particular emphasis on distributed AI as a foundation for managing applications and services across heterogeneous infrastructures. By extending intelligence from traditional cloud environments to the edge and far-edge/device layer, distributed AI empowers networks to dynamically allocate resources, efficiently orchestrate services, and adapt in real time to user demands. We present the vision of an AI-driven fully distributed network architecture that integrates orchestration frameworks, closed-loop automation, and collaborative AI/machine learning (ML) pipelines. Ultimately, distributed AI emerges as the cornerstone of a service-oriented 6G infrastructure, paving the way for intelligent connectivity that can meet the diverse needs of future digital societies.

title: DINOV3-Guided Cross Fusion Framework for Semantic-Aware CT Generation From MRI and CBCT
authors: Xianhao Zhou, Jianghao Wu, Ku Zhao, Jinlong He, Huangxuan Zhao, Lei Chen, Shaoting Zhang, Guotai Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Generating synthetic CT images from CBCT or MRI has a potential for efficient radiation dose planning and adaptive radiotherapy. However, existing CNN-based models lack global semantic understanding, while Transformers often overfit small medical datasets due to high model capacity and weak inductive bias. To address these limitations, we propose a DINOv3-Guided Cross Fusion (DGCF) framework that integrates a frozen self-supervised DINOv3 Transformer with a trainable CNN encoder-decoder. It hierarchically fuses global representation of Transformer and local features of CNN via a learnable cross fusion module, achieving balanced local appearance and contextual representation. Furthermore, we introduce a Multi-Level DINOv3 Perceptual (MLDP) loss that encourages semantic similarity between synthetic CT and the ground truth in DINOv3's feature space. Experiments on the SynthRAD2023 pelvic dataset demonstrate that DGCF achieved state-of-the-art performance in terms of MS-SSIM, PSNR and segmentation-based metrics on both MRI → CT and CBCT → CT translation tasks. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work to employ DINOv3 representations for medical image translation, highlighting the potential of self-supervised Transformer guidance for semantic-aware CT synthesis. The code is available at https://github.com/HiLabgit/DGCF.git.

title: A Comparative Study of Machine Learning and Deep Learning for Out-of-Distribution Detection
authors: Jihyeon Baek, Seunghoon Lee, Gitaek Kwon, Doohyun Park
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Out-of-distribution (OOD) detection is essential for building reliable AI systems, as models that produce outputs for invalid inputs cannot be trusted. Although deep learning (DL) is often assumed to outperform traditional machine learning (ML), medical imaging data are typically acquired under standardized protocols, leading to relatively constrained image variability in OOD detection tasks. This motivates a direct comparison between ML and DL approaches in this setting. The two approaches are evaluated on open datasets comprising over 60,000 fundus and non-fundus images across multiple resolutions. Both approaches achieved an AUROC of 1.000 and accuracies between 0.999 and 1.000 on internal and external validation sets, showing comparable detection performance. The ML approach, however, exhibited substantially lower end-to-end latency while maintaining equivalent accuracy, indicating greater computational efficiency. These results suggest that for OOD detection tasks of limited visual complexity, lightweight ML approaches can achieve DL-level performance with significantly reduced computational cost, supporting practical real-world deployment.

title: 2kV SiC Full-Bridge Converter Power Stage Design for SST-Based Extreme Fast Chargers
authors: Tohfa Haque, Wensong Yu, Srdjan Lukic
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents a power stage design for a 2 kV SiC full-bridge converter that minimizes parasitic inductance while meeting thermal, isolation, and mechanical constraints. Using cost effective 2 kV SiC discrete devices in a side-by-side and back-to-back configuration optimizes current distribution and reduces switching transients. A dual local decoupling capacitor structure and strategically placed DC-link capacitors suppress voltage overshoot and extend capacitor lifespan. Flux cancellation within interleaved PCB layers and multiple parallel decoupling capacitors enhances electromagnetic compatibility. Advanced thermal management with Aluminum Nitride (AIN), phase-change materials, pressed rubber, and optimized heatsinks ensure better efficiency. A 60 kW Active-Front End (AFE) prototype at 50 A and 1.5 kV validates reduced loop inductance, voltage overshoots less than 17% in a continuous test, and improved performance, making this approach ideal for scalable SST with medium voltage DC (MVDC) extreme fast charging (XFC) stations.

title: Lasagna-U-Net: Revisiting U-Shape Architecture for Histopathology Image Segmentation
authors: Junlai Qiu, Yunzhu Chen, Jiaqi Zhao, Yawen Huang, Fubo Wang, Yuexiang Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Histopathology images serve as the gold standard for clinical diagnosis, making their accurate analysis essential in medical practice. Although U-Net and its variants have been widely applied to histopathology image segmentation, most fail to effectively address the challenge of multi-scale lesion representation. In this paper, we propose Lasagna-U-Net, a novel U-Net variant that integrates convolutional neural network (CNN) and vision Transformer (ViT) within a unified U-shaped architecture. Two key modules-the multi-scale grouped convolution module and the grouped residual Transformer module-jointly enhance the model's capacity for local feature extraction and long-range dependency modeling. Evaluations on the public GlaS and KPIs datasets demonstrate that Lasagna-U-Net achieves state-of-the-art performance, particularly in segmenting lesions of varying sizes (https://github.com/Q4CS/Lasagna-U-Net).

title: Multi-Method Explainability for Similarity Assessment in AI-Denoised PET Imaging
authors: Mélanie Champendal, Théo Coutaudier, Daphné Faist, Maeva Ryser, Josh Schaefferkoetter, Maurizio Conti, Mario Jreige, Adrien Depeursinge, Lluis Borras Ferris, Cláudia Sá dos Reis, John O. Prior, Henning Müller, Ricardo Ribeiro
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Deep learning-based denoising has enabled substantial reductions in radiotracer dose for PET imaging, yet the absence of full-dose ground truth presents challenges in evaluating image fidelity. This study proposes a supervised classification framework to assess the similarity between AI-denoised and standard-dose PET images without relying on ground truth. A total of 872 features, including radiomic descriptors, quantitative metrics, and visual quality assessments, were extracted at patient and lesion levels. Binary classifiers were trained to predict image similarity across six dose levels. Ground truth labels were derived from a structured visual scoring grid co-developed with expert users and validated against objective similarity metrics. Explainability was integrated using global and local SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP), calibrated similarity scores, and textual feature descriptions. The most predictive features included anatomical detail, artefact presence and textural radiomics in the lungs (patient level), and SUV skewness, detection score, and texture strength (lesion level). The combined approach enables interpretable evaluation of AI-denoised PET reconstructions in settings where objective references are lacking, supporting transparency and clinical trust.

title: ST-SAM: A Spatio-Temporal Enhanced Segment Anything Model for Echocardiogram Video Segmentation
authors: Ningshu Li, Minglang Chen, Bin Pu, Jingqiao Li, Xiaomeng Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Echocardiogram video segmentation is crucial for enabling rapid, objective, and reproducible quantification of cardiac structures, which is the key for the diagnosis and treatment of cardiovascular diseases. Although recent SAM-based methods have achieved promising results, they often struggle to effectively capture spatio-temporal features in echocardiogram videos, resulting in limited performance for multi-structure segmentation. To this end, we propose the Spatio-Temporal Segment Anything Model (ST-SAM)—a novel framework comprising a Spatial Topology Module (STM) and a Temporal Graph Enhanced Module (TGEM) that jointly enhance spatial topological relationships and temporal consistency in echocardiogram videos. Experimental results demonstrate that ST-SAM outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods by an average of 3.0% Dice across four-chamber segmentation tasks. The source code can be found at https://github.com/xmed-lab/ST-SAM.

title: Self-adjusting LFER-based Dynamic Gate Driving Technique for Fast-Switching GaN HEMTs in 48-V Applications
authors: Xiangyu Teng, Junting Chen, Yuwei Wu, Ji Shu, Kevin J. Chen, Wei Jia Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This work presents a self-adjusting lateral field-effect rectifier (LFER)-based dynamic gate drive design for 48-V, high-current GaN power stages. Building on our previously reported 400-V GaN LFER-based two-stage driver, the proposed design is adapted for 48-V applications with an additional gate-to-source resistor, which modulates the effective resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">EFF</inf>) of the LFER branch as the gate current changes. Immediately after the turn-on, R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">EFF</inf> remains low and subsequently increases as the branch current grows. This self-regulating behavior allows fast switching transient while suppressing the gate ringing and shortens the post-switching damping interval. These features are critical for MHz range switching. Verified in a double-pulse test configuration, the proposed self-adjusting scheme has exhibited improved reduction in damping charge time by 25% compared to the prior LFER-based driver. The resulting gate overshoot has been lowered by 4.8% without compromising the fast turn-on time of 45 ns. These results indicate a higher switching frequency and lower EMI can be achieved for the 48-V converters at the multi-MHz frequencies.

title: Trust-Aware Intrusion Detection Framework for AI-Augmented Vehicular Digital Twin
authors: Khyati Raiththa, Mohammad Shabaz, Gyanendra Kumar
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: The driving experience has been enhanced through intelligent content processing, adaptive streaming and personalized interactions, as the increasing usage of artificial intelligence (AI) in vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) communication has advanced. Nonetheless, the more intricate intrusions, aggressive manipulation, and data exfiltration can be witnessed using these devices because of the augmented attack surfaces created by this growing intelligence. The traditional intrusion detection systems often lack the dynamism to adapt dynamically to the complex, and volatile threat environment that is linked with the V2V systems augmented with the artificial intelligence. This research paper integrates the adaptive learning methods and the trust evaluation models to introduce a Trust-Aware Intrusion Detection System (TA-IDS) designed for Vehicular Digital Twin (VDT) environments. The proposed system generates dynamic trust scores based on the constant monitoring of user interaction patterns, usage of the application, and communication patterns of devices. These ratings are subsequently used to commence the adequate security actions. TA-IDS helps ensure the violation of fraud is known early without affecting the performance or user experience of the device through a lightweight engine of anomaly detection and a trust management layer. As per the findings, TA-IDS maintains real-time operating efficiency and enhances accuracy of the intrusion detection by 39.0 percent and false positives by 27.0 percent as compared to traditional methods. This paper provides a defense architecture on the AI-enhanced vehicular communication that can be scaled and effectively defended by explaining the importance of incorporating the trust-awareness vehicular digital twin ecosystems today.

title: Stage-Aware Secondary Frequency Regulation for Coordinated Flywheel Energy Storage Arrays and Thermal Power Units
authors: Yubin Liu, Fang Fang, Le Wei, Zijian Li, Tong Tong
abstract: Coordinated secondary frequency regulation by flywheel energy storage arrays (FESAs) and thermal power units (TPUs) mitigates grid frequency oscillations caused by renewable integration. However, existing strategies often overlook stage-specific dynamics and multiobjective tradeoffs. To address this, a stage-aware, multiobjective, bilevel dynamic load allocation strategy is proposed based on grid assessment criteria. At the upper level, fuzzy adaptive control predicts TPU capacity, while field-based traction experiments establish precise physical power constraints for the FESA. An improved nondominated sorting genetic algorithm II (NSGA-II) with a comprehensive satisfaction model and smooth weights is developed to optimize power output across distinct regulation stages. At the lower level, a bidirectional coupling interface enables adaptive group selection, followed by an improved consensus algorithm based on the relative rate of change of state of charge (SOC) for rapid energy and state alignment. Validated with operational data from a 660-MW supercritical plant in Ningxia, China, results demonstrate superior robustness against 0.6 s network delays and 0.5 s unit failures. Compared to representative benchmarks, it increases regulation net profit by 21.83% and reduces energy losses by 19.8%, effectively balancing grid requirements with the operational safety and economic sustainability of storage-integrated plants.

title: VOLE-PDRAA: An Efficient Privacy-Preserving Data Retrieval Protocol with Anonymous Authorization Based on Vector-OLE
authors: Zuodong Wu, Dawei Zhang, Mianxiong Dong, Kaoru Ota
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: The General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) aims to enable the free flow of personal data while enhancing individual control. Integrating privacy-preserving data retrieval methods can provide stronger protection for personal privacy. However, existing approaches lack compliance mechanisms aligned with the GDPR, making it difficult in practice to simultaneously satisfy the principles of lawfulness and data minimization, while also exhibiting clear limitations in both security and efficiency. To address these problems, we propose VOLE-PDRAA, an efficient privacy-preserving data retrieval protocol with anonymous authorization based on the Vector-OLE (VOLE). Specifically, VOLE-PDRAA constructs a VOLE-blinded identifier by integrating pseudorandom linear encoding with VOLE-derived correlation vectors, enabling rigorous anonymity guarantees during authorization. Building on this, the protocol incorporates a non-interactive zero-knowledge proof (NIZK) to achieve anonymous authorization for the data subject and to generate verifiable informed consent proofs, thereby meeting the principle of lawfulness. Meanwhile, the data controller can verify whether each retrieval request falls within the scope authorized by the data subject without learning any identifiable information, thus maintaining adherence to the data-minimization principle in a post-quantum environment. Furthermore, VOLE-PDRAA utilizes labeled private set intersection (labeled-PSI) to safeguard the confidentiality of identifiers and their associated records under post-quantum security conditions, while enabling large-scale batch retrieval. Our protocol takes a comprehensive security analysis within the Universal Composability (UC) framework. Experimental evaluation validates its superiority through comparison with state-of-the-art work.

title: PQ-FRL: A Privacy-Preserving and Quality-Aware Federated Reinforcement Learning for UAV-Assisted Edge Computing
authors: Zifeng Dai, Hui Xie, Shengjun Wei, Changzhen Hu
abstract: Optimizing service performance in UAV-assisted Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) systems often relies on effective cooperative trajectory and resource allocation. Federated Reinforcement Learning (FRL) provides a distributed paradigm to collaboratively learn these policies without sharing raw observations. However, practical deployments face severe coupled challenges: the model quality degradation caused by Differential Privacy (DP) noise injection, heterogeneous environments (Non-IID data), and complex physical constraints. To mitigate these issues, we propose PQ-FRL, a Privacy-Preserving and Quality-Aware FRL framework. Our approach utilizes an Output Perturbation DP mechanism to provide strict per-round protection for uploaded policy updates, representing a pragmatic engineering trade-off between securing immediate operational privacy and maintaining continuous-control DRL convergence. To address the mixed-action space, we incorporate a Straight-Through Estimator (STE) for differentiable offloading decisions, guided by a conditionally shaped reward to prevent suboptimal local equilibria. Furthermore, a novel Quality-Aware (QA) aggregation mechanism dynamically assigns weights based on explicitly DP-protected local reward signals, helping to disentangle noise from inherently poor performance. Simulation results indicate that PQ-FRL can effectively balance realistic non-linear energy and latency constraints. Under the evaluated heterogeneous scenarios and strict privacy budgets, our method demonstrates robust utility preservation and exhibits graceful degradation as physical airspace congestion increases with larger swarm sizes, offering a stable and practical solution for privacy-sensitive UAV edge computing.

title: Tensor Product-Based Fractional Order Sliding Mode Control for Biological Pest Regulation in Agroecosystems
authors: Arsit Boonyaprapasorn, Parinya Sa Ngiamsunthorn, Suwat Kuntanapreeda, Kraisak Phothongkum, Thunyaseth Sethaput
abstract: Sugarcane is a key economic crop in subtropical and tropical regions, serving as a major raw material for the food and biofuel industries. However, its cultivation is severely affected by the sugarcane borer, a destructive pest. To mitigate this issue, biological control strategies—using natural enemies to regulate pest populations—offer an environmentally friendly alternative to chemical pesticides. This study proposes a tensor product–based fractional-order sliding mode control (TP-FOSMC) approach to formulate an effective biological control policy for a nonlinear pest–parasitoid agroecosystem. The controller design utilizes tensor product model transformation (TPMT) to facilitate the synthesis of the fractional-order sliding mode controller, which enhances system robustness and significantly reduces chattering in the control inputs. System stability is verified using the Lyapunov stability theorem. Numerical simulations under bounded disturbances demonstrate that the proposed TP-FOSMC effectively regulates both pest and parasitoid populations to their desired levels. Compared with the state-dependent Riccati equation (SDRE) and tensor product–based conventional sliding mode control (TP-SMC) strategies, the proposed method achieves faster convergence and substantial chattering reduction, confirming its robustness and practical feasibility for sustainable pest management.

title: A Multi-Faceted Performance Evaluation of Metaheuristic Optimization Techniques in Radial and Meshed Distribution Networks
authors: Anu Kuriakose, S. Balamurugan, R. R. Lekshmi
abstract: Directional overcurrent relay (DOCR) coordination, a nonlinear constraint optimization problem, is highly important to the reliability of the power distribution system. Five metaheuristic algorithms namely, Genetic Algorithm (GA), Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), Adaptive Differential Evolution (JADE), Moth-Flame Optimization (MFO) and Walrus Optimization Algorithm (WaOA) are compared on three benchmark networks of increasing complexity namely, a 5-bus radial feeder, a WSCC 9-bus mesh system, and the IEEE 33-bus distribution system. A multi-faceted assessment model is used with five objective functions, three standard IEC time-current characteristic (TCC) curves and two user-defined (UD1 and UD2) formulations. UD1 deploys a single set of non-standard optimized constants across the relays, while UD2 uses independently optimized shape constants for each relay. Each algorithm is compared under an identical computational budget, based on 30 independent runs, and performance measures are reported using descriptive statistics, Friedman and Wilcoxon signed-rank tests, convergence analysis, and verification of the coordination margin. The findings imply topology-related behavior in the algorithm's performance: JADE is observed to be statistically consistent in the radial system, whereas GA is most dominant in the mesh and large-scale systems in terms of total operating time across all topologies. It is also observed that the full range of standard IEC curves with realistic TMS limits are not suitable for 33-bus system. This indicates that user-specified TCC curves are not merely beneficial but a requirement for the coordination of medium-scale distribution systems. The UD2 feature yields the shortest operating time for any system topology, and the optimized coefficients give physically interpretable trends that flatten as the network depth increases. These findings offer statistically confirmed principles for the protection engineer in choosing algorithms, the types of curves they need, and relay hardware to achieve effective coordination in current distribution networks.

title: Toward Energy Efficiency in O-RAN: A Digital Twin for Carbon-Aware 6G Networks
authors: Elif Ak, Berk Canberk, Hyundong Shin, Trung Q. Duong
