abstract: Marine net cages (MNCs) are widely used for offshore aquaculture, where timely monitoring is essential to reduce economic loss and environmental impacts caused by cage damage and stock escape. Although synthetic aperture radar (SAR) can observe MNCs under all-weather conditions, the scattering mechanisms of MNC structures remain insufficiently understood, which limits robust feature interpretation and subsequent monitoring. To address this gap, we propose MNC-ISAR, a physics-driven framework that couples a sea-surface model with simplified cage geometries and electromagnetic settings to compute backscattering distributions and generate ISAR imagery. Specifically, a Pierson-Moskowitz spectrum with Monte Carlo sampling is adopted to construct sea-surface realizations, and an MNC-oriented polar format algorithm (MNC-PFA) is used for ISAR formation under Sentinel-1-like C-band conditions (5.4 GHz). Based on the South China Sea salinity-temperature range, the C-band skin depth is estimated as 4.80-8.41 mm, indicating that scattering is dominated by above-water components. Experiments under different polarizations show that MNCs and escaped fragments exhibit clearer and more salient signatures in cross-polarized ISAR images. The proposed method provides interpretable scattering insights that support the design of reliable SAR-based monitoring for marine net-cage aquaculture.

title: Synergistic Foundation Models for Semi-Supervised Fetal Cardiac Ultrasound Analysis: SAM-Med2D Boundary Refinement and DINOv3 Semantic Enhancement
authors: Tonghao Zhuang, Shanglong Hu, Yongsheng Luo, Zhiqi Zhang, Yu Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: We present a semi-supervised framework for joint segmentation and classification of fetal cardiac ultrasound images. Built upon the EchoCare multi-task backbone, our method integrates SAM-Med2D for boundary refinement and leverages DINOv3 to enhance pseudo-label quality. We introduce view-specific hard masking along with a two-stage optimization strategy: an EMA phase to consolidate segmentation capabilities, followed by a Classification Fine-Tuning phase that freezes segmentation parameters and resets the classification head to recover classification performance without compromising segmentation gains. Evaluated on the FETUS 2026 leaderboard, our method achieves a Dice Similarity Coefficient at 79.99%, Normalized Surface Distance at 61.62%, and F1-score at 41.20%, validating the effectiveness of our approach for prenatal congenital heart disease screening. Source code is publicly available at the following link: https://github.com/2826056177/zcst_fetus2026.

title: Fast Restart Method for Sensorless Vector-Controlled Induction Motors Considering Speed Variation
authors: Hye-Won Kang, Joon-Seok Kim, Joon-Hyoung Ryu, June-Seok Lee
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Induction motors are widely used in industrial applications because of their cost-effectiveness and high reliability. However, when a momentary power interruption occurs, the motor enters a free-running state driven by inertia. Under this condition, improper restarting may cause significant inrush currents, torque shock, and mechanical stress. This paper proposes a fast and stable sensorless restart strategy for induction motors. The proposed method applies three short-circuit current pulses during the free-running state to estimate the time-varying rotor speed and acceleration. Based on the estimated speed and acceleration, a short-circuit voltage–current model of the induction motor is employed to calculate the rotor flux axis position. The proposed strategy requires no additional power consumption, allowing rapid and reliable restart operation. As a result, the restart time is significantly reduced compared to conventional approaches, while stable and robust performance is maintained over a wide rotor speed. The effectiveness of the proposed strategy is verified through simulation results under nonlinear load conditions.

title: Self-Supervised Contrastive Learning for Intelligent Fault Detection in 400kV Super-High Voltage Transmission Systems: A Data-Efficient Deep Learning Approach
authors: Raghda AbduLateef Mezaal, Mohammed Aboud Kadhim, Mohammed Abdulla Abdulsada
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: The application of intelligent fault diagnosis in the extra-high voltage (EHV) transmission system is significantly hampered by the expensive cost and challenging work of manual annotated data collection for training model. Supervised learning methods are typically used in state-of-the-art FDD systems, which need to be trained on a very large set of labeled fault scenarios (typically few thousands) entailing significant cost and expert time. In this paper, we propose a new self-supervised contrastive learning scheme specifically for the 400kV EHV transmission system; it has reached $92.0 \%$ detection accuracy on average with the labeled data that is only required $10 \%$ of the labeled dataset compared with traditional methods. Contributions: The proposed model adopts a two-stage learning mechanism, with the first unsupervised representation learning stage on $4,000 \mathrm{P}~\mathrm{U}$ sample collected from SCADA systems at no cost and the second supervised fine-tuning stage on only 200 labeled samples belonging to 28 types of faults. Via contrastive pre-training, the model acquires generalizable feature representations that encode the underlying structure of normal and faulty EHV system activities in an unsupervised way). Experimental results show that our method performs within ${2 . 5 \%}$ of fully supervised baselines and reduces the amount of labels needed by more than $90 \%$, leading to costs saving of around ${\$}$9,000 per deployment. It can also detect various fault types (such as SLG fault, DLG fault, three-phase fault and LL fault), voltage quality disturbance, equipment failure, environmental influence and system stability. This is the first application of contrastive learning to 400kV EHV systems and provides a practical route for utilities to deploy AI based fault detection with very little data annotation requirement.

title: Gender Bias in Spoken Language Translation : A Review
authors: Jamaluddin, Chandresh Kumar Maurya
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)
abstract: Deep neural network architectures for speech have been found to learn representations that contain information about the speaker, such as the gender. Current speech-to-speech translation technologies often employ a series of steps in cascade, comprising automatic speech recognition, machine translation, and text-to-speech synthesis. Recent studies have discovered that many speech translation systems have a gender bias. This review focuses on research conducted between 2020 and 2025 on gender bias, as speech technology has evolved significantly over the past five years. Our review considers how this bias arises, spreads and continues in systems of speech translation. The sources of bias can be found at various levels of the system, encompassing the stages of data collection, representation, and how the model learns from data, as well as evaluating the performance of the ST system. Gender bias in the datasets is reviewed. Techniques to reduce bias are outlined. While much of the existing research into gender bias has been conducted in European languages, a lot of attention is being paid to languages in which there are fewer resources; languages spoken in India are a focal point of research into gender bias studies. Our review concludes by outlining open research directions and advocating for gender-aware, culturally grounded, and ethically informed approaches to the design and evaluation of future speech translation systems.

title: Active Input Power Control for SONAR Application
authors: Joseph Mathew, Akshat Saini, N. Lakshminarasamma, V N Panchalai
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: Power Amplifier are the key elements in delivering the desired power to the SONAR (Sound, Navigation, and Ranging) transducers. Sensing the load parameters like output voltage and current is increasingly difficult as the inverters and the sensing circuitry are supposed to be inside the ship vessel, while the long tow cables carry the power to the SONAR transducer. For SONAR applications, the range of detection is determined by the total amount of active power supplied to the transducer. Therefore, to get configurable range of detection, there is a need to specifically control the supplied active power. An input power-based control scheme for an inverter designed for a SONAR power amplifier application is proposed in this paper. The effectiveness of the proposed control strategy has been validated through both simulations and real-time implementation. The proposed control scheme has been experimentally verified using an 800 V, 1 kW prototype.

title: VAID: Valve Artifact Inpainting for Normal Pressure Hydrocephalus from a 3D MRI Diffusion Model
authors: Shimeng Wang, Shuwen Wei, Samuel W. Remedios, Jinwei Zhang, Blake E. Dewey, Ari M. Blitz, Mark G. Luciano, Aaron Carass, Jerry L. Prince
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Normal Pressure Hydrocephalus (NPH) is a neurological disorder characterized by significant ventricular enlargement, potentially leading to cognitive impairment with gait and incontinence issues. Shunt implants can be used to drain excess cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) from the ventricles and can be an effective treatment in the correct cohorts. However, the presence of the shunt valve introduces artifacts in magnetic resonance images (MRI), which can bias downstream image processing pipelines. This bias hinders the accurate computation of biomarkers in evaluating the efficacy of shunt treatment. To address this, we propose VAID (Valve Artifact Inpainting with Diffusion models), a valve artifact removal method based on a fine-tuned 3D MRI diffusion model. The probabilistic representation learned by the diffusion model serves to provide realistic inpainting in the valve region of the shunt. Experiments with real pre- and post-shunt surgery MRI data demonstrate that our inpainting approach improves the accuracy of biomarker quantification in downstream neuroimaging pipelines such as SLANT and FreeSurfer.

title: An Explainable Retrieval-Augmented Clinical Decision Support Chatbot for Mental Disorder Assessment Using DeepSeek R1 Distill-Llama-8B
authors: Francis Joseph G. Libres, Hanz Ian B. Silva, Kim Miguel P. Sobrepena, Lysa V. Comia
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: This study presents a retrieval-augmented clinical decision support chatbot designed to assist mental health assessment through knowledge-grounded conversational reasoning. The system integrates document chunking, semantic embeddings, and vector-based retrieval with the DeepSeek-R1 Distill-Llama-8B model to generate diagnostically relevant responses grounded in structured psychiatric knowledge. Diagnostic text was processed into semantic chunks, embedded using the all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model, and indexed in a ChromaDB vector database to enable efficient context retrieval. Evaluation across multiple simulated clinical scenarios demonstrated strong performance, achieving perfect relevance (1.00), high faithfulness (0.95), and mean cosine similarity of 0.76 between generated responses and reference diagnostic descriptions. The retrieval module also achieved robust results with recall of 1.0, precision of 0.79, and an F1-score of 0.88. These findings demonstrate that retrieval-augmented large language models can provide transparent, contextually grounded, and explainable support for conversational mental health decision systems.

title: Square-Wave-Fed Hybrid Symmetrical Cockcroft-Walton Voltage Multiplier for High-Voltage DC Power Supplies
authors: Sara Baldisserri, Riccardo Mandrioli, Lohith Kumar Pittala, Gabriele Neretti, Vincenzo Cirimele, Mattia Ricco, Andrea Cristofolini
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: Voltage multiplier (VM) circuits are widely used in high-voltage DC power supplies. Traditional VMs are typically fed by sinusoidal voltages delivered by a resonant converter coupled with a high-voltage step-up transformer. This paper explores a transformerless solution, achieved by feeding the VM directly with the inverter’s square wave output voltage. Although requiring additional multiplier stages to get the same output voltage level, this configuration simplifies the implementation and allows the elimination of the first smoothing capacitor, making it potentially suitable for size- and weight-sensitive applications. The performance and feasibility of the square-wave-fed hybrid VM are evaluated, and it is demonstrated that lower output voltage ripple and drop and better system dynamic response are achieved with this approach.

title: An Optimization Approach for Sustainable Transformer Design in Photovoltaic Power Plants
authors: Emir Yükselen, Ebrahim Rahimpour
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: Transformers are critical in delivering electrical energy generated from renewable sources to the grid and end users. However, traditional transformer design methods are generally based on continuous maximum load conditions and often result in oversizing and increased costs. The increasing utilization of renewable energy sources has further highlighted these inefficiencies in modern energy systems, where energy derived from renewables is primarily converted into electrical energy for widespread public consumption. This study focuses on the optimization methods used in designing a transformer for photovoltaic power plants with unique loading cycles to optimize transformer parameters, aiming for minimal material costs, a compact size, and improved efficiency. Additionally, an electromagnetic simulation analysis is performed to validate the design approach and ensure the accuracy of the applied methodology. The results highlight the potential of optimized transformer designs to improve operational efficiency and support the integration of renewable energy sources into smart grids.

title: Quantitative Evaluation of Explanation Quality in Fetal Ultrasound Quality Criterion Detection Using Saliency Maps
authors: Anirudh S Varrier, Santiago Alférez, Meritxell Riera-Marin, Jithin Velayudhan, Joan Sabria, Sikha O K
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Reliability and interpretability are crucial for deploying deep learning models in medical imaging, where decisions directly influence patient care. In fetal ultrasound, accurate detection and understanding of biometric quality criterions are vital for diagnostic quality. Although saliency-based methods like Grad-CAM provide visual explanations, their clinical use is limited by the absence of quantitative measures of explanation quality. This work presents a systematic framework for evaluating and validating explanations in fetal ultrasound quality criterion detection. A DenseNet-121 classifier identifies key quality quality criterions, while Grad-CAM and its variants generate saliency maps. We introduce a hybrid quality score that combines region-reduction analysis, perturbation-based robustness, and graph-based superpixel similarity with reference annotations to assign reliable quantitative scores to explanations. A decision logic module then integrates classification confidence and explanation quality to determine prediction reliability. Experiments on a fetal ultrasound dataset demonstrate that the proposed scoring mechanism effectively distinguishes clinically meaningful from spurious explanations, enabling automated quality control and reducing the need for repeat scans. This framework advances quantitative XAI in medical imaging, promoting safer and more trustworthy AI systems whose explanations are both reliable and clinically interpretable.

title: An Underwater Sonar Target Tracking Method Based on Improved YOLOv10
authors: Haoyu Zhao, Gang Wang, Ying Zhang, Caicui Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Vision, Advanced Imaging and Computer Technology (VAICT)
abstract: To address the identity switching problem in conventional object tracking caused by high appearance similarity among targets with distinct trajectories, this study optimizes the computation of appearance feature similarity. Within an underwater sonar target tracking framework, a lightweight YOLOv10 network integrated with a PSA attention mechanism module serves as the detector, while an enhanced DeepSonarSort algorithm functions as the tracker. Evaluated on an underwater sonar dataset and benchmarked against the original DeepSort method, the proposed approach validates the efficacy of DeepSonarSort in handling nonlinear motion dynamics. The DeepSonarSort pipeline mainly consists of three key parts: IoU matching, cascade matching, and state update and prediction based on the Unscented Kalman Filter. In terms of detection, due to the performance limitations of YOLOv8 in underwater scenarios, mainly caused by the NMS processing of redundant bounding boxes, an improved detector, YOLOv10s-BB, is introduced. This model adds the BiFormer Convolutional Attention Module into the backbone and the BiFormer attention module into the neck to enhance its ability to extract and fuse features. For tracking, DeepSonarSort adopts a nonlinear matching strategy to make it more robust against complex underwater motion patterns. The experimental results show that YOLOv10s-BB can achieve a mean average.

title: CALM-VLM: Calibration and Selective Prediction in Vision-Language Models for Reliable Brain MRI Classification
authors: Nikhil J. Dhinagar, Chirag Jagad, Pavithra Senthilkumar, Sophia I. Thomopoulos, Mahir H. Khan, Sook-Lei Liew, Paul M. Thompson
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Recent advances in vision-language models (VLMs) have demonstrated strong multimodal reasoning capabilities for medical image understanding. However, their confidence in predictions is often unclear, limiting adoption in clinical settings. We introduce CALM-VLM, which integrates confidence calibration and selective prediction into a generative 3D VLM. To create CALM-VLM, we fine-tuned the Med3DVLM architecture for Alzheimer's disease (AD) and stroke classification, as initial test cases. To improve reliability, we incorporated temperature scaling on the VLM's generative outputs. The calibrated model then selectively abstains from predictions when uncertain; this also improved its diagnostic accuracy. Experiments across multi-site MRI datasets show that CALM-VLM improved confidence relative to uncalibrated VLMs. Coverageadjusted test ROC-AUC increased by 5 % to 13 % for both tasks across independent test sets. The calibrated VLM achieved a test ROC-AUC of 0.951 for AD classification and 0.905 for Stroke classification. These findings highlight the importance of calibrated, uncertainty-aware VLMs for trustworthy neuroimaging AI.

title: AI Vision–Guided Autonomous Rescue Rover with LiDAR Safety Navigation
authors: Mohammed Harith Baqer Abdul Halim, Roshakimah Mohd Isa, Nur Hanani Ahmad Puaad, Nor Isdarwisha Ismail Mohammed, Wan Nurshazwani Wan Zakaria, Mohamad Dzulhelmy Amari
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: Search-and-rescue operations are generally conducted in places that are hazardous and have little visibility or are highly hazardous for human rescuers to go in at once. Existing rescue robots still lack three essential capabilities which are real-time visibility, intelligent tracking, and responsive obstacle awareness This project aims to overcome these limitations with the help of a remote-controlled rescue rover which will be fitted with an AI-based vision system using ReCamera along with a LiDAR sensor that will be used for distance-based navigation. The ReCamera module allows for detection and tracking of a human in real-time, while the LiDAR sensor installed on the rover provides distance feedback continuously, thus allowing for the safe navigation of the rover through obstacles. A dual-controller architecture that combines a Raspberry Pi 4B and an ESP32 controls the highlevel processing and Robot Operating System (ROS) communication, while handling low level motor control, thereby guaranteeing smooth rover operations. The initial tests showed that the AI camera could effectively detect persons, keep auto-centering, and support autonomous person following behaviour, while the LiDAR was responsive to the changes in the environment for collision prevention. The system achieved 30 fps real-time detection with 86% confidence and 0.22 m safety response. The proposed system demonstrates a practical low-cost solution for intelligent robotic assistance in disaster response environments.

title: Federated Few-Shot Segmentation for Rare Brain Tumours via Weighted Aggregation and Prototype-Guided Adaptation
authors: Shenghui Yan, Sidong Liu, Maurice Pagnucco, Yang Song
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Brain tumour segmentation is a fundamental task for neurooncological diagnosis and treatment planning. However, the scarcity of annotated data for rarer tumour types such as metastases limits the applicability of deep learning models. Federated learning (FL) enables multi-institutional collaboration without sharing patient data, yet existing approaches struggle with data imbalance, domain heterogeneity, and fewshot generalisation to new centers. We propose a federated framework designed for rare brain tumour segmentation that introduces three key modules: (1) client-specific weighted aggregation to improve fairness and stability under heterogeneous data quality, (2) a cross-attention decoder interaction to enable positive knowledge transfer between tumour types, and (3) a prototype-based decoder initialisation (ProtoInit++) for efficient few-shot adaptation to new centers. Experiments on seven multi-center datasets demonstrate that our method consistently outperforms FedAvg, FedPer, and FedRep, achieving higher Dice scores and lower Hausdorff distances. Leave-one-center-out evaluations further confirm superior generalisation performance to unseen hospitals.

title: IoT-Enabled Text-to-Image Generation: A Review of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Approaches
authors: Subuhi Kashif Ansari, Nusrat Hamid Shah, Mohammad Ashfaq Hussain, Roma Fayaz, Ahmad Unnisa Begum, Durdana Taranum Khan
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: Internet of Things (IoT) and text-to-image generation with the help of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) have made it very easy to generate contextually suitable and personalized visual content. In this paper, we investigate the combination of the IoT data streams with AI architectures (e.g., GANs and transformer-based models) for text-to-image generation. Incorporation of real-time contextual and environmental information input by IOT devices and such models, the models can generate images of better quality, contextuality and personalization. The technical difficulties like computational overhead and latency are analyzed in section III, and some mitigation could be achieved by leveraging edge computing. Issues of privacy and security that emerge out of use of sensitive IoT data are handled as well. We further demonstrate the potential applications of IoT-driven text-to-image generation in various sectors, including smart homes, healthcare, and urban computing. We here aim to enhance user experiences and improve decision-making. Lastly, we outline future research directions. Here we emphasize on the creation of innovative AI models that consider IoT domain context-awareness and data integration/fusion techniques, along with ethical and societal considerations. We conclude by discussing the significance of ethical innovation and interdisciplinary collaboration in the development, implementation, and regulation of IoT-based text-to-image generation.

title: A Validated 4D Clinical-Computational Taxonomy and Translation Framework for GAN-Based MRI Super-Resolution
authors: Satvik V. Khara, Monali Suthar, Prayas Chavda, Nisarg Chauhan, Anurag Soliya, Vraj Thakkar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) super resolution (SR) with deep learning techniques has recently been explored as a promising solution to improve spatial resolution without prolonging acquisition time, increasing hardware complexity or imposing additional burdens on patients. Among learning-based solutions, Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) have been shown to possess remarkable potential in the task of restoring high-resolution anatomical details and texture realism, although the current state of research is highly disorganized in terms of architectures, modalities, training strategies and applications. In support of the United Nations’ Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 3 - Good Health and WellBeing, which strongly promotes the availability of high-quality healthcare technologies for all, this paper conducts a systematic and reproducible literature review of GAN-based MRI superresolution techniques and proposes a validated Integrated FourDimensional Clinical-Computational Taxonomy (4D-CCT). A literature selection procedure is followed to ensure methodological stringency and then taxonomy mapping and meta-analysis of the trends of PSNR, SSIM and perceptual assessments are done. To enhance comparability and traceability, a benchmarking and reproducibility checklist for GAN-based MRI SR studies is suggested. To facilitate responsible and scalable clinical translation in accordance with SDG-driven innovation in healthcare, a five-phase clinical translation framework is developed, which includes multicenter validation, reader studies, workflow integration and regulatory readiness. The proposed taxonomy and evaluation framework facilitate method positioning, comparison and development in accordance with the clinical context, offering a reference model for advancing trustworthy and deploymentready GAN-based MRI super-resolution systems.

title: Assessing the Influence of Tractography Methods on Detected White Matter Microstructure in Alzheimers Disease
authors: Yuhan Shuai, Yixue Feng, Julio E. Villalon-Reina, Talia M. Nir, Sophia I. Thomopoulos, Paul M. Thompson, Bramsh Q. Chandio
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Tractometry enables detailed mapping of white matter microstructure along individual tracts and is widely used to study disease effects such as those seen in Alzheimer's disease (AD). However, how different tractography algorithms influence tractometry outcomes remains unclear. Here, we compared whole-brain deterministic and probabilistic tractography using the BUndle ANalytics (BUAN) framework in the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) dataset, including 118 AD and 728 cognitively normal (CN) participants. Both approaches revealed the expected pattern of lower fractional anisotropy (FA) and higher mean, radial, and axial diffusivity (MD, RD, AxD) in AD, consistent with white matter degeneration. Despite broadly similar global trends, substantial bundle-level differences emerged between the two tractography methods. Probabilistic tracking produced stronger and more spatially extended effects in the fornix, a small and highly curved limbic pathway vulnerable to AD-related degeneration, whereas deterministic tracking showed greater sensitivity in the posterior segments of the right superior longitudinal fasciculus (SLF_R). These discrepancies highlight that the choice of tractography algorithm can alter detecting disease effects, emphasizing the need for cross-method validation to ensure the robustness and interpretability of along-tract measures.

title: An Intelligent Blockchain Integrated Deep Learning Framework for Detection and Predictive Analysis of Early Neurodegenerative Disorder Diagnosis
authors: M.R. Kousalya, D. Paulraj, Kavitha. B
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: The early diagnosis of neurodegenerative diseases is essential in enhancing patient health but has been limited by the complications in neurological patterns and maintaining medical data management. This paper proposed Intelligent Blockchain-Integrated Deep Learning Framework to detect and predict neurodegenerative diseases in their early stages. It is a multimodal framework, which gives the benefit of convolutional neural network (CNN) combined with Bidirectional to extract the multimodal biomedical data. Privacy and interoperability issues are resolved by incorporating a blockchain-based decentralized architecture, which would guarantee reliable data exchange, integrity among healthcare organizations. Moreover, an Explainable AI (XAI) mechanism which doctors can use for personalized treatment and preventive care. Experiments with benchmark datasets indicate that the proposed system has high accuracy has low false detection rates, and better model transparency. The study builds a strong base of a smart, secure healthcare ecosystem which can support early diagnosis, data integrity and predictive healthcare innovation.

title: VAICT 2026 Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Vision, Advanced Imaging and Computer Technology (VAICT)

title: DC Modeling of 8 kV Depletion Mode Gallium Oxide MOSFET
authors: Abu Shahir Md Khalid Hasan, Mohammad Dehan Rahman, Md Majharul Islam, Md Maksudul Hossain, H. Alan Mantooth
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Gallium oxide (Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>) has recently gained significant attention for high-voltage power electronics owing to its ultra-wide bandgap, high critical electric field (~8 MV/cm), and the potential for cost-effective single-crystal wafer production. Among its polymorphs, β-Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> is the most stable and has enabled the demonstration of depletion-mode lateral MOSFETs with breakdown voltages up to 8 kV. Building on our recent work on a 750 V β-Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> MOSFET, this paper extends a physics-based compact modeling approach to higher-voltage devices. The model formulation combines channel current with drift resistance, where drift mobility is extracted from Hall mobility accounting for four dominant scattering mechanisms and their temperature dependence. Simulation results show good agreement with measured transfer and output characteristics, validating the model’s scalability across voltages. This compact model provides a physics-informed framework for incorporating Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> devices into power electronic circuit simulation and design.

title: Fault-Tolerant Transient Sensorless Control of Induction Motors Using Extended Kalman Filter
authors: Yacine Boudria, Raymond B. Sepe, Steven P. Bastien
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Intelligent Design and Control of Automation and Drive Systems (IDCD)
abstract: Transient sensorless control of induction motors is an enabling technology for high-performance electromechanical systems requiring reliability, reduced hardware complexity, and robustness. By eliminating speed and position sensors, sensorless control decreases system cost and weight while improving robustness and suitability for harsh environments. This paper presents a control-oriented modeling and sensorless control framework for a rotary induction motor emulating the dynamics of a linear electromagnetic launch system. Traditional sensorless induction motor control methods are not suitable for the rapid transients and high leakage inductance seen with linear induction motors in linear launching applications. Although implemented on a rotary platform, the control architecture and velocity profiles replicate linear motor operation, including rapid acceleration to a commanded launch speed followed by controlled deceleration to rest. These operating conditions introduce significant variations in effective inertia and load torque, imposing stringent requirements on torque regulation and transient stability. A dynamic motor–drive model is developed and integrated with a sensorless state estimation scheme that reconstructs rotor speed and position using electrical measurements only. Robustness is evaluated under parameter variations, and arbitrary switching between sensor and sensorless modes. Simulation results demonstrate accurate velocity, and position tracking in a highly transient and pulsed power mode, confirming the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed approach for high power-density, fast-transient electric drive applications.

title: Data Augmentation and Hyperparameter Optimization for Indonesian Sign Language Gloss Translation Using IndoBART
authors: Nisma Fadillah Abdul Rahman Onge, Tirana Noor Fatyanosa, Irawati Nurmala Sari
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: Communication barriers faced by the deaf community in Indonesia significantly impact their access to healthcare, education, and employment. While Indonesian Sign Language (BISINDO) serves as the primary communication method, the lack of automated translation systems limits broader accessibility. This paper presents an optimization approach for IndoBART, a pre-trained transformer model specifically designed for Indonesian language tasks. IndoBART was selected due to its proven effectiveness in Indonesian NLP tasks and its bidirectional encoder-autoregressive decoder architecture, which is well-suited for sequence-to-sequence translation. Previous research has explored hyperparameter optimization for text-togloss translation and achieved strong results using mBART on other sign languages, but limited work exists on Indonesianspecific pretrained models for BISINDO. Although augmentation techniques have shown promise in low-resource settings, their systematic evaluation for Indonesian sign language remains underexplored. To address this gap, back translation data augmentation at several proportions $(30 \%, 50 \%, 80 \%$, and $100 \%)$ is evaluated in combination with Optuna-based hyperparameter tuning. The fine-tuning process involves training IndoBART’s encoder-decoder architecture on text-gloss pairs, in which the encoder processes Indonesian text through bidirectional attention layers and the decoder autoregressively generates BISINDO gloss sequences. The combined use of $100 \%$ back translation augmentation and optimized hyperparameters yields the best performance, representing substantial improvements over the baseline. These findings establish a foundation for advancing accessible communication technology for the Indonesian deaf community.

title: Benchmark of H&E-to-Mif Virtual Staining in Digital Pathology
authors: Guillaume Balezo, Etienne Decencière, Albert Pla Planas, Thomas Walter
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Multiplex Immunofluorescence (mIF) provides detailed information on the abundance and spatial localization of clinically relevant proteins in tissue, yet its acquisition is costly and not widely accessible. Hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) staining, the pathological gold standard, is inexpensive and ubiquitous but lacks molecular specificity. Recent deep learning models can predict mIF signals from H&E, enabling in silico virtual staining, but standardized, publicly reproducible benchmarks are lacking, making it difficult to compare published methods. We propose a multi-level benchmark for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{H} \& \mathrm{E} \rightarrow$</tex> mIF prediction using public datasets. Representative models are trained on a shared dataset and evaluated across colorectal cancer cohorts. Performance is assessed at the pixel level for mIF reconstruction, at the cell level for cell-type classification, and with respect to cross-dataset generalization and computational cost. Our protocol provides a unified, reproducible framework to compare virtual-staining models in terms of both quantitative accuracy and biological relevance. Code and pretrained models are available on GitHub<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>1https://github.com/Sanofi-Public/MIPHEI-ViT.

title: Modeling and Optimization of MHz Self-resonant Planar Coil for Wireless Power Transfer
authors: Ghovindo Siadari, Jungwon Choi
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents a modeling and optimization framework for a high-frequency, self-resonant planar coil used in wireless power transfer (WPT) systems to maximize the coil’s quality factor (Q) and achieve high efficiency. While self-resonant planar coils inherently provide internal capacitance and enable compact, high-power operation, achieving high Q at MHz frequencies is challenging due to magnetic and conductor losses. To overcome this limitation, we develop a gradient-based optimization method using an equivalent circuit derived from closed-form expressions of inductance, capacitance, and series resistance based on an Archimedean spiral geometry. The optimized coil is driven by a class Φ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> inverter, chosen for its single-switch architecture, which simplifies control and reduces device stress compared to conventional inverter topologies. A 24 cm-diameter copper-sheet prototype tested at 13.56 MHz achieved a measured Q > 700 and delivered 640 W over a 13 cm transfer distance with 95% coil-to-coil efficiency, validating the proposed design methodology for high-power MHz WPT applications.

title: Integrated IMU-EMG Wearable System for Real-Time Gait Analysis and an Audio Feedback System
authors: M. Ponnibala, N. Divija, C.K. Gokulanand, R. Dharani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: Gait analysis is an instrumental technique for the assessment of human walking patterns, carrying important information relative to neuromuscular and musculoskeletal functions. Conventional techniques for gait analysis are based on optical motion capture systems, force plates, and videobased methods. Although these techniques provide accurate results, they are normally highly expensive, not easily portable, and confined to the laboratory. This work proposes an integrated real-time gait monitoring system using Inertial Measurement Units and Electromyography sensors. IMU sensors can measure all kinetic parameters of gait, including acceleration, angular velocity, and orientation, while EMG sensors detect muscle activation patterns throughout a gait cycle. This will continuously detect and analyse gait parameters in real time by comparing them with standard reference values for identifying abnormalities or deviations. Once processed, the results would be instantly conveyed through audio or an alert feedback system so as to enable immediate awareness and corrective measures. Thus, the system can provide a cost-effective, portable, efficient solution for continuous gait monitoring, rehabilitation, and performance assessment for clinical and personal health applications.

title: Vessel Motion State Recognition and Trajectory Calculation Based on Radar and AIS Fusion
authors: Yuhang Zhang, Qianqian Chen, Huafeng Kong
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: With the rapid development of China's economy, the volume of inland waterway freight is increasing and so is the number of ships. Radar and AIS, as the two most commonly used devices in the field of inland waterway maritime supervision, can provide important information for the relevant personnel to maintain the order of water traffic and ensure the safety of ship movement. In this context, a method is proposed to identify and calculate the ship motion status and trajectory based on radar and AIS. Firstly, the radar data is processed by time sliding window to establish the parameter indexes describing the position characteristics, speed characteristics and heading characteristics of the ship, which can be used to quickly distinguish the ships with different movement patterns; then different interpolation calculation models are selected to align the two types of tracks in time by the ship movement status reflected by the track points, and the position characteristics, speed characteristics and heading characteristics reflected by the two types of tracks are used to make coarse correlation judgments. Based on the generalised absolute grey correlation method, an improved correlation index system is proposed to measure the similarity of the ship motion characteristics reflected by the two types of trajectories, thus realising the correlation of trajectories; finally, the ship motion state is determined based on the two types of trajectory data within the same time period, and the corresponding Kalman filter equation is established to process the two types of trajectories, and the fusion weights are weighted according to the quality of the trajectories. The ship motion trajectory is calculated. The experimental results show that the proposed method can achieve correct correlation judgement in a dense environment, and the fused trajectories can correctly reflect the ship's motion characteristics, and the fused results are closer to the real trajectories.

title: Elastic Synergy: A Hybrid Price–Incentive Framework for Peak-Shaving and Utility Profit Optimization in Residential Demand Response
authors: Rahul Goswami, Ranjit Roy, Kamal Krishna Mandal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid demand response (DR) framework that integrates price-based and incentive-based mechanisms to manage residential electricity loads. The approach utilizes dynamic pricing via a price-elasticity matrix and time varying monetary incentives via incentive elasticities to balance peak demand reduction and utility profit maximization simultaneously. Three DR schemes are formulated—an incentive based model, a real time price based model, and a hybrid model—each governed by distinct demand reduction rules. The hybrid model combines both elasticity types, computing demand reduction as a weighted sum of price and incentive-driven responses. Numerical simulations involving 10 residential users over a 24-hour period, using realistic consumption profiles and calibrated parameters, demonstrate that the hybrid strategy consistently outperforms single-mode approaches. Notably, the hybrid model achieves deeper peak shaving, smoother load redistribution, and more than 200% higher utility profit compared to the incentive driven DR scheme and 90% higher utility profit compared to the price driven DR scheme. The results indicate that jointly leveraging price signals and targeted incentives provides a more effective and economically beneficial DR solution.

title: Arc Fault Localization in Low-Voltage Distribution Systems Using Transformer-Aided DOA Estimation
authors: Ratnakar Nutenki, Haraprasad Badajena, Aurobinda Routray, Ashok Kumar Pradhan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: The accurate localization of arc faults in low-voltage distribution systems is critical for the early detection and mitigation of electrical hazards. This study adapts direction-of-arrival (DOA) estimation techniques for arc-induced electromagnetic emissions using sensor arrays. While traditional subspace methods (MUSIC, Root-MUSIC, and ESPRIT) struggle with the non-stationary, broadband nature of arc discharges, we propose TransMUSIC, a transformer-based framework for robust DOA estimation under noisy conditions. TransMUSIC replaces eigendecomposition with a transformer encoder that learns the noise subspace from temporal snapshots, followed by a neural DOA estimator for arc localization from the MUSIC spectrum. Experimental results on synthetic arc discharge data, including colored noise and switching transients, show TransMUSIC significantly outperforms classical approaches in localization accuracy and low SNR robustness. The framework provides a scalable foundation for real-time arc fault localization in smart distribution systems, with future implementation targeting laboratory and field measurements.

title: Non-linear Discrete Time Modeling of a 1 MHz Dual Active Bridge Converter
authors: Naveed Ishraq, Writtik Dutta, Ayan Mallik, Saikat Dey, Changkyu Bai
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: For dual active bridge (DAB) converters operating at multi-MHz switching frequencies, particularly under light-load conditions, conventional reduced-order and averaging approaches often lose accuracy since they neglect dead-time effects, which occupy a significant portion of the switching cycle. The state-of-the-art discrete-time models incorporate the ZVS interval only for the primary-side tank voltage when characterizing the resonant dynamics. However, these modeling approaches are limited to basic single phase-shift (SPS) DAB operation and consider only the primary leakage inductance in the power-flow formulation, neglecting the influence of FET body capacitances and their role during dead-time transitions. To address this limitation, a discrete-time small-signal model of the T-network DAB is proposed, capturing the influence of zero-voltage switching (ZVS) intervals on converter dynamics as well as the impact of multi-phase-shift modulation strategies. This nonlinear, time-invariant state-space formulation serves as a robust foundation for controller design, facilitating accurate realization of the desired bandwidth and transient response without the need for repeated design iterations. Finally, the proposed model is validated on a 160 V to 120 V, 1 kW 1 MHz DAB hardware proof-of-concept which resulted in only 1.22dB error in the loop gain magnitude of the output voltage frequency response.

title: Federated Learning for Secure and Privacy-Preserving Malware Detection in IoT Networks
authors: A. Peter Soosai Anandraj, K.Bhaskar Datta, R.Chetan Sai Chowdary
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: IoT botnets, device-targeted malware, polymorphic malware, and adversarial attacks intended to elude conventional detection systems are just a few of the sophisticated cyberthreats that have surfaced as a result of the Internet of Things' (IoT) networks' explosive growth. Due to their reliance on centralized datasets and static detection rules, traditional intrusion detection systems (IDS) are unable to keep up, which raises privacy and performance issues. In order to enable multiple clients to cooperatively train deep learning models for malware detection without sharing raw device data, this study suggests an IDS framework for IoT networks based on federated learning. The framework offers flexibility for a variety of IoT environments by using neural network classifiers for supervised scenarios and autoencoders for unsupervised detection of anomalous device behavior. The dataset N-BaIOT is experimentally evaluated and suggests that the proposed federated approach gives a high detection accuracy and robustness while maintaining the privacy and the centralized models. These highlight the effectiveness of federated learning's as a safe, effective and preserves the privacy; which suites for real time malware detection in various IOT networks

title: Downscaling MODIS Land Surface Temperature Over Semi-Arid Wheat Fields Using Multi-Scenario Optical and SAR Indicators
authors: Zaineb Bouswir, Salah Er-Raki, Abdelhakim Amazirh, Saïd Khabba, Jamal Ezzahar, Ablila Youness, Abdelghani Chehbouni
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: In semi-arid agricultural systems in particular, land surface temperature (LST) is a crucial variable for evaluating crop water stress and analyzing surface energy exchanges. The 1 km spatial resolution of MODIS LST products (Moderate Resolution Imaging Spectroradiometer) is still too coarse for field-scale applications, despite their excellent temporal resolution (daily collections). In order to downscale MODIS LST from 1 km to 30 m, this work assesses a linear thermal disaggregation strategy that integrates optical and radar indications from Sentinel-2 (NDVI, surface albedo) and Sentinel-1 (VV and VH backscatter coefficients). Regression associations were developed at the MODIS scale and then converted to finer resolution by taking advantage of sub-pixel variability in the chosen predictors in seven disaggregation situations. During the 2017–2018 crop season, the technique was applied on a winter wheat field in the Haouz Plain (central Morocco) that was deficit-irrigated. The temporal dynamics and spatial variations of surface temperature are satisfactorily reproduced when validated against ground-based infrared observations. The combination of NDVI and VV polarization produced the best results (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.77; RMSE = 4.52°C), demonstrating the complementarity of optical and radar data: VV polarization is sensitive to surface roughness and soil moisture, whereas NDVI characterizes vegetation cover and its control on energy fluxes. Overall, the findings validate the promise of multi-sensor techniques for high-resolution LST mapping in semi-arid irrigated agroecosystems by confirming that disaggregation success is more dependent on the physical complementarity of input variables than on model complexity.

title: SLAM-AGS: Slide-Label Aware Multi-Task Pretraining Using Adaptive Gradient Surgery in Computational Cytology
authors: Marco Acerbis, Swarnadip Chatterjee, Christophe Avenel, Joakim Lindblad
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Computational cytology faces two major challenges: i) instance-level labels are unreliable and prohibitively costly to obtain, ii) witness rates are extremely low. We propose SLAM-AGS, a Slide-Label-Aware Multitask pretraining framework that jointly optimizes (i) a weakly supervised similarity objective on slide-negative patches and (ii) a selfsupervised contrastive objective on slide-positive patches, yielding stronger performance on downstream tasks. To stabilize learning, we apply Adaptive Gradient Surgery to tackle conflicting task gradients and prevent model collapse. We integrate the pretrained encoder into an attention-based Multiple Instance Learning aggregator for bag-level prediction and attention-guided retrieval of the most abnormal instances in a bag. On a publicly available bone-marrow cytology dataset, with simulated witness rates from 10 % down to 0.5%, SLAM-AGS improves bag-level F1-Score and Top 400 positive cell retrieval over other pretraining methods, with the largest gains at low witness rates, showing that resolving gradient interference enables stable pretraining and better performance on downstream tasks. To facilitate reproducibility, we share our complete implementation and evaluation framework as open source: https://github.com/Ace95/SLAM-AGS.

title: DINOv2-Enhanced Dual-View Unimatch for Joint Carotid Plaque Segmentation and Classification
authors: Yue Qi, Lei Mou, Zheng Li, Xin Han
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Carotid ultrasound analysis is significantly hindered by speckle noise, operator dependency, and the complexity of integrating multi-view information. Existing methods, often constrained by single-view perspectives and fully supervised paradigms, struggle to generalize across diverse clinical settings. To address these challenges, we propose DINOv2-Enhanced Dual-View UniMatch, a novel framework for joint carotid plaque segmentation and classification. Central to our approach is the utilization of the powerful DINOv2 foundation model as a shared backbone to extract robust, view-invariant features from complementary longitudinal and transverse scans, processed via independent DPT decoders. We introduce a decoupled two-stage training strategy integrated with the UniMatch protocol: (1) a semi-supervised stage to establish robust spatial priors using unlabeled data, followed by (2) a task-expansion fine-tuning stage that introduces a dedicated classification head for plaque vulnerability assessment, thereby mitigating gradient conflicts between dense and global tasks. Furthermore, an Exponential Moving Average (EMA) mechanism stabilizes teacher weights to ensure high-quality pseudo-label generation. Extensive experiments demonstrate the robustness of our approach, achieving a Seg-score of 62.59 and an F1-Score of 72.09 on the unseen test set. The code is publicly available on Github at https://github.com/Daheilou/CSV_Deeper.

title: Time-Aware Neural Operator for Liver Radiofrequency Ablation Planning
authors: Alexander Joseph Fezovich, Iman Yi Liao, Ean Hin Ooi
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Radiofrequency ablation (RFA) benefits from fast, accurate predictors of thermal dose, yet high-fidelity FEM solvers remain too slow for routine treatment planning. We present a time-aware, hybrid 3D Fourier Neural Operator (FNO) surrogate for liver RFA that performs one-shot prediction at arbitrary times. The model combines an FNO trunk with explicit time conditioning and a factorised spatial modulator that generates per-parameter scale/shift fields from temporal and biophysical (electrical conductivity, thermal conductivity and perfusion) inputs, enabling structured, physics-guided modulation of the operator. On a controlled 3D FEM benchmark (5-fold CV), the model achieves endpoint performance of MSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.074 \pm 0.033{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}^{2}$</tex>, RMSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.220 \pm 0.040{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>, and Dice at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50^{\circ} \mathrm{C} 0.968 \pm 0.004$</tex>, with 9.2 ms per-case inference. Relative to an RFA-specific 3D U-Net baseline, this represents <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 98 {\%}$</tex> lower MSE, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 89 {\%}$</tex> lower RMSE, and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 80 {\%}$</tex> reduction in Dice error. While a proof of concept on simulated data, the operator-based, time-conditioned design offers accurate, millisecond-scale predictions and a path toward fast, patient-specific RFA planning.

title: Potential of Ultraviolet Laser Diodes in Future Photonic Integrated Circuits
authors: Omar Alkhazragi
abstract: To fully unlock the potential of the applications of ultraviolet (UV) light in integrated systems, semiconductor laser diodes are needed. Here, we review the recent research efforts dedicated to the development of UV laser diodes and their potential use in emerging applications.

title: Isolated, Multi-Resonant Flying-Capacitor-Based Soft-Switching Flyback Converter With a Fractional-Turn Planar Transformer
authors: Kumar Joy Nag, Aleksandar Prodić
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: An isolated, multi-resonant, flying-capacitor-based flyback converter topology, and a complementary mixed-signal controller, suitable for extreme step-down and low-power applications (ranging from fraction of a watt to tens of watts) are introduced. The solution demonstrates high step-down conversion ratio, exceeding 150x, and operating condition independent soft-switching of all components, on all transition edges (thus achieving high efficiency). These features are achieved by utilizing two resonant circuits and operating a flyback transformer in an unconventional manner. The first resonant circuit comprises of a small primary side flying capacitor, that is fully charged/discharged, and the second resonant circuit has a resonant series capacitor. Both are being connected to the transformer inductances through a specific control scheme. The solution also allows for the use of a smaller transformer by operating at high switching frequency, owing to soft-switching. The effectiveness of the introduced converter is experimentally verified with a custom-built 200-400V to 3.3V/5V/6V, 100 W, discrete prototype, achieving peak power processing efficiency of 95.5%.

title: Deep-Learning-Based Scattering-Enhanced Dynamic Microwave-Induced Thermoacoustic Tomography Applying Single Measurement_supp1-3678045.mp4
authors: Xiong Wang
abstract: The rapid advancement of microwave-induced thermoacoustic tomography (MITAT) technology has shown significant potential in many biomedical application disciplines. However, conventional MITAT modalities struggle to achieve an optimal balance among quantity of measurement data, sampling speed, and system cost. This renders it difficult to apply MITAT to dynamic imaging, which is a highly active frontier in biomedical imaging. This work proposes a novel MITAT modality leveraging artificially introduced wave scattering and deep learning (DL) to perform dynamic imaging based on only one measurement. We name this technique as DL-based scattering-enhanced MITAT, that is, DL-SE-MITAT. The scattering induced by many scatterers can efficiently increase the amount of received information of the sample being imaged and improve image quality. The DL technique can facilitate the imaging and enhance image quality applying scattered signals using only one measurement. We perform proof-of-concept validation of the DL-SE-MITAT method for dynamic imaging by investigating saline solution flowing in tube-shaped blood vessel phantoms. Experimental results, including both images and videos, clearly demonstrate the efficacy of the DL-SE-MITAT method in faithfully reconstructing the time-varying sample at a frame rate of 1 frames/s. It is also found that deploying more scatterers can notably enhance the quality of images. This work presents a new avenue for dynamic imaging with single measurement and contributes to the potential applications of MITAT in various dynamic imaging fields as well as other microwave-related imaging methods.

title: Observer-Based Detection and Mitigation of False Data Injection Attacks in Smart Grids Using Discrete Event Systems
authors: Meriam Ghajdami, Mohammed Msaaf, Rafik Channa
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: Smart grids rely on extensive sensing and communication infrastructures to support real-time monitoring and control. While this cyber–physical integration enhances efficiency and reliability, it also introduces new vulnerabilities to cyberattacks. False data injection (FDI) attacks are especially difficult because they manipulate sensor measurements while remaining consistent with normal system behavior. In this paper, we model FDI attacks using Discrete Event Systems (DES) and finite automata. We demonstrate that FDI attacks can be understood as unobservable events that corrupt observations, resulting in observer belief deviation and incorrect control actions. This deviation may progressively drive the system toward cascading failures and large-scale blackouts. To overcome this issue, we provide an observer-based mitigation model that ensures unsafe states are never reached silently. The proposed approach provides formal guarantees on both safety and resilience against stealthy cyberattacks.

title: Online Reinforcement Learning Torque Controller for Electrical Motor Drives
authors: William K. Moreira, Bruno Lahm, Paulo H. A. S. e Silva, Rodrigo P. Vieira, Cassiano Rech
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel torque controller for electrical motor drives based on a Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) reinforcement learning (RL) algorithm, able to track the torque references while aiming to minimize the norm of the stator currents. The method is based on the design of the reward function by establishing a balance between torque tracking and current minimization. The algorithm is implemented in a digital signal processor (DSP) and tested in a synchronous reluctance machine (SynRM) simulated in a hardware in-the-loop (HIL) setup. The results show that the algorithm is able to correctly track the torque reference for different points of operation, without relying on any offline data, look-up table (LUTs) or edge-computing.

title: Masthead
authors: N/A

title: AI-Integrated Multi-Modal Model for Climate Hazard Assessment and Adaptation Planning
authors: Rajkumar Kuppuswami, Yogiraj Awati, Amit Dutt, Sumit Pundir, Pawan Kumar, Ambati Lakshmi Narayana Reddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: In this paper, a new AI-based multi-modal model aimed at providing a higher level of climate hazard assessment and adaptation planning is introduced. The proposed framework uses the power of deep learning to optimally process the heterogeneous amounts of data, such as satellite images, sensor networks, and historical climatic data, to make integrated hazard prediction. The model integrates convolutional and recurrent networks as a hybrid neural architecture to detect spatial-temporal patterns to improve detection of changing risks including floods and droughts. Multi-modal data fusion is a guaranteed robust feature representation which enhances model generalizability across different geographic areas. Multi-source experimental validation can prove that there is a great enhancement in the accuracy and timeliness of hazard predictions when compared with the earlier used uni-modal methods. It has an adaptation planning module, which is part of the structure, that converts predictive outputs into actionable recommendations through resource constraints and vulnerability index analysis. This study highlights how multi-modal AI systems can be used to overcome the most important issues in climate resilience, which can help on a large scale to support evidenced-based policy development and disaster risk management efforts.

title: ZVS Analysis of 3L-ANPC Based GaN DAB Converter with Non-Unity Voltage Conversion Ratio
authors: Yiju Wang, Xin Gao, JiangBiao He
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Dual active bridge (DAB) converters typically lose zero-voltage switching (ZVS) when operating at non-unity voltage conversion ratio. This paper analyzes the ZVS characteristics of a GaN-based 2-level to 3-level (2L–3L) step-up DAB converter in which the high-voltage side adopts a 3-level active neutral point clamped (ANPC) half-bridge structure to enable an 800 V DC output using 650 V gallium nitride (GaN) power devices. Non-ideal switch models are used to derive explicit resonant transitions at two critical switching instants, yielding analytical dead-time conditions and current boundaries for achieving ZVS across all switches within the ANPC bridge, including cases where only partial ZVS is attainable. The derived conditions identify the critical current boundary that limits light-load ZVS. Experimental results from a 200-to-450 V prototype operating at 100 kHz validate the ZVS analysis and confirm the predicted partial ZVS behavior.

title: A Controllable Emotional Intensity Data Augmentation Model for Mongolian Speech
authors: Lengduribu Da, Siyuan Guo, Kuibo Wang, Yixiong Qin
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: When augmenting Mongolian speech with emotional features, the emotional characteristics are derived from predefined emotional labels or fixed emotional speech samples. This approach lacks variations in emotional intensity, resulting in a singularity of emotional features in the augmented speech. To address this issue, this paper proposes a Mongolian speech data augmentation model with controllable emotional intensity, which is based on the StarGANv2-VC architecture and incorporates an Emotional Intensity Control Module (EICM). The EICM generates VAD values with specified emotional intensity through an intensity mapper. The emotional rendering unit then generates frame-level acoustic features based on these VAD values. The prosody prediction unit integrates the frame-level acoustic features with emotional intensity and the content features of the speech, and predicts prosodic variations according to the VAD values, thereby generating emotional intensity features to control the emotional intensity of the augmented speech. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model is capable of augmenting speech data with different emotional intensities and achieves high speech quality. The model outperforms in five evaluation metrics: FFE (F0 Frame Error), MCD (Mel Cepstral Distortion), DDUR (Differences of Duration), EECS (Emotional Embedding Cosine Similarity), and EIMD (Emotional Intensity Matching Degree), with respective values of 12.75%, 19.61, 0.37, 0.79, and 80%.

title: Context-Awareness for Dependable Low-Power IoT
authors: David E. Ruiz-Guirola, Prasoon Raghuwanshi, Gabriel M. de Jesus, Mateen Ashraf, Onel L. A. Lopez
abstract: Dependability is the ability to consistently deliver trusted and uninterrupted service in the face of operational uncertainties. Ensuring dependable operation in large-scale, energy-constrained Internet of Things (IoT) deployments is as crucial as challenging, and calls for context-aware protocols, where context refers to situational or state information. In this article, we identify four critical context dimensions for IoT networks, namely energy status, information freshness, task relevance, and physical/medium conditions, and show how each one underpins core dependability attributes. Building on these insights, we propose a twostep protocol-design framework that incorporates operation-specific context fields. Through three representative use cases, we demonstrate how context awareness can enhance system dependability by leveraging context-awareness.

title: Design of High-Frequency, High-Voltage Planar Transformer for Driving Dielectric Barrier Discharge Loads
authors: Saket Kapse, Hyeongmeen Baik, Jinia Roy
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Dielectric barrier discharge (DBD) systems require high-frequency, high-voltage (HF–HV) excitation to sustain stable plasma generation, typically necessitating step-up transformers capable of delivering several kV. Planar transformers (PT), in particular, offer advantages such as compact form factor and designs with controlled parasitics; however, their application in HV environments remains underexplored. This work presents the design and implementation of a HF-HV PT specifically designed for DBD power supplies. The transformer operates in the few-hundred-kHz range, supports 1.6 kW of power transfer, delivers a bipolar 2-kV output, and is insulated to withstand up to 5 kV of stress. Key design considerations including core material selection, HV winding layout, insulation coordination, and mitigation of parasitic capacitances and leakage inductance are systematically addressed. Both simulation and experimental results validate the proposed HF–HV planar transformer design and demonstrate its suitability for driving DBD loads.

title: Virtual Oscillator based Dual Loop Control for Grid Forming Modular Multilevel Converter
authors: Hitesh Malviya, Sahil Gaurav, Chandan Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: Modular multilevel converter (MMC) is widely explored for offshore wind farms and large-scale photovoltaic (PV) plants for high voltage (HV) and medium voltage (MV) power processing. The MMC comprises of sub-modules (SM) connected in series and can provide higher voltage levels. The dc-ac power conversion through MMC is done through grid forming (GFM) control to regulate voltage magnitude and frequency. In the GFM mode of operation, power converters are susceptible to low-order voltage and frequency oscillations and instability. This paper presents an Andronov-Hopf oscillator (AHO) based virtual oscillator (VO) control for MMC with dual voltage and current control to address such issues. The modulation scheme includes rule-based SM voltage balancing to maintain SM capacitor voltages. The simulation results show the stable operation of the MMC through load variations.

title: A Secure and Transparent Multi-Agent Framework for Automated Financial Decision Systems
authors: Uthayan K.R, Shanthanu G, Santhosh V
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Traditional credit decision systems rely on opaque machine learning models that lack transparency, auditability, and secure coordination between system components. This paper proposes a secure multi-agent artificial intelligence pipeline for automated credit decisioning using a centralized orchestration layer. The architecture integrates specialized agents for digital identity verification, fraud risk assessment using interpretable fuzzy risk scoring, credit risk prediction, regulatory policy retrieval, and tamper-proof audit logging.To ensure secure and trustworthy operation, the system incorporates zero-trust communication between agents, role-based access control, authenticated message exchange, and cryptographically verifiable audit trails. The proposed pipeline enables transparent, auditable, and regulator-compliant automated credit decisioning while ensuring secure handling of sensitive financial data.This work demonstrates how secure multi-agent orchestration can enable trustworthy, explainable, and secure artificial intelligence deployment in financial web applications.

title: A Portable Gamified Platform for Programming Education via a Dual-Role Puzzle Solving Framework: Conceptual Design and Architecture
authors: Wai Chong Chia, Richard Teck Ken Wong, Riady Siswoyo Jo, Nor Akmar binti Mohd Yahya
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: Programming skills have become essential in the modern digital era, influencing almost every industry that relies on computing. While gamification offers an effective strategy for programming education, existing platforms primarily focus on learning through solving pre-loaded puzzles. This paper presents the conceptual design and architecture of a novel dual-role peer-to-peer learning pedagogy supported by an interactive, portable educational gaming platform. The proposed system, comprising a custom hardware device developed based on Raspberry Pi 5 and accompanying Python-based software, allows students to alternate between the roles of passive puzzle solvers, active puzzle creators, and peer mentors. Grounded in constructivist learning theory, this mechanism enables students to both design and solve algorithmic puzzles through code implementation and provides opportunities for creators to serve as peer mentors. The integration of this pedagogy and gaming platform aims to support fundamental to moderately advanced programming concepts while cultivating a collaborative peer-to-peer learning environment.

title: Inverter leg with vertical interconnection and integrated shielding: opportunity for vertical WBG power components
authors: Mahery Rajemisaona, Pierre-Olivier Jeannin, Vincent Bley, Mickael Petit, Fabien Ndagijimana
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This article details the conception of an inverter leg with vertical interconnection and integrated shielding based on silicon MOSFET. Shields with constant voltage bias are used to shield semiconductors in order to minimize values of common mode capacitor. It allows to reduce common mode coupling. Shielded and unshielded inverter legs are compared and a reduction of the common mode current was observed up to -10dB with a shielded packaging. Printed circuit boards (PCBs) are used as dielectric substrates and electrical conductors. A discussion on the relevance of this structure for vertical WBG power components is presented.

title: From Global Radiomics to Parametric Maps: A Unified Workflow Fusing Radiomics and Deep Learning for PDAC Detection
authors: Zengtian Deng, Yimeng He, Yu Shi, Lixia Wang, Touseef Ahmad Qureshi, Xiuzhen Huang, Debiao Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Radiomics and deep learning both offer powerful tools for quantitative medical imaging, but most existing fusion approaches only leverage global radiomic features and overlook the complementary value of spatially resolved radiomic parametric maps. We propose a unified framework that first selects discriminative radiomic features and then injects them into a radiomics-enhanced nnUNet at both the global and voxel levels for pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) detection. On the PANORAMA dataset, our method achieved AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.96$</tex> and AP <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.84$</tex> in cross-validation. On an external in-house cohort, it achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{AUC}=0.95$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{AP}=0.78$</tex>, outperforming the baseline nnUNet; it also ranked second in the PANORAMA Grand Challenge. This demonstrates that handcrafted radiomics, when injected at both global and voxel levels, provide complementary signals to deep learning models for PDAC detection. Our code can be found at https://github.com/briandzt/dl-pdac-radiomics-global-n-paramaps.

title: BrainOrg-QC: Automated Morphological Quality Control of Cerebral Organoids
authors: Nuno Brás, Héloïse Castiglione, Jessica Rontard, Pierre-Antoine Vigneron, Anatole Chessel
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Cerebral organoids are three-dimensional (3D) self-organized cellular structures that recapitulate key aspects of human brain development. They have become promising models in neuroscience research, particularly for modeling neurological diseases and evaluating neurotoxicity. However, they exhibit substantial heterogeneity, notably in morphology and size, which hampers their reproducibility and scalability. Critically, current organoid characterization methods lack standardization, limiting their broader applicability. Here, we present an automated quality control (QC) framework for 60-day cortical organoids that integrates non-invasive imagebased assessment of morphology and growth. Our deep learning method combines organoid segmentation for quantitative growth profiling with a ConvNeXt-based multi-head ordinal regression (CORAL) network that predicts QC scores for three key morphological parameters: density, border integrity, and cyst formation. Overall, our approach enables fast, objective, and reproducible QC, supporting standardized and scalable organoid characterization.

title: Protostage-NET: A Clinically Guided Foundation Model With Prototypical Learning for Automated Amd Grading From Oct Images
authors: Mohamed Elsharkawy, Moumen El-Melegy, Asem Ali, Ali Mahmoud, Mohammed Ghazal, Ashraf Khalil, Wei Wang, Ayman El-Baz
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Age-related macular degeneration (AMD) is a major cause of vision loss, where accurate staging is essential for treatment. However, most recent approaches for OCT images remain limited to binary classification and do not capture the full clinical spectrum of the disease. To address this gap, we propose ProtoStage-Net, an explainable foundation model framework for fine-grained AMD staging. Our method leverages the DINOv2 vision foundation model as a backbone, fully fine-tuned for OCT data, and introduces two clinically inspired attention mechanisms: the Clinically Guided Channel Attention (CGCA) module, which emphasizes diseaserelevant feature channels, and the Lesion Localization Module (LLM), which highlights pathological retinal regions. Classification is performed through a hierarchical prototypical learning strategy at binary, ternary, and detailed six-class levels, providing transparent similarity-based decisions. In addition, we incorporate temporal ordinal constraints to enforce clinically valid AMD progression pathways. We evaluate ProtoStage-Net on a cohort of 954 OCT B-scans acquired from three different imaging devices. Our framework achieves a macro F1-score of 93.49 %, surpassing the baseline foundation model by more than 2 % and outperforming recent state-of-the-art methods. Extensive experiments further demonstrate the clinical explainability of our approach, with saliency maps confirming that ProtoStage-Net accurately localizes lesion regions across different AMD grades.

title: TM-UNet: Token-Memory Enhanced Sequential Modeling for Efficient Medical Image Segmentation
