authors: Yugal Kishore Maheshwari, Danyal Maheshwari, Mousa Karimi
abstract: This study introduces a novel approach for the edge detection task. We propose edge detection based on a biologically inspired neural network, also known as spiking neural network (SNN) using Leaky Integrate and Fire (LIF) neuron model. SNN is an emerging paradigm in computing attempts to mimic the biological functions of the human brain. The main objective of this research is to detect qualitative, clear, and bold edges of given input image using computational efficient SNN algorithm. To realize our approach, the initial stage involves selecting a random image and converted into specific size and into gray scale. Next, receptive fields are used to extract the vertical and horizontal edges and then spiking model is used to transform these edges (data) into spikes and integrate the edges at the output neuron, at last stage an edge detected input image is reformed from trains of spikes at output neuron. A python environment from scratch is used to accomplish this project task. A 5*5 rectangular receptive field is used along with 120 Hz rate encoding frequency to achieve the resultant edges. The obtained images are compared with the Sobel edge detection method as proof of concept of our proposed method and quality of resultant images.

title: An Improved YOLOv8n-Based Algorithm for Detecting Surface Welding Parts of Small Balancing Beams of Quay Crane
authors: Ruiqi Su, Chuancun Wang, Long Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: To address the problem of mechanical arm spray guns colliding with surface welding parts during the spraying operation of small balance beams for gantry cranes, this paper proposes an improved model, YOLOv8n-CAW, based on YOLOv8n. Firstly, a dual-channel CBAM mechanism is embedded to enhance the perception of welding part features; secondly, the ASFF module is introduced to optimize multi-scale feature fusion through adaptive weighting, eliminating feature inconsistencies; finally, the WIoU loss function is adopted to balance the sample weights, improving the model's generalization ability and robustness. Through multiple rounds of experiments, the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{m A P} \boldsymbol{@} 0.5$</tex> value of YOLOv8n-CAW on the self-built dataset increased from 88.2% to 91.3%, an increase of 3.1 percentage points, meeting the safety requirements of real-time and high-precision detection of welding parts by the mechanical arm spray gun.

title: Agriculture Guru: An AI-Based Multilingual Advisory System for Crop Recommendation and Yield Prediction
authors: Siva Shankar P, Nivetha S, Vasantharaj K
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Unpredictable weather patterns and declining soil health increasingly threaten global food security. Traditional cultivation practices often fail to adapt to rapid environmental change. The AGRICULTURE GURU system is a localized advisory platform designed to assist small-scale farmers by ranking crop suitability and forecasting yields based on site-specific conditions. The system bridges the disconnect between the basic environmental telemetry and field operations decisions using an organized workflow of precision agriculture. The data fusion approach is the strategy used in the system architecture to generate farm-level profile by combining real-time soil telemetry, meteorological predictions, and past farm records. The central variables of input are soil Nitrogen (N), Phosphorus (P), Potassium (K) and pH, which are processed using a Random Forest classifier. The ensemble-based model is robust to environmental noise and sensitive to the nutrient patterns of growth. Multi-class assessment is conducted so as to determine the best crop choices in different stress conditions. The validation of the experiment was carried out on 1,800 physical soil samples that were taken in arid, temperate and humid regions of the tropical areas. The results demonstrate a recommendation accuracy of 93.6%. The analysis indicates low regression error in yield prediction and the system supports multilingual integration with 22 languages of the region in support of practical deployment is facilitated. The findings confirm the effectiveness of ensemble learning to scalable agricultural decision support in the presence of climatic variable conditions.

title: MiXR-Intent: Intention Prediction for Immersive Remote Encountered-Type Haptics
authors: N. B. Takele, D. Delehelle, Y. Kim, S. Anastasi, N. Deshpande, J. Ortiz, D. G. Caldwell, C. Recchiuto, Y. T. Tefera
abstract: This paper introduces MiXR-Intent, a deep learning-based system for intention estimation in immersive telepresence with haptic feedback. MiXR-Intent uses gaze direction, upper body posture, and hand movement patterns to accurately predict user intentions and contact points within virtual reality (VR) environments. By interpreting these cues, the system aims to minimize interaction noise and latency—challenges that are especially critical when haptic feedback is employed for remote telepresence and teleoperation applications. In this paper, we propose a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network as a method for predicting user intentions and contact points during interaction. Our approach focuses on using three interaction types: pushing, pointing, and grasping, which could be used as a fundamental interaction types for enabling natural and intuitive user experiences. To improve robustness and generalization in all directions and positions, each interaction type data is collected across six distinct directional movements, to make sure diverse trajectory variations relative to the user’s body. This data variability allows the proposed LSTM neural network to capture user interaction under different spatial positions. The model is trained on a dataset of 6120 samples collected from 20 subjects, using 17 contact points and capturing gaze and upper body movements across six distinct directions. We evaluated the proposed system using various parameters of the LSTM architecture and conducted a performance analysis across multiple metrics, achieving up to 95% accuracy in classification.

title: Design of a Delta-Sigma Modulator for Biomedical Signal Acquisition Applications
authors: Jingtong Lin
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: A high-precision, energy-efficient Delta-Sigma modulator is presented for biomedical signal acquisition. The design employs a third-order CIFF architecture with 4-bit quantization, achieving high-order noise shaping while maintaining system stability. An innovative summing technique based on a two-stage floating inverter amplifier (FIA) is introduced to minimize power consumption, while a switched-capacitor charge-accumulation comparator is utilized to reduce mismatch errors. Implemented in SMIC 180 nm CMOS technology, the modulator achieves an SNDR of 104.49 dB over an 8 kHz bandwidth with a 1.8 V supply. With a total analog power consumption of only 600 μW and a Figure of Merit (FOM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">SNDR</inf>) of 176.2 dB, the design demonstrates competitive efficiency and precision suitable for biopotential acquisition systems.

title: Parallel Route Coherent Mixup Deep Learning for Single Source to Multi-Domain Generalized Prostate Cancer Segmentation for Mr Guided Adaptive Radiotherapy
authors: Josiah Simeth, Sudharsan Madhavan, Andreas Wibmer, Daniel Gorovets, Sean McBride, Himanshu Nagar, Victoria Brennan, Neelam Tyagi, Harini Veeraraghavan
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) guided adaptive radiotherapy for prostate cancer (PCa) allows daily treatment adaptation to the geometry of tumor and organs to deliver precise treatments while sparing organs from radiation. However, manual delineation of tumors and organs limits the adoption of this technology. Deep learning (DL) methods, though promising for in-distribution (ID) data that resembles training, deteriorate in performance when applied to out-of-distribution (OOD) datasets. Hence, we developed a novel parallel-route coherent feature mixup (PaRC-mix) training approach for single source to multi-domain generalization. PaRC-mix performs feature augmentations at multiple network layers through matching linear combinations of features extracted across training batches. PaRC-mix training was implemented in UNet++ to segment PCa from apparent diffusion coefficient images and compared to other mixup approaches. Models were trained on 2,029 samples from 3.0T GE MRI and tested on 557 PCa samples from 3 datasets used for diagnosis, treatment planning, and during radiation scans acquired using 3T Siemens, 3T Philips, and 1.5T Elekta Unity MR-Linac scanners from public and institutional datasets. PaRC-mix training led to significantly more accurate tumor detection (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{p}<$</tex> 0.001) and segmentation (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{p}<0.001$</tex>) compared to other methods.

title: Automated Midbrain-To-Pons Ratio Analysis for Differential Parkinsonism Diagnosis: Comparing U-net and Densenet Approaches
authors: Vipul Gohil, Manoj Sankhe, Mitusha Verma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: This paper tackles the problem of differential diagnosis in parkinsonian disorders by creating an optimized deep learning framework for automated midbrain to pons ratio measurement from MRI scans. While this ratio is a clinically validated biomarker, current manual measurement methods have inter-rater variability and are resource intensive. We test U-Net for segmentation and DenseNet for feature extraction on 43 PD and 40 controls from 2 datasets. Our results show U-Net is 96.77 % accurate and DenseNet is 95 % precise, better than previous methods and reducing measurement variability. This work solves the problems of small dataset, anatomical subtlety and clinical workflow integration.

title: Design and Evaluation of a Modern License-Managed Learning Platform: A Full-Stack Web Development Case Study
authors: Abdelali El Gourari, Yassine Ed-Daadoua, Oumaima El Kahlaoui, Mustapha Ezzini, Mustapha Raoufi, Mohammed Skouri
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: The digital transformation of education necessitates robust, scalable platforms to manage specialized content, such as licensed professional courses. This project details the design and deployment of a full-stack web application addressing the need for structured license and course management. The platform features a dual-role architecture serving end-users and administrators managing content and license lifecycles. The backend utilizes a Laravel-based RESTful API, while the frontend employs React.js with modern hooks for dynamic state management. Deployed via cloud services (Railway and Vercel), the system demonstrates a practical pathway from development to production. This case study illustrates the application of contemporary frameworks, API-centric design, and secure role-based access control. Key outcomes include validated strategies for integrating distinct technologies into a cohesive product and maintaining security within an educational context. The platform confirms the efficacy of the chosen stack for building maintainable, efficient, and user-centric educational tools.

title: Digital Outcome Measures for Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy: Machine Learning on Wearable Gait Signals Compared with Classical Classifiers and Deep Sequence Models for Good Health
authors: Saravanan. M. S, Siva Rama Krishna Prasad
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Digital outcome measures for Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD) were derived by applying machine learning to gait signals collected from waist-mounted smartphone accelerometers during walking and running in children with Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD) Digital outcome measures were obtained by using machine learning on gait signals filtered by waist mounted smartphone accelerometers during walking and running in patients with DMD and typical developing controls, and obtained temporospatial features (step length, step time, cadence, step velocity, and mediolateral power) to characterize DMD unique locomotor patterns. An example SVM classifier using the above example metrics gave a 94.3% accuracy, serving sensitivity of 93.8 and specificity of 94.7; a random forest model with 95.8 accuracy, 95.1 sensitivity and 96.2 specificity; a k nearest neighbors classifier with 92.5 accuracy, 91.9 sensitivity and 93.0 specificity; while deep sequence models, such as an LSTM network, obtained 99.6 accuracy, 99.2 sensitivity and 99.8 specific These accurate performance metrics ensure that both classical and deep learning models are valid to measure DMD specific signatures, which include reduced step length, and increased mediolateral power that may be likened to a Trendelenburg like gait and that deep sequence models are specifically useful in prescreening, monitoring and tracking progression of DMD in a real world environment with high accuracy and reliability, and outperform standard laboratory gait measurements and clinical scales, like the North Star Ambulatory Assessment (NSAA) when assessing DMD.

title: Adaptive Gradient Domain Normalization for One-Sided Unsupervised Medical Image Synthesis
authors: K. E. Giraldo Paniagua, P.-H. Conze, V. Jaouen, E. Angelini
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Unsupervised image-to-image translation plays a crucial role in medical imaging, enabling data harmonization and crossmodality synthesis without paired data. Existing approaches, including CycleGAN, UNIT, and CUT, often fail to preserve fine anatomical details, limiting their applicability for clinical use. In this work, we develop a novel one-sided unsupervised image-to-image translation method - Adaptive Normalized Gradient Field (AdaNGF) - which enforces structural consistency throughout the translation process. We adapt the normalization of the gradient field of the generated image to match the target gradient magnitude distribution, encouraging the generator towards anatomically-sound outputs with appropriate preservation or suppression of local gradient alignment. We evaluate our approach on intra-modality (BraTS: T1 → T2) and cross-modality (Gold Atlas: MRI → CT) datasets. Quantitative and qualitative results show that our method outperforms state-of-the-art unsupervised translation frameworks in terms of SSIM, PSNR, and structural similarity metrics. Code will be publicly shared on GitHub upon acceptance.

title:
Method for Determining Switching Timing of Gate Drive Current of Active Gate Driver to Maximize Turn-off Loss Reduction Under Fixed V
                    <sub>DS</sub>
                    Overshoot Condition in SiC MOSFETs
authors: Yohei Sukita, Toshiaki Inuma, Michihiro Ide, Kenichi Morokuma, Yukihiko Wada, Yuta Yamaoka, Yasushige Mukunoki, Makoto Takamiya
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: In the active gate drive, which switches the gate current setting (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</inf> setting) three times when turning off an SiC MOSFET, a method for determining the switching timing of I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</inf> setting is proposed that maximizes the reduction rate of switching loss under V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DS</inf> overshoot voltage aligned conditions, compared to conventional gate drive that does not change the gate drive current. Double pulse test measurements of SiC MOSFET are performed at 600 V and under four combinations of conditions including load current of 300 A and 500 A, and temperature of 25 °C and 125 °C, using proposed method. The proposed switching loss reduction rates under V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DS</inf> overshoot voltage-aligned conditions are −33% to −38%. These values are close to the true maximum reduction rates of −48% to −60%, thus confirming the validity of the proposed method.

title: Novel Concept of Universal Onboard Charger Applicable for DC and AC Grids
authors: Oleksandr Husev, Mohammad Mehdi Kashani, Gerald Deboy, Parham Mohseni, Matthias Kasper, Jacek Rabkowski
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This work presents a concept of a universal, bidirectional onboard charging structure for electric vehicles, designed for the ability to be connected to any type of residential ac and dc grids with minimized redundancy. Offering true plug-and-play flexibility for global charging infrastructure, the electric vehicle equipped with a universal onboard charger can be charged from single-phase and three-phase ac sources, as well as two-wire and three-wire dc supplies. The bidirectional power flow capability enables both vehicle-to-grid (V2G) and grid-to-vehicle (G2V) operation. Several possible solutions are proposed and compared. The hardware setup rated up to 11 kW was designed and experimentally validated, demonstrating all claimed features.

title: Detection of Cyber Attacks in Networked Electric Drives via Physics-Guided Contrastive Learning with Few-Shot Labels
authors: He Yang, Jin Ye
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper proposes a physics-informed few-shot anomaly detection framework for intelligent motor drives by integrating contrastive learning into a Convolutional Neural Network(CNN)-based architecture. The framework leverages physics-informed parameters—specifically, second-harmonic components of stator current spectra—to guide the embedding space learned through Contrastive Learning(CL). This enhances the model’s ability to discriminate between normal and anomalous states, particularly under low-data conditions.All training cases assume a few-shot label setting, where only a limited number of labeled attack samples are available. Transfer Learning(TL) is first applied to transfer generalizable knowledge from simulation data to real-world domains, and CL is then fine-tuned with a physics-weighted loss to emphasize subtle anomalies. Experimental results show that, even with only a few labeled samples, the proposed method achieves up to 99.8% accuracy and reduces the false alarm rate by nearly 95% compared with CNN and other baseline models.The proposed method provides a practical and interpretable solution for cyber-attack detection in intelligent motor drives with minimal labeled data.

title: Multimodal Distillation-Driven Ensemble Learning for Long-Tailed Histopathology Whole Slide Images Analysis
authors: Xitong Ling, Xiaoxiao Li, Jiawen Li, Mingxi Fu, Yuxuan Chen, Jing Peng, Minxi Ouyang, Yizhi Wang, Yonghong He, Xiaoping Liu, Lianghui Zhu, Tian Guan
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) plays a significant role in computational pathology, enabling weakly supervised analysis of Whole Slide Image (WSI) datasets. The field of WSI analysis faces a severe long-tailed distribution problem, where some classes have sparse samples while others are abundant, making it difficult for classifiers to accurately identify minority class samples. To address this issue, we propose an ensemble learning method based on MIL, which employs expert decoders with shared aggregators and consistency constraints to learn diverse distributions and reduce the impact of class imbalance on classifier performance. Moreover, we introduce a multimodal distillation framework that leverages text encoders pre-trained on pathology-text pairs to distill knowledge and guide the MIL aggregator in capturing stronger semantic features relevant to class information. To ensure flexibility, we use learnable prompts to guide the distillation process, avoiding limitations imposed by specific prompts. Our method, MDE-MIL, integrates multiple expert branches focusing on specific data distributions to address long-tailed issues. Consistency control ensures generalization across classes. Multimodal distillation enhances feature extraction. Experiments on Camelyon+-LT and PANDA-LT datasets show it outperforms state-of-the-art methods.

title: An AI-Powered Adaptive Intrusion Detection System for Smart Healthcare Networks
authors: Lavish Kansal, Sanjeev Kukreti, Swati, K. Sushma, Sriharsha Makineni, Srinivasa Rao Bittla
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: The IoT-enabled medical devices, cloud interoperability, and continuous patient monitoring systems are leading to an increase in sophisticated cyberattacks on smart healthcare networks. The paper suggests an Adaptive Intrusion Detection System (A-AIDS) that is AI-powered and customized to a heterogeneous smart healthcare setting. This system takes advantage of Self-Evolving Graph Neural Network (SE-GNN) and Temporal Neuro-Fuzzy Risk Evaluator (TNF-RE) to learn both device behaviors dynamically and determine whether communication patterns are anomalous, as well as the potential threat severity in real time. The suggested architecture is based on Federated Continual Learning (FCL) to support safe model regeneration across distributed hospital units without disclosing sensitive medical information as opposed to traditional IDS models. Moreover, a Lightweight Quantum-Resistant Operable Encoder (L - QRFE) improves the resistance towards upcoming cryptographic attacks and resource-restricted IoMT nodes. Investigations on the multi-modal renovation of healthcare traffic flows illustrate enhanced standing of detection, accelerated zero-day assault recognition, and weighty least overhead that can support the delay-unpleasant climate in the healthcare environments. The suggested A-AIDS model provides a more scalable, privacy-aware, and privacy-neutral, future-oriented security system to smart healthcare networks of the next generation.

title: Keyword Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)

title: Graphtrack: Automated Longitudinal Lesion Tracking in Ct Via Local-Global Reasoning With Contrastive and Graph Learning
authors: A. Vergara-Richart, C. Martín Vicario, G. Urbanos García, A. Alberich-Bayarri, A. Jiménez-Pastor
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Accurate lesion tracking in longitudinal CT scans is essential for streamlining treatment response and disease progression evaluation in oncology, but current clinical workflows remain largely manual and variable. We propose GraphTrack, a two-stage deep learning framework that unifies local and global reasoning for automated lesion correspondence. First, a self-supervised 3D Masked Autoencoder (3DMAE) pretrains transferable lesion representations, fine-tuned within a 3D Siamese Neural Network (3DSNN) via contrastive learning for pairwise similarity estimation. Then, these features are integrated into an Edge Graph Attention Network (EdgeGAT) with similarity-based curriculum learning to model spatial, temporal, and morphological dependencies. On a large, multicentric, thoraco-abdominal CT dataset, the method achieves 88.9/80.5/97.8% precision/recall/AUC-ROC, and 90.2/77.7/89.6% on DeepLesion, demonstrating strong generalization and reliable, scalable lesion tracking.

title: Vision Transformer for Breast Cancer Diagnosis: Layer-Wise Analyses Beyond Attention Mechanism
authors: Kengo Takahashi, Yuwen Zeng, Kei Ichiji, Zhang Zhang, Haoyang Liu, Ryusei Inamori, Takuma Usuzaki, Noriyasu Homma
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Early detection of breast cancer is essential for improving survival, yet diagnostic accuracy remains limited in dense breasts where tumors overlap with complex glandular structures. Considering that breast density itself is an independent risk factor, an artificial intelligence (AI) framework that captures both local lesions and global anatomical context is required. Vision Transformer (ViT) has emerged as a promising model for representing longrange dependencies, and its potential has also been investigated in breast cancer diagnosis. Recently, the effectiveness of shape-guided attention mechanisms has been suggested; however, it remains unclear how the guidance influences the representation flow beyond the attention mechanism. In this study, we analyzed ViT models for breast cancer diagnosis using quantitative frameworks that track information propagation, including Norm-based Analysis, Contextualization Change, and Logit Lens. Our model with shape guidance achieved an area under the receiver-operating characteristic curve of 0.883, sensitivity of 0.923, and specificity of 0.704. In addition, it was quantitatively confirmed that the guidance affected not only the attention mechanism but also subsequent components.

title: ADALORA-QAT: Adaptive Low Rank and Quantization Aware Segmentation
authors: Prantik Deb, Srimanth Dhondy, N. Ramakrishna, Anu Kapoor, Raju S. Bapi, Tapabrata Chakraborti
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Chest X-ray (CXR) segmentation is an important step in computer-aided diagnosis, yet deploying large foundation models in clinical settings remains challenging due to computational constraints. We propose AdaLoRA-QAT, a two-stage fine-tuning framework that combines adaptive low-rank encoder adaptation with full quantization-aware training. Adaptive rank allocation improves parameter efficiency, while selective mixed-precision INT8 quantization preserves structural fidelity crucial for clinical reliability. Evaluated across large-scale CXR datasets, AdaLoRA-QAT achieves 95.6% Dice, matching full-precision SAM decoder fine-tuning while reducing trainable parameters by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16.6 \times$</tex> and yielding <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.24 \times$</tex> model compression. A Wilcoxon signed-rank test confirms that quantization does not significantly degrade segmentation accuracy. These results demonstrate that AdaLoRA-QAT effectively balances accuracy, efficiency, and structural trustworthiness, enabling compact and deployable foundation models for medical image segmentation. Code and pretrained models are available at: https://prantik-pdeb.gi thub.io/adaloraqat.github.io/

title: An Approach to Estimate Battery State-of-Charge Under Dynamic AI Training Workloads
authors: Ali Alenezi, Prasad Enjeti
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents an enhanced equivalent circuit model (ECM) identification approach that addresses the dynamic power profiles of artificial intelligence (AI) training workloads. In AI data centers, clusters of graphics processing units (GPUs) generate large, rapidly fluctuating load currents. Lithium-ion batteries are sometimes employed to serve as critical buffers to minimize the load fluctuations on the grid side for power quality and backup. In such cases reliable operation demands accurate capture of battery dynamics under operating conditions to estimate the state of charge (SOC) accurately. The proposed method employs electrochemical impedance spectroscopy under varying DC bias (current) levels to parameterize the ECM, explicitly accounting for impedance variations caused by load current magnitude. The model is validated using dynamic current profiles that emulate low frequency fluctuations and transient ripples characteristic of AI training. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model tracks battery terminal voltage with higher precision, achieving a root mean square error (RMSE) not exceeding 5.4 mV for 1 Hz and 10 Hz dynamic loads. Built on the proposed ECM, an extended Kalman filter based SOC estimator achieves RMSE below 1.24 % under high current dynamic load profiles that emulate AI training workloads, which confirms its suitability for real time battery management systems deployment in AI data centers.

title: A Survey of Federated Graph Learning in Real-World Applications
authors: Lijun Guo, Zhihao Deng, Rong Li, Long Xu, Peng Gao, Lijia Ma
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: Federated learning (FL) combined with graph neural networks (GNNs) offers a powerful solution for processing distributed graph data, particularly in privacy-sensitive domains such as healthcare and finance. Building on this potential, this survey provides a systematic and structured overview of recent advances in federated graph learning (FGL). We propose a problem-driven taxonomy that organizes existing FGL research into four core challenges: (i) graph-specific characteristics, (ii) heterogeneity, (iii) privacy preservation, and (iv) communication efficiency. For each category, we review representative solutions and analyze their underlying design principles. In addition, we summarize commonly used datasets and evaluation metrics, and provide insights into their application scenarios. Finally, we discuss emerging trends, open problems, and promising future research directions, aiming to offer a comprehensive reference for the continued development of FGL.

title: 800V to 48V Two-Phase Unfolded Resonant Converter
authors: Christian Branas, Francisco J. Azcondo, Alberto Pigazo
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents a novel resonant converter architecture based on the parallel connection of two unfolded resonant converters. The design splits the 800 V bus into two 400 V segments on the AC side and utilizes a single current-doubler rectifier on the DC side. This configuration enables a high step-down voltage conversion ratio while minimizing both switching and conduction losses. A galvanically isolated output transformer couples the two unfolded converters and enables phase-shift control of the output voltage, all while maintaining a constant switching frequency. The unfolded structure simplifies transformer design by relaxing insulation requirements and allowing a turns ratio close to unity. Additionally, the circuit inherently provides short-circuit protection due to its voltage-controlled current-source behavior. The proposed architecture is validated through a 2.4 kW battery charger application, achieving an 800 V to 48 V step-down conversion

title: Integrated Resonant Structures Using Stray Capacitance of Litz Wire Strands for Resonant Converters
authors: Pramod Apte, Xiao Yu, Jens Friebe
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Integration of passive elements leads to a reduction in the volume of power converters. In case of resonant converters, the resonant inductor is often integrated with the transformer. This work builds upon the idea of integrating the resonant capacitor into the magnetic components by using modified litz wires. By arranging the individual strands in a litz wire into two groups, the parasitic capacitance across these strands can be utilized as a capacitor in the converter design. This paper shows the feasibility of using the integrated structures in kilowatt-level resonant converters and provides a simpler method of modelling the litz wire capacitance. Additionally, this paper outlines the design considerations for two different integrated resonant structures, taking into account converter requirements and the availability of magnetic cores and litz wire.

title: Uncertainty Analysis in Intravascular Oct Segmentation
authors: Leah Heil, Ruben van der Waerden, Rick Volleberg, Jos Thannhauser, Joske van der Zande, Thijs Luttikholt, Pierandrea Cancian, Xiaojin Gu, Bram van Ginneken, Clarisa Sanchez Gutierrez, Ivana Išgum, Niels van Royen, Simone Saitta
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Integrating uncertainty quantification (UQ) into optical coherence tomography (OCT) segmentation could enhance model reliability and clinical interpretability. Using an ensemble-based model, we estimated pixelwise entropy as a measure of uncertainty and compared it to segmentation accuracy and inter-observer variability. Higher entropy was associated with lower accuracy: uncertain predictions had lower Dice scores and larger measurement errors, particularly in complex structures. Regions of annotator disagreement coincided with elevated model uncertainty. Entropy-based UQ thus provides an interpretable confidence measure, supporting selective human review and more trustworthy AI-assisted OCT analysis.

title: Blood Procurement with Prescriptive Analytics and Deep Learning Method
authors: Xiaoke Liang, Yusen Xia, Xiaojun Wang, Zheng Luo, Xu Chen
abstract: Blood is a scarce and valuable resource in healthcare, making the effective management of blood inventory a persistent challenge for blood centers and hospitals due to irregular supply and heterogeneous demand. To address severe blood shortages, this study focuses on platelets as a representative case. It employs data-driven prescriptive analytics and deep learning to manage inventory under uncertain supply and prioritized demand, where emergency demand takes precedence over regular demand. First, the prescriptive analytics approach combines a local learning predictive method with optimization to directly predict order quantities based on historical demand and observed features, utilizing a distribution-free, one-step machine learning algorithm. Second, the deep neural network approach utilizes historical demand and feature data to build a neural network that produces optimal ordering decisions. Both approaches are evaluated against conventional methods through numerical experiments using real data from a regional blood center. Extensive multi-year out-of-sample evaluations demonstrate that prescriptive analytics and deep learning consistently outperform traditional data-driven methods across multiple performance metrics. Their relative performance evolves with dataset size: prescriptive analytics approaches exhibit strong robustness under limited data, whereas deep learning achieves superior cost efficiency as historical data accumulates. The results remain stable under varying cost parameters and feature configurations, confirming the robustness and scalability of the proposed framework. Feature selection is also crucial for improving algorithm performance. The findings provide both theoretical and practical contributions by offering insights into demand behavior and supporting cost-efficient, patient-centered blood inventory management strategies.

title: Digital Twin Architecture for Drone Logistics Operations
authors: Pachayappan Murugaiyan, Tanmoy Kundu, Stephan M. Wagner, Balan Sundarakani
abstract: This study proposes a digital twin (DT) model for drone logistics systems. While DT models typically rely on real-time data from physical assets, identifying and structuring such parameters for drone logistics remains underdeveloped in current literature. Existing studies primarily focus on routing and fleet optimization, offering limited guidance on the systematic design of DT architectures for drone operations. To address this gap, this work conducts an evidence-based, structured review of 116 drone logistics models to extract and consolidate key operational parameters, resulting in the development of a Drone Logistics Parameter Framework (DLPF). Building on this foundation, the study introduces the Drone Logistics Digital Twin (DLDT) architecture and model. The DLDT is a five-dimensional system consisting of a physical entity, a virtual entity, a service interface, a data layer, and connection layers. The DLDT enables essential capabilities, including real-time monitoring, predictive maintenance, route optimization, last-mile delivery management, and scheduling. To validate the DLDT architecture and model, a route-optimization case demonstration within the DT environment illustrates their internal coherence and practical utility. Hence, this study contributes to a structured approach for designing DT models in drone logistics, addressing a notable gap in the literature and offering actionable insights for advancing intelligent, data-driven logistics operations.

title: Zero-Trust Healthcare Data Exchange Using Permissioned Blockchain
authors: Neha Sharma, Anupam Tiwari, Harshendra Gite
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: The secure and accountable exchange of healthcare data between organizational boundaries still represents a pressing concern due to the presence of insider threats, inherent trust models, and changing audit logs. Current security models based on the perimeter have failed to ensure continuous verification and accountable access within multistakeholder healthcare scenarios that involve various stakeholders such as hospitals, labs, insurance companies, and government agencies. This paper proposes a zero-trust-based framework for secure healthcare data exchange using a permissioned blockchain. In the proposed framework, every access request is cryptographically verified and authorized while ensuring immutable audit logs. The proposed framework also incorporates least privilege access control through rolebased identities, context-based policy evaluation, and organizational trust models. Moreover, sensitive healthcare data are kept off-chain while ensuring secure and accountable access through a permissioned blockchain. A permissioned blockchain has been used as an audit log that maintains a record of both authorized and unauthorized access requests. A prototype has been implemented and tested using controlled experiments with 300 access requests for each experiment with varying block sizes. Experimental results have been reported with a stable rate of denial at 6.3% for all experiments. Moreover, verification time has been reported as less than 0.03 ms for each transaction. The proposed framework also shows an increase in throughput with moderate block sizes and a stable rate thereafter. This indicates that the proposed framework has effectively amortized the cost of zero-trust verification. Moreover, block commit time has been reported as increasing linearly from sub-millisecond values to 5 ms for a block with 200 transactions. This indicates that the proposed framework can be optimized for performance while ensuring zero-trust security. This also shows that zero-trust security and performance optimization can be decoupled. This validates the proposed framework as a feasible solution for secure and accountable healthcare data exchange.

title: Technological Pathways for Compact Electric Vehicle Onboard Charger
authors: Sadegh Esmaeili Rad, Sudip K Mazumder
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Intelligent Design and Control of Automation and Drive Systems (IDCD)
abstract: Nowadays, there is an ever-growing need for electric vehicles (EVs), and thus, it is of importance to design efficient and compact onboard chargers (OBCs) at low cost to facilitate the popularization of EVs. To achieve that, several factors should be considered when designing the OBCs. However, the focus herein will be on integrating various up-to-date technologies to enhance power density while maintaining high efficiency. To that end, this paper proposes technological pathways for developing a compact and efficient EV OBC.

title: Real-Time Embedded Signal Acquisition and Control of a 5 GHz Teleoperated Bionic Hand
authors: Nor Isdarwisha Ismail, Roshakimah Mohd Isa, Nur Hanani Ahmad Puaad, Mohammed Harith Baqer Abdul Halim, Muhammad Izzuddin Zulkurnain, Muhammad Addeen Zamali
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: This project presents the design and development of a $\mathbf{5 ~ G H z}$ Teleoperated Bionic Hand with a UI Dashboard, with the main goal of achieving accurate hand movement simulation with high accuracy and low latency. Conventional prosthetic systems often confront difficulties relating to the power of the processing unit, real-time data visualization, and the precision of the conversion of analog signals. To overcome the problems mentioned, this system applies four Flex sensors to record hand finger gestures, which enable more direct kinematic control. The central processing unit is Raspberry Pi 4 Model B, selected for its ability to handle multiple tasks, which works with an MCP3208 12-bit ADC to guarantee high-resolution sensor data acquisition. A Node-RED-based dashboard has been created to furnish an easy-touse interface for the real-time observation of the data from the sensors, the system condition, and the terminal deployment. The system makes use of 5 GHz Wi-Fi communication for powerful teleoperation, and thus operator control latency is minimized. This research discusses the hardware integration, software structure, and user interface of the developed system, which proves to be a scalable option for remote robotic manipulation. The final prototype experimental results demonstrate scalability and the reliable data logging of gesture metrics for performance analysis. The real-time interface enabled the user’s inputs to be synchronized with the bionic hand, and the Node-RED dashboard made it possible to see the sensor states, thus confirming the design as a strong contender for low-latency teleoperation with low observed latency.

title: TE-ViT: Gabor-Enhanced Vision Transformer for Breast Cancer Classification in Mammograms
authors: Dheeb Albashish, Nedaa Almansour, Shahnorbanun Sahran, Azizi Abdullah, Abdulla Amin Aburomman, Sofian Kassaymeh
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: Breast cancer (BC) is the most prevalent malignancy in women worldwide. Early detection of BC is critical for effective treatment and ultimately for enhancing survival rates. Nevertheless, nonspecific symptoms in the early phases of BC make it challenging for mammography to distinguish between benign and malignant lesions. Furthermore, conventional Vision Transformers (ViTs), although successful in computer vision tasks, tend to overlook subtle texture-based biomarkers in mammograms, including spiculation and microcalcifications, which are significant for proper diagnosis. In light of the above, this study presents the Texture-Enhanced Vision Transformer (TEViT), a new dual-stream framework that explicitly combines Gabor filter-based texture analysis and transformer- based global representation learning. The first stream uses a standard ViT to model global spatial relationships, and the second stream extracts multi-scale, multi-orientation texture features from a bank of 40 Gabor filters, followed by a lightweight CNN to effectively encode these texture representations. These features are fused with ViT embeddings through cross-attention mechanisms at strategic transformer layers. The architecture is assessed using the publicly accessible mammography datasets (DDSM). The proposed method outperforms current models in binary classification tasks, achieving an accuracy of 95.09%, a sensitivity of 97.21% and an AUC of 99.26%, and statistically significant improvements <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(p<0.05)$</tex> in various evaluation criteria. The approach can reduce the diagnostic volume of work, lessen diagnostic variability, and offer prompt, dependable assistance for clinical decision-making.

title: 100kW 3-phase ARCP soft switched SiC (S2SiC) inverter - design and in-lab test verification
authors: Yushi Yang, Nan Lin, Yuheng Wu, Dongyu Jin, Zach Wehri, Muhammad Fasih Uddin, Brij Singh, Long Wu, Yue Zhao
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The auxiliary resonant commuted pole (ARCP) inverter is one of the most promising soft switching inverter topologies. In the ARCP inverter, resonant current supplied by the auxiliary circuit ensures soft switching operation. Successful soft switching of inverter results in lower level of electrical and thermal stresses on semiconductor power devices, and leads to lower temperature rise, and higher inverter efficiency. In this paper, 100kW ARCP 3-phase SiC inverter system was designed, fabricated, and double pulse test (DPT) test-verified to prove out feasibility for continuous operation. An excessive voltage overshoot was found under hard start, which poses a potential risk for premature failure of SiC power devices. To mitigate excessive voltage overshoot, authors have developed inverter start-up technique that was test-verified by DPT method. As expected with the developed soft-starting technique, power devices in the soft switched SiC (S2SiC) inverter don’t experience excessive voltage overshoot. The developed hardware for the S2SiC inverter and its control method is ready for interior permanent magnet (IPM) motor control. In this final paper, results from S2SiC inverter feeding a three-phase R-L load are included. Results to be obtained from a back-to-back motor dynamometer-based set-up will be reported in a future paper, where ARCP-based S2SiC inverter will drive an interior permanent magnet (IPM) motor with objective to prove out vitality and a compelling application of the 100kW S2SiC inverter.

title: Abdominal Segmentation and Blood Vessel Detection in CTA Scans for Diep Reconstruction Procedures
authors: Shir Ashkenazi, Nahum Kiryati, Dor Freidin, Ariel Tessone, Shai Tejman-Yarden, Arnaldo Mayer
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Deep Inferior Epigastric Perforator (DIEP) flap reconstruction requires precise identification of perforator vessels traversing the abdominal muscle to supply the fat and skin tissues. Computed tomography angiography (CTA) reveals contrast-enhanced blood vessels critical for the DIEP surgical harvesting selection. CTA annotation is time-consuming and expertise-dependent, requiring clinicians to trace small perforator vessels through hundreds of image slices. We present an end-to-end automated pipeline that receives raw CTA scans and performs ROI extraction, deep learning multi-class segmentation and detection of perforation points. The pipeline achieves effective segmentation with reliable detection of critical anatomical landmarks. It can reduce pre-operative preparation time while maintaining clinical accuracy for improved surgical planning and navigation.

title: Center-Aware Detection with Swin-Based Co-DETR Framework for Cervical Cytology
authors: Yan Kong, Yuan Yin, Hongan Chen, Yuqi Fang, Caifeng Shan
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Automated analysis of Pap smear images is critical for cervical cancer screening but remains challenging due to dense cell distribution and complex morphology. In this paper, we present our winning solution for the RIVA Cervical Cytology Challenge, achieving 1st place in Track B and 2nd place in Track A. Our approach leverages a powerful baseline, integrating the Co-DINO framework with a Swin-Large backbone for robust multi-scale feature extraction. To address the dataset's unique fixed-size bounding box annotations, we formulate the detection task as a center-point prediction problem. Tailoring our approach to this formulation, we introduce a center-preserving data augmentation strategy and an analytical geometric box optimization to effectively absorb localization jitter. Finally, we apply track-specific loss tuning to adapt the loss weights for each task. Experiments demonstrate that our targeted optimizations improve detection performance, providing an effective pipeline for cytology image analysis. Our code is available at https://github.com/YanKong0408/Center-DETR.

title: Data-Driven Switching Models Realization in Power Electronic Converters via Symbolic Regression
authors: Navid Hadifar, Ali Haji Ali Biglo, Pablo Moscato, Enrico Santi
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This study presents a data-driven symbolic regression framework for modeling power electronic converters, targeting applications in digital-twin environments. The proposed method automatically uncovers both the structural form and parameter values of governing equations directly from measured or simulated data, producing concise and interpretable discrete-time models capable of reproducing nonlinear switching behavior. A non-ideal boost converter is used as a case example, where conduction and switching losses are explicitly included to ensure a realistic dynamic representation. In contrast to conventional black-box machine learning approaches, the proposed strategy offers high computational efficiency and maintains strong predictive capability even when trained on relatively small datasets. The resulting models exhibit close agreement with physics-derived baselines, demonstrating the approach’s accuracy, scalability, and suitability for power electronics applications.

title: How Important are Inter-Dataset Interactions for Large Scale Analysis of FMRI Data: A Multi-Dimensional Comparison
authors: Clément Cosserat, Lucas Gois, Emilie Chouzenoux, Vince Calhoun, Tülay Adali
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Analyzing multi-subject functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) data requires methods that can jointly capture shared and individual patterns of brain activity across participants. Joint blind source separation (JBSS) techniques, such as independent vector analysis (IVA), provide a principled framework for this purpose by modeling dependencies across subjects while identifying distinct functional networks. Constrained IVA variants, including adaptive-reverse cIVA-G (ar-cIVA-G) and threshold-free cIVA-G (tf-cIVA-G), further enhance interpretability through the use of reference templates and inter-subject correlation constraints. Alternatively, regression-based methods like IVA-G regression (regIVA-G) and reference-guided component analysis (RGCA) process subjects individually, aligning their components to references with improved computational efficiency. Despite their potential, systematic evaluations of reference-based JBSS approaches for fMRI analysis remain limited. In this work, we present a comparative study of these methods to assess their capacity for identifying schizophrenia-related biomarkers using real fMRI data from subjects with schizophrenia and healthy controls. Our results demonstrate that both constrained IVA and regression-based methods effectively extract meaningful biomarkers while the latter achieve comparable performance at substantially reduced computational cost.

title: Grid Reactive Power Control in Three-phase Unfolding-based Architectures
authors: Sanat Poddar, Regan Zane
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Three-phase unfolding-based power converter architectures provide a highly efficient and power-dense alternative to conventional multi-stage grid-tied converters. However, the absence of bulk energy storage inherently limits their functionality. This work proposes an auxiliary converter that interfaces with the soft dc-link voltages at the output of the unfolder and circulates only a fraction of the total load power to regulate grid reactive power. A key novelty is that the auxiliary converter is designed to operate with the characteristic time-varying dc voltages produced by a three-phase unfolding stage. The associated challenges of accommodating this voltage variation, as well as identifying a suitable converter topology, design, and control strategy, are addressed. The proposed system is evaluated through simulation and experimentally validated using a 1 kW prototype. The results demonstrate that integrating a partial-power auxiliary converter extends the functionality of unfolding-based architectures and allows for reactive power control.

title: A Dataset-Based Comparative Analysis of Student Academic Performance
authors: Gulnar Naghiyeva
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: This study investigates student academic performance prediction as a key challenge in data-driven educational decision-making. Using the publicly available UCI Student Performance dataset, a comparative analysis of three regression-based machine learning models-k-Nearest Neighbors (k-NN), Random Forest, and Support Vector Machine Regression (SMOreg)-is conducted. Model performance is evaluated using correlation coefficient, Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) under a $\mathbf{1 0}$-fold cross-validation framework. The experimental results show that ensemble-based learning, particularly Random Forest, significantly outperforms instance-based methods by achieving the highest correlation and lowest prediction errors. In addition to predictive robustness, the regression-based analysis provides interpretable insights into influential academic factors, identifying prior grades (G1 and G2) and absenteeism as the most significant predictors of final performance. The findings highlight the value of integrating robust predictive modeling with interpretable analytics to support early identification of at-risk students and informed instructional planning. Overall, the study contributes to the development of reliable, data-driven learning analytics systems aimed at enhancing educational quality and student retention.

title: Mitigating the Reconstruction-Detection Trade-Off in Vae-Based Unsupervised Anomaly Detection
authors: Agathe Senellart, Maëlys Solal, Stéphanie Allassonnière, Ninon Burgos
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Variational autoencoders are widely used for unsupervised anomaly detection. Model selection however remains an open-question: to remain fully unsupervised, hyperparameters are often chosen to minimize the reconstruction error on normal samples. In this paper, we reveal a trade-off between reconstruction quality and anomaly detection among <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta$</tex>-VAE models. Models with constrained latent space reach higher detection metrics but lower reconstruction quality. We also assess the performance variability across random seeds and show it is linked to the distance between normal and abnormal latent distributions. From this analysis, we justify and investigate two methods to mitigate the reconstructiondetection tradeoff: beta-scheduling and the Sparse VAE. The latter especially shows an improvement in detection while maintaining high reconstruction quality.

title: PnP-SAF: A Plug-and-Play Semantic-Attribute Fusion Framework for Data-Efficient Medical Image Diagnosis
authors: Shengqi Chen, Yi Huang, Haoran Liu, Wenbo Liu, Tao Deng
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: In recent years, deep learning has achieved remarkable success in medical diagnosis. However, its performance is often limited by small medical datasets, leading to overfitting and poor generalization. Accurate diagnosis also depends on non-image semantic attributes (e.g., age, sex, lifestyle factors), which provide critical complementary context. To address these challenges, we present PnP-SAF, a plug-andplay Semantic-Attribute Fusion framework that enhances data-efficient medical image diagnosis. PnP-SAF can be seamlessly integrated into both CNN- and Transformer-based architectures, enabling effective fusion of semantic attributes with image features in a flexible manner. Extensive experiments on the ODIR and PAD-UFES-20 datasets demonstrate that PnP-SAF mitigates overfitting, improves generalization, and significantly boosts diagnostic accuracy, highlighting its robustness and practical applicability in clinical scenarios.

title: Series Arc and Hotspot Detection in Power Distribution Box for Industrial Loads
authors: Kuldeep Singh Shivran, Sarasij Das
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: The Industrial Power Distribution Box (PDB) distributes incoming power supply to diverse loads, including resistive, inductive, and power electronics-based devices, each with distinct current profiles. Circuit breakers allocate these loads to designated sections of the distribution unit. Mechanical vibrations in industrial environments can loosen connections at breaker poles, causing series arc faults. Such faults often remain undetected due to their low intensity, leading to progressive damage, potential shutdowns, and significant economic losses. Early detection of arc faults is therefore critical. While literature addresses series faults, it lacks effective methods for detecting arc faults in application-specific circuits with diverse loads. This work proposes a hybrid detection solution for resistive and power electronic (i.e., DC and RF generator) loads using current measurement and harmonic component analysis, complemented by a temperature-based detection algorithm for localized secondary protection. Arc current and temperature data are logged wirelessly, eliminating communication cabling and reducing space requirements. The proposed method is validated in semiconductor industry PDBs with varied loads, demonstrating high detection accuracy under different loading and noise levels.

title: Joint Multiscale Image Learning for Segmentation in Macroscopic and Microscopic Pathology Images
authors: Yijie Zhu, Ayesha Azam, Ibrahim Alsanie, Ricardo Marques, Syed Ali Khurram, Shan E Ahmed Raza
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Image segmentation at both macroscopic and microscopic level is important in digital pathology. In these images, visually similar regions within the same image often share semantic characteristics, offering an opportunity for intra-image reference learning. We propose a novel binary segmentation framework using a joint multiscale image learning strategy. Our approach builds upon a whole image segmentation model and introduces a complementary part level module that enables mutual referencing among image regions. By computing the feature similarity, each target part image identifies its corresponding reference part images. Cross-attention is then applied between the target and reference part images to exchange information. This strategy not only enhances segmentation consistency but also increases the effective training sample size, improving generalization in small datasets. The proposed model significantly outperforms competitive methods in both macroscopic and microscopic datasets.

title: Physics-Accurate Modeling of Quantum Effects in Photonic Integrated Circuits
authors: Richard Chung, Mahdi Nikdast, Kaveh Rahbardar Mojaver
abstract: We present a physics-accurate modeling framework for analyzing quantum effects in photonic integrated circuits. The framework is validated through comparisons with Hong-Ou-Mandel interference experimental data, and used to analyze the fidelity and bi-photon transmittance of Mach-Zehnder interferometer (MZI)-based meshes implementing Haarrandom matrices.

title: Deep Learning-Based Fetal Lung Segmentation and Lung Development Evaluation from Diffusion-Weighted Mri Images
authors: Zhennan Xiao, Katharine Brudkiewicz, Zhen Yuan, Rosalind Aughwane, Magdalena Sokolska, Joanna Chappell, Trevor Gaunt, Anna L. David, Andrew P. King, Andrew Melbourne
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Fetal lung development is critically important for optimal neonatal outcomes. Neonates with structural anomalies or from pregnancies affected by fetal growth restriction often suffer chronic lung disease due to poor lung development, but this is difficult to predict prenatally. Intra-voxel incoherent motion (IVIM) analysis shows promise for non-invasive fetal lung development assessment but relies on labor-intensive manual segmentation, which limits its clinical applicability. In this work, we present an automated fetal lung development evaluation pipeline for diffusion-weighted magnetic resonance images that consists of a deep learning-based fetal lung segmentation model and a model-fitting lung development assessment. A 3D nnU-Net model was trained on manually segmented images selected from the baseline frames of 4D diffusion-weighted MRI scans. The segmentation model demonstrated robust performance, yielding a mean Dice coefficient of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$82.14 \pm 6.68 \%$</tex>. There was no statistically significant difference between IVIM parameters derived from automated and manual masks, demonstrating the reliability of the automated approach for quantifying tissue microstructure and perfusion. This single-centre feasibility study indicates that automated segmentation can provide IVIM measurements equivalent to manual delineation.

title: Physics-Guided Dual-Domain Network with Attention-Based Fusion for Portable MRI Reconstruction
authors: Efe Ilıcak, Baris Imre, Chloé Najac, Ruben van den Broek, Beatrice Lena, Andrew Webb, Marius Staring
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Portable low-field magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) systems have gained renewed interest owing to their cost effectiveness and point-of-care imaging capabilities. Yet, portable MRI systems suffer from relatively low signal-to-noise ratio and limited hardware capabilities. While previous works have proposed the use of deep learning based reconstruction methods to improve low-field image quality, these operated only in the image-domain. Unlike other imaging modalities, MRI directly acquires data in the Fourier-domain (k-space), and exploiting both k-space and image-domain information can improve reconstruction quality. Here, we introduce DUNDD, a novel physics-guided 3D network for portable MRI reconstruction, with parallel dual-domain branches whose outputs are combined together via an attention-based fusion network. To demonstrate the performance of the proposed method, we present in vivo reconstructions obtained from both emulated datasets as well as images acquired with a 47mT Halbach-based portable MRI system. Our results show that DUN-DD outperforms state-of-the-art classical, datadriven, and physics-guided methods on both emulated and real portable MRI acquisitions.

title: Potential of Masked Vision Transformer for Predicting Macrovascular Invasion on Liver CT Images: Graph-Based Association Analysis of Attention Heads
authors: Kengo Takahashi, Yuwen Zeng, Kei Ichiji, Zhang Zhang, Haoyang Liu, Issei Yoshizumi, Ryusei Inamori, Takuma Usuzaki, Noriyasu Homma
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Macrovascular invasion (MI) in hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) is a key predictor of recurrence and poor survival due to tumor cell dissemination, thereby playing a crucial role in making treatment decisions. To capture the latent influence of HCC and MI on the internal liver structure, artificial intelligence (AI) for predicting MI with a liver-guided strategy is required. Vision Transformer (ViT) is a promising AI model for capturing long-range dependencies, while the influence remains unclear: (a) potential of shape-guided multi-head attention extended to entire liver and its appropriate application layers. (b) the influence of shape guidance on the interrelationships of attention heads. Our study investigated the optimal encoder stage of ViT for liverguided strategy, and applying the strategy on deep encoder layer were effective. We also introduced a new method to explore the inter-relationships among attention heads using graph. Interestingly, some heads were attracted to opposing area despite liver-guided processing. These findings suggest the need for careful interpretation of attention mechanism when implementing such models.

title:
M
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    Net: Multi-Scale Large Kernel Convolution with Multi-Branch Cooptimization Network
authors: Peinan Hao, Huicong Ning, Ying Zhang, Jiajia Chen, Tianzi Yan
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: In the processing of degeneration phenomenon of underwater visual image, the traditional single dimension convolution to meet edge blur leads to inadequate feature extraction,especially prone to error checking in establishing long-rangedependence, and the leak problem. To solve the problem, we propose M2Net based on the Inception multibranching and decomposed multi-scale large kernel attention: using different sizes of big kernel convolution to capture multiscale feature information, retain richer detailed features, and enhance the adaptability of the model to the change of the target scale; combining the Inception multibranching structure, the feature information is processed in parallel on different branches, and through the inter-branching interaction between the branches to enhance the expression of features. The experimental results on the public dataset J-EDI show that compared with the benchmark algorithm YOLOv11n, the mAP50 and mAP50-95 of the improved algorithm on the validation set are increased by 2.9 and 1.1 percentage points, respectively. The mAP50 and mAP50- 95 on the test set are increased by 0.9 and 3.3 percentage points, respectively. In addition, the FPS of the improved algorithm is 256.4, which can realize the requirement of real-time target detection in the industrial field.

title: Discontinuous Space Vector PWM Strategies for Field-Oriented Control of Five-Phase Induction Motors with Reduced Switching Losses
authors: Jeeban Kumar Nayak, Ranjan Kumar Behera
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: The increased demand for high-efficiency and fault-tolerant motor drives has led to the adoption of multiphase machines, specifically the five-phase induction motors (FPIMs), emerging as a promising solution for high-power drive applications. The two-level voltage source inverter (VSI)-fed FPIM with space vector pulse width modulation (SVPWM)-based field-oriented control (FOC) offers precise torque and flux control, but the conventional SVPWM technique results in more switching transitions and thus the associated losses increase. However, in high-power and high-speed applications, the mechanical inertia of the FPIM rotor is very large and sufficient to damp out the torque ripple for smooth operation of the FPIM drive. This paper presents the implementation of discontinuous SVPWM (DSVPWM) strategies within the FOC framework for FPIMs to reduce switching losses without disturbing the dynamic performance of the drive. The proposed DSVPWM techniques reduce switching transitions in each modulation cycle. A comparative analysis between conventional SVPWM and DSVPWM is conducted using MATLAB/Simulink, evaluating switching losses and current ripple. In addition, the proposed technique is verified in hardware-in-the-loop mode with the Opal-RT real-time simulator (OP5700). The results demonstrate that DSVPWM significantly reduces switching losses while maintaining waveform quality and dynamic response, making it suitable for high-efficiency drive applications.

title: A Robust Deterministic Switched Hybrid GWO-P&O MPPT Algorithm for PV Systems under Partial Shading and Low Irradiance
authors: Fatima Zahra Loudifa, Soukaina El Daoudi, Ali Margal, Abderrazzak El Abdalaoui, Rabii Azdoud, Asma Karama
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: Partial shading conditions (PSC) challenge conventional Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) algorithms like Perturb & Observe (P&O), which often converge to local optima instead of the global maximum. Metaheuristic methods such as the Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO) can locate the global peak but may cause steady-state oscillations and instability, especially at low irradiance. To address these issues, this paper proposes a Deterministic Switched Grey Wolf Optimizer and Perturb & Observe (DS-GWO-P&O) algorithm. It uses a power threshold (P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</inf>) to switch dynamically between strategies: a high-precision P&O under low-power conditions (P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">pv</inf> < P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</inf>) to enhance stability and reduce oscillations, and GWO under nominal conditions to ensure global peak tracking. Simulations confirm the approach’s effectiveness, achieving 99.4% tracking efficiency and 0.12 s convergence time, outperforming conventional MPPT methods in both dynamic and steady states.

title: Oculomix: Hierarchical Sampling for Retinal-Based Systemic Disease Prediction
authors: Hyunmin Kim, Yukun Zhou, Rahul A. Jonas, Lie Ju, Sunjin Hwang, Pearse A. Keane, Siegfried K. Wagner
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Oculomics — the concept of predicting systemic diseases, such as cardiovascular disease and dementia, through retinal imaging — has advanced rapidly due to the data efficiency of transformer-based foundation models like RETFound. Image-level mixed sample data augmentations, such as CutMix and MixUp, are frequently used for training transformers, yet these techniques perturb patient-specific attributes, such as medical comorbidity and clinical factors, since they only account for images and labels. To address this limitation, we propose a hierarchical sampling strategy, Oculomix, for mixed sample augmentations. Our method is based on two clinical priors. First (exam level), images acquired from the same patient at the same time point share the same attributes. Second (patient level), images acquired from the same patient at different time points have a soft temporal trend, as morbidity generally increases over time. Guided by these priors, our method constrains the mixing space to the patient and exam levels to better preserve patient-specific characteristics and leverages their hierarchical relationships. The proposed method is validated using ViT models on a five-year prediction of major adverse cardiovascular events (MACE) in a large ethnically diverse population (Alzeye). We show that Oculomix consistently outperforms image-level CutMix and MixUp by up to 3% in AUROC, demonstrating the necessity and value of the proposed method in oculomics.

title: A Source-Free Segmentation Quality Estimation of a Model Adapted to a New Domain
authors: Ido Nahum, Rotem Nizhar, Jacob Goldberger
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: We address the challenge of estimating segmentation quality in source-free domain adaptation (SFDA), where access to labeled source data is restricted and no labels are available in the target domain. Our approach leverages the segmentations produced by the source model as pseudo-labels and refines them to construct enhanced surrogates of the unknown ground truth. We show that standard metrics such as Dice and ASSD, when computed with these pseudo-labels, closely approximate the true segmentation quality of the adapted network. Evaluated on cardiac MRI and prostate MRI benchmarks, our method computes segmentation quality estimation that is more accurate than methods requiring labeled source data. These results demonstrate that pseudo-labels, commonly used for adaptation, can also provide a simple and effective basis for post-hoc segmentation quality estimation.

title: Adaptive Split Federated Learning with Behavioral Digital Twins and Multi-Head Temporal Networks for Internet of Vehicles Security
authors: Farhan Ullah, Khalid Mahmood, Umar Raza, Jawad Ahmad, Jenhui Chen, Rubal Jeet, Yue Zhao
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: The rapid growth of the Internet of Vehicles (IoV) ecosystem has produced complicated security concerns that require a modern Intrusion Detection System (IDS) that can respond to emerging threats and maintain data protection. The dynamic behavior of clients and the heterogeneity of the system can affect the convergence and performance of models in federated learning for IoV security. Traditional federated learning may overlook data quality, computational resources, and client confidence. This issue is resolved by behavioral digital twin technology, which generates virtual replicas of real-world IoV clients that continuously monitor, analyze, and forecast client behavior. Heterogeneous client attributes become a robust IDS with adaptive client selection, customized optimization, and trust-based aggregation using digital twins. This paper proposes a novel approach, DT-ASFL, an adaptive Split Federated Learning (SFL) system that combines behavioral digital twins with multi-head temporal networks to improve IoV security. The lightweight multi-head Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) extracts temporal features, and SFL stores sensitive vehicle data on edge devices. These attributes are analyzed by a more advanced classifier on a distant server, so raw data never leaves the device. For each client, behavioral digital twins dynamically modify aggregation weights based on trust and health measures, evaluate data quality, and monitor performance. This adaptive method provides protection against malicious users and system heterogeneity. TCN architecture captures diverse temporal patterns for optimal feature fusion using parallel processing heads with different dilation rates and an attention mechanism. Evaluation on several security datasets demonstrates considerable increases in attack detection accuracy and robustness. The approach ensures privacy and system flexibility while outperforming conventional federated learning techniques.

title: Enhancing Information Security using Implicit Transmission
authors: Dhanushki Hewawaduge, John P. Fonseka
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: In recent studies, it has been shown that Generalized implicit transmission (GIT) can transmit a significantly higher amount of information implicitly over any N number of implicit sequences while transmitting a small fraction of information explicitly over a channel. Such GIT coding schemes can reach data rates higher than the Shannon limit. In this study, it is demonstrated that the same GIT coding technique can be used to transmit information with a level of security much higher than that offered by currently known encryption methods. Specifically, this study proposes adjustments to GIT coding schemes by employing independent interleavers on each sequence, and independently randomizing message bits that are forced to zero during encoding on every sequence, to enhance security.

title: Functional Semantic Inference Method for Industrial Control Proprietary Protocol
authors: Yahui Yang, Yangyang Geng, Qiang Wei, Man Zhou, Zhuo Lv, Liupeng He
abstract: Industrial control protocols (ICPs), especially proprietary protocols, lack public specifications due to security and commercial considerations, posing profound challenges for security audits and vulnerability detection. Although the function code fields in ICPs define critical operational semantics (e.g., PLC start/stop), existing protocol reverse engineering (PRE) methods struggle to accurately infer the functional implications of their value dependencies. To address this critical gap, we propose FuncSeinfer, an operation-traffic correlation-driven method for inferring functional semantics in proprietary ICPs by associating graphical user interface (GUI) operations of programming software with network traffic. FuncSeinfer first employs an entropy-based dynamic field extraction algorithm to generate candidate fields. Subsequently, it applies heuristic rules and clustering scoring rules to accurately infer function code fields. Finally, it identifies semantics through variance analysis of traffic marked with operational labels. We rigorously evaluated FuncSeinfer on 7 widely used ICPs (S7comm, UMAS-A, UMAS-B, Modbus, PCCC, Melsoft, and Fins) in 9 real-world PLCs from 5 leading manufacturers, achieving 100% accuracy in function code field inference, outperforming state-of-the-art PRE tools such as Netzob, FieldHunter, Netplier, and FSIBP. Furthermore, FuncSeinfer can accurately identify the semantic meaning of 78 function codes, which is an average improvement of 20.2% compared to Wireshark. This study fills the gap in functional semantics within ICP reverse engineering, enabling in-depth security analysis without requiring protocol specifications.

title: Model-Free Dynamic Consensus in Multi-Agent Systems: A Q-Function Perspective
authors: Maryam Babazadeh, Naim Bajcinca
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: This paper presents a new method for dynamic consensus in linear discrete-time homogeneous multi-agent systems (MAS). Achieving state consensus in such systems involves constraints linked to the graph's spectral properties, complicating the design of coupling gains, especially in large-scale networks. The proposed approach reformulates the dynamic consensus problem with a prescribed convergence rate by introducing a state–action value function within a synthetic linear quadratic regulation (LQR) framework, thereby expressing the problem as a semidefinite program (SDP). The resulting SDP supports the joint design of local feedback and coupling gains in both model-based and model-free settings. To handle non-convex feasibility conditions, a convex–concave decomposition strategy is developed, guaranteeing convergence to a stationary point. In the fully model-free case, the method eliminates the need for system identification or explicit knowledge of agent dynamics, relying solely on input–state data to construct an equivalent data-driven SDP. Finally, a new algorithm balancing feasibility, convergence rate, and energy efficiency enhances design flexibility.
