title: TeleSim: A Network-Aware Testbed and Benchmark Dataset for Telerobotic Applications
authors: Zexin Deng, Zhenhui Yuan, Longhao Zou
abstract: Telerobotic technologies are becoming increasingly essential in fields such as remote surgery, nuclear decommissioning, and space exploration. Reliable datasets and testbeds are essential for evaluating telerobotic system performance prior to real-world deployment. However, there is a notable lack of datasets that capture the impact of network delays, as well as testbeds that realistically model the communication link between the operator and the robot. This paper introduces TeleSim, a network-aware teleoperation dataset and testbed designed to assess the performance of telerobotic applications under diverse network conditions. TeleSim systematically collects performance data from fine manipulation tasks executed under three predefined network quality tiers: High, Medium, and Low. Each tier is characterized through controlled settings of bandwidth, latency, jitter, and packet loss. Using OMNeT++ for precise network simulation, we record a wide range of metrics, including completion time, success rates, video quality indicators (Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) and Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM)), and quality of service (QoS) parameters. TeleSim comprises 300 experimental trials, providing a robust benchmark for evaluating teleoperation systems across heterogeneous network scenarios. In the worst network condition, completion time increases by 221.8% and success rate drops by 64%. Our findings reveal that network degradation leads to compounding negative impacts, notably reduced video quality and prolonged task execution, highlighting the need for adaptive, resilient teleoperation protocols. The full dataset and testbed software are publicly available on our GitHub repository: https://github.com/ConnectedRoboticsLab/TeleSim and YouTube channel: https://youtu.be/Fz_1iOYe104.

title: Feature-Guided Stereoscopic Video Retargeting with Comfort-Oriented Depth Remapping
authors: Qi Wu, Zhenhua Tang, Linghui Cai, Yulin Li, Guangqing Li, Yongwen Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Effective screen resource allocation is crucial for maintaining the geometry of salient objects and depth perception in stereoscopic video retargeting. Existing methods, however, predominantly depend on global resizing or heuristic cropping, which often cannot separate perceptually important regions from complex backgrounds and may lead to distortion of salient structures. To overcome this limitation, we propose a stereoscopic video retargeting framework that incorporates two complementary feature metrics—Depth Structural Complexity and Significant Information Loss—to explicitly model background depth distribution and the importance of salient content. Together, these metrics steer a feature-aware intelligent cropping scheme, allowing adaptive allocation of limited screen space to perceptually meaningful areas. Furthermore, a visual-comfort-oriented depth constraint strategy is integrated to adaptively remap excessive disparities in videos that cause viewing fatigue. Experimental results show that the proposed approach successfully preserves the structure of salient objects while also enhancing stereoscopic visual comfort.

title: Lung Cancer Prediction Using Multi Input Model
authors: M. Raghini, M. Satheesh Kumar, S. Sasikala, R. Preetha, J. J. Shivadharshini
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Lung cancer is a serious disease which forms cancer cells that lines the air passages of the lungs. It has become a leading cause of death in both men and women due to lifestyles. Early screening of the individuals, vulnerable to lung cancer helps to increase the survival rate. In many research papers, researchers have performed several machine learning, deep learning, and image classification techniques to predict the lung cancer. Machine learning and image classification techniques produce more accurate predictions thereby aiding early treatment. The proposed paper involves a combination of two models each trained with different datasets. Multi-input model is used to make predictions accurate. CNN (Convolutional Neural Network) is used for the image data and traditional algorithms like Random Forest and Gradient boosting is used for structured data. The final performance of the multi-input model is measured using several metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall and ROC-AUC. The results demonstrate that the combination of image data and the clinical data improves the accuracy compared using the either dataset alone. This can aid the health care professionals in making more informed diagnostic decisions.

title: Fake Job Post Detection Using NLP
authors: Muthuraj D, Veera Kumar M M, K. Mahalakshmi
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Fake job postings are a growing global problem that target applicants via deceptive descriptions, stolen logos, and coordinated recruiter behavior. Systems that look only at text often miss tricks that combine images and account-level signals. We propose a practical multi-modal detection pipeline that combines transformer-based text embeddings, CNN-based image verification (with perceptual hashing), and recruiter-behavior analytics. The fusion model returns a calibrated fraud risk score and human-friendly explanations (SHAP contributions, highlighted phrases, Grad-CAM overlays). We report extensive experiments, ablation studies and a small human-in-the-loop evaluation to show the approach is both effective and usable. On our 18k-post dataset the fused model yields 95.8% accuracy and significantly reduces dangerous false negatives compared to text-only baselines. We also discuss deployment considerations, monitoring strategies for model drift, and practical steps for integration into moderation workflows.

title: Lithium Battery Fault Detection Method Based on Feature Extraction Images
authors: Zekai Zhao, Wenjie Zhang, Zhihao Zhang, Nianfu Li, Chentao Wang, Mifeng Ren
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: Aiming at the weak early signals, difficult identification and delayed early warning of sudden internal short circuits in power batteries for new energy vehicles, a power battery fault detection method integrating multi-dimensional cell feature extraction and Vision Transformer (ViT) is proposed. Firstly, battery operation data is classified into four typical working conditions (charging, dynamic driving, standby, vehicle-off) via adaptive working condition analysis technology, with more than 26 cell aging features highly correlated with internal short circuits extracted. Secondly, an internal short circuit feature evolution database is constructed by combining lithium battery failure mechanisms, which mitigates the scarcity of fault samples. Finally, a ViT-based internal short circuit recognition model is designed to accurately identify weak early fault features. Test results show that the model achieves a fault recognition accuracy of 97% and a precision of 85%, with the maximum F1-score difference across datasets <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 5 {\%}$</tex>. Its core performance is significantly superior to the traditional CNN+LSTM model, breaking through the weak anomaly identification bottleneck of conventional monitoring technologies and providing a feasible technical solution for the early warning of internal short circuits in power batteries.

title: Overload Investigation of 3.3kV Si-IGBT and SiC-MOSFET Power Modules
authors: Bochen Liu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: The overload capability of two power modules, i.e. $3.3 \mathrm{kV} / 450 \mathrm{~A} \mathrm{Si}$-IGBT and $3.3 \mathrm{kV} / 750 \mathrm{~A}$ SiC-MOSFET, are investigated for STATCOM using delta-connected chain-link MMC. The critical indicator, i.e. overload duration limit, is utilized to compare the overload capability under various operational conditions, including grid-connection voltage, device working current, semiconductor case temperature and allowed maximum junction temperature. To achieve this, the power losses coupling with temperature are iteratively calculated and thus the junction temperature can be obtained with respective thermal models. The results indicate a comparable overload capability for the SiC module, with the expected advantage of a higher allowable temperature limit.

title: Sentiment Analysis and LSTM-Based Hybrid Approach for Bitcoin Price Prediction
authors: Ranim Khedhiri, Dalila Amara, Latifa Rabai
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Cryptocurrency markets are highly volatile and tend to respond sharply to changes in public sentiment. While a few studies have explored the influence of investor emotions on price fluctuations, there remains a notable gap in integrating social media sentiment with advanced predictive models. This paper proposes a framework that combines sentiment analysis of Reddit discussions with deep learning techniques to improve Bitcoin price forecasting. Sentiment scores are obtained through the VADER model and subsequently integrated with past pricing information to serve as inputs for a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network. The findings show that integrating sentiment data substantially improves prediction accuracy compared to models that depend only on historical price information. This approach highlights the value of behavioral finance when paired with machine learning and offers practical benefits for investors and decision-makers in the crypto space.

title: Blockchain Practical Teaching based on Dual-Platform
authors: Ke Xu, Deyou Tang
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The industrial development of blockchain technology demands high-quality talents with both theoretical foundations and practical engineering capabilities. To address the prominent challenges in current blockchain talent cultivation, such as over-reliance on a single blockchain platform, fragmented practical teaching, and scarce high-quality teaching resources, this paper designs and implements a dual-platform practical teaching system underpinned by in-depth university-industry collaboration. Centered on two mainstream consortium blockchain platforms (Hyperledger Fabric and FISCO BCOS), the system develops two core courses: "Blockchain Technology and Applications" and "Blockchain Practical Training", both featuring layered and progressive practical content. Additionally, a "Blockchain Practical Teaching Case Repository" covering finance, supply chain, public services, and other related fields is constructed. Teaching practice demonstrates that this system significantly improves student learning outcomes, strengthens the demonstrative impact of teaching reform, and gains wide recognition from the industry. It thereby provides a replicable model for cultivating blockchain engineering talents in higher education.

title: Explainable Distillation-Based Machine Unlearning for Sensitive Subgroup Removal in Diabetic Healthcare Data
authors: Ramalingam Murugan, Rajeswari Chengoden
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Healthcare is a domain that demands more privacy with respect to individual information, as it is more sensitive in nature. AI and Machine learnings now-a-days used widely in the healthcare domain to assist medical professionals. Due to this, models remember the data on which it got trained, and this leads to a violation of the privacy policy of individuals. After the launch of unlearning approaches, data are removed from the model, but it still retains knowledge of sensitive information. This work proposes a selective unlearning framework using a teacher-student architecture to eliminate the influence of a specific demographic group without retraining the model from scratch. A deep neural network is used as a teacher model to learn from the diabetic readmission dataset. The dataset is further subdivided into keep and forget set based on the race. The teacher model is getting trained on the full dataset, whereas the student model is trained on the keep set using knowledge distillation. The soft probability outputs generated from the teacher model help in guiding the learning process. No data from the forget set is used during Student training. Experimental results demonstrate that the Student model preserves performance on the retained data while showing a substantial performance reduction on the forgotten subgroup. Evaluation metrics and explainable AI analysis further confirm the effectiveness of the selective unlearning process, making the proposed approach suitable for privacy-sensitive healthcare applications.

title: Explainable Emotion Recognition in Real-World Images via Caption-Aligned Dual-Branch Contextsubject Fusion Framework
authors: Rauki Yadav, Vineet Kumar Goel
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Emotion recognition in real-world images remains a challenging frontier in affective computing, constrained by intricate interactions between scene context and human subjects. Existing approaches demonstrate limited interpretability and struggle with caption-visual misalignment, resulting in suboptimal performance on unconstrained image datasets. This paper introduces CADF-Net, a novel Caption-Aligned Dual- Branch Framework (CADF) that systematically disentangles contextual and subject-centric emotional cues through a dual- branch architecture with explicit caption-image alignment. Our framework leverages Vision Transformers (ViT) for subject- branch encoding and ResNet-50 with spatial attention for context- branch encoding, achieving hierarchical feature fusion via cross- modal attention mechanisms. A caption-guided alignment module ensures semantic coherence between visual representations and descriptive cues, enhancing explainability through attention visualization and feature attribution maps. Evaluated on AffectNet (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n =250 ~\mathrm{K}$</tex> images, 8 emotions), RAF-DB (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n=30 ~\mathrm{K}$</tex> images), and a curated real-world dataset (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n=15 K$</tex> images), CADF- Net achieves 89.4% accuracy on AffectNet and 87.6% on RAF- DB, representing 4.2 % and 3.8 % improvements over state-of- the-art dual-branch methods. Explainability metrics demonstrate superior interpretability through caption consistency analysis <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\kappa=0.82$</tex> Fleiss' kappa agreement with human annotators) and saliency map coherence. Our framework advances the frontiers of trustworthy emotion recognition for real-world deployment in affective computing, human-computer interaction, and clinical psychology applications.

title: Temporal Consistency-Driven 3D Human Pose Estimation from RGB Videos
authors: G. Ms.Sakthi Priya, K. Vijayalakshmi, K. Vignesh Saravanan, KM. Hemapriyah, B. Kavya Srinidhi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Estimating accurate 3D human pose from monocular RGB video remains a challenging task due to depth ambiguity, self-occlusion, and temporal instability across consecutive frames. While recent monocular pose estimation methods achieve reasonable per-frame accuracy, many approaches lack explicit structural constraints and robust temporal modeling, leading to anatomically inconsistent poses and motion jitter in dynamic sequences. To address these limitations, the paper proposes a temporal consistency-driven framework for monocular 3D human pose estimation that integrates structural skeleton representation with motion-aware temporal modeling. The proposed framework consists of three key components, skeleton topology construction to preserve joint connectivity and anatomical structure, body-aligned coordinate transformation to decouple global body orientation from local joint articulation, and temporal motion modeling to capture limb-wise dynamics and ensure smooth pose transitions across frames. The framework enables stable and structurally coherent 3D pose reconstruction from video sequences. The results highlight the effectiveness of combining structural constraints with temporal motion analysis for robust and reliable 3D human pose estimation from monocular video data.

title: Broad Learning Control for Uncertain Strictfeedback Systems with Asymmetric Full-State Constraints
authors: Youzhi Dong, Donghao Zhang, Linghuan Kong, Wei He
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: In the paper, a novel adaptive control scheme based on broad learning system (BLS) is developed for uncertain strictfeedback systems with fixed-time convergence under the requirement of asymmetric full-state constraints. Compared with existing works, a great difference is that the boundary of virtual control in the paper is known a prior and furthermore can be adjusted by changing controller gains. Moreover, a barrier function is used to guarantee the asymmetric full-state constraints under the fixed-time convergence, and the considered constraint is time-varying. Then, a broad learning control scheme is proposed, which not only eliminates the effects of unknown dynamics, but also ensure that system states converge to a neighborhood of zero in a certain time interval. With Lyapunov stability theory, it is proved that all the state variables are semi-globally uniformly ultimately bounded (SGUUB) with a fixed-time convergence. A simulation example is used to validate the effectiveness of the proposed schemes.

title: TCN-DA1: A Dual Attention Enhanced Temporal Convolutional Network for Inertial Sensor-driven Pet Behavior Recognition
authors: Chaoyuheng Yang, Zhiwei Li, Yuan Xi, Hongshan Liu, Juan Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: In recent years, the pet smart device market has been expanding, and pet behavior recognition based on inertial sensors has become a research focus. Traditional machine learning methods have limitations in extracting temporal features and are unable to fully capture the long-term dependencies of pet behaviors, leading to poor differentiation of static behaviors. To address this, we propose an improved Temporal Convolutional Network model (TCN-DA1), which builds on the Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) and incorporates a dual attention mechanism, consisting of channel attention and temporal attention. The channel attention module (SENet) optimizes the expression ability of six-axis inertial sensor signals (three-axis accelerometer and three-axis gyroscope) through adaptive channel re-weighting, while the temporal attention module dynamically weighs key behavioral segments to enhance the model’s ability to capture complex behavioral patterns. Experiments conducted on the publicly available dog behavior dataset (Movement Sensor Dataset for Dog Behavior Classification) demonstrate that the proposed model significantly outperforms traditional machine learning methods, as well as baseline models such as LSTM and CNN-LSTM, with notable improvements in precision (79.05%), recall (82.88%), and F1-Score (83.11%), especially in the recognition of static behaviors like Sitting and Standing. This provides an efficient technical solution for behavior recognition in pet smart collars.

title: Practical Study on Integrating Knowledge Graph with BOPPPS in Health Statistics Teaching
authors: Dandan Zhang, Xiangwen Li, Di Wu, Li Song, Na Sun, Qiling Liu, Rongqiang Zhang, Ying Wang
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: To implement the educational reform requirement of integrating large-scale education with personalized cultivation through digital empowerment, and to address the teaching dilemma that Health Statistics is characterized by abstract content and that traditional teaching models fail to cater to students' personalized learning needs, this study constructed a Knowledge Graph for the Health Statistics course and deeply integrated it into all modules of the BOPPPS model. Targeted empowerment was provided in each teaching module to enhance learning flexibility and students' learning initiative, thus realizing the integrated synergy of the two approaches. Taking undergraduate students majoring in Preventive Medicine at Shaanxi University of Chinese Medicine as the research subjects, this study conducted a controlled teaching practice comparing the integrated Knowledge Graph-BOPPPS model with the traditional BOPPPS model. The results demonstrate that the integrated model achieves significantly superior teaching effects and leads to higher student satisfaction in terms of improving students' learning initiative, stimulating their learning interest, and enhancing autonomous learning ability, while showing no notable advantages in fostering teamwork ability and broadening students' thinking horizons. The research findings provide a clear direction for the construction of Knowledge Graphs and the exploration of AI-assisted teaching in Health Statistics and related courses.

title: Neural-Network-Free Face Recognition on STM32 using a Lightweight Embedded Vision Framework
authors: Ved Khandeparkar, M. Sujith Khanna, M. Mathan Kumar, R. Vinoth Raj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: In response to the problems of high computational cost, large memory footprint, and high-power consumption of deep learning models in the era of Edge AI, this paper designs and implements a lightweight facial recognition system based on the STM32 microcontroller (NUCLEO-F334R8). The STM32 is used as the core control center, combined with the OV7670 camera module for image acquisition. To overcome the resource constraints of embedded systems, the system utilizes a pipeline of Principal Component Analysis (PCA) for dimensionality reduction and a Linear Support Vector Machine (SVM) for classification, replacing traditional Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN). The Labeled Faces in the Wild (LFW) dataset was optimized and balanced to train the model. Experimental tests show that the system achieves a projected accuracy of 80.29 % for limited person recognition, realizing the automatic identification of individuals with good real-time performance and low memory usage, which meets the design requirements of embedded security applications.

title: Rolling Bearing Fault Diagnosis Based on a Bayesian-Optimized CNN-Transformer Hybrid Network
authors: Boyuan Du, Yanyu Pan, Long Liu, Weiguo Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: To overcome the insufficient integration of local and global features in rolling bearing fault diagnosis, as well as the reliance on empirical experience in setting activation function hyperparameters, this paper proposes a Bayesian-optimizationbased CNN-Transformer hybrid fault diagnosis method (BO-CT). The proposed approach develops a hybrid architecture that combines CNN-driven local feature extraction with Transformerbased global temporal modeling, while a Bayesian optimization strategy, using a Gaussian process surrogate model, is employed to automatically determine the negative slope parameter <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha$</tex> of the LeakyReLU activation function. Experimental results on the CWRU dataset indicate that, in a ten-class classification task, the BO-CT model achieves markedly superior diagnostic accuracy and feature clustering performance compared with existing baseline models. These findings confirm that adaptive hyperparameter optimization effectively enhances the performance of hybrid deep learning networks.

title: Stop Treating At-Risk Student Prediction as a Pure Classification Problem
authors: Fatma Ben Said, Yessine Hadj Kacem
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Predicting which students are at risk of academic failure has become a common goal in educational data analysis. Many existing approaches rely on historical grades and attendance records to classify students as at-risk or not, aiming mainly for high predictive accuracy. However, this approach oversimplifies the complexities of learning and the challenges educators face. Academic risk is not a fixed label but a dynamic condition influenced by student progress, local educational context, and institutional factors. Treating it solely as a classification problem can limit the usefulness of predictive models and may inadvertently contribute to ineffective interventions or student stigmatization. In this work, we argue that at-risk student prediction should focus on guiding actionable decisions rather than achieving maximum accuracy. We discuss the shortcomings of current classification-based models and suggest a shift toward approaches that emphasize intervention strategies, dynamic risk assessment, and contextual awareness. Our perspective aims to encourage the development of predictive systems that more directly support meaningful educational outcomes and practical decision-making.

title: Integrating Node Embeddings and Biomedical Features via Graph Fusion for Disease–Disease Link Prediction
authors: Ghada Kahlaoui, Neila Rjaibi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Predicting disease–disease links is essential for understanding comorbidity patterns and uncovering novel biomedical associations. Traditional similarity-based or machine learning approaches typically rely on either graph structure or handcrafted features, but rarely exploit both sources of information jointly. In this work, we propose a hybrid graph fusion framework that integrates structural node embeddings (Node2Vec) with biomedical disease attributes using a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN). This fusion mechanism learns enriched node representations that simultaneously encode biological similarity and network connectivity. Edge embeddings are then constructed using the Hadamard operator and used to train several machine learning classifiers for disease–disease link prediction. Experiments conducted on a real disease–disease network from the SNAP repository, enriched with disease attributes from DoMiner, indicate that the fused embeddings successfully capture both structural and biomedical information required for accurate link prediction. Among the evaluated models, Random Forest achieved the highest overall performance, obtaining a ROC-AUC of 99.95%.

title: Large Language Model Empowered International Education Resource Sharing and Personalized Recommendation for Graduate Students
authors: Jie Zuo, Quan Liu, Wenjun Xu, Wei Meng
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: With the rapid advancement and widespread deployment of large language models (LLMs) and related artificial intelligence technologies, internationalization of higher education faces unprecedented opportunities and challenges. This paper addresses practical problems encountered in the internationalized training of postgraduate students in electronic and information engineering, such as fragmentation of teaching and research resources, inefficiencies in cross-cultural collaboration, and coarse-grained approaches to individualized training. Guided by constructivist learning theory and long-tail theory, we propose a three-layer LLM-empowered platform architecture, i.e. data layer-algorithm layer and application layer, for integrating and sharing international teaching-research resources. A GNN-based entity-relation extraction method is used to construct a domain knowledge graph, and an intelligent recommendation algorithm is introduced that implements a three-step mechanism: multi-source data fusion, personalized learning-path planning, and dynamic path adjustment. Experimental evaluation indicates that the platform achieves professional domain knowledge coverage and professional terminology comprehension accuracy both exceeding 95%. The platform therefore has strong potential to improve postgraduate students’ international academic competitiveness, cross-cultural collaborative ability, and practical AI skillset, and offers a transferable solution for cultivating globally competitive, high-caliber innovative talents.

title: ThermGNN: Physics-Informed Graph Neural Networks with Spatiotemporal Attention for Vehicle Thermal Management Prediction
authors: Bo Feng, Zichen Xu, Ya Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Vehicle electrification demands precise thermal management to ensure battery safety, extend component lifespan, and optimize energy efficiency under dynamic driving conditions. However, existing approaches face three critical limitations: (1) computational fluid dynamics simulations are prohibitively expensive for real-time deployment, requiring hours to model transient thermal events, (2) lumped-parameter models oversimplify multi-component thermal coupling, failing to capture complex heat transfer pathways across battery packs, motors, and cooling circuits, and (3) data-driven methods ignore fundamental thermodynamic principles, producing physically implausible predictions during extreme operating conditions. We propose ThermGNN, a physics-informed graph neural network framework that synergistically integrates domain knowledge with deep learning for spatiotemporal temperature prediction. Our approach introduces three key innovations: (1) a thermal graph construction module that encodes conduction, convection, and radiation mechanisms through learnable physics-guided adjacency matrices, (2) a spatiotemporal attention mechanism that explicitly models heat transfer pathways and thermal inertia effects with component-specific time constants, and (3) thermodynamic regularization constraints ensuring energy conservation and symmetric heat exchange throughout training. Extensive experiments on real-world electric vehicle datasets demonstrate that ThermGNN achieves 0.845°C RMSE, outperforming LSTM and Transformer baselines by 46.2% and 28.9% respectively, while maintaining physical consistency across diverse thermal scenarios including cold start, acceleration, and regenerative braking cycles.

title: A Hybrid Framework for Faradaic Processes Simulation at Electrochemical Interfaces
authors: Haoyu Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: The accurate atomistic modeling of pseudocapacitive materials is a grand challenge, hindered by the immense computational cost of quantum mechanical methods required to describe Faradaic charge transfer. While DFT-based molecular dynamics (DFT-MD) provides the necessary quantum accuracy, its application is limited to small systems and short timescales, precluding the study of realistic electrode interfaces. To bridge this critical gap, we introduce ReaQ-ML (Reactive Quantum / Machine Learning), a novel hybrid simulation framework. ReaQ-ML partitions the system into a quantum mechanical (QM) region, encompassing the active site of a Faradaic reaction, and a machine learning (ML) region for the remainder of the polarizable electrode. This approach extends recent developments in ML electrode models for non-Faradaic systems by explicitly incorporating electron transfer between the electrode and electrolyte. The ML component, trained on high-fidelity DFT data, learns to reproduce the complex, non-local electronic response of the entire electrode to a local reactive event at a fraction of the cost of a full QM calculation. This enables quantumaccurate simulations of charge storage mechanisms, such as proton adsorption on MXenes or redox reactions on transition metal oxides, at unprecedented scales.

title: Design and Practice of Intelligent Manufacturing Training Mode Based on "VR + Digital Twin"
authors: Jianhua Cao, Zhigang Jiang, Xiang Liu, Xuhui Xia
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Addressing the pain points of high equipment costs, significant safety risks, and weak virtual-real interaction in traditional intelligent manufacturing practical teaching, a practical teaching model for intelligent manufacturing that integrates virtual reality (VR) and digital twin technology is proposed. A "three-tier, five-dimensional" practical teaching framework has been established, which achieves immersive, repeatable, and low-risk practical teaching of the entire process of intelligent manufacturing through real-time mapping and interaction between high-fidelity virtual simulation environments and physical entity data. Through teaching practice cases in intelligent manufacturing engineering, the effectiveness of this model in enhancing students’ practical skills, innovative thinking, and ability to solve complex engineering problems has been verified, providing an operable path for cultivating intelligent manufacturing talents in the new era.

title: Hierarchical segmentation method of substation equipment point cloud for identification of live equipment
authors: Guanhua Sun, Zheng Shen, Yongsheng Liang, Qi Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: With the development of digital intelligence of power grid, in view of the urgent need of automatic segmentation of three-dimensional point cloud in substation under the background of digital twin power grid construction, the processing of these data has become the key to realize digital management and intelligent diagnosis of equipment. The segmentation of power equipment based on three-dimensional point cloud model can provide basic information for intelligent inspection planning of substation. A hierarchical point cloud segmentation method is proposed to solve the problem that the traditional segmentation method is not accurate because of the complex structure of substation point cloud, tight connection of equipment and massive noise. This method adopts the processing framework of "from coarse to fine, step by step". Firstly, the dimension of the passed data is reduced; Then, the macro-regional division is completed by combining elevation stratification; Finally, based on the detection of abrupt curvature change of boundary point cloud, the fine separation of connected devices is realized. The experimental results show that this method is significantly superior to the traditional Euclidean clustering and region growing methods in accuracy, intersection ratio and other evaluation indexes, which verifies its effectiveness and superiority in realizing high-precision and automatic equipment segmentation in complex scenes of substations, and provides reliable technical support for digital modeling and intelligent inspection of substations.

title: ResNet-YOLO-SLAM: An Integrated Deep Learning Framework for Autonomous Hospital Navigation
authors: Zhi Cheng, Xinyu Pang, Jiaxin Deng, Yuanyuan Xu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: This paper presents an intelligent medicine delivery robot system integrating advanced computer vision algorithms for autonomous hospital navigation. We propose a hierarchical ROS2-based framework combining ResNet18 for deep learning-driven line following, YOLOv5 for real-time obstacle detection, and depth-enhanced visual SLAM for robust localization. The system leverages the RDKX3 AI accelerator to achieve 28 FPS ResNet18 inference and 45 ms YOLOv5 detection, enabling <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$87 \pm 12ms$</tex> end-to-end control latency. Deployed on an Ackermann chassis with RGB-D sensors, our approach demonstrates superior performance in hospital corridor environments with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.8 \pm 2.1cm$</tex> path deviation, zero collision incidents across 20 autonomous trials, and 16.7% improved energy efficiency compared to LiDAR-based systems. Comprehensive benchmarking in laboratory-simulated hospital corridors against traditional CV, YOLOv3, MobileNetV2, and LiDAR-SLAM baselines validates the technical feasibility of our integrated deep learning pipeline as a proof-of-concept for medical logistics automation, pending clinical validation and medical device certification.

title: Ambient Parametric Test Reduction in Post-Silicon Production Testing via Temperature-Dependent Modelling: Three Approaches and a Case Study
authors: Bianca Carbunescu-Stoenescu, Emilian David, Mihai Popovici, Valentina Davidoiu, Marina Topa, Andi Buzo, Georg Pelz
publish: 2026 IEEE 29th International Symposium on Design and Diagnostics of Electronic Circuits and Systems (DDECS)
abstract: The post-silicon integrated circuits (IC) testing is a very expensive process, especially in automotive industry where chip functionality has to be guaranteed by verifying multiple electrical parameters over a wide range of operating conditions. However, some costs can be avoided by reducing the amount of redundant tests, as certain parameters may exhibit a predictable behavior on the operating conditions variation. This paper presents a comparison between three approaches that use temperature parameters behavior for reducing the number of IC tests. The methods are tested on a production dataset consisting of 25 parameters and 300000 chips, results showing that the best method saves approximately 12 % of the measurements.

title: Research and Application of Wiring Matrix Technology in Factory Testing of Hualong Unit Nuclear Power DCS Control Systems
authors: Deshan Kong, Yongxue Qie, You Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: The nuclear power DCS (Distributed Control System) comprises numerous functional subsystems, each of which is designed with a large number of input/output (I/O) interfaces for interaction between field devices and systems in a nuclear power plant. If only traditional manual methods are used for I/O channel interface verification, it will not only result in low testing efficiency but also introduce human errors during the testing process.Through systematic analysis and scientific classification of interface types between field devices and systems in the nuclear power DCS, combined with an in-depth evaluation of existing manual testing methods, this study reveals that the current verification process involves nearly 30,000 variable points. Relying solely on manual or existing semi-automated single-point testing methods leads to insufficient efficiency, especially in the context of the current batch construction of nuclear power plants, where such inefficient testing approaches can no longer meet the required construction timelines. To address these challenges, this study innovatively proposes a new method for nuclear power DCS interface verification. By introducing a wiring matrix technology, this method enables integrated batch testing with a single wiring setup for interface variables. Practical application results demonstrate that the wiring matrix technology significantly improves testing efficiency (approximately 60 % faster than traditional methods) while reducing human error rates to below 0.5 %. Additionally, this method exhibits strong portability, allowing for technical reuse across different nuclear power projects, thereby effectively shortening the factory commissioning cycle of nuclear power DCS systems (by an average of 30 %). This technological breakthrough provides crucial support for the batch construction of nuclear power plants.

title: Framing the Fringe: Dynamics of Ingroup and Outgroup Narratives in Fringe Telegram Channels
authors: Upasana Dutta, Yasmine Houri

title: Quantifying Media Representation Dynamics Across 25 Years of News Reporting on Policing-related Deaths
authors: Farhan Samir, Jappun Dhillon, Meghna Ravikumar, Syed Ishtiaque Ahmed, Vered Shwartz
publish: Proceedings of the 18th ACM Web Science Conference 2026
abstract: None

title: Intelligent Correlation of Indicators of Compromise Using AI for Proactive Threat Detection
authors: Orieb Abualghanam, Hadeel Alazzam, Malik Al-Essa, Wesam Almobaideen, Mohammad Qatawneh
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: The increasing complexity of cyber threats, characterized by sophisticated multi-stage attacks, exposes a fundamental weakness that conventional Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) systems are suffering from in term of their reliance on reactive, and isolated alerts. This limitation increases false positives alerts, leads to operational fatigue for analysts, and delays in identifying genuine threats. Proactive defense in SIEM can benefit from the integration of external Threat Intelligence (TI) in providing essential context. Indicator of Compromise (IoC) repositories can be used to support this via enabling the correlation of internal security events with data related to global threats. In this paper, a systematic methodology for integrating multi-source Threat Intelligence into SIEM platforms is proposed. The methodology implementation is done via a custom IoC repository within the Splunk SIEM environment, designed to facilitate the ingestion, normalization, and contextual correlation of external threat data. Additionally, correlation rules are developed using an autoencoder-based technique to synthesize this external intelligence with internal telemetry from diverse log sources and to identify complex attack patterns. An empirical evaluation of the proposed system is conducted using three distinct log sources: Windows Event Logs, Firewall Logs, and Web Server Logs. Results indicate a significant improvement in detection accuracy across all evaluated datasets when compared to traditional, intelligence-agnostic correlation. These results demonstrate that the strategic integration of TI substantially reduces alert noise and enhances the detection of novel, multi- vector attacks, thereby advancing SIEM functionality from basic log aggregation to contextual, proactive threat intelligence.

title: KasUMKM: Mobile Application Development Emphasizing Sustainable and Cost-Effective Software Approach for Food and Beverage MSMEs
authors: Aghna Jaya Pranada, Feren Lisady, Vanessa Carlinsia Tjong, Anderies
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSME) is a small-scale business that plays a vital role in Indonesia's economy, employing 97% of the workforce and contributing more than half to the national Gross Domestic Product (GDP) the authors conducted survey that indicates 41.9% MSMEs perform manual transaction record and 67.7% of MSMEs have difficult managing their financial records, which lead to inaccurate financial data and impacting MSMEs growth and limiting their competitiveness. Previous research has introduced several MSMEs application, however most of them required stability in internet connection and provide exorbitant subscription fees for MSMEs. This study develops mobile applications for MSMEs called KasUMKM, that provides cost-effective methodology supporting offline and on demand online synchronization through popular Backend-as-a-service platform. This system was developed using a reactive framework and a widely utilized offline Database Management System. The application's performance and usability were tested through load testing resulting in 394.66 MB of RAM usage, 28.86% of CPU usage, and 35,013 edge function executed, stress testing which achieves 99.97% success rate, and user questionnaires involving food and beverage MSMEs with results showing positive feedback regarding its effectiveness and simplicity. In comparison with existing research, KasUMKM introduces a distinctive contribution as a hybrid mobile application and cost-effective application designed for MSMEs with limited financial resources.

title: Positional Segmentor-Guided Counterfactual Fine-Tuning for Spatially Localized Image Synthesis
authors: Tian Xia, Matthew Sinclair, Andreas Schuh, Fabio De Sousa Ribeiro, Raghav Mehta, Rajat Rasal, Esther Puyol-Antón, Samuel Gerber, Kersten Petersen, Michiel Schaap, Ben Glocker
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Counterfactual image generation enables controlled data augmentation, bias mitigation, and disease modeling. However, existing methods guided by external classifiers or regressors are limited to subject-level factors (e.g., age) and fail to produce localized structural changes, often resulting in global artifacts. Pixel-level guidance using segmentation masks has been explored but requires user-defined counterfactual masks, which is tedious and impractical. Segmentor-guided Counterfactual Fine-Tuning (Seg-CFT) addressed this by using segmentation-derived measurements to supervise structurespecific variables, yet it remains restricted to global interventions. We propose Positional Seg-CFT, which subdivides each structure into regional segments and derives independent measurements per region, enabling spatially localized and anatomically coherent counterfactuals. Experiments on coronary CT angiography (CCTA) show that Pos-Seg-CFT generates realistic, region-specific modifications, providing finer spatial control for modeling disease progression.

title: A 3.3V to 200V Hybrid Series-Parallel Switched Capacitor Integrated Circuit for Dielectric Actuators with 94.2% Peak Efficiency
authors: Bahlakoana Mabetha, Yanqiao Li, Jason T. Stauth
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents a step-up hybrid series-parallel DC-DC converter for soft-charging dominantly capacitive dielectric actuators such as piezoelectric actuators, dielectric elastomer actuators, and ultrasound transducers. The hybrid switched capacitor (SC) converter includes a buck-boost stage which soft charges (and discharges) both the SC network and the capacitive load. The prototype is based on a high-voltage 180nm SOI CMOS integrated circuit (IC) that achieves 200V output voltage from a 3.3V input voltage supply with 94.2% peak reactive power efficiency and delivers up to 250mW peak reactive power. This is the first dielectric actuator driver IC to demonstrate full hybrid soft charging and discharging, enabling higher efficiency for a given passive component area, weight, and cost.

title: Multi-Agent Deep Q-Networks (MADQN) for Adaptive Self-Healing and Orchestration of Cloud-Native Network Functions
authors: Karthi Palanisamy
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: The continuously increasing complexity of cloud-native network functions (CNFs) demands the application of sophisticated orchestration approaches in order to guarantee effective deployment, resilience, and high availability. Neural networks and reinforcement learning, as types of deep learning (DL), are increasingly employed to solve the dynamic problems of CNF deployment, management, and recovery. To achieve the proposal of this paper, a Multi-Agent Deep Q-Networks (MADQN) model is proposed to facilitate the adaptive self-healing of CNFs in cloud-native environments. With the help of MADQN, the framework allows network function agents to automatically anticipate and respond to failures and coordinate corrective actions, including scaling, migration, and fault recovery in the network. This will guarantee the smoothness of service provision and make the system more reliable. Moreover, a dependency-sensitive package-orchestration engine is incorporated that enables the sequential deployment and coordination of dependency CNFs to reduce deployment latency and limit the deployment to misordered dependencies. Evaluation results demonstrate that the application of MADQN improves system performance, enhancing fault tolerance and resource optimization by 27% compared to existing methods. The proposed approach improves the reliability of CNF deployments and optimizes resource usage while maintaining service quality, making it a promising solution for future cloud-native networks.

title: Fault Classification in Power Transmission Lines Using Entropy-Aided Temporal Windowing and Hybrid Distance Measures
authors: Nirmalya Mallick, Chandan Kumar Shiva, Vedik Basetti, Sachidananda Sen, V. Mukherjee, Shriram S. Rangarajan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: The present power system requires a fault detection system which can identify faults with both speed and accuracy to maintain system stability while avoiding equipment damage. The research studies a fault classification method which uses entropy to classify faults through its application of optimized wavelet transforms and hybrid distance measurement techniques. The method uses temporal windowing to extract important fault data through its implementation. The adaptive wavelet selection tool delivers its most suitable frequency output. The development of hybrid distance classification framework enables automatic parameter optimization to function as its matching component. The test-bed achieved exceptional results with 95% total accuracy and perfect area-under-curve results and F1-scores which ranged from 0.80 to 1.00.

title: SQET-MOE: Two-Stage Brain Age Estimation using Squeeze-and-Excitation Transformer and Mixture-of-Experts
authors: Rizuki Oura, Koichi Ito, Takafumi Aoki
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Brain age estimation is crucial for identifying brain disorders and developing biomarkers. While deep learning methods have been proposed for high-accuracy age estimation from T1-weighted images, further improvements are still needed. This paper proposes a novel brain age estimation method called SQET-MoE, utilizing a two-stage estimation framework. In the 1st stage, the Squeeze-and-Excitation Transformer (SQET), which fuses the benefits of CNNs and Transformers, performs coarse age estimation and feature extraction. The 2nd stage employs a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) module, which uses SQET's output to estimate and correct the residual associated with systematic errors and complex non-linearity. This task division stabilizes training and maximizes estimation accuracy. Through a set of experiments using large-scale datasets, we demonstrate that the proposed SQET-MoE achieves the highest estimation accuracy compared to conventional methods.

title: Immersive 3D Virtual Laboratory with an AI-Powered Pedagogical Avatar for Remote Practical Learning
authors: Abdelali El Gourari, Mustapha Ezzini, Mohammed Ennejjar, Mustapha Raoufi, Mohammed Skouri
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: Remote practical education faces significant challenges in replicating hands-on laboratory experiences. This paper presents a novel immersive 3D virtual laboratory integrated with an AI-powered pedagogical avatar for electronics education. The system combines a web-based architecture (Next.js) with an interactive Three.js environment featuring realistic component manipulation on a virtual breadboard. A Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) avatar provides contextual assistance using a specialized electrical engineering knowledge base. Preliminary results demonstrate significant improvements in student engagement and comprehension compared to traditional remote learning approaches.

title: Design of Low-Power, High-Performance Hybrid Full Adder Circuit
authors: K J Khavin, V Lalith Vishnu, T Ravi, Bhuvana B P, Yokesh V
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Applications based Hybrid logic styles in low-power VLSI design. Hybrid logic styles have received considerable attention in system design because they provide the benefits of more than one circuit design technique. The hybrid full adder presented in this work is a hybrid design based on the Pass Transistor Logic (PTL) and complementary CMOS design in 90 nm CMOS technology. The suggested architecture is created based on an effective XOR-XNOR module, which is used as the main component of the sum and carry generation circuits. To improve the output voltages, two design variants are used, namely, a 13-transistor (13T) hybrid adder without swing restoration and a 15-transistor (15T) hybrid adder with swing restoration. The operation of the proposed adders is assessed in terms of average power analysis of the operating state when working at low power and in different operating frequencies. Simulation findings indicate that the 13 T design will be less power, its transistor count was very low and the 15 T design is better in signal integrity with full the swing outputs with a small power increment. The given hybrid adder provides a good compromise between power consumption and voltage swing, which allows its use in the low power and high-speed arithmetic of deep submicron VLSI based systems.

title: Nonlinear Modeling and Linearization of a Motor-Driven Aircraft Arrestment System: Theory, Structure, and Experimental Validation
authors: Yacine Boudria, Raymond B. Sepe, Steven P. Bastien, Musa Jouaneh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Intelligent Design and Control of Automation and Drive Systems (IDCD)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive, control-oriented nonlinear physics-based model of a motor-driven aircraft arrestment system. The arrestment system is represented as a set of tightly coupled mechanical, electrical, and geometric subsystems, including the aircraft, arresting cables, shock absorbers, spools, shafts, brakes, and electric motors, each described by explicit dynamic equations. The model explicitly captures dominant arrestment nonlinearities, including asymmetric cable forces, time-varying spool radius, inertia variation due to cable payout, nonlinear spring–damper behavior in the shock absorbers, frictional effects, actuator dynamics, and constraints arising from limited sensor availability. A physically motivated aircraft-on-deck dynamic model is formulated to describe coupled longitudinal and lateral motion during arrestment in the absence of direct aircraft state measurements, enabling force and energy transmission to be modeled consistently. The resulting nonlinear plant is systematically linearized about multiple operating points distributed along the arrestment trajectory, yielding a family of linear time-varying models suitable for analysis and synthesis. A subscale experimental arrestment system is used to validate the modeling framework. Comparisons between simulated and experimental responses show that the model reproduces the dominant arrestment dynamics, including cable tension evolution, torque transmission, and vehicle deceleration, establishing a reliable modeling foundation for subsequent control-oriented studies.

title: An Intelligent Machine Learning Framework for Ddos Attack Detection and Prediction
authors: X. Arogya Presskila, S. Bangaru Setty, N. Vamsi Krishna
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: The rapid evolution of digital technologies has now made DDoS attacks a critical worry for network security, which in turn has a negative impact on the availability of significant online services. The common limitations in conventional detection methods include high rates of false-positive results, and slow reactions, mainly because of continuously evolving attack patterns. To find solutions to these problems, the paper provides AMPETR++ (Adaptive Multi-Phase Ensemble with Temporal Reinforcement) - a novel hybrid model which is used to detect and classify DDoS attacks using machine learning. The suggested framework works in three unique stages, namely, Statistical Analysis, Frequency- Oriented Feature Extraction, and Behavioral Profiling, which allows the behavior of network traffic to be represented more comprehensively. Efforts to include Random Forest, XGBoost, and LSTM models into a composite model under a dynamic weighting approach results in AMPETR++ improving detection accuracy and consistency. Moreover, there is a Temporal Reinforcement Learning Mechanism that optimizes the predictions of the models in accordance to the previous temporal patterns to enhance flexibility with time. It is experimentally proven that AMPETR++ has a high accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score than current methods on benchmark datasets. Its success in changing according to changing attack patterns makes it a strong solution to the problem of real-time DDoS detection and prediction in cybersecurity solutions, which is proven by its results.

title: A Multimodal Slice Discovery Framework for Systematic Failure Detection and Explanation in Medical Image Classification
authors: Yixuan Liu, Kanwal K. Bhatia, Ahmed E. Fetit
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Despite advances in machine learning-based medical image classifiers, the safety and reliability of these systems remain major concerns in practical settings. Existing auditing approaches mainly rely on unimodal features or metadata-based subgroup analyses, which are limited in interpretability and often fail to capture hidden systematic failures. To address these limitations, we introduce the first automated auditing framework that extends slice discovery methods to multimodal representations specifically for medical applications. Comprehensive experiments were conducted under common failure scenarios using the MIMIC-CXR-JPG dataset, demonstrating the framework's strong capability in both failure discovery and explanation generation. Our results also show that multimodal information generally allows more comprehensive and effective auditing of classifiers, while unimodal variants beyond image-only inputs exhibit strong potential in scenarios where resources are constrained.

title: An Optimized Graph Neural Network Framework Using Whale Optimization Algorithm for Patient Medication Cost Prediction
authors: Dhinesh Kumar K.S, R. Annamalai
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Accurate prediction of patient medication costs is important for healthcare budgeting, insurance risk assessment, and personalized treatment planning. Traditional machine learning models often fail to capture the complex relational dependencies amongst patients, diseases, prescriptions, and healthcare providers, which can lead to inaccurate predictions of medication costs and hinder effective healthcare decisionmaking. To address this limitation, this paper proposes an optimization-driven Graph Neural Network (GNN) framework improved with the Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA) for accurate medication cost forecasting. The proposed model represents healthcare data as a heterogeneous graph, where nodes correspond to patients, medications, diagnoses, and healthcare providers, while edges signify their interactions and prescription relationships. The GNN learns structural and contextual dependencies through message-passing mechanisms, allowing accurate representation of multi-relational healthcare patterns. WOA is used to modify important hyperparameters such as the learning rate, embedding dimensions, number of graph convolution layers, and regularization coefficients to improve prediction accuracy. WOA's global search capacity helps to avoid local minima and progresses convergence stability. In terms of RMSE, MAE, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> metrics, results on real-world healthcare spending datasets show that WOA-optimized GNN performs better than conventional regression models and nonoptimized graph-based models. All things considered, the suggested approach offers a scalable and clever way to support data-driven financial decision-making in clinical settings and economical healthcare planning.

title: Synthetic Data Generation for Deep Learning-Based Subpart Detection in Robotics
authors: Daniele Caldana, David José Castro, Rui Coimbra, Vítor Filipe, Manuel F. Silva, João P. C. De Souza
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Autonomous Robot Systems and Competitions (ICARSC)
abstract: Synthetic data has become a crucial enabler for training deep learning models in robotic perception tasks. This work presents a configurable synthetic data generation pipeline, built on BlenderProc, that leverages CAD model annotations to produce part-based labels directly at the geometry level. The proposed approach enables the automatic generation of Oriented Bounding Box (OBB) annotations and supports the definition of multiple regions of interest through a vertex-level annotation tool. The pipeline is validated in an warehouse environment use case involving a storage container for automotive parts for autonomous forklift operations. Experimental results show that YOLO11m-Oriented Bounding Box model trained exclusively on synthetic data achieve strong performance on real-world images, reaching up to 0.768 mAP@0.5:0.95. Furthermore, increasing synthetic data diversity consistently improves detection performance, and hybrid training with a small amount of real data provides an effective trade-off between annotation effort and accuracy.

title: Image Quality Assessment of Imputation-Induced Biomarker Distortions in Multiplex Immunofluorescence
authors: Priyanka Rana, Xingnan Li, Tuba N. Gide, Nurudeen A. Adegoke, Yizhe Mao, James S. Wilmott, Sidong Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Stain imputation in multiplex immunofluorescence (mIF) aims to reconstruct missing biomarkers but can inadvertently distort inter-marker dependencies essential for biological interpretation, leading to misleading downstream analyses. Conventional metrics such as Structural Similarity Index Measure or Mean Absolute Error quantify pixel-level fidelity but do not ensure the preservation of biologically meaningful relationships. We present a biologically informed image quality assessment (IQA) framework that assesses imputation-induced distortions in inter-marker relationships. Images with imputed CD8 generated by OS2CR-Diff and Stain Imputation in Multiplex Immunofluorescence Imaging (SIMIF) are labelled according to changes in CD8-CD16 colocalisation (inflated, deflated, or preserved states), and a deep learning model is trained to identify these states directly from the imputed images, enabling quality control of stain-imputation outputs. To address class imbalance in the curated dataset, we introduce a Correlation-Aware Augmentation (CAA) approach that perturbs CD8 spatial distributions relative to CD16, simulating imputation-relevant inflation and deflation patterns. CAA consistently improved classification performance (AUC and macro-F1) across both datasets, outperforming conventional augmentation approaches.

title: A Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning Based Framework for Solving Complex Vehicle Routing Problems
authors: Jicong Zhou, Yunfan Wang, Zhengjun Wang, Yachao Li, Minghui Sha
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: As a pivotal challenge in logistics distribution, the Vehicle Routing Problems (VRPs) critically impacts operational efficiency. Traditional optimization algorithms suffer from efficiency degradation with increasing node scales, while existing data-driven approaches demonstrate limited generalization ability in multi-objective optimization scenarios with complex constraints. This article proposes an innovative hierarchical reinforcement learning framework for efficient VRP resolution: First, the problem is decoupled into constraint/objective planning and route optimization phases. The upper layer employs heuristic algorithms or mathematical solvers to generate constraint-satisfying candidate solutions, while the lower layer utilizes pointer network architecture for optimal sub-problem routing. A feedback mechanism integrating Q-Learning/REINFORCE algorithms dynamically optimizes upper-layer strategies based on lower-layer solutions. Experimental results demonstrate that compared with mainstream deep learning methods, this framework achieves superior solution quality across diverse complex VRP scenarios, exhibiting exceptional generalization ability.

title: A Medium-Voltage Bidirectional Isolated DC Transformer Using 3.3 kV SiC Devices
authors: Junjie Li, Hongyu Lin, Peng Jiang, Hao Feng, Li Ran
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: As DC distribution network attracts more attention for its ability to efficiently and reliably integrate distributed generation systems, bidirectional isolated DC transformers are recognized as a critical solution for interconnecting low-voltage (LV) DC and medium-voltage (MV) DC. To reduce cost, it is important to minimize such DC transformers’ volume and weight. SiC MOSFETs with higher voltage ratings readily achieve this objective by reducing the number of series-connected devices or modules. This paper presents a 3.3 kV SiC MOSFETs-based neutral-point-clamped (NPC) type dual-active-bridge (DAB) converter. Key design aspects of NPC inverter, including the main power loop, isolated gate driver, and medium frequency (MF) transformer are elaborated, with details on thermal management and insulation challenges. A back-to-back test with an LLC converter achieves 900 V to 3 kV conversion. At 26 kW cycling power, the efficiency of DAB converter is estimated at 98.7%.

title: Design and Validation of a 1MHz Double-pulse Test (DPT) Methodology for 900V SiC MOSFET
authors: Jinyuan Hu, Jungwon Choi
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents an advanced double-pulse test (DPT) methodology for accurate dynamic characterization of 900 V SiC MOSFETs operating at 1 MHz. While operating in the MHz range enables higher power density, parasitic inductances and resistances introduce severe ringing that distorts waveforms and degrades measurement accuracy, which necessitates MHz-range characterization of SiC MOSFETs using DPT. To overcome these issues, we design the DPT platform for a 900 V SiC MOSFET, including passive components selected based on device ratings and operating conditions, and extract parasitic values using a vector network analyzer to improve LTspice modeling accuracy. The proposed damping approach is additionally validated on a 1200 V SiC MOSFET. A time-domain mathematical analysis of the ohmic-region model is then developed to guide the design of damping resistors for oscillation suppression. Simulation and experimental validation of the design’s practicality and effectiveness, reducing ringing peak magnitude by over 60% and shortening oscillation cycles to one-third.

title: KA-GAT: Kolmogorov-Arnold Decomposition Empowered Graph Attention Networks
authors: Jianfan Chen, Xiaoshuang Sha, Liangyi Gong, Hongtao Li, Zhiwei Yan, Yu-Beng Leau
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have emerged as powerful tools for graph-structured data analysis, yet their efficacy is often hindered by the inherent challenges of high-dimensional feature spaces and complex nonlinear interdependencies. To address these limitations, we propose KA-GAT, a novel graph representation learning framework that synergizes Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) with multi-head graph attention mechanisms. KA-GAT pioneers a dual innovation: (1) leveraging the Kolmogorov-Arnold representation theorem to decompose high-dimensional node features into interpretable univariate functions, thereby reducing feature space complexity while preserving nonlinear relationships; and (2) integrating dynamic multi-head attention to adaptively focus on critical graph substructures, enhancing both representational capacity and model explainability. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets (Cora, Citeseer, Pubmed) demonstrate KA-GAT’s superiority, achieving state-of-the-art accuracy improvements of 7.8% on Cora and 5.8% on Citeseer over baseline models like GraphSAGE. Notably, the framework’s attention visualization reveals interpretable node interaction patterns, aligning with real-world graph semantics.

title: Interactive Power Electronics Education Using Jupyter Notebook and the GSEIM Simulation Package
authors: Nakul Narayanan K, Mahesh B. Patil
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: An innovative way for teaching/learning power electronics using Jupyter Notebooks is presented. The Notebooks are interactive, allowing users to modify circuit parameters and observe their impact on circuit behavior. The GSEIM simulation engine is interfaced with the Notebooks for solving the circuit equations. Simulation examples are discussed to illustrate the capability of the Notebooks in teaching power electronics.

title: A Novel Diagonal Convolutional Network for Industrial Polluting Gas Recognition via E-noses
authors: Changfu Zhang, Jia Yan
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: Electronic nose (e-nose) systems face challenges in achieving high accuracy and robustness when complex volatile compounds are analyzed due to limitations in traditional machine learning methods. To address this, we propose the diagonal convolutional network (DiagCN), a novel deep learning framework that incorporates multidirectional convolution and residual downsampling for enhanced feature extraction from time series of gas sensor data. By converting raw signals into Gramian Angular Field (GAF) images and employing direction-adaptive convolution kernels, DiagCN effectively captures diagonal-dominant patterns while maintaining computational efficiency. The experimental results on an industrial gas dataset demonstrate that DiagCN achieves 97.22% accuracy, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art models. The proposed method offers a lightweight yet high-performance solution for e-nose applications in environmental monitoring.

title: Vehicle Motion Planning with Actor-Critic Learning and Human-Machine Interactive Evaluation
authors: Xincheng Xu, Xinxin Yao, Haotian Cao, Xinglong Zhang
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: Motion planning is pivotal for enabling intelligent vehicles to navigate complex, dynamic scenarios effectively. Reinforcement learning (RL)-based motion planning methods excel but struggle with designing generalized reward functions and adapting to rapidly changing environments. To address these limitations, we propose a hybrid framework combining receding-horizon actor-critic (RHAC) with human-machine interaction evaluation. First, a maximum entropy inverse reinforcement learning (MEIRL) algorithm autonomously extracts reward functions from expert driving trajectories, minimizing subjective biases in manual reward design. Second, a human-machine interaction evaluation mechanism is introduced to incorporate human expert feedback, which refines the reward function by evaluating trajectory quality, thereby improving adaptability to dynamic scenarios where pre-learned rewards may fall short. Finally, the optimized reward function is integrated into the RHAC architecture for online learning of robust motion planning policies. Simulations in dynamic off-road environments demonstrate that this approach significantly outperforms baseline motion planning methods, achieving enhanced adaptability and performance.

title: Is it Possible to Control AI Hallucinations?

title: Boundary Conduction Mode Control Considering Circulating Current for Two-Parallel Interleaved Vienna Rectifier
authors: Joonhyung Kim, Juyeon Lee, June-Seok Lee
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: A boundary conduction mode (BCM) control can reduce both turn-on and turn-off losses by turning switches on at zero current point and lowering the peak values of the phase currents compared to discontinuous conduction mode (DCM) control. However, when applying the BCM control in a two-parallel interleaved Vienna rectifier, the interleaving operation generates circulating current, which alters the slopes of the phase currents. This prevents the single-Vienna BCM control from deriving the proper switching frequency for BCM operation, which leads to phase current distortion and failure in loss optimization. This paper proposes an optimized BCM control for a two-parallel interleaved Vienna rectifier. The proposed BCM control determines the switching frequency by predicting the slope of the phase currents for each converter, taking into account the effect of circulating current. Consequently, the accurate BCM operation is achieved, thereby reducing power losses and input current distortion. The effectiveness of the proposed control is verified through simulation results.

title: AC-powered Dual-Active-Bridge (DAB) EV Charger with a Reduced-Current Stress Converter Using an Adaptive DC Link in a Three-Phase System
authors: Ranjeet Kumar Sah, Shashank Kurm, Shailendra Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: This article presents a converter topology for electric vehicle (EV) charging systems, comprising a three-phase Active Front-End Converter (AFEC) and a Dual Active Bridge (DAB) converter. In conventional configurations, the AFEC maintains a fixed DC link voltage, resulting in a significant voltage difference across the DAB transformer's inductor. This leads to a triangular inductor current waveform with a high RMS value, causing increased conduction losses and reduced system efficiency. In the proposed architecture, the control strategy of the AFEC is enhanced to generate a variable DC link voltage that dynamically adapts to the EV battery’s voltage condition. This ensures the DC link voltage remains sufficiently above the AC input voltage while aligning the voltage levels of the DAB transformer's primary and secondary windings. As a result, a load-dependent DC link is established, effectively reducing the RMS current by approximately 8% to 53%. This reduction minimises current stress on the transformer, lowers conduction losses, and significantly improves overall system efficiency. Simulation results obtained using MATLAB/Simulink validate the effectiveness and performance gains of the proposed EV charging system.

title: Labor Relationship Management Based on Big Data and Perception Algorithm Control
authors: Yao Tang, Hongbing Yi, Lingzi Zhang, Xiuqin Lv
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: In order to ensure the maintenance and development of labor relations and improve recruitment efficiency, an innovative labor relations management method has been developed.This method first collects structured information through big data technology and calculates its similarity.Then, a classification method based on Bert and feature embedding is used to classify positions, and a perception algorithm is used to match positions with job seekers.The experimental results show that this method achieves 85.4% in matching accuracy and 82.0% in F1 Score metric, both of which are higher than other algorithms.The ablation experiment further confirmed the contributions of each component in the model, especially when the bidirectional long short-term memory network Attention, Word2Vec, bidirectional gated recurrent unit, and convolutional neural network module are used in combination to achieve optimal performance.The above results indicate that the proposed method has strong potential in labor relations management, especially in improving the accuracy of job seeker job matching.

title: Life Insurance Risk Classification Using Bi-Model Siamese TabNet Architecture
authors: Jasmin Z.K. Chu, Joel C.M. Than, Colin C.L. Tan, Ngoc Dinh Tan
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: Effective risk assessment is essential in life insurance underwriting, yet existing studies using the Prudential Life Insurance dataset report modest predictive performance and often rely on single-model approaches with limited analysis of feature importance, dimensionality reduction, and class separability. The highly overlapping structure of multi-class insurance data remains insufficiently addressed, leading to suboptimal discrimination across eight risk levels. To overcome these limitations, this study evaluates multiple Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) models for multi-class risk classification, including an advanced bi-model architecture, SiameseTabNet, alongside Random Forest, XGBoost, Deep Neural Networks (DNN), and Artificial Neural Networks (ANN). The dataset is preprocessed using missing value imputation, label encoding, and feature importance-based selection, and both the full feature set (126 features) and reduced subsets (Top 77, Top 20, and Top 10) are systematically examined. Performance is assessed using accuracy, precision, recall, and $\mathbf{F 1}$-score, with comparisons to prior studies. Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and contour plots are further used to analyze class separability. Results show that the proposed bi-model architecture achieves the highest accuracy of 57.12% with the full feature set and consistently outperforms baseline models. Overall, the findings highlight the challenges of life insurance risk classification and show the effectiveness of advanced bi-model architecture, SiameseTabNet in learning discriminative representations from complex tabular data.
