title: PulmoNet: A Deep Learning Framework for Pneumonia Detection in Chest X-Ray Images with Feature Analysis
authors: Amjad Albashayreh, Maria Yousef, Loai Al-Alnemer, Alhareth Abdelmuhdi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: Pneumonia remains a major global health challenge, underscoring the need for accurate and automated diagnostic solutions. Previous studies have widely applied transfer learning–based deep learning models for pneumonia detection; however, many rely on a limited range of architectures and provide insufficient analysis of model interpretability and decision-making behavior. To address these limitations, this paper proposes PulmoNet, a comprehensive deep learning framework for pneumonia detection that systematically evaluates multiple high-capacity architectures while integrating feature-level explainability analysis. The proposed framework encompasses data collection, preprocessing, feature engineering, model training, performance evaluation, and explainable feature attribution. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that among the evaluated architectures, ResNet152 achieved the best performance, attaining an accuracy of 94.39% and an F1-score of $94.31 \%$, with a detection time of 13.31 seconds. Beyond classification accuracy, the framework incorporates explainability techniques to enhance transparency and clinical interpretability. These findings suggest that PulmoNet provides a structured and interpretable evaluation pipeline that can support reliable and efficient pneumonia diagnosis in clinical settings.

title: PID-Based Position Control of a Robotic Arm with Adaptive Gain Tuning
authors: Murad Bashabsheh, Maen Alzubi, Mohammad Alrefai, Alaa AlZoubi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: Position control is a critical for robotic arms operating in environments with varying dynamic conditions and external disturbances. Although proportional-integralderivative (PID) controllers are widely used due to their simplicity, fixed controller gains often result in degraded performance, when the system dynamics change or disturbances occur. This paper addresses this limitation by proposing a position control strategy based on an adaptive PID controller, in which the proportional and derivative gains are dynamically adjusted in real time according to the tracking error and joint velocity, resulting in up to 66% reduction in RMSE and 50% faster settling time compared to conventional PID controllers. Simulations are conducted on a two-link planar robotic arm with step inputs, sinusoidal trajectory tracking, and external disturbances, using a 1 ms sampling time for a detailed dynamic evaluation. Quantitative performance metrics are compared with conventional fixed-gain PID and Ziegler-Nichols tuned PID controllers, including root mean square error, maximum deviation, and recovery time. Simulation results demonstrate that the adaptive PID controller achieves improved tracking accuracy, faster transient response, and enhanced robustness. Specifically, the proposed approach decreases the tracking error by approximately 0.006 rad, exhibits lower overshoot, and recovers more rapidly from external disturbances compared to the benchmark controllers. The disturbance rejection results further confirm that real-time gain adaptation effectively enhances system robustness without increasing controller complexity. These findings indicate that the proposed adaptive PID controller provides an effective and practical solution for high-precision robotic arm position control, with strong potential for future experimental validation and extension to multi-joint robotic systems.

title: A Unified Multi-Task Learning Framework for Ultrasound Image Analysis
authors: Hongshun Ling, Jinjing Wu, Shao Tong, Li Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Ultrasound imaging involves diverse anatomical targets and variable acquisition settings, which complicates the creation of broadly applicable analytical tools. Conventional deep learning approaches are often narrowly tailored to individual tasks, which restricts their potential to serve as versatile foundation models in clinical contexts. In response, the Foundation Model Challenge for Ultrasound Image Analysis (FMC_UIA) 2026 establishes a comprehensive benchmark aggregating 27 distinct tasks-including segmentation, classification, detection, and regression. This paper describes our proposed solution for FMC_UIA 2026, which adopts a unified Multi-Head Multi-Task Learning (MH-MTL) architecture with hard parameter sharing. A MiT-B5 backbone pre-trained on ImageNet provides rich feature representations. For different tasks, we use multiple task heads for prediction, and we use a routing mechanism to control the task heads. Training proceeds in two phases-initial multitask joint learning, followed by targeted fine-tuning per task group. The experimental results have confirmed the feasibility and stability of this unified method. Finally, our total score on the test set was 0.9215, and we ranked third. The code is publicly available at https://github.com/linghs/ISBI-FMC-UIA-Challenge.

title: Optimizing Gate Resistance in RC-IGBTs Through Analysis of Dynamic Switching Characteristics
authors: Hideo Komo, Michael Rogers, Mark Steiner, Kentaro Yoshida, Tsuyoshi Osaga, Daiki Sasahara
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: In response to the increasing demand for higher efficiency in vehicle electrification, silicon (Si) Reverse-Conducting Insulated Gate Bipolar Transistor (RC-IGBT) technology offers an effective solution. By integrating the freewheeling diode within the IGBT structure, RC-IGBTs significantly reduce chip area and simplify module architecture, thereby contributing to overall system miniaturization. This paper presents a quantitative evaluation comparing the performance of an RC-IGBT-based module with conventional bare-die configurations, focusing on thermal resistance, reverse recovery behavior, and switching losses. Additionally, the required total chip area under identical operating condition is compared between separate IGBT and diode chipsets and RC-IGBTs. The results demonstrate that RC-IGBT technology provides a promising pathway toward more compact and efficient automotive power modules.

title: Exploring Intrinsic Hierarchical Organization for Medical Diagnosis
authors: Chengzhi Cao, Min Xu
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: We propose a structured knowledge-driven framework that explores intrinsic hierarchical organization to represent highorder dependencies within and across biological scales for interpretable diagnosis. Our approach is motivated by the inherent hierarchical structure of biological systems, where complex physiological functions arise from interactions across multiple organizational levels, including intra-level interactions and inter-level relationships. By integrating them into a unified hypergraph representation, we derive interpretable features that preserve structural dependencies across scales and offer a natural scaffold to integrate heterogeneous medical data in a unified, structured way. Each layer first models group-wise interactions through the hyperedges within the hypergraph, and then fuses information along the bidirectional path formed by the connections between different scales throughout the entire hierarchical structure. In this way, local and hierarchical information can interweave and complement each other. Probabilistic inference over the hypergraph space allows us to uncover latent hierarchical patterns that are predictive of disease states while remaining aligned with biological plausibility. Experimental results on medical report generation and medicalVQA tasks demonstrate improved diagnostic accuracy, suggesting that modeling biological data through hierarchy-based structures offers a principled path for medical diagnosis.

title: Realizing High-Q Quasi-BIC Through Monolithic Dimer Metasurface in SOI Platform
authors: Abdullah Bin Shams, Shadman Shahriar Sharar, J Stewart Aitchison
abstract: We introduce a novel monolithic dimer meta-atom design that exhibits a coupled resonant behavior characteristic of a conventional dimer. Such metasurface, relative to dimers, demonstrate higher Q-factor, lower fabrication error and less optimization parameters. These attributes are ideal to achieve an experimental high-Q Quasi-Bound-States-in-Continuum.

title: Geometric, Photometric, and Semantic Multi-Layer 3D Mapping Towards a Forest Digital Twin
authors: Dinis S. Simões, Arush Mendon, M. Eduarda Andrada, Afonso E. Carvalho, Mário P. Cristóvão, Tobias Marsh-Hulland, Duarte Cruz, André M. Silva, Luís M. Gaspar, Paulo Peixoto, David Portugal, Carlos Viegas, Domingos X. Viegas
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Autonomous Robot Systems and Competitions (ICARSC)
abstract: Accurate, rich 3D forest representations are essential for perception, analysis, scene understanding and decision-making in woodland and off-road rugged terrain scenarios. This paper presents an approach for multi-layer 3D mapping for forest environments, integrating geometric, photometric, and semantic representations towards unified spatial models.The geometric layer is constructed using a state-of-the-art factor graph–based SLAM formulation with global georeferencing, enabling consistent large-scale mapping. The photometric layer is obtained through precise multi-sensor registration between LiDAR and RGB imagery, allowing the projection of color information onto the 3D structure. For the semantic layer, a transformer-based deep learning model is employed to perform dense semantic segmentation, enriching the map with comprehensive classification of entities, terrain, and structures.Experimental results in a real-world wildland-urban interface environment highlight the capabilities and limitations of each layer, as well as their combined suitability for off-road rural scene representation. The proposed mapping methods constitute a key step towards interpretable digital twins for forest management.

title: Multi-Label Eye Disease Diagnosis from Paired Fundus Images Using EfficientNet-ML-Decoder and SAM with Grad-CAM Explanations
authors: Nabeel Ahmed Awan, Adeel Ahmed Awan
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: Multi-label eye disease diagnosis from retinal fundus images is central to scalable screening, yet it remains challenging due to co-morbidity, class imbalance, and distribution shift across acquisition settings. This paper presents an explainable and reliability-aware screening pipeline based on a strong pub-lic baseline that combines EfficientNet feature extraction with an ML-Decoder multi-label head. Using the ODIR-5K paired-fundus benchmark, we summarize a reproducible evaluation across threefold cross-validation and off-site/on-site testing. The best configuration (EfficientNet-B5 + MLDecoder + Sharpness-Aware Minimization) achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 8. 9 6 \%}$</tex> Cohen's kappa, 92.48% macro-F1, and 94.80% macro-AUC on threefold cross-validation, and maintains strong discrimination under domain shift with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 0 2 \%} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{9 3. 8 4 \%}$</tex> macro-AUC on off-site/on-site testing, respectively. Class-wise analysis shows high accuracy for DR, AMD, hypertension, cataract, and myopia, while “Normal” and “Other abnormalities” remain comparatively difficult, indicating the impact of intra-class variability and label ambiguity. To support transparency, we include Grad-CAM explanations for qualitative verification and error analysis. The results demonstrate that an EfficientNet + ML-Decoder foundation, strengthened with SAM, provides a competitive and practical baseline for multi-disease fundus screening suitable for engineering-oriented deployment studies.

title: Soft-Start Control for SS-WPT in EV Charging
authors: Amirreza Mizani, Azam Jaman, Sajad Saberi, Jaber Abu Qahouq
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The Series-Series (SS) compensation topology is widely adopted in wireless power transfer (WPT) systems due to its simple structure and load-independent characteristics. However, it is highly susceptible to short-circuit and open-circuit conditions, particularly during startup, which can cause severe inrush currents and voltage spikes. In electric vehicle (EV) applications, these transients pose significant safety and reliability challenges when transferring power from the transmitter to the receiver. Specifically, short-circuit conditions can lead to excessive inrush currents on the receiver side, while open-circuit conditions may result in high currents on the transmitter side, consequently producing overvoltage on the receiver side and potentially damaging semiconductor devices. To address these issues, the proposed method regulates the voltage applied to the transmitter coil through gradual switch activation and switching frequency adjustment. This soft-start strategy effectively mitigates both short-circuit and open-circuit risks during system startup. The proposed control approach is thoroughly analyzed, and its effectiveness is experimentally validated using a 1.4-kW laboratory prototype.

title: Boltzmann-Inspired Model for FMRI Time-Series Classification
authors: Joseph Van Duyn, Pavel Popov, Armin Iraji, Yang Liu, Zening Fu, Vince D. Calhoun, Sergey M. Plis, Alex Fedorov
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Accurate classification of psychiatric and neurological conditions and age prediction from resting-state fMRI remain challenging, as current state-of-the-art methods often depend on complex architectures that are difficult to interpret. We propose a Boltzmann-inspired Model (BiM) for fMRI time-series classification that introduces learnable bilinear interactions to capture second-order dependencies between brain regions, analogous to the pairwise term in a Boltzmann energy function. BiM processes each time point with parallel bilinear and linear pathways to produce time-step representation, which is then aggregated over time for classification. We evaluate BiM on multiple disorder and disease datasets as well as an age-classification task using both ICA-derived components and ROI parcellations, and compare it against state-of-the-art time-series models and functional network connectivity-based baselines. The proposed model demonstrates competitive or superior classification performance relative to the compared methods. These results suggest that relatively simple, bilinear architectures can match or surpass more complex models.

title: Adaptive Boost Converter-Controlled Power Management System for Resilient Multi-Cell Batteries
authors: Sivaramakrishnan R, M Lakshmi Keerthana, T Rajalakshmi
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: The use of multi-cell lithium battery packs has increased widely in many modern electronic and energy storage systems. Despite this growth, maintaining safe and stable operation of these batteries remains an important challenge. In multi-cell setup, the degradation or failure of even a single cell can disturb the voltage balance of the entire pack. Such imbalance can reduce the overall efficiency and may also lead to unsafe operating conditions or sudden power loss. Most conventional battery management systems are designed with fixed configurations and may not operate effectively when partial faults occur within the battery pack. In this work, a re-configurable and fault-tolerant battery management approach is proposed to improve the reliability of multi-cell battery systems. The proposed system adapts its operation depending on the condition of the available cells and handles abnormal situations in a more practical manner. As a result, the system can continue operating without immediate shutdown even when certain cells begin to degrade. This improves system reliability, enhances operational safety, and allows better utilization of the available energy. Therefore, the proposed method can be useful for battery-powered applications where continuous and dependable power supply is essential.

title: Hybrid LightFM Framework for Personalized Gamified Learning: A Novel Recommender Approach for Toddlers
authors: Vijayalaxmi N Rathod, R.H. Goudar, Dhananjaya G.M.
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Personalized learning has become an essential paradigm in technology-enhanced education, yet most existing recommender systems are designed for older learners and rarely address the unique needs of toddlers. Early childhood learning requires approaches that account for developmental diversity, short attention spans, and the importance of engaging stimuli. This paper presents a hybrid LightFM-based recommendation framework that integrates toddler behavioral attributes from autism screening datasets with gamified content descriptors such as difficulty level, sensory load, and adaptive rewards. The goal is to deliver developmentally appropriate, motivating, and inclusive learning pathways for young children. A synthetic engagement simulation protocol was introduced to overcome the absence of large-scale toddler interaction data, enabling the generation of reward signals based on completion rates and time-on-task thresholds. Experimental evaluation compared the proposed framework against traditional baselines, including random, content-based, and supervised predictors, as well as ablated versions of LightFM. Results demonstrate that the proposed model achieved superior predictive accuracy (AUC 0.83, Precision@1 0.62, NDCG@5 0.71) while also maximizing cumulative rewards, minimizing regret, and ensuring higher diversity and faster convergence during simulated interactions. These findings validate the methodological soundness of the approach and highlight its potential to support personalized gamified learning in early childhood. Future work will involve real-world user studies, multimodal feature integration, and privacy-preserving deployment strategies to strengthen the practical applicability of the framework.

title: Design and Implementation of an Integrated Pharmacy Management System with Customer Medication Reminder and Location Services
authors: Sarah Al-Shammary, Shefa M. Tawalbeh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: This paper presents a design and implementation method of a Pharmacy Management System (PMS) that combines pharmacy operational management with customer support services. Such integrated platforms align with the ongoing digital transformation in PMSs and improve efficiency, reliability, and data handling through centralized storage and controlled access. Existing solutions focus on single function; such as inventory management, reminders, or pharmacy location; leading to fragmented frameworks. This work proposes a unified system that combines PMS and patient support in a single platform. The PMS presented here consists of three main modules; administrator, pharmacist, and customer modules. The administrator is capable of managing, controlling, and overseeing of the whole system. Pharmacists perform pharmacy operations through inventory management, medicine validity monitoring, prescription handling, and sales processing. Customer module provides customer services such as viewing medication, scheduling medication reminders, locating nearby pharmacies using medication reminder and location services. The system components were implemented and integrated using three main software’s. Specifically, the central hub of the system was ASP.NET Web forms with C# application, where it was used for user interface development and server-side processing, while C# implemented all CRUD operations, user authentication, and access control functions. SQL Server served as the system’s backend database. Finally, Python tools were used to implement and integrate the reminder and the interactive pharmacy locator map services. Overall, functional and integration testing indicate reliable workflow execution across modules, improved usability, and enhanced safety through prescription-related checks and validity monitoring. The PMS application can be downloaded from GitHub: sarah-alshammary/Pharmacy-Management-System, README file contains steps to download, configure, and run the application.

title: RTFGM-Net: A Deep Learning Model for Auto-Detection of Hypsarrhythmia EEG in West Syndrome
authors: Chenghao Xue, Yifei Bai, Yuanning Li, Dake He, Lin Xu
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: West syndrome (WS) is a neurodevelopmental disorder characterized by developmental delays and motor spasms, often diagnosed through visual detection of hypsarrhythmia in long-term electroencephalography (EEG) recordings. However, manual analysis is time-consuming and prone to errors, underscoring the need for automated diagnostic approaches. In this paper, we propose ResNet-based Time-Frequency Gating Mamba Network RTFGM-Net, an efficient deep learning framework for automated hypsarrhythmia detection in EEG signals. The model integrates a Bidirectional Mamba (BiMamba) and an Efficient Frequency-Time Gating (EFTG) module. The EFTG module combines point-wise and depthwise convolutions with a learnable frequency-domain filter for effective feature extraction, while the BiMamba module captures long-range temporal dependencies through bidirectional processing. Experimental results demonstrate that RTFGM-Net outperforms existing methods, achieving an accuracy of 91.01% and F1 score of 90.33%, highlighting its potential to facilitate reliable WS diagnosis in clinical applications.

title: Operational Mode Analysis of the Isolated Three-Phase DC/DC Converter
authors: Stefan Subotic, Max Dupont, Ralph Burkart, Thomas Gradinger, Drazen Dujic
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of the operational modes of the unidirectional DC/DC converter, featuring a medium frequency Y-Y transformer and output filter inductor (3-ph DC/DC). All modes of operation are presented for the first time for this converter and mechanisms and conditions governing the transitions between the modes are discussed. The converter steady-state model is given. Steady-state operation is studied in detail and trade-offs between operational modes are identified from the perspective of input/output voltage controllability, device current stress, output current ripple and soft switching range implications, providing the necessary basis for the converter design. To validate the theoretical findings, a small-scale prototype of the converter was commissioned and used for experimental tests.

title: Multi-Target Cervical Cancer Risk Prediction: Ensemble Machine Learning and Deep Learning Approaches with Advanced Feature Engineering
authors: K Smith Newton, K Achuthan, B.Senthil Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: Cervical cancer remains a serious health concern, particularly in low- and middle-income countries, were limited access to early screening increases mortality. Accurate risk classification across multiple diagnostic modalities is essential for optimal clinical decision-making. Accurate risk classification in a variety of diagnostic modalities is essential to an optimal clinical decision-making. The research presents a multi-target cervical cancer prediction framework incorporating advanced feature engineering, target-specific feature selection, and class imbalance mitigation using the Synthetic Minority Over-Sampling Technique (SMOTE). Five machine-learning algorithms, including the Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, Gradient Boosting algorithms and CatBoost algorithm, and two deeplearning architectures, i.e., Deep Neural Network and Residual Network were evaluated in a comparative analysis. The framework is intended to simultaneously forecast four clinically relevant outcomes, including Schiller reaction, cytologic classification, biopsy status, and Hinselmann test. The practical evidence obtained based on the UCI Cervical Cancer Risk Factors dataset (n=858) suggests that the models based on the tree are better than deep-learning ones. Specifically, the overall accuracy of Random Forest was 87.6 per cent and the mean area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC-ROC) was 0.89 with all targets, however, Schiller had a value of 0.92. The interpretation of the models was promoted using SHAP that measured the contribution of the individual features. However, these findings are derived from a single-source dataset of 858 patients, so it need a cross-validation, external validation on larger multi-institutional cohorts is required to confirm generalizability.

title: Dirichlet-Based Evidential Loss for Uncertainty-Aware Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Blanca Rodriguez-Gonzalez, Javier Hernandez-Rubia, Borja Rodriguez-Vila, Angel Torrado-Carvajal, Norberto Malpica
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Uncertainty quantification (UQ) is critical in medical image segmentation, where model predictions directly impact clinical decisions. However, widely used UQ methods like Monte Carlo Dropout and Deep Ensembles rely on sampling strategies that increase inference time and complicate deployment. To address these limitations, we propose ENSE, a Dirichletbased framework tailored for multiclass segmentation. ENSE replaces the conventional softmax head with a Dirichlet formulation, enabling simultaneous prediction of class probabilities and voxel-wise uncertainty in a single forward pass. Built upon a 2.5 D Attention U-Net, ENSE is trained using a composite loss that balances segmentation accuracy and uncertainty calibration. We benchmark ENSE against deterministic and Bayesian-inspired baselines across three anatomical regions, showing strong segmentation performance and wellcalibrated uncertainty maps. These results highlight ENSE's potential to improve the reliability of automated segmentation systems in clinical environments.

title: A Real-Time Speech-to-Sign Language Translation Framework Using Transformer-Based Gesture Synthesis
authors: Hemantha Kumar B N, Vijay Kumar S, Suchithra H N, Sachitha M S, Shivaprasad B M, Prashanth R Kaigaddi
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Effective communication between hearing individuals and the deaf community continues to be a great challenge because of the linguistic complexity and the visual-spatial element of sign languages. This paper introduces a real-time speech-to-sign language translation framework based on Transformer-based gesture synthesis for natural, expressive and interactive communication. The proposed system includes the Conformer Automatic Speech Recognition module for converting speech to text accurately, and then the Transformer linguistic reordering model to generate sign gloss sequences in accord to the sign language grammar. An editable, intermediate, and human readable representation in a format of editable interview with elements that allow a form of human-in-the-loop correction to allow users to “refine” hand shapes, facial expressions, timing and emphasis. For the task of generating gestures, a Transformer - Mixture Density Network is used to generate smooth and probabilistic joint-level movement trajectories in which both manual and non-manual sign components are taken into consideration. The system utilizes motion statistics based upon motion data from the How2Sign dataset to increase the expressiveness and realism of the system. Experimental evaluation shows the overall accuracy is 92.8% with low end-to-end latency of about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 3 m s}$</tex> guaranteeing real-time performance. User studies have further shown that there is an improvement in comprehension along with an increase in trust and decreased cognitive load. The framework as proposed is a comprehensible and scalable way of providing a practical solution for inclusive speech-to-sign language communication.

title: Ranking Xai Methods for Head and Neck Cancer Outcome Prediction
authors: Baoqiang Ma, Djennifer K. Madzia-Madzou, Rosa C.J. Kraaijveld, Jin Ouyang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: For head and neck cancer (HNC) patients, prognostic outcome prediction can support personalized treatment strategy selection. Improving prediction performance of HNC outcomes has been extensively explored by using advanced artificial intelligence (AI) techniques on PET/CT data. However, the interpretability of AI remains a critical obstacle for its clinical adoption. Unlike previous HNC studies that empirically selected explainable AI (XAI) techniques, we are the first to comprehensively evaluate and rank <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3}$</tex> XAI methods across <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4}$</tex> metrics, covering faithfulness, robustness, complexity and plausibility. Experimental results on the multi-center HECKTOR challenge dataset show large variations across evaluation aspects among different XAI methods, with Integrated Gradients (IG) and DeepLIFT (DL) consistently obtained high rankings for faithfulness, complexity and plausibility. This work highlights the importance of comprehensive XAI method evaluation and can be extended to other medical imaging tasks.

title: Benchmarking Deep Learning for Future Liver Remnant Segmentation in Colorectal Liver Metastasis
authors: Anthony Wu, Arghavan Rezvani, Kela Liu, Roozbeh Houshyar, Pooya Khosravi, Whitney Li, Xiaohui Xie
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of the future liver remnant (FLR) is critical for surgical planning in colorectal liver metastases (CRLM) to prevent fatal post-hepatectomy liver failure. However, this segmentation task is technically challenging due to complex resection boundaries, convoluted hepatic vasculature and diffuse metastatic lesions. A primary bottleneck in developing automated AI tools has been the lack of high-fidelity, validated data. We address this gap by manually refining all 197 volumes from the public CRLM-CT-Seg dataset, creating the first open-source, validated benchmark for this task. We then establish the first segmentation baselines, comparing cascaded (Liver → CRLM → FLR) and end-to-end (E2E) strategies using nnU-Net, SwinUNETR, and STU-Net. We find a cascaded nnU-Net achieves the best final FLR segmentation Dice (0.767), while the pretrained STU-Net provides superior CRLM segmentation (0.620 Dice) and is significantly more robust to cascaded errors. This work provides the first validated benchmark and a reproducible framework to accelerate research in AI-assisted surgical planning.

title: A Winding Thermal Path Analysis Model Considering Conductor-Loop Thermal Resistance
authors: Yachao Liu, Yiming Dong, Bo Ma, Xiaohui Huang, Ji Zhang
abstract: Winding overheating is a key factor limiting the power density of motors. However, equivalent thermal modeling of windings remains challenging due to their fine-scale structure. To address this issue, this paper proposes a winding thermal path analysis model considering conductor-loop thermal resistance, which extends the conventional equivalent thermal conductivity model. The proposed model considers two parallel heat-conduction paths along the main heat-conduction direction of the winding, allowing the thermal behavior inside the winding to be described more accurately. Finite element analysis (FEA) and analytical calculations are then performed to investigate the effects of stator structure and winding pattern on the equivalent thermal conductivity of the winding. Finally, winding samples are fabricated and tested, and the experimental results validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: MDV: Resolving the Auxiliary Data Dilemma in Model Extraction Defenses
authors: Chuang Liang, Jie Huang, Shuaishuai Zhang, Zeping Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Current studies have discovered that model extraction attacks (MEA) can steal the functionality of deep learning (DL) models, thus causing economic loss and other security threats. Extraction attackers can build a clone model locally that has a different structure but similar functionality to the victim model. To counter MEA, defenders utilize realistic auxiliary data to enhance the victim model and produce misleading predictions for attack data. However, these defense methods have three critical problems caused by utilizing realistic auxiliary data. First, in some scenarios, realistic auxiliary data is absent and difficult to obtain. Secondly, the defense effectiveness brought by realistic auxiliary data is unstable. Finally, the realistic auxiliary data did not protect all categories of training data, resulting in higher clone accuracy for some categories. To address these issues, we propose Model Defense Variational Autoencoder (MDV) to generate virtual auxiliary data as a replacement for realistic auxiliary data. MDV combines the Variational Autoencoder (VAE) and classifier, compelling the latent features to obey different multivariate Gaussian distributions according to the categories. Then, MDV samples deep features from low-likelihood regions of different distributions as realistic auxiliary data. During the experimental phase, we apply our auxiliary data to different defense methods that use auxiliary data and compare the defense effects in different scenarios. Experimental results demonstrate that our method effectively addressed the three aforementioned issues.

title: Matrix Transformer for Data Center Applications with Field-Balanced Secondary Winding and Integrated Shielding
authors: Thomas Jochmans, Hans Wouters, Wout Vanderwegen, Diego Acevedo-Bueno, Wilmar Martinez
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Due to the increasing demand and usage of artificial intelligence and cloud-based computing, a significant increase in electricity consumption for data centers is expected in the coming years. To power these data centers, high-efficiency, high-power-density DC-DC converters are needed that can handle large output currents. Matrix transformers are considered a promising candidate for handling the high output current requirements in future data center power supplies. This work presents a 400-48 V four-leg matrix transformer, featuring an improved interleaving structure that reduces current densities within the primary and secondary windings, and generates equal current sharing between the parallel secondary windings. This transformer also features integrated shielding layers to reduce common-mode noise caused by high-frequency operation. Lastly, the influences of core and winding dimensions on several design parameters, including core losses, winding losses, stray capacitances, volume, and footprint, were investigated to determine the most optimal design. A 1 kW 800 kHz four-leg matrix transformer prototype was evaluated for a 400/48 V LLC converter. FEA results show that the current sharing is improved by the proposed winding structure. Furthermore, the capacitance model of the transformer with integrated shielding is validated in Ansys Maxwell and experimentally. The decrease of inter-winding capacitance is quantified, and self-resonance due to the increased intra-winding capacitance is confirmed to be at 1.3 MHz, sufficiently far away from the operating frequency of 800 kHz.

title: Discrete Diffusion-Based Sampling for Massive MIMO Detection
authors: Lanxin He, Zheng Wang, Zhen Gao, Shaoshi Yang, Yongming Huang, Dusit Niyato
abstract: In this paper, we study a sampling-based detection strategy for massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems, driven by a modified discrete diffusion model formulated as an analytical, non-learning sampling process. Built upon this framework, the proposed discrete diffusion-based sampling (DDS) algorithm improves decoding performance by leveraging residual-dependent sampling, compared to the independent randomized successive interference cancellation (SIC). Specifically, the modified diffusion model incorporates a shortcut perturbation toward the SIC solution, a forward diffusion step to enhance diversity, and step-wise alignment with the perturbed received signal. Within this framework, the DDS algorithm further adopts one-dimensional discrete Gaussian distribution, involving a reformulated discrete Gaussian noise and an explicitly characterized sampling range, but retains computational complexity amenable to practical deployment. Moreover, we theoretically demonstrate an improved expected decoding radius over randomized SIC. Finally, simulation results based on massive MIMO detection are presented to confirm performance gain of the proposed DDS algorithm.

title: Onboarding Software Professionals in a Hybrid World
authors: Klaas-Jan Stol, Viktoria Stray, Nils Brede Moe, Tor Sporsem
abstract: Onboarding is an important activity to integrate new employees into an organization as soon as possible, in order to support them in becoming productive. Whereas onboarding has traditionally been done primarily onsite, it is now increasingly common for new software professionals to be onboarded either fully remotely or partially remotely in hybrid settings. We ask how software professionals develop a sense of belonging through the onboarding process. Using a conceptual framework from the general onboarding literature, we report on case studies of onboarding of software professionals in three different settings on the hybrid continuum, drawing on a dataset comprising interviews with 46 informants, Slack logs, observations, and company documents. In Case 1, we focus on the onboarding of consultant developers in two divisions: in one division, the consultants work internally with the consulting organization. In the other division, the consultants are placed with clients. In Case 2, we study the onboarding of software professionals in a hybrid setting, and in Case 3, we study the onboarding of a new team of remote developers based in Portugal, who joined a company in Norway. We extend the literature on onboarding of software professionals by shedding light on how software organizations conduct onboarding in different types of hybrid settings. We extend and refine the initial conceptual model, and formalize it through the development of a series of propositions. We then discuss a number of recommendations for software organizations who wish to onboard new software professionals in different settings on the hybrid continuum.

title: Dynamic performance analysis of BESS in grid disturbance scenarios
authors: Pola Soma Sekhar Reddy, J. Sreedevi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: This paper investigates the dynamic response and stabilizing capabilities of Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) in a power grid under various disturbance scenarios using PSS®E software simulations. Four cases are examined: sudden loss of a large hydro generator, abrupt load shedding, a three-phase fault, and generator loss under different BESS droop settings. The study demonstrates that BESS plays a critical role in maintaining grid stability by supporting both frequency and voltage regulation. In the case of generation loss, BESS discharges active power to limit frequency decline, while during load shedding, it absorbs power to mitigate over-frequency conditions. Under fault conditions, BESS helps reduce rotor angle oscillations and enhances voltage recovery. Additionally, the impact of droop settings on frequency control performance is analysed, highlighting the need for optimal parameter tuning. The results confirm that BESS significantly enhances system resilience and dynamic performance, making it a valuable asset in modern power systems with high renewable penetration.

title: On the Robustness of Foundational 3D Medical Image Segmentation Models Against Imprecise Visual Prompts
authors: Soumitri Chattopadhyay, Başar Demir, Marc Niethammer
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: While 3D foundational models have shown promise for promptable segmentation of medical volumes, their robustness to imprecise prompts remains under-explored. In this work, we aim to address this gap by systematically studying the effect of various controlled perturbations of dense visual prompts, that closely mimic real-world imprecision. By conducting experiments with two recent foundational models on a multi-organ abdominal segmentation task, we reveal several facets of promptable medical segmentation, especially pertaining to reliance on visual shape and spatial cues, and the extent of resilience of models towards certain perturbations. Codes are available at: https://github.com/ucsdbiag/Prompt-Robustness-MedSegFMs

title: TriSafe: Real-Time AI-Driven Fall, Anomaly, and Toxic-Air Protection for Sanitation Workers
authors: Gayathiri Devi G, Kadirvel A, Nivashini M, Devi G
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Sanitation workers face persistent threats from toxic-gas exposure, accidental falls, and hazardous confined spaces, yet existing safety systems remain reactive, fragmented, and rarely tailored to this occupational setting. This paper presents TriSafe, a real-time IoT- and AI-enabled safety monitoring system designed specifically for sanitation environments. The system employs a multi-sensor setup comprising MQ2, MQ6, and MQ135 gas sensors, a DHT11 temperature-humidity sensor, an HC-SR04 ultrasonic module, and an ADXL345 accelerometer, with data transmitted through ESP8266 to the ThingSpeak cloud. Three optimized machine-learning pipelines operate in parallel: (i) a CatBoost classifier for fall detection achieving 87.77% accuracy, (ii) an XGBoost-based air-quality classifier reaching 91.31% accuracy with 0.92 weighted F1-score, and (iii) an Autoencoder for unsupervised anomaly detection. A Flask-based dashboard provides live alerts and trend monitoring with sub-second inference latency. Evaluation over 136,789 sensor records demonstrates the system's accuracy, responsiveness, and practical deployment potential for municipal sanitation scenarios.

title: An IoT-Enabled Deterministic Computational Model for Real-Time Water Quality Assessment of the River Ganges
authors: Yugansh Sachdeva, Achintya Singhal, Manoj Kumar Singh, Vimal Dungdung
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)
abstract: The River Ganges is under enormous pressure due to rapid urban expansion, industrial effluent discharge, agricultural runoff, and increasing hydrological variability. Conventional laboratory-based water-quality monitoring, while analytically sound, suffers from limited temporal resolution and is often unable to record short-term and rapidly evolving changes in water quality. This paper presents an Internet of Things based deterministic (rule-based and reproducible) computational framework for water-quality assessment of the River Ganges. The system integrates in-situ sensing with a computational pipeline aligned with the Central Pollution Control Board guidelines. The pipeline performs parameter transformation, sub-index generation (parameter-wise quality scoring), and weighted aggregation to compute the water quality index. The primary contribution of this work is the development of a transparent, interpretable, and operationally viable framework intended to serve as a foundation for future large-scale deployments. The framework was validated under controlled laboratory conditions across three water-quality scenarios, confirming correct Central Pollution Control Boardaligned Water Quality Index computation across full classification range. Field deployment along the River Ganges is currently in progress. The developed framework provides a structured basis for continuous water-quality surveillance and offers a scalable approach to the development of digitally enabled river management practices.

title: Bridging the Density GAP: Diffusion Model for Stepwise Generation of Dense Cell Images from Sparse Data
authors: Masashi Tahara, Kazuya Nishimura, Shumpei Takezaki, Ryoma Bise
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: High annotation costs are a significant barrier in time-lapse cell imaging, as cell density increases over time, leading to numerous adjoining and difficult-to-distinguish boundaries. Our work is based on the observation that sparse images, which are less costly to annotate, still contain local regions with cell densities comparable to those in dense data. We aim to generate dense data from these sparse images for data augmentation. While attempting to bridge the significant distribution gap between the training (sparse) and target (dense) data in a single large step is a viable approach, it can lead to instabilities in the generation process, resulting in unrealistic images. We find that generation is more stable when this gap is bridged incrementally. Therefore, we propose stepwise generation, a diffusion model based method that incrementally bridges this distribution gap. This approach successfully generates realistic images usable for augmentation. Furthermore, a cell detector trained with data augmented by the generated images achieved high detection accuracy (F1 score) on dense data, surpassing conventional methods. This code is available at https://github.com/Masashi-Tahara/Bridging-the-Density-Gap.

title: SFENet: Shallow Feature Excitation for Retinal Vessel Segmentation
authors: Lei Zhang, Zihuang Wu, Xinyu Xiong, Ming Li, Guanbin Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Retinal vessel segmentation is crucial for ophthalmic diagnosis, yet deploying deep learning models on mobile devices remains challenging due to high computational complexity. We propose a lightweight framework, SFENet, that achieves accurate segmentation with minimal resource requirements. Unlike traditional U-Net-based models that rely on deep, computationally intensive encoders, SFENet employs a streamlined single-branch architecture and emphasizes shallow features. Its core innovations include three modules: a hierarchical receptive field module (HRFM) for efficient multi-scale feature extraction, a feature refinement module (FRM) for enhancing vessel detail through dual-branch fusion, and a spatial context mining module (SCMM) for modeling long-range dependencies to ensure vessel continuity. This design significantly reduces model parameters and inference time while maintaining high accuracy, making SFENet suitable for real-time applications on edge devices. Experiments on multiple public datasets confirm that SFENet outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods.

title: External Spatial Adapter Tuning: Memory-Efficient Adaptation of Foundation Model for Sddh Diagnosis with Ultrasound Images
authors: Xueying Zhou, Jing Shi, Jun Du, Qian Wang, Jun Shi
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Ultrasound-based computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) has indicated effectiveness for developmental dysplasia of the hip (DDH). Recently, foundation models have shown promising potential to further improve the performance of a CAD model. However, their deployment in clinical applications remains challenging due to the high computational cost and overfitting risk of full fine-tuning. To address this issue, a new memory-efficient adaptation framework, named External Spatial Adapter Tuning (ESAT), is proposed for the CAD of DDH. The ESAT develops spatial adapters based on lowrank depthwise separable convolution at each layer of foundation model, so as to extract and refine hierarchical spatial features. These adapter outputs are further fused externally for the classification task, so that the gradient flow is fully decoupled from the foundation model. In real-world DDH diagnosis experiments, the ESAT requires only 30.16 % and 0.304 % of the memory and parameter usage on ViT-Base (as foundation model) compared with the full fine-tuning, yet achieves higher diagnostic accuracy than the full fine-tuning and other parameter-efficient tuning methods.

title: Technology, Talent, and Taxation: A Framework for Sustainable Financial Outcomes Through Audited Statements in Circular Business Models
authors: Rajeswaran Ayyadurai, J. Poongodi, Moses Adah Agana, Muhammad Sajjadur Rahim, Oluyemi Adewole Okunlola, Bibhu Kalyan Mishra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: This work further elucidates the manner in which talent, technology, and tax converge to effectuate sustained financial success for companies in a circular economic mode. This study investigates 272 companies across manufacturing, service, and distribution sectors in reference to how emerging technologies, a skilled and competent labour force, and open-book accounting for financial reporting affect profit generation and long-term viability. The relationships among the core variables considered, namely, total assets, liabilities, social sustainability income, revenue, and financial income, were investigated using multivariate techniques including factor analysis, multiple regression analysis, and ANOVA. The results obtained affirm the fact that the complementarity of advanced technologies in synergy with a competent and skilled workforce raises resource efficiency and profitability; alongside a fair taxation system and audited books of accounts, this significantly enhances the credibility of financial transparency and propels the process of sustainable development. The findings would serve as a guide for these companies to earn every single extra dollar in the circular economy paradigm.

title: Automated CRYPT Architecture Categorization Using Advanced Endoscopy
authors: Ujwala Bhangale Chaudhari, Bisi Bode Kolawole, Cecilia Pugliano, Irene Zammarchi, Giovanni Santacroce, Rocio Del Amor, Pablo Meseguer, Valery Naranjo, Subrata Ghosh, Marietta Iacucci, Enrico Grisan
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Alteration of mucosal structure of the colon is one of the major hallmarks of disease activity in inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD) such as Ulcerative Colitis (UC) and Crohn Diseases (CD). Ultra-magnification Endocytoscopy (ECS) enables real-time, in vivo assessment of mucosal microstructure, but its clinical application remains limited due to the complexity of image interpretation. This work focuses on the assessment of the crypt architecture of the colon mucosa using Endocytoscopy data leveraging recent foundational models in endoscopy, we classify ECS frames as showing normal crypt architecture or necrotic mucosa, domain aware pretrained ResNet-50 model with GastroNet weights is used to extract the features; using 5-fold cross-validation, we achieve a frame level classification accuracy of 79 % and when aggregating the frame scores to obtain a video-level classification, the overall accuracy obtained is 89 % with 83 % sensitivity, 90 % specificity and 91% AUC.

title: Deliver-MPI: Diffusion Embedded-Physics Model for Magnetic Particle Imaging Reconstruction under Strong Liver Background
authors: Ziwei Chen, Wenxuan Zou, Gen Shi, Jian'an Ye, Ning Sun, Zhongwei Bian, Yu An, Jie Tian
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Magnetic Particle Imaging (MPI) is an emerging, radiationfree modality for quantitatively mapping superparamagnetic tracer concentration, yet liver uptake often overwhelms nearby tumor signals. We propose a physics-constrained diffusion reconstruction that injects the system matrix into a pre-trained diffusion prior, alternating denoising with a onestep data-consistency update and null-space conditioning. Without changing hardware or tracers, the method enforces physical fidelity while preserving fine structures. On simulated and measured liver-tumor phantoms, it reduces artifacts and more reliably recovers small lesions adjacent to strong liver signals compared with traditional reconstructions.

title: An Ultra Compact RGB LED Driver with Only One Off-Chip Component, 97.25% Peak Efficiency, and Nearly Full-Range Color Gamut Coverage
authors: Wangkang Lan, Caiyu Tong, Yuan Gao
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The proliferation of intelligent electric vehicles imposes stringent demands on energy efficiency, integration, and reliability for on-board LED drivers, especially in RGB multi-color applications (e.g., automotive ambient lighting) requiring precise color mixing. Conventional LDO or DC-DC buck-boost solutions cannot simultaneously achieve high efficiency and high integration. This paper presents a highly integrated 3-switch RGB LED driver that requires only one off-chip inductor. Its topology merges DC-DC converter switches with RGB channel control switches, reducing switching losses and enhancing integration. The proposed design ensures robust operation against input voltage variation. An integration-comparison control scheme enables stable regulation of RGB color ratios. The driver maintains high efficiency across a wide color gamut and offers an efficiency–color gamut trade-off, enabling expanded gamut coverage at an acceptable efficiency cost. Fabricated in a 180nm CMOS process, the prototype IC achieves a peak efficiency of 97.25% under 5V input with mixed-color light output, demonstrating its potential for energy-efficient, space-constrained automotive LED applications.

title: Dissolving a Digital Relationship: A Critical Examination of Digital Severance Behaviours in Close Relationships CSCW014
authors: Michael Yin, Angela Chiang, Robert Xiao
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Human-Computer Interaction
abstract: None

title: Comparative Analysis of EMI in Full-Bridge Inverters with Si and SiC Power Devices
authors: Sarvvesh R, Dinakar R, Saniya Ahamad, Sai Charan K, Soniya Agrawal, Prema V
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: The versatility and simplicity of the full-bridge inverter make it essential for modern systems like electric vehicles and solar inverters. Silicon (Si) devices remain prevalent due to their cost-effectiveness, reliability, and mature manufacturing. Conversely, silicon carbide (SiC) devices offer higher thermal performance, greater breakdown voltage, and enable higher switching frequencies, reducing losses and system size. However, the fast switching of SiC devices introduces increased electromagnetic interference (EMI) and electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) concerns due to higher dv/dt rates. This paper presents a comparative EMI analysis of a full-bridge inverter using Si and SiC power devices. The study further focuses on the separation and evaluation of common-mode (CM) and differential-mode (DM) EMI components to better understand their individual contributions to total conducted noise. LTSpice, an open-source circuit simulation software, is utilized to model and analyze EMI behavior at a switching frequency of 50 kHz.

title: Self-contrastive Learning to Boost Weakly Supervised Anomaly Detection
authors: Jingyou Chen, Zhangfa Wu, Yi Hua, Hongqi Li, Yilei Shi, Hongping Gan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Weakly supervised anomaly detection methods (WADMs) can effectively utilize incomplete label data to address the issue of imbalanced samples, thereby reducing the reliance on the quantity of labeled data and demonstrating superior anomaly detection capabilities in network and service management. However, when confronted with significant label noise and missing labels, existing WADMs still struggle to adequately extract the deep feature information of samples, leading to a decline in model performance. To tackle this challenge, we propose a self-contrastive enhanced weakly supervised anomaly detection framework, called SEAD-Net, which enhances the feature representation capability of data in the model’s feature space, thereby improving the accuracy and robustness of anomaly detection. Specifically, we first design a personalized data enhancement module that augments data representation by applying various transformations to the raw data. Subsequently, a self-contrastive enhanced learning module is introduced to impose hybrid constraints on the augmented samples, constructing the overall distribution structure while learning deep sample feature spaces under complex scenario disturbances. Finally, we extract contrastive enhancement features within the deep sample feature space and perform probabilistic generation to enable effective decision-making via an anomaly probability generation module. Experimental results on a series of public benchmark datasets demonstrate that our SEAD-Net outperforms the second-best WADM by 5.95% in average AUC-ROC and 16.28% in average AUC-PR.

title: Dynamic Out-of-Band Distortion Suppression With Efficiency Enhancement of Power Amplifier for 6G Flexible Spectrum Application
authors: Huihui Fei, Yucheng Yu, Peng Chen, Chao Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article proposes a dynamic out-of-band (OOB) distortion suppression technique with simultaneous efficiency enhancement of power amplifier (PA) for 6G flexible spectrum applications. By injecting a control signal into the PA’s output network, the proposed method dynamically manipulates the output impedance—realizing in-band matching for efficiency enhancement and OOB mismatching for distortion suppression. The approach enables impedance manipulation across different center frequencies and bandwidths, providing dynamic suppression and improved efficiency in support of flexible spectrum operation. Experimental results show that the proposed technique achieves more than 24 dB adjacent channel leakage ratio (ACLR) improvement and increases the power added efficiency (PAE) by 12% compared with conventional digital predistortion (DPD) under the same ACLR constraint when transmitting a 40-MHz signal at 3.2, 3.4, and 3.6 GHz.

title: Functional Connectivity Alterations in Major Depressive Disorder
authors: Malvika. Sridhar, Sir-Lord Wiafe, Bradley Baker, Vince. D. Calhoun
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Major depressive disorder (MDD) is associated with brain-wide network disruptions. This study investigates a large resting-state functional magnetic resonance imaging dataset <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{N}=519)$</tex> to analyze static and dynamic functional network connectivity (FNC). Using independent component analysis, our analysis revealed hyperconnectivity within sensorimotor and temporal subdomains, hypoconnectivity from higher cognitive networks, and hyperconnectivity from the default mode and sensorimotor domains in MDD. A novel frequency-sensitive dynamic approach identified disruptions in the temporal synchrony of brain states engaging the default mode-paralimbic, sensorimotor, and frontal regions, as well as the subcortical limbic, frontal, and salience regions. Overall, the findings highlight the utility of combining static and dynamic approaches in large neuroimaging datasets to elucidate the neural underpinnings of MDD pathology.

title: A Mathematical Framework for Quantifying DC Capacitor Utilization in Resonant Converters
authors: Sayed Amir Hashemi, Arsalan Rasoolzadeh, Majid Pahlevani
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: In this paper, a mathematical framework is presented to quantify the utilization of DC capacitors in resonant converters. The method begins by defining a base capacitor and dividing it into smaller elements, which are then distributed across all DC-capacitor locations in the circuit. The allocation is obtained through a Lagrangian-based optimization that forces all capacitors to operate under equal electrical stress, thereby minimizing total loss. After the capacitance distribution is determined, the voltage ripple of each capacitor is calculated. By combining the resulting ripple and loss values, a utilization metric is formed that allows fair comparison and ranking of different circuits, since all topologies are evaluated using the same total capacitor material. To demonstrate and validate the proposed method, several rectifier circuits commonly used in resonant converters were analyzed to identify the topology with the highest capacitor utilization. Three of these rectifiers were also implemented in hardware, and the measured results confirm that the proposed framework accurately predicts the performance of each circuit.

title: Hybrid Cryptography Approaches for Securing Data Transmission in Wireless Body Sensor Networks
authors: M. Shamila, Swati, Shitanshu Jain, A. Gopi, Yogesh Ramaswamy, Manish Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: The Wireless Body Sensor Networks (WBSNs) require security measures, which are capable of resisting the ever-changing cyber-threats without violating the tight computational and energy limits. The conventional hybrid cryptography products are based on the combination of fixed symmetric primitives and asymmetric primitives, which can cast some vulnerabilities to key-predictability, small entropy, and even increasing post-quantum threats. To overcome these drawbacks, this paper presents a new security architecture nicknamed Adaptive Multiphase Cryptographic Fusion (AMCF) which is aimed at fostering a dynamically fashioned cryptography landscape that is optimal towards wearable physiological sensing systems. AMCF combines three fundamental innovations: Physiological micro-variation and chaotic micro-reactor combining physiological micro-variation and chaotic dynamics to produce per-segment ephemeral keys; Multiphase Encryption Core for switching between various encryption “phases” from a lightweight symmetric cryptography to hybrid elliptic curve encryption (HEC) and finally to quantum resilient lattice-based encryption; Micro-Segment Transport Encoder to chop up the transmissions into independently encrypted pieces called “micro-units” to avoid interception and reconstructing under interception. A decisionmaking system that is sensitive to energy is used to select a phase that enables a robust security but does not impact the battery life. Cooperation of adaptive cryptographic depth, physiological entropy harvesting and probabilistic engagement of postquantum components in AMCF, improves significantly confidentiality and resistance to interception, spoofing, and future cryptographic attacks. The suggested approach lays the groundwork of safe, scalable, and energy-congruous information security in wearable healthcare systems of the next generation. The proposed AMCF technique attained an overall accuracy of 97.4%.

title: Design and Implementation of an Adaptive Control for Seamless Operation of Multiple Rooftop Solar PV Systems for Remote Zones With Local Grid
authors: Subhadip Chakraborty, Bhim Singh, B.K. Panigrahi
abstract: In remote regions, access to a stable utility grid is often unreliable, necessitating development of autonomous power solutions. Integrating multiple rooftop solar photovoltaic systems (SPVSs) with centrally coordinated battery energy storage (BES) enables cooperative power sharing and energy self-sufficiency through BES-based power levelling and backup support, forming a local sub-grid. Such integration introduces significant challenges in maintaining seamless operational continuity and ensuring high power quality during transitions between grid-connected and standalone modes. Guaranteeing voltage stability and maintaining high-quality injected currents from each SPVS are crucial to safeguard sensitive loads and minimize line losses. Moreover, compliance with stringent voltage and current quality requirements of the IEEE Std. 1547 2018 is essential for interoperability and reliable performance. To address these challenges, this research presents a multisource rooftop SPVS architecture interfaced with a BES-supported local grid that facilitates seamless mode transitions and reliable synchronization with utility grid based on its availability. Proposed control strategy ensures stable and disturbance resilient operation by providing uninterrupted power during periods of low solar generation or a complete grid outage. Effectiveness of proposed control is thoroughly validated through detailed simulation studies under extreme operating scenarios, and through real-time results. Results justify conformity with power quality standards

title: NeuralTouch: Neural Descriptors for Precise Sim-to-Real Tactile Robot Control
authors: Yijiong Lin, Bowen Deng, Keju Pu, Chenghua Lu, Max Yang, Efi Psomopoulou, Nathan F. Lepora
abstract: Grasping accuracy is a critical prerequisite for precise object manipulation that may require careful alignment between the robot hand and an object. Neural descriptor fields (NDFs) offer a promising vision-based method to generate grasping poses that generalize across object categories. However, NDFs alone can produce inaccurate poses due to imperfect camera calibration, incomplete point clouds, and object variability. Alternatively, tactile sensing enables more precise contact, but existing approaches limit policies to simple predefined contact geometries. In this work, we introduce NeuralTouch, a multimodal framework that integrates NDFs and tactile sensing to enable accurate generalizable grasping through gentle physical interaction. Our approach leverages NDFs to implicitly represent the target contact geometry, from which a deep reinforcement learning policy is trained to refine the grasp using tactile feedback. This policy is conditioned on the neural descriptors and does not require explicit specification of contact types. We validate NeuralTouch through ablation studies in simulation and with zero-shot transfer to real-world manipulation tasks, such as peg-out/in-hole and bottle lid opening. Our results show that NeuralTouch significantly improves grasping accuracy and robustness over baseline methods, offering a general framework for precise, contact-rich robotic grasp-based manipulation.

title: Counter-Based Scatter Matrix Estimation for One-Bit Centered Bivariate Cauchy Signals
authors: Yi-Heng Lin, Chun-Lin Liu
abstract: Scatter matrix or covariance matrix estimation under data with outliers is crucial to signal processing applications. These data can be modeled as Cauchy or elliptically symmetric (ES) distributions, under which many estimators have been developed. However, these estimators primarily assume continuous-valued data. For implementation purposes, these data are quantized into discrete levels, which can be represented by finite bits. The complexity of the estimators is also restricted. These hardware constraints degrade the performance of existing scatter matrix estimators. This paper proposes the Cauchy onebit scatter matrix estimator (COSME). The COSME is founded on the proposed integral expressions of the marginal and joint probability for one-bit centered bivariate ES (CB-ES) data. These probabilities are simplified in closed forms for one-bit centered bivariate Cauchy (CB-Cauchy) data. For equal normalized quantization thresholds, these forms admit the COSME with lowcomplexity steps. In particular, these steps involve counting the occurrences of the events in the one-bit CB-Cauchy data and evaluating trigonometric functions. The minimum number of snapshots and the multivariate extension of the COSME are also investigated. Numerical examples demonstrate that the COSME exhibits shorter CPU time under the same number of snapshots and improved estimation performance under the same number of bits, compared to several existing estimators. Empirical results show the robustness of the COSME to Student’s t distributions and parameter variations.

title:
Multimodal MRI and
                    <sup>18</sup>
                    F-FPIA PET for Non-Invasive IDH Mutation Prediction Using MRI-Foundation Model Embeddings
authors: Marianna Inglese, Shah Islam, Laura McLeavy, Francesco Mauri, Tara Barwick, Adam D Waldman, Kevin O'Neill, Matthew Williams, Eric O Aboagye, Nicola Toschi
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Non-invasive prediction of isocitrate dehydrogenase (IDH) mutation status in gliomas remains a major challenge. We assessed the applicability of an MRI-based foundation model for IDH classification in two complementary settings. (1) MRI-only setting: XGBoost classifiers were trained on the BraTS dataset and externally validated on an independent clinical 18F-FPIA PET-MRI (mpFPIA) cohort using features derived exclusively from MRI sequences (T1, FLAIR, ASL, ADC). (2) Multimodal PET-MRI setting: static 18F-FPIA-PET-derived embeddings were integrated with MRI embeddings, and performance was evaluated via 5 -fold crossvalidation within the mpFPIA cohort. In the MRI-only analysis, the T1+FLAIR+ASL combination achieved the strongest external validation (accuracy 0.80, AUC 0.90) after dimensionality reduction to five principal components. When PET embeddings were incorporated, the multimodal model reached its highest accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.90 \pm 0.20)$</tex>, driven primarily by PET. These findings provide preliminary evidence that MRItrained foundation model embeddings transfer effectively to PET, supporting unified multimodal pipelines for molecular glioma characterization.

title: Privacy-preserving Decision-focused Learning for Multi-energy Systems
authors: Yangze Zhou, Ruiyang Yao, Dalin Qin, Yixiong Jia, Yi Wang
abstract: Decision-making for multi-energy system (MES) dispatch depends on accurate load forecasting. Traditionally, load forecasting and decision-making for MES are implemented separately. Forecasting models are typically trained to minimize forecasting errors, overlooking their impact on downstream decision-making. To address this, decision-focused learning (DFL) has been studied to minimize decision-making costs instead. However, practical adoption of DFL in MES faces significant challenges: the process requires sharing sensitive load data and model parameters across multiple sectors, raising serious privacy issues. To this end, we propose a privacy-preserving DFL framework tailored for MES. Our approach introduces information masking to safeguard private data while enabling recovery of decision variables and gradients required for model training. To further enhance security for DFL, we design a safety protocol combining matrix decomposition and homomorphic encryption, effectively preventing collusion and unauthorized data access. Additionally, we develop a privacy-preserving load pattern recognition algorithm, enabling the training of specialized DFL models for heterogeneous load patterns. Theoretical analysis and case studies, including real-world MES data, demonstrate that our framework not only protects privacy but also consistently achieves lower average daily dispatch costs compared to existing methods.

title: IEEE Career Center
authors: N/A

title: Mobility-Aware Wireless Path Optimization with Physics-Guided Surrogates and Reinforcement Learning
authors: Wenyuan Zhu, Ramin Khosravi, Rashid Mirzavand
abstract: Reliable, low-latency wireless links for mobile Internet of Things (IoT) in complex indoor environments are critical for digital twins and augmented reality, where motion and blockage rapidly change line-of-sight and reflected path quality. High-fidelity ray tracing accurately evaluates such paths but is too costly to run online, while reinforcement learning (RL) enables fast sequential path selection yet requires a physically meaningful, transfer-capable representation. In addition, practical mobility-aware operation should optimize not only received power but also propagation delay. To address these needs, this paper proposes a mobility-aware physics-guided surrogate framework for dual-objective wireless path optimization under mobility, together with controller benchmarking and hardware validation. We extend a static surrogate-guided path-optimization pipeline into a mobility-aware framework that combines deployment-aligned state design, controller benchmarking, and competence-based training under receiver motion. Crucially, we integrate a Zad-off–Chu (ZC) correlation-based Time-of-Arrival (ToA) tracker for deployment-time measurement and hardware validation of received power and propagation delay under mobility. Hardware validation confirms that the proposed framework preserves strong agreement between surrogate predictions and measured received power / propagation delay under static and mobile conditions. Experiments show strong agreement between surrogate predictions and measurements, with coefficient of determination for both predicted received power (R2 = 0.9907) and propagation delay (R2 = 0.9848). Combined with initial zero-shot transfer to a held-out environment, the results demonstrate a practical framework that substantially narrows the sim-to-real gap for mobility-aware digital-twin and IoT systems.

title: Fourier-Net+: Band-Limited Spatial Representation for Efficient Medical Image Registration
authors: Xi Jia, Alexander Thorley, Alberto Gomez, Wenqi Lu, Dipak Kotecha, Alejandro F. Frangi, Jinming Duan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: U-Net style networks are commonly utilized in unsupervised image registration to predict dense displacement fields in the full-resolution spatial domain. For high-resolution volumetric image data, this process is, however, resource-intensive and time-consuming. To address this challenge, we propose Fourier-Net+, an image-domain deformable registration framework that operates on real-valued images for broad modality compatibility. Fourier-Net+ uses deterministic Fourier-domain band-limiting for efficient down- and up-sampling and employs a parameter-free, model-driven decoder to learn a band-limited, low-dimensional representation of the displacement field; all learnable network layers are real-valued. In addition, to enhance the registration performance and encourage diffeomorphism, we propose the cascaded and diffeomorphic versions of Fourier-Net+. We evaluate the proposed methods on five datasets, including two brain MRI, one 3-D cardiac MRI (3-D-CMR), one abdominal CT-MR dataset, and one noisy ultrasound cardiac dataset, comparing them against various state-of-the-art approaches. Our Fourier-Net+ and its variants achieve comparable results with these approaches while exhibiting faster training and inference speeds with a lower memory footprint and fewer multiply add operations (mult-adds). For example, on the 3-D-CMR dataset, our Diff-Fourier-Net+ significantly outperforms strong baselines such as TransMorph, TransMatch, and SACB-Net in Dice and HD, as well as in clinical metrics including end-diastolic (ED) volume and ejection fraction estimation, while using substantially less memory and computational cost. This efficiency enables large-scale 3-D registration training on low-VRAM GPUs.

title: Adaptive User Interface Optimization Using a Cognitive-Load-Aware Double Q-Learning Framework
authors: Vishal Nayakwadi, Ajay Chhajed, Aarya Shendaware, Gaurav Tijare, Nimisha Tirpude, Abhijeet Uphade
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: Currently, the majority of applications simply have a single interface that cannot be changed regardless of how individuals use the application. The common method, Q-Learning, can give things a strange or annoying fix, which RL can fix, as it will make things smarter and customized, yet it is overly optimistic. To solve this we implemented Double Q-Learning (DQL) to create an interface that actually changes. We have two AI evaluators, one suggests a change, and the other one verifies whether it is possible. This makes the interface continuously develop and prevents poor decisions. With users in real time; when they click on something, the duration of interaction with a feature, which feature to visit, our framework will continue to make minor adjustments to the layout, content, and priorities of features. We realized that this is the best way to make software significantly easier to interact with, to maintain user engagement and increase customer satisfaction. It performs excellently with websites, phones, and also assistive technology.

title: Super-Resolution MRI Using Latent Fusion and Flow Matching
authors: Yunzhi Xu, Li Zhao
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: High-resolution 3D Magnetic Resonance Images are important for clinical and neuroscience applications, but are timeconsuming. 2D imaging protocols can provide motion-freeze images, but their low through-plane resolution inhibits 3D analysis. Therefore, reconstruction of isotropic 3D MRI from low-resolution scans is highly demanded. Although diffusion models and latent space have been used in super-resolution reconstruction, how to build an effective latent space is still unclear. In this work, a novel two-stage generative framework is proposed, in which a 3D Variational Autoencoder (VAE) is trained on high-resolution images to learn a latent space prior and then fine-tuned to learn low-resolution image features. A conditional flow matching model is trained to bridge the distribution divergence between the VAE latent space and the space representing 3D high-resolution MRIs. Additionally, a latent fusion strategy based on gate maps is proposed to effectively integrate information from the lowresolution MRIs.
