The proposed method demonstrates superior image quality compared with existing techniques in both quantitative metrics and visual fidelity. Code is available at: https://github.com/yunzxu/LFFM_MRI.

title: Learnable Total Variation with Lambda Mapping for Low-Dose CT Denoising
authors: Yusuf Talha Başak, Mehmet Ozan Unal, Metin Ertas, Isa Yildirim
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: While Total Variation (TV) excels in noise reduction and edge preservation, its reliance on a scalar regularization parameter limits adaptivity. In this study, we present a Learnable Total Variation (LTV) framework coupling an unrolled TV solver with a LambdaNet that predicts a per-pixel regularization map. The proposed framework is trained end-to-end to optimize reconstruction and regularization jointly, yielding spatially adaptive smoothing. Experiments on the DeepLesion dataset, using realistic LoDoPaB-CT simulation, show consistent gains over classical TV and FBP+U-Net, achieving up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{+ 3. 7} \mathbf{~ d B}$</tex> PSNR and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 \%}$</tex> relative SSIM improvement. LTV provides an interpretable alternative to black-box CNNs for low-dose CT denoising.

title: When Vision-Language Models Look but Don't See: Anatomical Bias in Endoscopic Spatial Reasoning
authors: Diego Bravo, Daniel Wolf, Juan Hurtado-Tobar, Martín Gómez, Eduardo Romero
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Reliable spatial understanding is essential for complete mucosal inspection during upper gastrointestinal endoscopy, particularly under the Systematic Screening Protocol for the Stomach (SSS). Vision-Language Models (VLMs) show promise for assisting navigation but often rely on textual priors rather than visual information, a limitation known as anatomical bias. To evaluate spatial reasoning, we introduce EndoSSS-RP, a benchmark based on the GastroHUN dataset (380 patients, 2,796 images, 3,678 binary questions). Each question, targeting left/right or above/below relationships between gastric surfaces (e.g., anterior vs. posterior wall), is tested on original, flipped, and rotated images to assess model robustness in determining relative position across different orientations. Prompts are structured across three levels: L1 (anatomical terms only), L2 (anatomical terms with visual markers), and L3 (visual markers only). We evaluate four VLMs (GPT-4o, Gemini-2.5-Flash, JanusPro-7B, and LLaMA-3.2) and find that accuracy declines under transformations when using anatomical prompts (L1, GPT-4o: 68% original, 61% flipped, 46% rotated) but perform consistently better with visual–marker only prompts (L3: 87%). These results reveal anatomical bias and underscore the need for models to rely more heavily on visual evidence in endoscopic AI systems. Code, data, prompts, and evaluation scripts are available at: https://github.com/DiegoBravoH/EndoNavQA.

title: Multi-View Consistent Wound Segmentation with Neural Fields
authors: Remi Chierchia, Léo Lebrat, David Ahmedt-Aristizabal, Yulia Arzhaeva, Olivier Salvado, Clinton Fookes, Rodrigo Santa Cruz
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Wound care is often challenged by the economic and logistical burdens that consistently afflict patients and hospitals worldwide. In recent decades, healthcare professionals have sought support from computer vision and machine learning algorithms. In particular, wound segmentation has gained interest due to its ability to provide professionals with fast, automatic tissue assessment from standard RGB images. Some approaches have extended segmentation to 3D, enabling more complete and precise healing progress tracking. However, inferring multi-view consistent 3D structures from 2D images remains a challenge. In this paper, we evaluate WoundNeRF, a NeRF SDF-based method for estimating robust wound segmentations from automatically generated annotations. We demonstrate the potential of this paradigm in recovering accurate segmentations by comparing it against state-of-the-art Vision Transformer networks and conventional rasterisationbased algorithms. The code will be released to facilitate further development in this promising paradigm.

title: Autonomous Orchestration of IoMT Applications with Network Slicing in Distributed Edge Computing
authors: Hafiz Faheem Shahid, Muhammad Zeeshan, Erkki Harjula
abstract: The extensive use of Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) services requires deployment in distributed computing infrastructures that provide high reliability, low latency and efficient resource usage. However, current orchestration approaches for resource-intensive and dynamic IoMT services face challenges similar to those encountered in conventional networks, such as hardware constraints and limited computing resources at the edge. In this paper, we introduce an autonomous orchestration system framework for deploying IoMT applications by applying resource and network slicing methods in distributed edge computing. The proposed system leverages 5G network slicing to enable three primary categories: uRLLC, mMTC, and eMBB, thereby addressing the distinct Quality of Service (QoS) requirements of IoMT. The discrete-event experiments account for resource constraints at edge nodes, including CPU, GPU, memory, and storage capacity, unlike many prior approaches that primarily focus on latency and bandwidth. This architecture enables dynamic task offloading, supports user mobility, and NS-aware service deployment within the computing zones. The experiment with resource-aware orchestration using an isolated network slice significantly improves system performance compared to the baseline solution, including enhancements in service deployment, user handover count with a high acceptance ratio, minimizing blocking probability, and overall resource efficiency.

title: Single Inverter Driven Segmented DWPT Transmitter Track Based on Separated-Rectangular Quadrature Pad
authors: Hai Xu, Junwei Liu, Jingyu Wang, Fei Xu, Xian Zhang, Zhicong Huang
abstract: Stable output feature and wide array distance are the contradictions of segmented dynamic wireless power transfer (DWPT) systems. Hybrid coupler designs, such as double-D quadrature pad (DDQP) and rectangular solenoidal pad (RSP), are effective technical routes to mitigate this contradiction based on the magnetic flux complementary. However, the magnetic flux zero-crossing polarity reversal results in the need for a specific angle driven via dual-inverters or additional detection and active control efforts, which increases circuit components, size, cost, and complexity of DPWT systems. To overcome this challenge, this paper proposed a novel single inverter driven segmented DWPT transmitter track, which integrates auxiliary separated-rectangular pads and quadrature pads (SRQPs), while the receiver side configures a simple rectangular pad. This segmented coupler exhibits axisymmetric and peak-to-valley complementary features of mutual inductances during dynamic displacement to achieve stable output. Such that this design serves to reduce circuit complexity and simplify the control method, thereby eliminating the need for active detection and control mechanisms. Additionally, a novel DWPT system with a self-decoupled transmitter design method to eliminate cross-coupling effects and a detailed parameter design methodology for further stable power output based on the proposed coupler is developed. Finally, a 300 W DWPT experimental prototype is built to experimentally verify the proposed design. The experiment results show that the output current is around 3 A with less than 4.5% fluctuation, and the efficiency can be maintained at about 89% during the receiver pad displacement process.

title:
Enhanced Polarization Switching in HfZrO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    MFS FeFET Using WO
                    <sub>x</sub>
                    Interlayer
authors: Eunjin Kim, Hyoungjin Park, Jiae Jeong, Jiyong Woo
publish: IEEE Journal of the Electron Devices Society
abstract: We demonstrate that the memory window (MW) of HfZrO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> (HZO)-based ferroelectric field-effect transistors (FeFETs) with an InZnO channel/HZO/W gate stack can be noticeably widened to over 2 V by introducing an amorphous WO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.7</sub> interlayer (IL) into the gate interface. Although an orthorhombic (o-) phase is clearly observed in HZO using microscopic images and diffraction patterns, a narrow MW of 0.5 V is obtained from the FeFET. Unlike those obtained from the HZO FeFET, a large 2 remnant polarization of 19 μC/cm<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> and intense diffraction peaks corresponding to the o-phase are obtained from the HZO/WO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.7</sub> FeFET. Surface topology and elemental analyses reveal that most of the phases of HZO can be uniformly transformed into the o-phase by the amorphous WO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.7</sub> IL with a low thermal expansion coefficient of ~1 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">–6</sup> K<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">–1</sup>. In addition, the WO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.7</sub> IL prevents unwanted defect generation at the HZO/W gate interface. Therefore, the number of dipoles in the HZO/WO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.7</sub> IL FeFET participating in switching increases without charge trapping, thereby improving cycling endurance and low-temperature (–150 °C) operation.

title: Energy-efficient Beamforming for STAR-RIS-aided ISAC with Hardware Impairments: A Generative AI-enabled DRL Method
authors: Yu Yao, Jinju Sun, Pu Miao, Gaojie Chen, Rahim Tafazolli
abstract: This paper investigates an energy-efficient beamforming design for a hardware-impaired simultaneous transmitting and reflecting reconfigurable intelligent surface (STAR-RIS)-aided integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) system, where the base station (BS) concurrently performs target sensing and multi-user communication. Accounting for hardware impairments (HWIs) at the BS, user equipments (UEs) and STAR-RIS, a joint optimization problem is posed to maximize the system energy efficiency, subject to constraints on required sensing and transmission capabilities, and the total power budget. To tackle the intractable conflicts among sensing and transmission metrics introduced by HWIs, we propose a novel learning-based method that integrates a denoising diffusion probabilistic model (DDPM) into a twin-delayed deep deterministic policy gradient (TD3) algorithm enhanced with prioritized experience replay (PER). By leveraging the DDPM and PER for beamforming policy determination, our approach accurately models the complex dynamics, achieving a better balance between sensing and communication performance. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed PER-DDPM-TD3-based beamforming strategy achieves a 69.3% higher energy efficiency performance than the existing deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based method.

title: A Neurosymbolic Multi-Agent System for Explainable and Traceable Decision-Making in Organizational Contexts
authors: Asimina Mertzani, Panagiotis G. Giannopoulos, Vangelis Malamas, Panos T. Chountalas, Thomas K. Dasaklis
abstract: The increasing use of Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Human Resource Management (HRM) raises critical challenges for transparency, explainability, and accountability. While Large Language Models (LLMs) offer powerful natural-language interfaces, their tendency to hallucinate and their non-transparent reasoning limit their suitability for decision-making in HR contexts. We propose a transparent-by-design neurosymbolic decision-support system that integrates a provenance-aware knowledge graph with a modular multi-agent architecture and a constrained LLM interface. A scenario-based evaluation on a public HR dataset using a set of operational, strategic, and evidence-retrieval HR analytics queries shows that the system achieves mean fact-level performance of F1 = 0.677, mean traceability completeness of 0.680, hallucination resistance of 0.733, and mean end-to-end latency of 0.067 seconds, alongside a query-level accuracy of 0.733. The paper contributes to Information Retrieval and Generative AI research by illustrating how neurosymbolic grounding, provenance-aware retrieval, and agent-based architectures can enable trustworthy and governable information systems.

title: Robust Wearable Motion Recognition Using a Two-Stage FMG Pipeline with Rest Filtering and Placement Adaptation
authors: Ye-Jin Lee, Kyoung Tae Kim, Keonyoung Oh, Kang-Ho Lee
abstract: Wearable force myography (FMG) sensors enable continuous motion monitoring through mechanical interaction with soft tissue, but their practical deployment is challenged by rest-related signal ambiguity and placement-induced variability. This study proposes a two-stage Action Discriminator– Action Classifier (ADAC) pipeline that explicitly separates rest-dominant segments from motion-related signals and applies domain-adaptive inference to mitigate placement-induced distribution shifts. Using a lightweight two-channel air-bladder cuff sensor, the ADAC pipeline achieves stable motion recognition under realistic wearing conditions. Under unified cross-participant evaluation with pooled multi-subject data, the proposed framework demonstrates an overall accuracy of 94.9% and an F1-score of 87.9% using cuff-only sensing, without requiring subject-specific calibration. Compared with conventional FMG systems that rely on dense sensing configurations or per-user recalibration, the ADAC pipeline achieves robust performance using only two pressure channels through integrated rest-aware filtering and placement-adaptive classification. These results demonstrate the feasibility of lightweight cuff-type FMG sensors for continuous motion recognition in real-world, multi-user scenarios and underscore the importance of system-level design in pressure-based wearable sensing.

title: Counter-Based Scatter Matrix Estimation for One-Bit Centered Bivariate Cauchy Signals_supp1-3694498.pdf
authors: Chun-Lin Liu
abstract: Scatter matrix or covariance matrix estimation under data with outliers is crucial to signal processing applications. These data can be modeled as Cauchy or elliptically symmetric (ES) distributions, under which many estimators have been developed. However, these estimators primarily assume continuous-valued data. For implementation purposes, these data are quantized into discrete levels, which can be represented by finite bits. The complexity of the estimators is also restricted. These hardware constraints degrade the performance of existing scatter matrix estimators. This paper proposes the Cauchy onebit scatter matrix estimator (COSME). The COSME is founded on the proposed integral expressions of the marginal and joint probability for one-bit centered bivariate ES (CB-ES) data. These probabilities are simplified in closed forms for one-bit centered bivariate Cauchy (CB-Cauchy) data. For equal normalized quantization thresholds, these forms admit the COSME with lowcomplexity steps. In particular, these steps involve counting the occurrences of the events in the one-bit CB-Cauchy data and evaluating trigonometric functions. The minimum number of snapshots and the multivariate extension of the COSME are also investigated. Numerical examples demonstrate that the COSME exhibits shorter CPU time under the same number of snapshots and improved estimation performance under the same number of bits, compared to several existing estimators. Empirical results show the robustness of the COSME to Student’s t distributions and parameter variations.
